description: Unique identifier for each assumption detail record. Primary key for the assumption detail product.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Foreign key reference to the parent assumption set that governs this detail record. Links this detail to its controlling assumption set for valuation runs and experience studies.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: assumption_detail.mortality_table_reference (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the mortality table underlying a mortality-type assumption detail record. Adding mortality_table_id → actuarial.mort
Self-referencing FK on assumption_detail (prior_assumption_detail_id)

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Actuarial assumption details (mortality rates, lapse rates) are segmented by underwriting risk class per PBR/VM-20 requirements. Direct FK to the canonical risk_class definition ensures assumption seg

Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: assumption_detail.underwriting_class is a plain denormalized attribute. Actuarial assumptions (mortality rates, lapse rates) are set at the underwriting class level. Linking to underwriting_class enab
The date on which this assumption detail record was formally approved by the appointed actuary or assumption governance committee.

The basis or philosophy underlying this assumption detail. Indicates whether the assumption is best-estimate, conservative, prescribed by regulation, based on company experience, industry table, or a blended approach.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements, ASC 944

description: Current lifecycle status of this assumption detail record. Indicates whether the assumption is in draft, actively used in valuations, superseded by a newer version, retired, or under review.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Granular subtype within the assumption type. Examples: qx (mortality rate), lapse_rate, disability_incidence, per_policy_expense, credited_rate, credibility_weight. Provides additional specificity for the assumption being modeled.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Type discriminator indicating the category of assumption this detail record represents. Determines which dimensional attributes are applicable and how the rate value should be interpreted.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements, ASC 944

description: Version identifier for this assumption detail record. Supports assumption change tracking and audit trails. Examples: v1.0, v2.1, 2024Q1.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: The attained age of the insured for which this assumption rate applies. Used for age-based segmentation in mortality, lapse, and morbidity assumptions. Null if assumption is not age-dependent.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Free-text comments or notes regarding this assumption detail record. Used for documenting rationale, special considerations, or assumptions about the assumption.
The timestamp when this assumption detail record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Credibility weight applied to company experience versus industry tables. Value between 0 and 1, where 1 represents full credibility to company experience. Used in PBR credibility blending calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Reference to the data source or experience study that supports this assumption detail. Examples: experience_study_2023_Q4, SOA_2017CSO, company_mortality_study_2022, industry_lapse_benchmark.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Distribution channel for which this assumption rate applies. Examples: career_agent, independent_broker, direct, worksite, bank. Used for channel-based lapse and expense segmentation. Null if assumption is channel-neutral.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: The date from which this assumption detail record becomes effective. Used for assumption versioning and time-based assumption changes.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to base assumption rates based on company experience studies. Values greater than 1 increase the base rate; values less than 1 decrease it. Null if no experience adjustment is applied.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: The date on which this assumption detail record expires or is superseded. Null for currently active assumptions with no planned expiration.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Face amount band or size category for which this assumption rate applies. Examples: 0-50k, 50k-100k, 100k-250k, 250k-500k, 500k+. Used for size-based lapse and expense segmentation. Null if assumption is size-neutral.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Gender classification for which this assumption rate applies. M=Male, F=Female, U=Unisex or Unknown. Used for gender-based segmentation in mortality and morbidity assumptions. Null if assumption is gender-neutral.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Geographic region or state code for which this assumption rate applies. Used for regional mortality and lapse segmentation. Null if assumption is geography-neutral.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Issue age band for which this assumption rate applies. Examples: 18-30, 31-40, 41-50, 51-60, 61-70, 71+. Used for issue-age-based segmentation in lapse and mortality assumptions. Null if assumption is issue-age-neutral.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: The timestamp when this assumption detail record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.

Margin percentage added to the base assumption for conservatism or profit loading. Expressed as a percentage. Used in pricing and reserve calculations to provide cushion above best-estimate assumptions.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements, ASC 944

description: The actuarial modeling platform or system that uses or generated this assumption detail. Examples: Prophet, AXIS, MoSes, GGY, internal_model. Supports traceability to modeling systems.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Mortality improvement scale applied to the base mortality table. Examples: Scale_G2, Scale_AA, Scale_BB, none. Applicable for mortality assumption types with projected improvements.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements, SOA Mortality Tables

description: The policy duration (years since issue) for which this assumption rate applies. Used for duration-based segmentation in lapse and mortality assumptions. Null if assumption is not duration-dependent.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Product segment or product type code for which this assumption rate applies. Examples: term, whole_life, universal_life, indexed_universal_life, variable_universal_life. Enables product-specific assumption calibration. Null if assumption applies across all products.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: Unit of measure for the rate_value. Clarifies whether the value is a percentage, decimal fraction, currency amount, per-thousand rate, per-policy amount, or per-unit cost.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, ACORD Data Standards

description: The numeric assumption value for this detail record. Interpretation depends on assumption_type: mortality qx, lapse percentage, expense dollar amount, interest rate percentage, credibility factor. Supports high precision for actuarial calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements, ASC 944

description: The regulatory or accounting framework for which this assumption detail is intended. Examples: GAAP, SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles), IFRS17, PBR (Principle-Based Reserving), VM-20, tax compliance.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, FASB ASC 944, IFRS 17, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Smoking status classification for which this assumption rate applies. Used for risk-class segmentation in mortality assumptions. Null if assumption is not smoker-status-dependent.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: The valuation date for which this assumption detail is calibrated or applicable. Typically aligns with the valuation run date or experience study period end date.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, VM-20 PBR Requirements

description: Unique identifier for the actuarial assumption set. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Assumption sets require formal peer review documentation and actuarial sign-off per ASOP standards. The existing documentation_url is a denormalized text reference; replacing it with a proper FK to do
Reference to the experience study from which this assumption set was derived or updated. Null if not based on a formal experience study.
Reference to the prior version or parent assumption set from which this set was derived. Null if this is the initial version.
The date on which the assumption set was formally approved by the appointed actuary or governance committee.

The basis or philosophy underlying the assumption set: best estimate (unbiased), prudent estimate (conservative), prescribed (regulatory mandate), pricing (product development), experience adjusted (updated from studies), or regulatory minimum.
Detailed narrative description of the assumption set, including its purpose, key assumptions, data sources, and any special considerations or limitations.

Current lifecycle status of the assumption set: draft (in development), under review (pending approval), approved (ready for use), active (currently in use), superseded (replaced by newer set), or archived (historical reference only).
Timestamp when this assumption set record was first created in the system.

Statistical credibility factor (0.00 to 1.00) applied to experience data when blending company-specific experience with industry tables. 1.00 indicates full credibility; lower values indicate partial credibility requiring industry benchmarks.
Reference to the data sources used to calibrate this assumption set (e.g., 'Company Experience 2020-2023', 'SOA 2017 CSO', 'Industry Benchmark Study Q3 2023').
The date from which this assumption set becomes effective for use in actuarial calculations and models.
Reference to the expense assumptions used in this set (e.g., 'Unit Expense Study 2023', 'Per-Policy Maintenance $50', 'Acquisition 100% of Premium'). Links to detailed expense assumption records.
reference: NAIC VM-20, Internal Expense Allocation Policy

description: Percentage loading for expenses (acquisition, maintenance, overhead) applied in pricing or valuation calculations. Expressed as a percentage of premium or account value.
The date on which this assumption set ceases to be valid for new calculations. Null if the set remains open-ended until superseded.

Description of the interest rate scenario or yield curve used in this assumption set (e.g., 'Deterministic Base Scenario', 'Stochastic Mean', 'Rising Rate Scenario', 'NAIC Prescribed Curve'). Links to detailed interest rate assumption records.
reference: NAIC VM-20, FASB ASC 944, ALM Policy

description: Assumed annual investment return rate (net of investment expenses) used in cash flow projections and present value calculations for this assumption set.
reference: NAIC VM-20, FASB ASC 944, ALM Policy

description: Reference to the lapse rate assumptions used in this set (e.g., 'Dynamic Lapse Model 2024', 'Flat 5% Annual', 'Experience Study Q4 2023'). Links to detailed lapse assumption records.
Timestamp when this assumption set record was last modified or updated.

The actuarial modeling platform or system in which this assumption set is used (e.g., Prophet, MoSes, AXIS, GGY, Polysystems, Excel, Custom in-house system). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Prophet|MoSes|AXIS|GGY|Polysystems|Excel|Custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Reference to morbidity (disability, critical illness, LTC) assumptions used in this set. Applicable for products with living benefits or health riders. Null if not applicable.
reference: SOA Morbidity Tables, NAIC Health Valuation Standards

description: Reference to the base mortality table used in this assumption set (e.g., '2017 CSO', '2012 IAM', 'VBT 2015', 'Company Experience Table 2023'). Links to detailed mortality assumption records.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual, SOA Mortality Tables

description: Target new business value (present value of future profits) for pricing-purpose assumption sets. Used in product profitability analysis. Null for non-pricing sets.
reference: Internal Pricing Policy, IFRS 17 CSM Calculation

description: Indicates whether this assumption set has undergone peer review by a second qualified actuary (True) or not (False). Required for certain regulatory filings.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Opinion Requirements, SOA Code of Conduct

description: The date on which peer review of this assumption set was completed. Null if peer review has not been performed.

Comma-separated list or description of product lines, product codes, or product families to which this assumption set applies (e.g., 'Term Life', 'UL', 'IUL', 'SPIA', 'All Annuities'). May reference product_id or product_code in related systems.
reference: Internal Product Catalog, NAIC Product Classification

description: Target profit margin percentage for pricing-purpose assumption sets, expressed as a percentage of premium or account value. Used in product development and rate setting. Null for non-pricing sets.
reference: Internal Pricing Policy, SOA Pricing Practice Notes

description: The regulatory or accounting framework this assumption set supports: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), PBR (Principle-Based Reserving), VM-20, LDTI (Long Duration Targeted Improvements), or tax reserve calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GAAP|SAP|IFRS|PBR|VM20|LDTI|tax_reserve — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC SAP, FASB ASC 944, IFRS 17, NAIC VM-20

description: Indicates whether this assumption set includes reinsurance cession assumptions (True) or is on a gross basis (False). Used to distinguish gross vs. net reserve calculations.
Target return on investment percentage for pricing-purpose assumption sets. Represents the expected ROI on allocated capital. Null for non-pricing sets.
Short alphanumeric code for the assumption set used in actuarial systems (e.g., 'BE2024M', 'PRC2024Q1').
Business-friendly name for the assumption set (e.g., '2024 Best Estimate Mortality', 'Q1 2024 Pricing Assumptions').

Primary business purpose of the assumption set: valuation (reserve calculations), pricing (new product development), repricing (rate adjustments), experience update (assumption refresh), stress testing, or sensitivity analysis.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Standards, SOA Practice Notes

description: Number of stochastic scenarios (e.g., 1000, 10000) used in this assumption set for PBR or IFRS calculations. Null if deterministic.

The IRC Section 7702 compliance test basis for pricing-purpose assumption sets: GPT (Guideline Premium Test), CVAT (Cash Value Accumulation Test), 7702 (general life insurance definition), 7702A (Modified Endowment Contract test), or none if not applicable.
reference: IRC Section 7702, IRC Section 7702A, IRS Revenue Rulings

description: The as-of date for which this assumption set is calibrated or intended to be used (e.g., EOY 2023, BOY 2024). Relevant for reserve snapshots and experience studies.
Sequential version number for this assumption set. Increments with each revision or update to support version control and audit trails.

description: Reference to the mortality improvement scale applied to the base table to project future mortality improvements (e.g., Scale G2, Scale AA, MP-2021). Null if no improvement scale is applied (static table).

description: Free-form text field for additional comments, usage restrictions, special considerations, or actuarial notes regarding the mortality table. May include guidance on appropriate application contexts or known limitations.

Code of the mortality table that this table supersedes or replaces. Null if this is an original table with no predecessor. Provides backward lineage tracking.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Comma-separated list or description of product types for which this mortality table is applicable (e.g., Term Life, Whole Life, Universal Life (UL), Indexed Universal Life (IUL), Variable Universal Life (VUL), Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA), Deferred Annuity).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, NAIC Model Regulation 582

description: Date on which the mortality table was officially published or released by the issuing organization (NAIC, SOA, or company). May differ from the effective date.

description: Indicates whether the mortality table is prescribed or permitted for use under Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP) for regulatory financial reporting. True if compliant, False otherwise.

description: Number of years in the select period during which mortality rates reflect the impact of recent underwriting. After the select period, rates transition to ultimate rates. Null if the table has no select period (ultimate-only table).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Smoking status segmentation in the mortality table: smoker (tobacco users), non_smoker (non-tobacco users), aggregate (blended rates), preferred_non_smoker (best non-smoker class), standard_non_smoker (standard non-smoker class), or tobacco_user (broader tobacco definition).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Code of the mortality table that replaces this table. Null if the table has not been superseded. Used to track table version lineage and ensure proper transition to updated tables.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Standardized code identifier for the mortality table (e.g., CSO2017, VBT2015, 2001CSO). Used as the business key for referencing the table in actuarial systems.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Full descriptive name of the mortality table (e.g., 2017 Commissioners Standard Ordinary Mortality Table, 2015 Valuation Basic Table).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Current lifecycle status of the mortality table: active (currently in use), superseded (replaced by newer table), archived (historical reference only), pending_approval (awaiting regulatory or actuarial approval), or withdrawn (no longer valid).

Classification of the mortality table by its intended actuarial use: valuation (statutory reserve calculations), pricing (product development), underwriting (risk assessment), experience (company-specific studies), reinsurance (treaty pricing), or illustration (sales projections).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, NAIC Model Regulation 582

description: Maximum attained age covered by the ultimate mortality rates in the table. Represents the oldest age for which mortality rates are defined, typically 100, 110, or 120.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Minimum attained age covered by the ultimate mortality rates in the table. Typically the age at which select period ends or the youngest age in an ultimate-only table.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables

description: Version identifier for the mortality table (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Used to track revisions and updates to company-specific or custom tables. Null for static regulatory tables.

Reference to the life insurance or annuity product for which this pricing basis applies.

Total number of observed events (deaths, lapses, disabilities, claims, surrenders) during the study period. Numerator for actual experience rate calculation.

Actual-to-expected ratio calculated as actual_events divided by expected_events. A/E ratio of 1.00 indicates experience matches assumptions; >1.00 indicates worse-than-expected experience; <1.00 indicates better-than-expected experience. Primary output metric for experience studies.
Date on which the experience study was formally approved by the Chief Actuary or Actuarial Committee for use in assumption setting and valuation. Marks transition from draft to approved status.

Boolean indicator (True/False) of whether the assumptions used in this study are locked for regulatory or contractual reasons and cannot be changed despite study results. True indicates assumptions are locked (e.g., PBR deterministic reserve locked assumptions); False indicates assumptions are open for update.

Name or identifier of the assumption table (mortality, lapse, disability, expense) applied to calculate expected events. Examples: '2017 CSO Preferred', '2008 VBT', 'Company Lapse Table v3.2', '2013 IAM Basic'.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual, SOA Mortality Tables

description: Frequency at which this experience study is conducted and assumptions are reviewed for potential updates: annual (yearly), semi-annual (twice per year), quarterly, ad-hoc (event-driven), triennial (every three years).

Free-text field for actuarial commentary, observations, caveats, data quality notes, or special considerations related to this experience study. Used to document context that does not fit structured fields.

Statistical confidence level (e.g., 90.00, 95.00, 99.00) used for credibility calculations and significance testing. Indicates the probability that the observed A/E ratio is not due to random variation.
User ID or name of the actuary or analyst who created this experience study record. Audit trail for accountability and data lineage.
Timestamp when this experience study record was first created in the actuarial system. Audit trail for data lineage and governance.

Qualitative classification of credibility level: full (credibility_weight >= 1.0, fully credible), partial (0.25 <= credibility_weight < 1.0, partially credible), limited (0.0 < credibility_weight < 0.25, limited credibility), none (credibility_weight = 0.0, not credible).

Statistical credibility factor (0.0 to 1.0) assigned to this study based on exposure volume, variance, and confidence level per ASOP 25. Higher credibility indicates greater statistical reliability. Used to blend actual experience with industry tables when setting new assumptions.

Name of the actuarial valuation or experience analysis system from which this study was generated (e.g., 'MoSes', 'AXIS', 'Prophet', 'Milliman Arius', 'Moody's ALFA'). Identifies the system of record for this experience study.

Description of policies or data excluded from the study population (e.g., 'Policies in grace period', 'Reinsured policies', 'Policies with incomplete data', 'First-year policies'). Documents scope limitations and data quality filters.
Total number of expected events based on current assumption tables applied to the study population exposure. Used as the benchmark for actual-to-expected (A/E) ratio calculation.
Total exposure measure for the study population, typically expressed in policy-years, life-years, or face amount-years depending on the study type. Denominator for calculating experience rates.
Estimated financial impact (in USD) on Risk-Based Capital (RBC) or economic capital if the recommended assumption adjustments are adopted. Used for capital planning and solvency assessment.

Estimated financial impact (in USD) on statutory or GAAP reserves if the recommended assumption adjustments are adopted. Positive values indicate reserve increases; negative values indicate reserve decreases. Used for materiality assessment and management decision-making.

Boolean indicator (True/False) of whether the experience study results exceed the company's materiality threshold for assumption changes. True indicates that the variance is material and requires formal assumption update; False indicates variance is within acceptable tolerance.
User ID or name of the actuary or analyst who last modified this experience study record. Audit trail for change tracking and accountability.
Timestamp when this experience study record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for change tracking and version control.
Scheduled date for the next experience study review or update based on the assumption_update_cycle. Used for assumption governance tracking and planning.
Full name of the independent peer reviewer (typically a senior actuary or external consultant) who validated the study methodology and results. Required for material assumption changes.
Total number of policies included in the experience study population during the observation period. Used for credibility assessment and statistical significance.

Product line or segment scope for this study (e.g., 'Term Life', 'Whole Life (WL)', 'Universal Life (UL)', 'Indexed Universal Life (IUL)', 'Variable Universal Life (VUL)', 'Fixed Annuity', 'Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA)', 'Disability Income (DI)'). May be aggregated or granular depending on study design.
reference: NAIC Product Classification, SOA Product Taxonomy

description: Date on which the experience study results and recommendations were published and distributed to stakeholders (pricing, valuation, risk management, senior management). Marks official release.

Actuarially recommended multiplicative adjustment factor to apply to current assumptions based on study results. Calculated using credibility-weighted blend of A/E ratio and current assumptions. Factor of 1.05 means increase assumption rates by 5%; 0.95 means decrease by 5%.

Percentage change recommended for assumption rates, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for +5%, -3.50 for -3.5%). Derived from recommended_adjustment_factor. Used for management reporting and assumption governance.

description: The IFRS 17 portfolio identifier to which this cohort belongs. Portfolios group insurance contracts subject to similar risks and managed together.

Reference to the parent actuarial valuation run that produced this reserve calculation. Links to the valuation cycle execution (e.g., Q4 2023 year-end valuation).
The name of the qualified actuary responsible for this reserve calculation. Required for statutory opinion and regulatory compliance.

description: The change in reserves due to updates or unlocking of actuarial assumptions (mortality, lapse, expense, interest). Required under LDTI for GAAP reporting.
The reserve balance at the beginning of the valuation period (typically January 1 for annual valuations). Used for reserve rollforward analysis.

The actuarial method or formula used for this reserve calculation (e.g., Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method, Gross Premium Valuation, Seriatim Projection, Model Office). Free-text field for method documentation.
The current status of this reserve calculation in the valuation workflow. Tracks progression from draft through final approval.
The date and time when this reserve calculation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.
The CTE confidence level used for stochastic reserve calculations under VM-20 (e.g., 70 for CTE70, 98 for CTE98). Represents the average of the worst X% of scenarios.
The deterministic reserve calculated under VM-20 using prescribed scenarios. One of the three components (DR, SR, NPR) used to determine the final PBR reserve.
The interest rate used to discount future cash flows in the reserve calculation. May be a statutory rate, GAAP upper-medium grade rate, or IFRS discount curve rate depending on reporting basis.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, FASB ASC 944, IFRS 17

description: The reserve balance at the end of the valuation period (typically December 31 for annual valuations). Represents the closing liability position.
The per-policy or per-unit expense assumption used in the reserve calculation. Includes maintenance expenses and overhead allocation.
The change in reserves due to actual experience differing from expected (e.g., actual mortality vs. expected mortality). Component of reserve variance analysis.
The increase in reserves due to interest accretion at the valuation interest rate during the period. Component of the reserve rollforward.
The annual lapse rate assumption applied in the reserve calculation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5% lapse rate).
The change in reserves due to changes in the actuarial valuation model, methodology, or system (e.g., migration from AXIS to Prophet). Tracked separately for audit and reconciliation.
The version identifier of the actuarial model used for this calculation. Critical for model governance, reproducibility, and audit.
The net amount at risk, calculated as death benefit minus account value or reserve. Represents the insurer's exposure to mortality risk.
The net premium reserve calculated using traditional actuarial methods. One of the three components (DR, SR, NPR) used to determine the final PBR reserve, and serves as the floor.
The change in reserves attributable to new business issued during the valuation period. Component of the reserve rollforward.
Free-text field for actuarial notes, assumptions, methodology details, or special considerations for this reserve calculation. Used for documentation and audit trail.
Indicates whether the NPR floor was applied in the final PBR reserve calculation. True if the NPR exceeded the greater of DR and SR.
The portion of the reserve that has been ceded to reinsurers under reinsurance treaties. Reduces the net reserve liability for the direct writer.
The amount recoverable from reinsurers for this reserve. Represents an asset on the balance sheet offsetting the gross reserve liability.

The accounting or regulatory basis under which this reserve is calculated: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles), IFRS 17 (International Financial Reporting Standards), PBR (Principle-Based Reserving per VM-20), or Tax basis.
The calculated reserve amount in the reporting currency. This is the primary liability value for the policy, cohort, or model segment as of the valuation date.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reserve amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).
The reserve amount per unit of coverage (e.g., per $1,000 of face amount). Used for cohort-level and model-segment-level calculations.

The specific type of reserve calculated: NPR (Net Premium Reserve), GPR (Gross Premium Reserve), IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported), DR (Deterministic Reserve per VM-20), SR (Stochastic Reserve per VM-20), LFPB (Liability for Future Policy Benefits under LDTI), CSV (Cash Surrender Value), DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost), PVFB (Present Value of Future Benefits). The number of stochastic scenarios run for VM-20 stochastic reserve (SR) calculations. Typically 1,000 to 10,000 scenarios depending on model complexity.
The change in reserves due to policy terminations (deaths, lapses, surrenders, maturities) during the valuation period. Typically a negative value.
The date and time when this reserve calculation record was last modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes.
The as-of date for this reserve calculation. Typically end-of-year (EOY) or end-of-quarter (EOQ) for statutory and GAAP reporting.

The unamortized deferred acquisition cost balance at the snapshot date. This actuarial calculation feeds into finance.dac_amortization for GAAP booking. This is the actuarial calculation output; finance.dac_amortization is the SSOT for the accounting entry.
The per-policy or aggregate expense assumption used in the reserve calculation. May represent maintenance expenses, claim processing costs, or other operational expenses.

Identifier for the scenario set to which this scenario belongs (e.g., NAIC_2024_VM20_10K, ALM_Q1_2024, ORSA_2024_BASE). Groups scenarios generated together for a specific purpose and valuation date.

The date on which the board of directors approved this stress scenario analysis and capital adequacy conclusions. Required for ORSA regulatory filing and corporate governance documentation.

description: Status of board of directors approval for this stress scenario and its conclusions. ORSA requires board oversight and approval of stress testing results and capital adequacy assessments. 'Pending' indicates awaiting board review. 'Approved' indicates board acceptance. 'Rejected' or 'Requires Revision' indicate the board has requested changes.
reference: NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual Section 3, SOX Section 302

description: The date to which the scenario generator parameters were calibrated (typically the valuation date or a recent market observation date). Ensures scenarios reflect current market conditions.
reference: NAIC VM-20 Section 7.E, American Academy of Actuaries ESG Guidance

description: Management's conclusion regarding capital adequacy under this stress scenario. 'Adequate' indicates the company remains well-capitalized. 'Marginal' indicates the company approaches action levels. 'Inadequate' indicates the company falls below minimum thresholds. 'Requires Management Action' or 'Requires Capital Raise' indicate remediation is needed. Used in ORSA reporting to the board and regulators.
The timestamp when this scenario record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404, NAIC Model Audit Rule

description: The CTE confidence level for which this scenario is used in reserve calculations (e.g., 70.00 for CTE 70 per VM-20, 98.00 for CTE 98 for Variable Annuity reserves per VM-21). Represents the average of the worst X% of scenarios.
reference: NAIC VM-20 Section 4.A, NAIC VM-21 Section 6

description: Serialized representation of the equity index return path for this scenario across all projection periods. Typically stored as a comma-separated or JSON array of monthly or annual returns (e.g., 0.08,-0.02,0.12...). Used for Variable Universal Life (VUL), Variable Annuity (VA), and Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) crediting calculations.

Serialized representation of the interest rate path for this scenario across all projection periods. Typically stored as a comma-separated or JSON array of monthly or annual spot rates (e.g., 0.0325,0.0340,0.0355...). Used in discounting cash flows and crediting rates for universal life and annuity products.

business_glossary_term: Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) Filing Year
description: The calendar year for which this scenario was used in the ORSA filing (e.g., 2024). ORSA filings are required annually by NAIC for insurers meeting size thresholds. Links scenarios to specific regulatory submissions.
The total number of months for which economic paths are projected in this scenario (e.g., 360 months for 30-year projection horizon). Defines the length of the cash flow projection period.

The random number generator seed used to produce this scenario. Ensures reproducibility of scenario generation for audit and validation purposes. Critical for demonstrating compliance with VM-20 requirements that scenarios be reproducible.
reference: NAIC VM-20 Section 7.E, American Academy of Actuaries ESG Guidance

description: The company's Risk-Based Capital (RBC) ratio (Total Adjusted Capital / Authorized Control Level RBC) after applying this stress scenario. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 350.00 means 350% RBC ratio). Used to assess whether the company remains above regulatory action levels (200% Company Action Level, 150% Regulatory Action Level, 100% Authorized Control Level, 70% Mandatory Control Level) under stress.
reference: NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) for Life and Health Insurers, NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual

description: The date on which scenarios and stress testing results were submitted to state insurance regulators as part of ORSA or other regulatory filings. Tracks compliance with annual ORSA filing deadlines.

Free-text description of this scenario, including any special characteristics, stress assumptions, or business context. Used for documentation and communication with management, board, and regulators.

The date on which this scenario was generated by the economic scenario generator. Distinct from valuation_date (the as-of date for reserves) and calibration_date (the market data date). Used for audit trail and version control.
Version number of the scenario generator software used (e.g., 8.1.2). Ensures traceability and reproducibility of scenario generation for regulatory audit.
Sequential number of this scenario within the scenario set (e.g., 1 to 10,000 for VM-20 stochastic reserve calculations). Used for ordering and referencing individual paths.

Business purpose for which this scenario was generated. Discriminates between Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) stochastic reserve calculations, Asset Liability Management (ALM) stress testing, Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) stress scenarios, hedging analysis for Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) and Indexed Universal Life (IUL) products, pricing, and product development.
reference: NAIC VM-20, NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual

description: Current lifecycle status of this scenario. 'Draft' indicates initial generation. 'Validated' indicates actuarial review completed. 'Approved' indicates ready for use in reserve calculations. 'In Use' indicates actively used in current valuation. 'Archived' indicates retained for historical reference. 'Rejected' indicates failed validation and should not be used.

Statistical weight assigned to this scenario for aggregation purposes. For equally weighted scenarios (e.g., NAIC 10,000 scenarios), this is typically 0.0001 (1/10,000). For importance sampling or stratified scenarios, weights may vary. Sum of all weights in a scenario set equals 1.0.

Severity level of the stress applied to this scenario. 'None' for baseline scenarios. Severity can be qualitative (Moderate, Severe, Extreme) or quantitative (1-in-X year event). Used in ORSA and Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) frameworks to assess capital adequacy under adverse conditions. description: File system or cloud storage path where detailed valuation output files (cash flows, seriatim results, summary reports) are stored.
Indicates whether this valuation run follows Principle-Based Reserving methodology per NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20 and VM-21 (True) or uses formula-based reserves (False).
Status of the reconciliation process between valuation results and general ledger or prior period reserves.
reference: SOX Section 404, NAIC Financial Condition Examiners Handbook

description: Indicates whether reinsurance ceded and assumed treaties are reflected in the reserve calculations (True) or if this is a gross reserve run (False).
Total elapsed time in minutes for the valuation model execution, used for performance monitoring and capacity planning.
Date and time when the valuation model execution completed (successfully or with errors).
Descriptive name assigned to the valuation run for identification purposes, often including valuation basis and period (e.g., 'Q4 2023 GAAP Statutory Reserve Calculation').
Business-facing identifier for the valuation run, typically formatted as YYYY-MM-RUN### for external reference and audit trails.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Opinion and Memorandum Regulation

description: Date and time when the valuation model execution was initiated.

Current execution and approval status of the valuation run in its lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Initiated|Running|Completed|Failed|Cancelled|Under Review|Approved — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the valuation run purpose: Production (official reserves), Test (validation), Pricing (product development), Sensitivity (scenario analysis), Experience Study (assumption review), or Ad Hoc (special analysis).
Number of stochastic economic scenarios executed in this valuation run for PBR or IFRS calculations (typically 1 for deterministic, 1000+ for stochastic).
Aggregate account value across all universal life and annuity contracts included in the valuation, representing policyholder fund balances.
Aggregate death benefit face amount across all policies included in the valuation, expressed in the reporting currency.
Aggregate reserve liability calculated by this valuation run, representing the present value of future policy benefits and expenses.
System timestamp when this valuation run record was last modified, supporting audit trails and data lineage.

The accounting or regulatory framework under which reserves are calculated: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Tax basis, Principle-Based Reserving (PBR), or Embedded Value.
The financial reporting date for which reserves and liabilities are being calculated (e.g., December 31, 2023 for year-end valuation).
The reporting period classification: Beginning of Year (BOY), End of Year (EOY), Quarterly, Monthly, or Interim valuation snapshot.
reference: NAIC Quarterly and Annual Statement Filing Requirements

description: The change in total reserve amount compared to the previous valuation period, used for reserve movement analysis and financial reporting.
Total Value of Business Acquired intangible asset balance for acquired blocks of business, amortized over the life of the policies.
Unique surrogate identifier for the guaranteed benefit base record associated with a living benefit rider on an annuity contract. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Living benefit (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB) benefit base net amount at risk drives rider reserve calculations. The assumption set (lapse, utilization, mortality) used for those reserves must be traceable from the
Reference to the annuity contract to which this benefit base and living benefit rider are attached. Links the benefit base to the parent annuity contract record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Benefit base values (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB) are the primary inputs to LDTI/GAAP MRB fair value reserve calculations. Actuaries and finance teams must link each benefit base record to its GAAP reserve

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) create market risk hedged via derivative portfolios. Actuarial teams calculate net amount at risk, hedge effectiveness (ASC 815), and RBC C3 charges by linking

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB) have version-specific roll-up rates, step-up provisions, and guaranteed withdrawal rates. Essential for benefit base calculations, rider charge assessments, and

Foreign key linking to annuity.rider_benefit. Business justification: benefit_base manages the guaranteed benefit base amounts for living benefit riders (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB). rider_benefit is the master record for each optional rider attached to an annuity contract. A ben
Reference to the specific living benefit rider (GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB) attached to the annuity contract that governs this benefit base record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Benefit base amounts drive CARVM/PBR statutory reserve calculations for living benefit riders. State regulators require insurers to demonstrate reserve adequacy at the benefit base level; statutory ac

Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Benefit base values (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) are calculated during actuarial valuation runs. Regulatory reserve reporting requires tracing benefit base amounts to the specific valuation run that produced th

For GMWB riders, the maximum dollar amount the contract owner may withdraw annually while maintaining the guaranteed benefit base. Calculated as the benefit base multiplied by the guaranteed withdrawal rate. Recalculated upon step-up events.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module; IRC Section 72

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the benefit base record, used in policyholder statements, actuarial reports, and regulatory filings. Typically derived from the contract number with a rider suffix.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation on Annuity Suitability

description: Current lifecycle state of the benefit base. Active = benefit base is in force and accumulating or being drawn down; Suspended = temporarily inactive due to excess withdrawal or contract lapse; Exhausted = benefit base has been fully depleted; Terminated = rider has been surrendered or cancelled; Annuitized = contract has entered payout phase under GMIB election.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates whether the contract owner has formally elected to begin receiving guaranteed benefits under the rider. Not Elected = owner has not yet begun taking guaranteed withdrawals or income; Elected = owner is actively receiving guaranteed withdrawals (GMWB) or income (GMIB); Annuitized = contract has been annuitized under GMIB; Exhausted = benefit base depleted.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module; IRC Section 72

description: The most recent contract anniversary date, used as the reference point for automatic step-up evaluation, roll-up credit application, and annual rider charge assessment. Derived from the contract issue date but stored here for operational processing efficiency.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The total dollar amount of withdrawals that have been charged against the benefit base since rider inception. Used to track benefit base depletion and determine remaining guaranteed withdrawal capacity. Excess withdrawals beyond the annual guaranteed amount reduce the benefit base proportionally.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module; IRC Section 72

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all benefit base monetary amounts are denominated (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency annuity platforms and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The current guaranteed benefit base balance as of the most recent valuation date, reflecting all roll-up credits, step-up elections, and withdrawal reductions applied since inception. This is the principal amount used to calculate the guaranteed withdrawal or income benefit. Critical for actuarial reserving under LDTI and IFRS 17.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates whether the contract owner has taken a withdrawal in excess of the annual guaranteed withdrawal amount in the current or any prior contract year. An excess withdrawal triggers a proportional reduction of the benefit base rather than a dollar-for-dollar reduction, and may reset or suspend certain rider provisions.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module

description: The date on which the contract owner first took a withdrawal against the benefit base, triggering the transition from the accumulation phase to the distribution phase. Null if no withdrawals have been taken. Marks the end of the roll-up credit period for most product designs.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module; IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Guaranteed Floor
description: For GMAB riders, the minimum Account Value guaranteed at the GMAB maturity date. If the actual AV at maturity is below this floor, the insurer credits the difference to the contract. Null for GMWB and GMIB riders. Key input for LDTI and IFRS 17 reserve calculations.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Maturity Date
description: For GMAB riders, the contractually defined date on which the guaranteed minimum accumulation amount becomes payable if the contract Account Value is below the guaranteed floor. Null for GMWB and GMIB riders. Triggers actuarial reserve strengthening as the maturity date approaches.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Annuitization Rate
description: For GMIB riders, the guaranteed annuitization rate (expressed as a decimal income factor per dollar of benefit base) used to convert the benefit base into a guaranteed lifetime income stream upon GMIB election. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders. Locked in at rider inception regardless of prevailing interest rates at annuitization.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Eligible Date
description: For GMIB riders, the earliest date on which the contract owner may elect to annuitize under the GMIB guarantee. Derived from inception date plus the GMIB waiting period. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders. Used in benefit election processing and policyholder communications.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Waiting Period (Years)
description: For GMIB riders, the minimum number of contract years that must elapse before the contract owner is eligible to elect the GMIB annuitization option. Typically 10 years. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Withdrawal Rate
description: For GMWB riders, the contractually guaranteed annual withdrawal percentage applied to the benefit base to determine the maximum guaranteed annual withdrawal amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.050000 for 5%). May vary by age band at first withdrawal election.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Indicates whether the living benefit guarantee is currently in-the-money, meaning the benefit base exceeds the contract Account Value (AV). True = guarantee is in-the-money (NAR > 0); False = guarantee is out-of-the-money (AV >= benefit base). Used for actuarial triage, reserve prioritization, and risk management reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) Framework

description: The date on which the living benefit rider and its associated benefit base became effective on the annuity contract. Marks the start of the benefit base accumulation or guarantee period. Used as the anchor date for roll-up credit calculations.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: The initial guaranteed benefit base amount established at rider inception, typically equal to the contract Account Value (AV) at the time the rider was issued or the premium amount for a single-premium contract. Serves as the floor for step-up and roll-up calculations.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: The most recent date on which the benefit base was stepped up to the contract Account Value under the step-up provision. Null if no step-up has occurred since inception. Used to track step-up history and determine eligibility for future step-ups.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The benefit base amount recorded at the time of the most recent step-up event. Provides an audit trail of the step-up history and supports actuarial analysis of step-up frequency and magnitude.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The highest contract Account Value (AV) recorded on any contract anniversary date since rider inception. Used as the step-up candidate value in automatic anniversary step-up provisions. The benefit base is stepped up to the MAV if it exceeds the current benefit base.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The excess of the current benefit base over the contract Account Value (AV), representing the insurer's current financial exposure on the living benefit guarantee. A positive NAR indicates the guarantee is in-the-money. Critical input for actuarial reserving under LDTI, IFRS 17, and RBC calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) Framework; NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP)

description: The age of the contract owner (in whole years) at the time of the first withdrawal from the benefit base. Determines the applicable guaranteed withdrawal rate tier for age-banded GMWB products. Null if no withdrawals have been taken.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: The timestamp at which this benefit base record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and state insurance department data retention regulations.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The timestamp at which this benefit base record was most recently updated in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Indicates whether the annual rider charge is assessed as a percentage of the benefit base or the contract Account Value (AV). Benefit Base = charge applied to the guaranteed benefit base amount; Account Value = charge applied to the current AV. Affects the charge amount when AV diverges from the benefit base.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements

description: The annual fee rate charged for the living benefit rider, expressed as a decimal applied to the benefit base (e.g., 0.010000 for 1.00% per annum). Deducted from the contract Account Value periodically. Used in product profitability analysis and policyholder cost disclosure.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements; FINRA Rule 2330

description: The date on which the living benefit rider and its associated benefit base guarantee period ends, if applicable. Null for riders with no fixed expiry. Relevant for GMAB riders with a defined accumulation period end date.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Classification of the living benefit rider governing this benefit base. GMWB = Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit; GMIB = Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit; GMAB = Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit; GMDB = Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit. Determines the benefit calculation methodology applied.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17

description: The total cumulative dollar amount of guaranteed roll-up credits applied to the benefit base from rider inception through the current valuation date. Key metric for actuarial cost-of-guarantee analysis and LDTI/IFRS 17 reserve adequacy assessment.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP)

description: The cumulative dollar amount of guaranteed roll-up credits applied to the benefit base in the current contract year. Resets at each contract anniversary. Used for in-force management reporting and actuarial experience studies.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The calendar date on which the guaranteed roll-up credit period expires for this benefit base. Derived from the inception date plus the roll-up period in years, but stored explicitly for operational processing and actuarial projection.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The maximum number of contract years during which the guaranteed roll-up rate is applied to grow the benefit base. After this period expires, the benefit base no longer accrues roll-up credits. Common values are 10 or 20 years depending on product design.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The contractually guaranteed annual percentage rate at which the benefit base grows during the accumulation phase, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 for 6%). Applied to the benefit base each year regardless of actual contract Account Value performance. Ceases upon first withdrawal or at the end of the roll-up period.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Code identifying the operational annuity administration system from which this benefit base record was sourced (e.g., SAPIENS_ANNUITY, FAST_ANNUITY). Supports data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The cumulative number of step-up events that have occurred on this benefit base since rider inception. Used in actuarial experience studies and product profitability analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates the type of step-up provision available under the rider. Automatic Anniversary = benefit base is automatically stepped up to the contract AV on each anniversary if AV exceeds current benefit base; Owner Elected = policyholder must actively elect a step-up; None = no step-up provision available.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The most recent date as of which the benefit base values (current benefit base, cumulative withdrawals, roll-up credits) were calculated and recorded. Aligns with the actuarial valuation cycle for LDTI and IFRS 17 reserving.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each annuity contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the annuity_contract master entity.

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Annuity contracts require billing accounts for premium collection, systematic withdrawals, and benefit disbursements. Real business process: premium payment processing, bank account management for own

Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI (ASC 944) and IFRS 17 require every annuity contract to be assigned to an actuarial cohort for CSM, LFPB, and DAC amortization measurement. This is a direct regulatory accounting requirement; eve

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Fixed and indexed annuity general account liabilities are backed by investment portfolios. ALM analysis, reserve adequacy testing, RBC C3 interest rate risk calculations, and ORSA stress testing requi

Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Production reporting, override commission calculations, and territory performance management require linking each annuity contract to its distribution hierarchy node. Insurers roll up contract counts

Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: Annuity contracts are grouped into IFRS 17 cohorts by issue year and profitability for CSM calculation, risk adjustment, and international financial reporting. Required for companies reporting under I

Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: IRS compliance and MEC determination require knowing which IRC 7702 parameter set governs a contract. annuity_contract has tax_qualification_status and is_mec fields whose values are calculated from i

Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Contracts can only be issued in states where carrier holds active license. Links contract state_of_issue to jurisdiction license for validation at issue and ongoing compliance monitoring. Prevents una

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Annuity contracts are issued by specific legal entities for NAIC company code reporting, statutory reserve allocation, RBC calculations, and consolidated financial statements. Fundamental regulatory a

Reference to the party record representing the contract owner — the individual, joint owner, trust, or entity that holds legal ownership of the annuity contract and has rights to make changes, withdrawals, and surrender. Maps to the owner record in the CRM or policyholder master.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; IRC Section 72

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.pbr_model_segment. Business justification: VM-21 principle-based reserving requires each annuity contract to be assigned to a PBR model segment for stochastic and deterministic reserve calculations. This is a direct NAIC regulatory requirement

Reference to the annuity product definition record in the product master, identifying the specific product series, plan code, and feature set (e.g., crediting strategy options, rider availability, surrender charge schedule template) associated with this contract.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Product Filing Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Contracts issued under specific plan versions require version-level rate guarantees, benefit structures, and regulatory compliance parameters. Critical for rate action processing, benefit calculations

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Contracts must use state-approved policy forms at issue. Links contract to approved form filing for state_of_issue validation. Essential for market conduct exam defense and form usage audits. Prevents

Reference to the party record representing the annuitant — the individual whose life expectancy governs the payout calculations and upon whose death the death benefit or survivor benefit is triggered. May differ from the contract owner in trust-owned or entity-owned contracts.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; IRC Section 72

description: Reference to the agent or producer record in the Agent and Commission Management System who sold and submitted this annuity contract. Used for commission calculation, suitability review attribution, and distribution analytics.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model Act; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation #275

description: Foreign key linking to product.rate_action. Business justification: Rate actions drive renewal cap/participation rate changes on FIA contracts. Policyholder notification, opt-out processing, and actuarial impact reporting require linking each contract to the rate_acti

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Life-contingent annuity contracts (life-only payout, GMDB riders) are issued under a specific underwriting risk class governing mortality assumptions, COI charges, and statutory reserve calculations.

Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Agency-level commission statements, production reports, and state appointment verification at contract issuance require linking each annuity contract to its selling agency. The existing producer_id ca

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity contracts are issued under a registered separate account (SEC/NAIC requirement). This link supports contract administration, SEC registration reporting, and NAIC Schedule D filing. A

Foreign key linking to product.state_approval. Business justification: State-specific contract terms (free look period, grace period, nonforfeiture options) derive from the state_approval record for the plan/state combination. DOI compliance and state-specific benefit ad

Foreign key linking to product.suitability_rule. Business justification: Regulatory compliance (FINRA Rule 2330, state suitability laws) requires tracing which suitability_rule version governed each contract at issuance. Compliance audits and DOI examinations depend on thi

Reference to the surrender charge schedule definition record that governs this contract's declining surrender charge percentages by contract year, free withdrawal allowance percentage, MVA applicability, and waiver conditions (nursing home, terminal illness, unemployment, disability). Determined at issuance based on product and state.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Annuity contracts often originate from underwriting applications (deferred annuities sold with life insurance, standalone annuity applications requiring underwriting for large cases or impaired risk a

Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: New business processing and audit trails require tracing which underwriting decision authorized contract issuance. Compliance and new business operations teams use this link to verify approved face am

Current Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of the last valuation date — the total accumulated value including all premiums paid, interest/index credits earned, and minus all withdrawals, fees, and charges. The AV is the primary valuation metric for deferred annuity contracts and drives CSV, death benefit, and GMWB benefit base calculations.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: The valuation date as of which the account_value field was last calculated and recorded. Essential for point-in-time reporting, actuarial reserving, and reconciliation with the Actuarial Valuation System (e.g., AXIS, Prophet).
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: The date on which the contract transitioned from the accumulation phase to the payout (annuitization) phase. Null for contracts still in accumulation. Populated when contract_status = ANNUITIZED. Drives payout benefit calculations and IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio computations.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Externally-known, human-readable contract number assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity) at issuance. Used in all owner correspondence, statements, and regulatory filings. Maps to the contract_id field in the source system.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle state of the annuity contract as maintained in the Annuity Administration Platform. ACTIVE = in-force accumulation or payout; SURRENDERED = full surrender processed; ANNUITIZED = converted to payout phase; LAPSED = terminated for non-payment; PENDING = application received, not yet issued; DEATH_CLAIM = death benefit claim in progress.
reference: NAIC Life & Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Classification of the annuity product type: FIXED (traditional fixed annuity), FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity), VARIABLE (Variable Annuity / VUL-linked), SPIA (Single Premium Immediate Annuity), or MYGA (Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity). Drives product-specific processing rules, crediting methods, and regulatory treatment.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation #275; IRC Section 72

description: The owner's investment in the contract (cost basis) for IRC Section 72 tax purposes — the total after-tax premiums paid minus any previously recovered basis through tax-free distributions. Used to calculate the taxable portion of withdrawals and the exclusion ratio upon annuitization. Carried forward from prior contract in 1035 exchanges.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R

description: The timestamp when this annuity contract record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the data ingestion audit trail timestamp, not the contract issue date. Required for SOX audit trail and data lineage compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Data Quality Standards

description: The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) — the net amount the contract owner would receive upon full surrender, calculated as the Account Value minus applicable surrender charges and market value adjustment (MVA). Represents the owner's actual liquidity value at any point in time.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the annuity contract is denominated (e.g., USD). All monetary values on this contract record are expressed in this currency. Supports multi-currency operations for international annuity products.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The surrender charge percentage currently applicable to this contract based on the current contract year within the surrender charge schedule (e.g., 0.0700 = 7%). Updated annually on the contract anniversary. Used to calculate CSV and net surrender proceeds.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: The contract or policy number of the prior insurance or annuity contract that was exchanged into this contract via a 1035 Exchange. Populated only when is_1035_exchange = True. Required for IRS cost basis continuity and tax reporting compliance.

business_glossary_term: Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) Index Crediting Strategy
description: For FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity) contracts, the selected index crediting strategy (e.g., S&P 500 Annual Point-to-Point with Cap, S&P 500 Monthly Sum with Cap, Bloomberg US Dynamic Balance Index). Determines how index-linked interest credits are calculated. Null for non-FIA contract types. May reference a crediting strategy code from the product definition.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: The date on which the owner's free-look (right to return) period expires. During the free-look period, the owner may cancel the contract and receive a full refund of premiums paid. Typically 10–30 days from contract delivery, as mandated by state insurance regulations.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: The percentage of the account value (or premium) that the owner may withdraw each contract year without incurring surrender charges, as defined in the contract (e.g., 0.1000 = 10% free withdrawal allowance). Unused free withdrawal amounts typically do not carry forward.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: The first premium payment received at contract issuance, in contract currency. For single-premium products (SPIA, MYGA), this is the total purchase payment. For flexible-premium products, this is the first deposit. Used as the baseline for surrender charge calculations and cost basis tracking.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this annuity contract was funded via a 1035 Exchange (tax-free exchange of an existing life insurance policy, endowment, or annuity contract under IRC Section 1035). True = funded by 1035 exchange; False = direct premium payment. Affects cost basis tracking and tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Form 1099-R; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applies to surrenders or withdrawals in excess of the free withdrawal allowance during the surrender charge period. True = MVA applies (adjusts surrender proceeds up or down based on interest rate movements); False = no MVA. Relevant primarily for MYGA and fixed annuity products.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: The date on which the annuity contract was formally issued and became effective. Serves as the anchor date for surrender charge schedule calculations, free-look period, and contract year determinations. Corresponds to the policy_effective_date in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which the annuity contract reaches its scheduled maturity, typically triggering mandatory annuitization or lump-sum distribution. Relevant for MYGA and deferred annuity products. Null for SPIA contracts which begin payout immediately.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Categorizes the legal ownership structure of the annuity contract: INDIVIDUAL (single natural person), JOINT (two natural persons, typically spouses), TRUST (e.g., ILIT — Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust), or ENTITY (corporation, partnership, or other non-natural person). Affects tax treatment, RMD rules, and death benefit distribution.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: The frequency at which annuity income payments are made to the annuitant or payee during the payout phase (MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, SEMI_ANNUAL, ANNUAL). Null for contracts in accumulation phase or with LUMP_SUM payout option.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; IRC Section 72

description: The elected payout option governing how annuity income is distributed during the payout phase: LIFE_ONLY (payments for annuitant's lifetime), LIFE_PERIOD_CERTAIN (lifetime with guaranteed minimum period), JOINT_SURVIVOR (payments continue to survivor), PERIOD_CERTAIN (fixed period regardless of survival), LUMP_SUM (single payment). Null for contracts still in accumulation phase.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; IRS Publication 575

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract is currently in the ACCUMULATION phase (premiums growing tax-deferred) or the PAYOUT phase (periodic income distributions being made to the annuitant). Drives which processing modules are active in the Annuity Administration Platform.

business_glossary_term: Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Amount — Current Year
description: The calculated Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount that must be distributed from this contract in the current calendar year, based on the prior year-end account value and the IRS Uniform Lifetime Table. Null if rmd_required = False. Tracked by the RMD module of the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; SECURE Act 2.0

description: Indicates whether this contract is subject to IRS Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) rules. True = qualified contract (IRA, 403b, etc.) where the owner has reached RMD age and annual distributions are mandated; False = not subject to RMD (non-qualified or owner below RMD age). Drives RMD tracking module in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; SECURE Act 2.0

description: The two-letter US state code of the state in which the annuity contract was issued and delivered. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, surrender charge requirements, free-look period, suitability rules, and tax withholding requirements. Aligns with NAIC state-specific filing requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The total number of contract years during which surrender charges apply, as defined by the product's surrender charge schedule (e.g., 7-year, 10-year surrender charge period). After this period, the contract is fully liquid without surrender charges.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Filing Requirements

description: Indicates the tax qualification status of the annuity contract under IRS rules. NON_QUALIFIED = funded with after-tax dollars, IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio applies; TRADITIONAL_IRA / ROTH_IRA / 403B / 457B / SEP_IRA / SIMPLE_IRA = qualified plan funding with specific RMD and distribution rules. Drives tax reporting (1099-R) and RMD calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NON_QUALIFIED|TRADITIONAL_IRA|ROTH_IRA|403B|457B|SEP_IRA|SIMPLE_IRA — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Sections 72, 408, 403(b), 457; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The timestamp when this annuity contract record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every change event sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform. Required for SOX audit trail, change data capture, and data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Data Quality Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each index crediting strategy or sub-account allocation record associated with an annuity contract. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
Reference to the annuity contract to which this index crediting strategy or sub-account allocation belongs. Links the strategy to its parent contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72
