description: Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Contract-level strategy elections reference product crediting strategy definitions for cap/participation rates, index types, crediting methods, and allocation limits. Essential for index credit calcul

Foreign key linking to investment.derivative_contract. Business justification: Each FIA index crediting strategy term is hedged by a specific index option (derivative contract). This link supports GAAP ASC 815 hedge accounting, NAIC Schedule DB derivative reporting, and hedge ef

Foreign key linking to investment.security. Business justification: FIA index strategies reference specific market indices (S&P 500, NASDAQ-100) that are securities in the investment master. Crediting calculations, cap/participation rate setting, and option pricing re

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: FIA crediting strategies with cap rates, participation rates, and index options are version-specific and change with product filings. Required for rate renewal processing, strategy availability valida

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: FIA crediting strategies (cap, participation, spread rates) must match approved policy forms and illustrations. Links strategy elections to approved form language for regulatory compliance validation.

Reference to the predecessor index crediting strategy record that this record replaced or was renewed from. Enables transfer and rebalance history tracking and supports audit trail requirements for policyholder allocation changes.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Compliance

description: Foreign key linking to product.rate_action. Business justification: FIA renewal rate-setting is driven by rate_action records. index_crediting_strategy has renewal_election and renewal_rate_flag indicating rate renewal events. Linking to rate_action enables renewal ra

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: FIA index crediting strategy parameters (cap rates, participation rates, spreads) are subject to state rate filings. The index_crediting_strategy must reference the approved rate filing to validate th

Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: FIA index crediting strategies are priced and hedged using economic scenario sets for option budget calculations and hedge effectiveness testing. Actuaries use this link to validate that credited rate

Percentage of new premium and/or account value directed to this specific strategy or sub-account. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 25.0000 = 25%). All active strategy allocations for a contract must sum to 100%. Governs how premium deposits and rebalancing flows are distributed.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle status of this strategy election on the contract. 'active' = currently receiving allocations and crediting; 'pending' = elected but not yet effective; 'terminated' = no longer active; 'suspended' = temporarily halted; 'transferred' = funds moved to another strategy.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Account Value (AV) allocated to this FIA strategy at the end of the crediting term after interest has been credited, in USD. Reflects the starting AV plus credited interest minus any withdrawals during the term. Null for open terms and VA sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: Account Value (AV) allocated to this FIA strategy at the beginning of the crediting term, in USD. Serves as the basis for interest crediting calculations at term end. Null for VA sub-account strategies where unit-based valuation applies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: Percentage of index loss absorbed by the carrier before the policyholder bears downside risk, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 10.0000 = 10%). Used in buffered or RILA-style FIA strategies as an alternative to a floor. Null for floor-based strategies and VA sub-accounts.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; SEC Registration (for registered index-linked annuities)

description: Maximum index-linked interest rate that can be credited to the FIA strategy in a given crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 8.5000 = 8.5%). Limits the upside participation in index gains. Null for uncapped strategies and VA sub-accounts. Set by the carrier and subject to contractual minimums filed with state regulators.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; State Department of Insurance Product Filing

description: Timestamp when this index crediting strategy record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Compliance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Actual index-linked interest rate credited to the FIA strategy at the end of the crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage. Reflects the application of cap, participation rate, spread, floor, and buffer mechanics to the raw index change. Populated only after term completion. Null for open terms and VA sub-accounts.

Duration of the FIA index crediting term in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 24 for two-year). Defines the measurement period over which index performance is evaluated and interest is credited. Null for VA sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Fixed interest rate declared by the carrier for a fixed declared rate strategy bucket, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 3.5000 = 3.5%). Applicable only to FIXED_DECLARED and DECLARED_RATE strategy types. Null for index-linked and variable sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; State Department of Insurance Product Filing

description: Indicates whether this strategy is part of a Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) program, where premium is systematically transferred from a fixed account into this strategy over a defined period. Common in variable annuity new business processing.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Date on which this strategy election became or becomes effective for the contract. For FIA strategies, this typically aligns with the start of the crediting term. For VA sub-accounts, this is the date the allocation instruction took effect.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Identifies the business event that created or last modified this strategy election. 'new_business' = elected at contract issue; 'policyholder_change' = changed via service request; 'renewal' = auto-renewed at term end; 'rebalance' = created by rebalancing event; 'transfer' = result of a fund transfer; 'dca_program' = created by DCA program.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Date on which this strategy election expires or is scheduled to terminate. For FIA strategies, this is the end of the crediting term. Null for open-ended variable annuity sub-account allocations. Used to trigger renewal or reallocation workflows.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: Minimum index-linked interest rate guaranteed to be credited to the FIA strategy in a given crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0000 = 0%). Protects the policyholder from negative index returns. Typically 0% for most FIA products. Null for VA sub-accounts.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; State Department of Insurance Product Filing

description: Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (CUSIP) identifier for the variable annuity sub-account fund. Uniquely identifies the registered investment fund underlying the sub-account. Null for FIA index crediting strategies and fixed declared rate strategies. Required for SEC-registered variable products.
reference: SEC Regulation; FINRA; CUSIP Global Services

description: Exchange ticker symbol for the underlying investment fund of a variable annuity sub-account (e.g., 'VFINX', 'AGTHX'). Used for market data feeds and NAV lookups. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.
reference: SEC Regulation; FINRA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The closing value of the underlying market index on the crediting term end date. Used in conjunction with the index start value to calculate the index change percentage for interest crediting. Populated at term maturity. Null for VA sub-account strategies and open terms.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The closing value of the underlying market index on the crediting term start date. Used as the baseline for calculating index-linked interest at term end. Captured at term inception and locked for the duration of the crediting term. Null for VA sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Current market value of the policyholder's holding in the variable annuity sub-account, in USD. Calculated as units held multiplied by the current unit value. Updated daily. Null for FIA index crediting strategies where AV-based valuation applies.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Maximum allocation percentage permitted for this strategy as specified in the product filing, expressed as a decimal percentage. Enforced during allocation change processing to prevent over-concentration in a single strategy.
reference: State Department of Insurance Product Filing; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: Minimum allocation percentage required for this strategy as specified in the product filing, expressed as a decimal percentage. Enforced during allocation change processing to ensure compliance with product design constraints.
reference: State Department of Insurance Product Filing; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: Percentage of the index gain credited to the FIA strategy, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 80.0000 = 80%). Determines the proportion of index performance passed through to the policyholder's account value. Null for VA sub-accounts. Subject to contractual minimums per state-filed product specifications.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; State Department of Insurance Product Filing

description: Classifies the annuity product type to which this strategy applies. FIA = Fixed Indexed Annuity; VA = Variable Annuity; SPIA = Single Premium Immediate Annuity; FIXED = Traditional Fixed Annuity; IUL = Indexed Universal Life. Determines which crediting mechanics and regulatory frameworks apply.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; IRC Section 72

description: Indicates whether automatic periodic rebalancing is enabled for this strategy allocation. When True, the contract's account value is periodically rebalanced back to the target allocation percentage. Applicable primarily to variable annuity sub-account strategies.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Frequency at which automatic rebalancing is performed for this strategy allocation. Applicable when rebalancing_flag is True. Null or 'none' when automatic rebalancing is not elected.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Policyholder's standing instruction for what to do with the FIA strategy allocation at the end of the crediting term. 'renew_same' = automatically renew into the same strategy; 'reallocate' = redistribute per new allocation instructions; 'annuitize' = begin annuity payout; 'surrender' = surrender the contract. Drives automated renewal processing.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this strategy record originated (e.g., 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY', 'FAST_ANNUITY'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and ETL reconciliation.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Compliance

description: Margin or asset fee deducted from the index gain before crediting to the FIA strategy, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.0000 = 2%). Also known as the 'margin' or 'asset fee'. Reduces the effective credited rate. Null for strategies using cap or participation rate mechanics and for VA sub-accounts.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this strategy is currently available for new elections or reallocation on the contract. A strategy may become unavailable if the carrier discontinues it, if it is closed to new money, or if the policyholder's contract vintage does not support it.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Product Filing

description: End date of the current FIA index crediting term. The index value on this date serves as the ending point for point-to-point crediting calculations. Interest is credited to the account value at term end. Null for VA sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Start date of the current FIA index crediting term. The index value on this date serves as the starting point for point-to-point and other crediting method calculations. Null for VA sub-account strategies.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; Sapiens Annuity Platform

description: Indicates whether transfers out of this strategy are currently restricted (e.g., due to market timing restrictions, surrender charge periods, or contractual lock-in provisions). When True, transfer requests from this strategy will be pended for compliance review.
reference: SEC Regulation; FINRA Rule 2010; NAIC Model Regulation 245

business_glossary_term: Unit Value (Net Asset Value per Unit)
description: Current Net Asset Value (NAV) per accumulation unit for the variable annuity sub-account, in USD. Updated daily based on the underlying fund's NAV. Used to calculate the current market value of the policyholder's sub-account holding. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; FINRA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Number of accumulation units held in the variable annuity sub-account as of the most recent valuation date. Multiplied by the unit value to derive the current sub-account market value. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this index crediting strategy record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every change to the record. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: SOX Compliance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date as of which the current unit value, units held, and market value figures are reported. For VA sub-accounts, this is the most recent business day NAV date. For FIA strategies, this is the most recent account value calculation date.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each rider or optional benefit record attached to an annuity contract. Primary key of this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contractual benefit attachment with its own lifecycle, charge structure, and eligibility rules.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: Rider eligibility (LTC, joint-life, disability waiver, nursing home waiver) requires annuitant age, health status, and ADL impairment count. rider_benefit.annuitant_age_at_election is denormalized fro

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Living benefit rider reserve calculations (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB) depend on actuarial assumption sets for lapse, utilization, and mortality. This link supports rider reserve adequacy review, charge r
Reference to the parent annuity contract to which this rider or benefit is attached. Links the rider to its owning contract record in the annuity contract administration system.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Rider benefits (GMDB, GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) are classified as Market Risk Benefits under LDTI, requiring fair value measurement in GAAP reserves. Finance teams link each rider benefit election to its GAAP

Foreign key linking to investment.derivative_contract. Business justification: GMWB/GMDB/GMIB riders are hedged with specific derivative contracts (equity options, variance swaps). This link supports GAAP ASC 815 hedge effectiveness testing, rider-level NAR hedge reporting, and

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Optional riders (death benefit enhancements, income riders) create guaranteed liabilities hedged via derivative portfolios. Risk management calculates net amount at risk, hedge effectiveness, and RBC

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Rider availability, charge rates, benefit structures, and eligibility rules vary by plan version. Essential for rider charge calculations, benefit eligibility validation, and tracking version-specific

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Rider forms require separate state approval; rider_form_number must match approved filing. Links rider elections to approved form filings for compliance validation. Essential for market conduct exams

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Living benefit rider terms (charge_rate, rollup_rate, withdrawal_rate) are derived from actuarial pricing_basis assumptions. This link supports product profitability analysis, charge rate adequacy rev

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Rider charges (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB charge rates) are subject to state rate filings and DOI approval. rider_benefit must reference the approved rate filing to validate that charge_rate complies with filed

Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Contract rider elections reference product rider definitions for benefit structure, charge rate schedules, eligibility rules, and underwriting requirements. Critical for rider charge calculations, ben

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMIB, GMWB, GMDB, LTC) are priced and reserved using the insured's underwriting risk class. Rider charge rates and benefit eligibility thresholds are risk-class-dependent. Actua

Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Living benefit riders require dedicated statutory reserve components under CARVM and PBR. Appointed actuaries must link rider benefit records to statutory reserve calculations for annual statement Exh

Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Living benefit rider elections (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) require suitability reviews under NAIC Senior Protection regulations, particularly for annuitants over 65. The rider_benefit must reference its suitab

business_glossary_term: Activities of Daily Living (ADL) Trigger Count
description: Minimum number of Activities of Daily Living (ADL) impairments required to trigger benefit eligibility under an LTC rider. Typically 2 out of 6 standard ADLs per HIPAA Section 7702B. Null for non-LTC riders.
reference: HIPAA Section 7702B; NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702B

description: Current dollar value of the guaranteed benefit base associated with this rider. For GMWB/GMIB/GMAB riders, this is the protected value used to calculate guaranteed withdrawals or income. Distinct from account value (AV); may be higher than AV after market downturns. Tracked separately in benefit_base product but captured here as the rider's principal financial fact.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates whether the contract owner has formally elected to begin receiving benefits under this rider. Not_elected = rider is in force but benefits not yet activated; Elected = owner has triggered benefit commencement; In_payout = benefit payments are actively being made; Exhausted = benefit pool fully depleted; Waived = owner waived benefit election rights.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; IRC Section 72

description: Earliest calendar date on which the contract owner becomes eligible to elect or receive benefits under this rider, calculated from the effective_date plus any applicable waiting period. Used in customer service, benefit election processing, and proactive outreach campaigns.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The financial base against which the rider charge rate is applied. Benefit_base = charge applied to the guaranteed benefit base; Account_value = charge applied to the current account value (AV); Premium_base = charge applied to cumulative premiums paid; NAV = charge applied to net asset value for variable products.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Frequency at which the rider charge is assessed and deducted from the contract. Drives billing cycle integration and account value deduction scheduling in the annuity administration platform.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Annual rider charge rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.009500 = 0.95% per annum) assessed against the benefit base or account value. This is the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used in premium billing, account value deduction calculations, and profitability analysis.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; IRC Section 72

description: Timestamp when this rider benefit record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.
reference: SOX Section 404; Internal Data Governance Policy; FASB ASC 944

description: Specifies the type of death benefit provided by this rider for GMDB or enhanced death benefit riders. Return_of_premium = beneficiary receives at least cumulative premiums paid; Highest_anniversary = DB equals highest contract anniversary value; Earnings_enhancement = DB includes a percentage of earnings; Standard = base contract DB with no enhancement; None = rider does not provide a death benefit.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Calendar date on which the rider becomes contractually effective and benefit guarantees begin to accrue. Marks the start of the rider's binding period. Used in benefit base calculations, waiting period tracking, and actuarial reserving.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date on which the contract owner formally elected or added this rider to the annuity contract. May differ from effective_date if there is a processing lag or waiting period. Relevant for suitability review and new business processing audit trails.
reference: FINRA Suitability Rules; NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Type of inflation protection feature included in the rider, primarily relevant for LTC riders. None = no inflation protection; Simple = benefit increases by a fixed percentage of the original benefit; Compound = benefit increases by a fixed percentage of the current benefit; CPI_linked = benefit increases tied to the Consumer Price Index.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: Indicates whether this rider, once elected, cannot be removed by the contract owner during the contract's life (True = irrevocable; False = owner may remove the rider). Relevant for LTC riders and certain GMIB riders that become irrevocable upon benefit election.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: Indicates whether this rider was optionally elected by the contract owner (True) or is a mandatory benefit included in the base contract without separate election (False). Drives charge assessment logic and suitability review requirements.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation; FINRA Suitability Rules

description: Indicates whether this rider covers both the primary annuitant and a joint annuitant (True = joint life coverage; False = single life coverage). Joint life riders typically carry a higher charge rate and provide continued benefits upon the death of the first annuitant.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Maximum number of months for which long-term care benefits are payable under an LTC rider. Applicable only to LTC rider types. Null for non-LTC riders. Used in LTC benefit pool calculations and NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation compliance reporting.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; HIPAA Section 7702B; State Departments of Insurance

description: Contractual ceiling on the total benefit payable under this rider over its lifetime. Applicable to riders with aggregate benefit caps (e.g., LTC riders with lifetime maximum benefit pools, enhanced death benefit riders with maximum DB multiples). Null if no contractual maximum applies.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Contractually guaranteed maximum annual charge rate that the insurer may assess for this rider, expressed as a decimal. Protects the contract owner from unlimited charge increases. Required disclosure under NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance — Policy Form Filing Requirements

description: Maximum age of the annuitant at which this rider may be elected, as defined in the rider's contractual terms. Used in new business eligibility validation and underwriting rules engine checks.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance — Policy Form Filing Requirements

description: Minimum age of the annuitant at which this rider may be elected, as defined in the rider's contractual terms. Used in new business eligibility validation.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance — Policy Form Filing Requirements

description: Indicates whether the risk associated with this rider is subject to a reinsurance treaty (True = reinsured; False = retained). Relevant for GMDB and LTC riders which are commonly reinsured. Drives cession processing in the reinsurance management system.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; Swiss Re IRIS — Treaty Management Module; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Accounting basis under which actuarial reserves for this rider are calculated. GAAP = Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (FASB ASC 944 / LDTI); SAP = Statutory Accounting Principles (NAIC); PBR = Principle-Based Reserving; IFRS17 = International Financial Reporting Standards 17. Used in actuarial valuation system configuration.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles; NAIC Principle-Based Reserving (PBR); IFRS 17

description: Current lifecycle state of the rider or benefit attachment. Active = rider is in force and charges are being assessed; Suspended = rider temporarily not in force (e.g., during free-look period); Terminated = rider has been removed or contract surrendered; Pending = rider elected but not yet effective; Lapsed = rider lapsed due to non-payment of rider charge; Matured = rider has reached its contractual maturity date.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Version identifier of the rider product design, corresponding to a specific generation of benefit terms, charge rates, and eligibility rules. Insurers periodically update rider versions; this field distinguishes legacy riders from current offerings and is critical for actuarial valuation and reserving under LDTI.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Maximum number of years during which the guaranteed rollup rate is applied to grow the benefit base. After this period, the benefit base stops rolling up but the rider may remain in force. Null if rollup continues indefinitely until benefit election.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Annual guaranteed growth rate applied to the benefit base during the accumulation (deferral) phase, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.00% per annum). Applicable to GMWB, GMIB, and GMAB riders. Null for riders without a rollup feature. Critical input for actuarial reserving and LDTI cash flow projections.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP) No. 25

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rider benefit record was sourced (e.g., 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY', 'FAST_ANNUITY'). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and Silver layer audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Native identifier of this rider record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Sapiens Annuity internal rider ID). Enables back-referencing to the source system for reconciliation, issue resolution, and ETL lineage tracing.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Comma-separated list of US state codes (2-letter USPS abbreviations) in which this rider form has been approved and is available for sale. Null or 'ALL' if available in all states. Used in new business eligibility validation and regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: State Departments of Insurance — Policy Form Filing Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Frequency at which the automatic step-up (ratchet) evaluation occurs for the benefit base. Annual = evaluated each contract anniversary; Triennial = every 3 years; Quinquennial = every 5 years; On_demand = owner may request step-up at any time subject to conditions; None = no step-up provision. Null if step_up_provision is False.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates whether this rider includes an automatic or optional step-up (ratchet) provision that locks in market gains by resetting the benefit base to the current account value if the account value exceeds the benefit base on a specified anniversary date. True = step-up provision exists; False = no step-up.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Calendar date on which the rider was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null if the rider is still in force. Populated upon rider lapse, voluntary removal, contract surrender, annuitization, or death claim settlement.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: The contractual condition or event that caused or will cause this rider to terminate. Surrender = full contract surrender; Death = death of the annuitant or owner; Annuitization = contract converted to income stream; Account_value_zero = account value depleted to zero; Owner_request = voluntary rider removal by owner; Non_payment = rider charge could not be deducted. Null if rider is still active.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this rider benefit record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance under SOX. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.
reference: SOX Section 404; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Number of contract years that must elapse before the contract owner is eligible to elect benefits under this rider. Common for GMIB riders which typically require a 10-year deferral period before annuitization. Zero if no waiting period applies.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Annual guaranteed withdrawal percentage of the benefit base that the contract owner may withdraw each year without reducing the guaranteed benefit, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.050000 = 5.00%). Applicable to GMWB riders. May vary by age band at election. Null for non-GMWB riders.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; Sapiens Annuity — Withdrawal Processing Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each RMD schedule record within the annuity administration platform. Primary key for the rmd_schedule data product.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module)

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: IRS RMD rules require the annuitant's date of birth and life expectancy for distribution period calculation. rmd_schedule already has party_id but annuitant holds annuity-specific demographics (issue_
Reference to the qualified annuity contract for which this RMD schedule is maintained. Links the RMD obligation to the underlying annuity contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — contract administration module); IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 590-B

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: RMD distributions generate journal entries for federal/state tax withholding remittances and distribution accounting. Finance teams reconcile RMD payment amounts to GL postings for IRS Form 945 withho

Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: RMD calculations use IRS life expectancy tables (Uniform Lifetime, Single Life, Joint Life) which are mortality tables. Required for accurate RMD calculation, IRS compliance, and audit trail of which
Reference to the annuity contract owner (policyholder) who is subject to the RMD obligation. The owner's age and applicable IRS life expectancy table are derived from this party record.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: IRC 401(a)(9) requires annual RMD notices be sent to contract owners. The rmd_schedule drives notice generation; linking the issued notice document to the schedule enables regulatory proof of timely n

Identifier grouping multiple IRA or annuity contracts owned by the same individual for RMD aggregation purposes. IRS rules permit owners to aggregate RMDs across multiple IRAs and satisfy the total from one or more accounts. Used for cross-contract RMD satisfaction tracking and IRS Form 5498 reporting.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Reg. 1.408-8; IRS Form 5498

business_glossary_term: April 1 First-Year RMD Deferral Elected Flag
description: Indicates whether the contract owner has elected to defer the first-year RMD to April 1 of the following calendar year as permitted by IRS rules. Applicable only when first_rmd_year_flag is True. When True, the rmd_due_date is set to April 1 of the year following the first RMD year.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-5

business_glossary_term: Designated Beneficiary Age at RMD Year
description: Age of the designated beneficiary (typically spouse) as of their birthday in the RMD calculation year. Required only when the Joint and Last Survivor Table is applicable (sole beneficiary is a spouse more than 10 years younger than the owner). Null when Uniform Lifetime or Single Life tables are used.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-9; IRC Section 72

description: The gross RMD amount calculated for the calendar year, derived by dividing the prior year-end Account Value (AV) by the applicable IRS distribution period factor. This is the minimum amount the contract owner must withdraw to satisfy IRS RMD requirements and avoid the IRC Section 4974 excise tax.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; IRC Section 4974; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-5

description: The timestamp when this RMD schedule record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trails, data lineage, and Silver Layer record management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC data governance standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: The life expectancy factor (in years) retrieved from the applicable IRS life expectancy table for the owner's age (and beneficiary's age if Joint table applies) in the RMD year. This divisor is applied to the prior year-end account value to compute the RMD amount. Updated per SECURE Act 2.0 tables effective 2022.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-9; SECURE Act 2.0

business_glossary_term: IRC Section 4974 Excise Tax Exposure Amount
description: The dollar amount of the potential IRS excise tax exposure under IRC Section 4974 if the RMD is not fully satisfied by the due date. Calculated as 25% of the remaining_rmd_balance (reduced to 10% if corrected within the correction window under SECURE Act 2.0). Supports proactive owner outreach and compliance risk management.
reference: IRC Section 4974; SECURE Act 2.0; IRS Publication 590-B

business_glossary_term: First Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Year Flag
description: Indicates whether this is the first calendar year in which the contract owner is subject to RMD requirements. When True, the owner may elect to defer the first RMD to April 1 of the following year per IRS rules, which may result in two RMDs being taken in the same calendar year.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; SECURE Act 2.0

description: Indicates whether IRS Form 1099-R (Distributions From Pensions, Annuities, Retirement or Profit-Sharing Plans) has been issued to the contract owner for distributions taken toward the RMD in the calendar year. Supports tax reporting compliance and owner communication.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; NAIC

description: The date on which the IRS Form 5498 reporting the RMD obligation was filed or transmitted to the IRS. Null if form_5498_reported_flag is False. Supports tax reporting audit trails.
reference: IRS Form 5498; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 408(i)

description: Indicates whether the RMD obligation for this calendar year has been reported to the IRS on Form 5498 (IRA Contribution Information), which requires insurers to report the RMD amount and indicate that an RMD is required for the year. Supports IRS tax reporting compliance.
reference: IRS Form 5498; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 408(i); NAIC

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract is an inherited (beneficiary) contract subject to the IRS 10-year rule or life expectancy payout rules under SECURE Act 2.0. When True, the Single Life Expectancy Table and beneficiary-specific RMD rules apply.
reference: SECURE Act 2.0; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72(s); IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)

description: The channel through which the RMD notification was delivered to the contract owner. 'suppressed' indicates the owner opted out of notifications or the notification was withheld for a documented reason.
reference: IRS Reg. 1.408-8 Q&A-10; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity); CRM and Customer Engagement Platform

business_glossary_term: Original Contract Owner Date of Death
description: The date of death of the original annuity contract owner, applicable when inherited_contract_flag is True. Determines the commencement of the beneficiary's RMD obligation and the applicable distribution rules under SECURE Act 2.0 (10-year rule vs. life expectancy payout).
reference: SECURE Act 2.0; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72(s); IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-3

business_glossary_term: Contract Owner Age at RMD Year
description: The age of the annuity contract owner as of their birthday in the RMD calculation year. Used to look up the applicable distribution period from the IRS life expectancy table. Per IRS rules, age is determined as of the owner's birthday in the distribution year.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-5; IRC Section 72

description: The date on which the contract owner was notified of their RMD obligation and calculated amount for the calendar year. IRS regulations require insurers to notify IRA owners of their RMD amount by January 31 of the distribution year. Supports regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: IRS Reg. 1.408-8 Q&A-10; IRS Publication 590-B; NAIC

business_glossary_term: Prior Calculated Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Amount
description: The RMD amount from the most recent prior calculation before the current recalculation. Populated only when recalculation_flag is True. Supports audit trails and change management for IRS reporting.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module)

description: The Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of December 31 of the year immediately preceding the RMD calculation year. This is the IRS-mandated valuation date for RMD calculation purposes. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform's end-of-year account value snapshot.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; IRS Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-5; Annuity Administration Platform (FAST Annuity; Sapiens Annuity)

business_glossary_term: Prior Year-End Account Value (AV) Valuation Date
description: The specific date on which the prior year-end Account Value (AV) was captured for RMD calculation purposes. Should be December 31 of the prior year. Recorded explicitly to support audit trails and IRS Form 5498 reporting.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Form 5498; IRC Section 72

description: The type of qualified retirement plan or IRA under which the annuity contract is held, which determines the applicable RMD rules, tax treatment, and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: traditional_ira|sep_ira|simple_ira|401k|403b|457b|inherited_ira — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 590-B; ERISA; DOL fiduciary standards; IRS Form 5498

description: Indicates whether the RMD amount for this calendar year has been recalculated after the initial calculation, typically due to a correction in the prior year-end account value, a change in the applicable life expectancy table, or a data correction. When True, recalculation_reason should be populated.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module)

description: Free-text description of the reason the RMD was recalculated for the calendar year. Applicable only when recalculation_flag is True. Examples include account value restatement, beneficiary designation change, table correction, or data quality remediation.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module)

description: The outstanding RMD amount still required to be distributed to satisfy the IRS obligation for the calendar year. Computed as calculated_rmd_amount minus ytd_distributions_applied. A zero or negative value indicates the RMD has been satisfied. Used for proactive owner notifications and IRS compliance monitoring.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 4974; IRC Section 72

description: The date on which the RMD amount was calculated by the Annuity Administration Platform for the given calendar year. Supports audit trails and recalculation tracking when account values are restated or corrected.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module); IRS Publication 590-B

description: The IRS deadline by which the RMD must be fully distributed to avoid the IRC Section 4974 excise tax. For most owners, this is December 31 of the RMD year. For the first RMD year (the year the owner reaches the applicable RMD age), the deadline may be extended to April 1 of the following year per IRS rules.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; IRC Section 4974; SECURE Act 2.0 (RMD age changes)

description: The date on which the RMD obligation for the calendar year was fully satisfied (i.e., cumulative distributions reached or exceeded the calculated RMD amount). Null if the RMD has not yet been satisfied. Used for IRS Form 1099-R reporting and compliance monitoring.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; IRC Section 4974

description: The IRS-mandated age at which the contract owner is required to begin taking RMDs from the qualified annuity. Reflects the applicable RMD age under current law (e.g., age 73 under SECURE Act 2.0 for owners born 1951–1959; age 75 for owners born 1960 or later). Stored to support historical accuracy as legislation changes.
reference: SECURE Act 2.0 (Consolidated Appropriations Act 2023); IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 590-B

description: Current lifecycle status of the RMD obligation for the given calendar year. 'pending' = RMD calculated but no distributions taken; 'in_progress' = partial distributions applied; 'satisfied' = full RMD amount distributed; 'waived' = IRS-approved waiver applied (e.g., first-year RMD deferral); 'overdue' = due date passed without satisfaction; 'cancelled' = contract no longer subject to RMD (e.g., annuitized, surrendered).
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 4974 (excise tax on insufficient distributions); SECURE Act 2.0

description: The calendar year (YYYY) for which this RMD obligation applies. Each qualified annuity contract generates one RMD schedule record per applicable calendar year. Determines the December 31 prior-year account value snapshot used in the RMD calculation.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 590-B; IRS RMD rules under SECURE Act 2.0

description: The identifier of the operational source system record from which this RMD schedule was originated or last updated (e.g., Sapiens Annuity contract ID, FAST Annuity record key). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL audit trails in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — RMD tracking module); ACORD data standards

business_glossary_term: Systematic Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Program Flag
description: Indicates whether the contract owner is enrolled in the annuity platform's automatic systematic RMD distribution program, which automatically calculates and distributes the RMD amount on a scheduled basis (monthly, quarterly, or annually) to ensure timely satisfaction.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — withdrawal processing module); IRS Publication 590-B

description: The frequency at which systematic RMD distributions are scheduled when the owner is enrolled in the automatic RMD program (systematic_rmd_flag = True). Null when systematic RMD is not elected.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — withdrawal processing module); IRS Publication 590-B

description: The date on which the contract owner's enrollment in the systematic RMD distribution program became effective. Null when systematic RMD is not elected.
reference: Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — withdrawal processing module)

description: The timestamp when this RMD schedule record was most recently modified in the data platform. Updated whenever distributions are applied, recalculations occur, or status changes. Supports audit trails and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC data governance standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: The reason code explaining why the RMD obligation has been waived for the calendar year. Applicable only when rmd_status is 'waived'. Examples include first-year deferral election, IRS-approved waiver, contract annuitization, full surrender, or regulatory relief (e.g., COVID-19 CARES Act waiver).
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; CARES Act (2020 RMD waiver); SECURE Act 2.0

description: The cumulative amount of distributions taken from the annuity contract during the RMD calendar year that have been applied toward satisfying the RMD obligation. Updated each time a qualifying withdrawal is processed. Used to compute the remaining RMD balance.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 72; Annuity Administration Platform (FAST Annuity; Sapiens Annuity — withdrawal processing module)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each sub-account allocation record within a variable annuity contract. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the variable annuity contract to which this sub-account allocation belongs. Links the allocation to the parent annuity contract record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Reference to the investment fund (sub-account) available within the variable annuity separate account. Identifies the specific fund option selected by the contract owner.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Each sub-account allocation maps to a specific investment.separate_account for daily unit value pricing, NAV reporting, and SEC/NAIC regulatory filings. The existing separate_account_code is a denorma

Identifies the originating source of the allocation instruction. Distinguishes between owner-directed, agent-directed, system-default, automatic rebalancing, DCA transfer, and system-generated allocations. Used for suitability review and audit trail.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation; DOL Fiduciary Rule; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this sub-account allocation record, as assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity). Used in client statements and correspondence.

Percentage of new premium and/or rebalancing proceeds directed to this sub-account fund, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 25.0000 = 25%). All active allocations for a contract must sum to 100%. Used in premium allocation and rebalancing processing.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle status of the sub-account allocation. 'active' indicates the allocation is in force; 'pending' indicates awaiting effective date or processing; 'suspended' indicates temporarily halted; 'terminated' indicates closed; 'transferred' indicates moved to another fund.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Target allocation percentage used specifically for automatic rebalancing calculations. May differ from the current allocation_pct if the owner has submitted a pending rebalancing instruction not yet effective.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Aggregate cost basis (investment in contract) allocated to this sub-account, used for tax reporting under IRC Section 72 to determine the taxable portion of withdrawals and annuity payments.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Timestamp when this sub-account allocation record was first created in the Annuity Administration Platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Annual Statement

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this allocation record (e.g., 'USD'). Supports multi-currency annuity contracts and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; NAIC Financial Condition Examiners Handbook

description: Date on which the Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) transfer program for this sub-account allocation is scheduled to end. Null if DCA is not active or has no defined end date.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Periodic dollar amount transferred into this sub-account under the Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) program per the defined frequency. Applicable only when dollar_cost_averaging_flag is True.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this sub-account allocation participates in a Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) program, where funds are systematically transferred from a fixed account or money market into this sub-account over a defined period.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date on which this sub-account allocation instruction became effective for the contract. Represents the business event date when the allocation was applied, distinct from the record creation timestamp.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Annual expense ratio of the underlying investment fund expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.7500 = 0.75%). Includes management fees and fund operating expenses. Required for prospectus disclosure and suitability analysis.
reference: SEC Rule 482; FINRA Rule 2210; NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether this sub-account allocation is part of the contract's default investment allocation (e.g., applied when no explicit owner instruction is on file). Used in new business processing and NIGO resolution workflows.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this sub-account fund is subject to trading restrictions (e.g., market timing restrictions, fund closure to new investments). When True, new allocations or transfers into this fund may be blocked.
reference: SEC Rule 22c-2 (market timing); FINRA Rule 2010; NAIC Market Conduct Annual Statement

description: Most recent date on which a fund transfer (in or out) was processed for this sub-account allocation. Used to enforce transfer restriction periods and market timing compliance.
reference: SEC Rule 22c-2; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Current fair market value (FMV) of the contract owner's holdings in this sub-account, calculated as units_held multiplied by unit_value as of the valuation date. Contributes to the total Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract.
reference: FASB ASC 820 (Fair Value Measurement); NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Mortality and Expense (M&E) Risk Charge Rate
description: Annual Mortality and Expense (M&E) risk charge rate assessed against this sub-account's market value, expressed as a percentage. Covers the insurer's mortality risk and expense guarantees. Required for cost disclosure and actuarial reserving.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements

description: Date of the most recent fund prospectus under which this sub-account allocation was made. Required for SEC disclosure compliance and ensures the contract owner received current fund information.
reference: SEC Rule 482; Securities Act of 1933 Section 10; FINRA Rule 2210

description: Indicates whether automatic portfolio rebalancing is enabled for this sub-account allocation. When True, the contract's holdings are periodically rebalanced back to the target allocation percentages per the rebalancing frequency.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Frequency at which automatic portfolio rebalancing is performed for this sub-account allocation, when rebalancing_flag is True. Determines how often the allocation is reset to the target percentage.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Standardized risk rating category assigned to this sub-account fund based on its investment objective and volatility profile. Used for suitability matching, asset allocation reporting, and client disclosure.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation; FINRA Rule 2111; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements

description: Code identifying the operational source system from which this allocation record originated (e.g., 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY', 'FAST_ANNUITY'). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Status of the suitability review for this sub-account allocation instruction, per NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation and DOL fiduciary standards. Tracks whether the investment selection has been reviewed and approved as suitable for the contract owner.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation (Model #275); DOL Fiduciary Rule; FINRA Rule 2111

description: Date on which this sub-account allocation was terminated, transferred, or zeroed out. Null if the allocation is still active. Used for historical allocation tracking and audit trail.
Minimum number of calendar days that must elapse between transfers into and out of this sub-account fund. Enforces market timing restrictions per SEC Rule 22c-2 and fund prospectus requirements.

Current Net Asset Value (NAV) per accumulation unit for this sub-account fund as of the valuation date. Sourced from the Investment Management System (e.g., Charles River IMS, SimCorp Dimension) daily pricing feed. Used to compute sub-account market value.
reference: SEC Rule 22c-1; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: Number of accumulation units held in this sub-account as of the most recent valuation date. Multiplied by the current unit value to derive the sub-account market value. Core field for NAV-based Account Value (AV) calculation.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SEC Rule 22c-1 (forward pricing)

description: Cumulative number of accumulation units purchased in this sub-account during the current calendar year. Used for investment activity reporting and tax cost basis tracking under IRC Section 72.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Cumulative number of accumulation units redeemed from this sub-account during the current calendar year due to withdrawals, transfers, or charges. Used for investment activity reporting and cost basis tracking.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this sub-account allocation record. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Annual Statement

description: Business date as of which the unit value, units held, and market value are reported. Corresponds to the NAV pricing date from the Investment Management System. Critical for daily AV reconciliation and regulatory reporting.
reference: SEC Rule 22c-1; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 820

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each periodic account value snapshot record in the annuity valuation ledger. Primary key for this table. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete periodic valuation event for an annuity contract.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17

description: Reference to the annuity contract for which this account value snapshot is recorded. Links to the annuity contract master record in the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity).
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Daily account value changes (credited interest, rider charges, index credits, COI deductions) generate GAAP/SAP journal entries. Finance teams reconcile account value movements to GL postings each val

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Account values must be attributed to issuing legal entity for consolidated balance sheet reporting, statutory reserve calculations, and regulatory capital adequacy (RBC) filings. Required for multi-en

Reference to the annuity contract owner (policyholder/annuitant) associated with this valuation record. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for policyholder annual statement generation and KYC/AML compliance.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Account value snapshots are produced within actuarial valuation cycles. Linking to valuation_run enables reconciliation between contract-level account values and aggregate reserve totals, a standard a

business_glossary_term: Beginning of Valuation Period Account Value (AV)
description: The Account Value (AV) at the beginning of the valuation period (BOY/BOM/BOQ/BOD depending on cycle). Represents the opening balance before any transactions, credits, or charges are applied during the period. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform contract administration module.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; IFRS 17; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Cost of Insurance (COI) charge deducted from the account value during the valuation period. Applicable primarily to variable annuities and indexed products with mortality and expense risk charges. Calculated based on the Net Amount at Risk (NAR) and applicable COI rates.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 7702

description: Current phase of the annuity contract lifecycle at the time of this valuation: accumulation (premium growth phase), annuitization (conversion to income stream), payout (systematic income payments), surrender (contract terminated by owner), or lapse (contract terminated for non-payment). Critical for determining applicable valuation rules.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The contract year number at the valuation date, calculated from the contract issue date. Used to determine applicable surrender charge rates, free withdrawal corridors, and benefit base roll-up periods.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Timestamp when this account value record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP

description: Total interest credited to the contract account value during the valuation period for fixed and fixed-indexed annuity products. Includes declared interest rates for fixed products and guaranteed minimum interest for FIA products. Excludes index credits which are tracked separately.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the annuity contract at the valuation date, representing the net amount the contract owner would receive upon full surrender after deducting applicable surrender charges, market value adjustments (MVA), and outstanding loans. Key metric for policyholder statements and regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Current death benefit (DB) payable upon the death of the annuitant or insured at the valuation date. May equal the account value, a return-of-premium amount, or a stepped-up value depending on the contract terms and any applicable GMDB rider.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

business_glossary_term: End of Valuation Period Account Value (AV)
description: The Account Value (AV) at the end of the valuation period (EOY/EOM/EOQ/EOD depending on cycle). Represents the closing balance after all transactions, credits, and charges have been applied. This is the primary AV figure used for policyholder statements, ALM, and actuarial reserving.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; IFRS 17; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The maximum index credit rate (cap) applied to the linked index return for FIA products during the crediting period (as a decimal, e.g., 0.06 = 6%). Null for non-FIA products or strategies without a cap.
reference: NAIC FIA Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The minimum index credit rate (floor) guaranteed for FIA products during the crediting period (as a decimal, e.g., 0.00 = 0%). Protects the contract owner from negative index returns. Null for non-FIA products.
reference: NAIC FIA Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The index crediting strategy elected by the contract owner for FIA products (e.g., S&P 500 Annual Point-to-Point with Cap, S&P 500 Monthly Sum, MSCI EAFE Participation Rate). Determines how index credits are calculated. Null for non-FIA products.
reference: NAIC FIA Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC SAP

description: The participation rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.80 = 80%) applied to the linked index return when calculating index credits for FIA products. Null for non-FIA products. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform crediting parameters.
reference: NAIC FIA Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Maximum amount that can be withdrawn from the contract during the current contract year without incurring surrender charges, typically 10% of the account value or premium. Used for withdrawal processing and policyholder servicing.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: GAAP reserve for this annuity contract at the valuation date, calculated per FASB ASC 944 Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI). Represents the present value of future benefit payments less the present value of future gross premiums under GAAP accounting.

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed minimum account value at the end of the accumulation period. Null if no GMAB rider is attached.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed floor for the death benefit, which may be higher than the current account value due to step-up provisions or roll-up features. Used for NAR calculation and actuarial reserving under LDTI.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the accumulation value used to calculate the guaranteed minimum annuitization income. Null if no GMIB rider is attached.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the principal amount from which guaranteed withdrawal amounts are calculated. May grow through roll-up credits or step-ups. Null if no GMWB rider is attached.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

description: Total gross amount withdrawn from the contract account value during the valuation period, including both systematic withdrawals and ad-hoc partial surrenders. Used to calculate surrender charges, tax reporting under IRC Section 72, and GMWB benefit base adjustments.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) Index Credit Amount
description: Index-linked interest credit applied to the contract account value during the valuation period for Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) products. Calculated based on the performance of the linked index (e.g., S&P 500), subject to applicable cap, floor, and participation rate. Zero for non-FIA products.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC FIA Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether this annuity contract was funded in whole or in part through a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035). Affects cost basis tracking, tax reporting, and gain/loss calculations for IRC Section 72 purposes.
reference: IRC Section 1035 (1035 Exchange); IRS Regulations; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of withdrawals and loans, triggering LIFO tax treatment and potential 10% penalty.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS Regulations; NAIC SAP

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract is a qualified contract (held within a tax-qualified retirement plan such as IRA, 401(k), 403(b)) or a non-qualified contract. Determines applicable tax treatment under IRC Section 72 and RMD requirements.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Regulations; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB)
description: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMDB guarantee, calculated as the excess of the GMDB benefit base over the current account value (max of zero). Used for COI charge calculation, reinsurance cession, and actuarial reserving under LDTI/IFRS 17.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — Living Benefit Guarantee
description: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for living benefit guarantees (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB), representing the present value of the excess of guaranteed benefit payments over the projected account value. Used for actuarial reserving under LDTI and IFRS 17 and reinsurance reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IFRS 17

description: Total net premium (after applicable premium loads and taxes) credited to the contract account value during the valuation period. Includes initial premiums, subsequent premiums, and 1035 exchange proceeds applied during the period.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 1035 (1035 Exchange)

description: Internal product code identifying the annuity product type (e.g., FIA, VUL, SPIA, GMWB annuity). Used to apply product-specific valuation rules, crediting methods, and benefit base calculations.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Product Filing Requirements

description: Classification of the annuity product: Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA), Variable Annuity (VA), Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA), Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity (MYGA), Fixed Premium Deferred Annuity (FPDA), or Fixed Premium Immediate Annuity (FPIA). Drives crediting method and benefit calculation logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIA|VA|SPIA|MYGA|FPDA|FPIA|RILA|DIA — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Total of all rider charges (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB, LTC, etc.) deducted from the account value during the valuation period. Represents the aggregate cost of all optional benefit riders attached to the contract.
reference: NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount calculated for this contract for the current tax year, applicable to qualified annuity contracts held in IRAs or employer-sponsored retirement plans. Tracked per IRS regulations to ensure compliance with IRC Section 401(a)(9).
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS RMD Regulations; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The native record identifier from the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity) for this account value snapshot. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability between the operational system and the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 302/404 (Data Lineage)

description: Estimated statutory reserve for this annuity contract at the valuation date, calculated per NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP) and Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) requirements. Sourced from or reconciled with the Actuarial Valuation System (e.g., MoSes, AXIS, Prophet).
reference: NAIC SAP; NAIC PBR (VM-21 for Variable Annuities; VM-22 for Fixed Annuities); FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: Net investment gain or loss from variable annuity sub-account performance during the valuation period. Applicable only to Variable Annuity (VA) and Variable Universal Life (VUL) products. Reflects market value changes in the separate account sub-accounts.
reference: SEC Variable Annuity Regulations; FINRA Rules; NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Applicable surrender charge that would be assessed if the contract were fully surrendered at the valuation date. Calculated based on the surrender charge schedule and contract duration. Used for CSV calculation and policyholder disclosure.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Applicable surrender charge rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.07 = 7%) at the valuation date based on the contract's surrender charge schedule and contract year. Used to calculate the surrender charge amount and free withdrawal corridor.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; NAIC SAP; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this account value record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Populated on restatements, corrections, or status changes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP

description: Frequency classification of this valuation snapshot indicating whether it is a daily, monthly, quarterly, or annual valuation record. Drives downstream actuarial and ALM processing cadence.

The as-of date for this account value snapshot. Represents the principal real-world business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). May be daily, month-end, quarter-end, or year-end depending on the valuation cycle. Used for actuarial reserving, ALM, and regulatory reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP); IRC Section 72

description: Current lifecycle status of this account value record indicating whether the valuation is preliminary (in-process), final (approved), restated (corrected after finalization), or voided (cancelled). Supports LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; SOX Section 302/404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each contract valuation snapshot record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
