Reference to the contract owner/policyholder party record. Supports policyholder-level aggregation and annual statement generation.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Contract valuation snapshots must reference the investment portfolio driving reserve calculations for ALM reporting, GAAP/STAT reserve attribution, and investment performance reporting. Actuaries and

Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: Variable and FIA annuity contract valuations under VM-21 CTE70 stochastic reserving reference a specific economic scenario set. This link enables traceability of which scenario set drove a contract-le

Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Valuation run results directly populate statutory reserve amounts for regulatory filings. Actuarial valuation outputs feed reserve balances reported in NAIC Annual Statement and state regulatory submi

Identifier for the actuarial valuation batch run that produced this record. Enables traceability of valuation results back to a specific run of the actuarial valuation system (MoSes, AXIS, or Prophet). Supports audit and restatement workflows.
reference: SOX; Internal Actuarial Governance Policy; Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

description: The total Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of the valuation date. Represents the accumulated fund value including premiums paid, credited interest/index credits, and less withdrawals and charges. Core financial metric for annuity contract administration.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Reserve Standards; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity) — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Total rider charges accrued but not yet deducted from the AV as of the valuation date. Includes charges for GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB, and other optional benefit riders. Used for accurate AV reconciliation and income statement accruals.
reference: FASB ASC 944; Annuity Administration Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The date on which the contract transitioned from the accumulation phase to the annuitization/payout phase. Null if the contract has not yet been annuitized. Triggers shift from AV-based to income-stream-based valuation.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Reserve Standards; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) available to the contract owner upon full surrender as of the valuation date. Equals AV less applicable surrender charges and market value adjustments. Used for policyholder statements and surrender processing.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation 830; Annuity Administration Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module

description: Current lifecycle phase of the annuity contract at the valuation date. Accumulation phase tracks AV growth; annuitization/payout phase tracks income stream; surrender and death claim phases indicate terminal events.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; Annuity Administration Platform

description: The owner's investment in the contract (cost basis) as of the valuation date per IRC Section 72. Represents after-tax premiums paid less any tax-free distributions received. Used to determine the taxable gain upon surrender or annuitization.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The timestamp when this contract valuation record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX.

The current credited interest rate applied to the contract AV for the valuation period. For fixed annuities, this is the declared rate. For FIA contracts, this reflects the effective rate after index crediting. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045 = 4.5%).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this valuation record. Typically USD for domestic US annuity contracts.

The Death Benefit (DB) payable to the beneficiary upon the death of the annuitant or contract owner as of the valuation date. May equal AV, a return-of-premium guarantee, or a stepped-up value depending on contract terms.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Reserve Standards; Annuity Administration Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) reserve for this contract as of the valuation date, calculated under FASB ASC 944 including LDTI requirements. Supports financial statement reporting and SOX compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); GAAP; SOX; Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base as of the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed minimum AV at the end of the accumulation period, protecting against investment losses.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Rider Active Indicator
description: Indicates whether the GMAB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMAB benefit base and maturity guarantee calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Annuity Administration Platform

description: The Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) base or benefit amount as of the valuation date. Represents the minimum death benefit guaranteed regardless of investment performance. Applicable for variable and indexed annuities with GMDB riders.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Variable Annuity Reserve Standards; Actuarial Valuation System (AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base as of the valuation date. Used to calculate the minimum annuitization income stream the contract owner is guaranteed upon exercising the GMIB rider.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Rider Active Indicator
description: Indicates whether the GMIB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMIB benefit base calculations and annuitization guarantee valuations.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Annuity Administration Platform

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Annual Withdrawal Amount
description: The maximum annual withdrawal amount guaranteed under the GMWB rider as of the valuation date. Calculated as the GMWB benefit base multiplied by the applicable withdrawal percentage. Used for RMD compliance checks and policyholder benefit illustrations.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS RMD Rules; NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Annuity Administration Platform — Benefit Calculations Module

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Benefit Base
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base as of the valuation date. This is the notional amount used to calculate the guaranteed annual withdrawal amount, which may differ from AV due to step-ups, roll-ups, or bonus credits.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Rider Active Indicator
description: Indicates whether the GMWB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMWB benefit base and NAR calculations in the valuation.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Annuity Administration Platform

description: The index credit amount applied to the contract AV during the current index crediting period as of the valuation date. Applicable for Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) contracts. Reflects the gain linked to the selected index strategy.
reference: NAIC FIA Model Regulation; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity) — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates whether this contract was funded via a 1035 tax-free exchange from a prior insurance or annuity contract. True if funded by 1035 exchange; False if funded by direct premium. Affects cost basis tracking and tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Form 1099-R; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of withdrawals and loans. True if MEC; False otherwise.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The original issue date of the annuity contract. Used to calculate contract duration, surrender charge schedule position, and benefit base roll-up periods.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applicable to the contract as of the valuation date. Positive or negative adjustment applied to the surrender value based on changes in interest rates since contract issue. Applicable for MVA-type fixed annuities.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 830; Annuity Administration Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module

description: The contractual maturity date of the annuity contract, after which the contract must be annuitized or surrendered per contract terms. Used for GMAB maturity benefit calculations and policyholder notifications.
reference: NAIC Annuity Reserve Standards; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB)
description: The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMDB rider as of the valuation date. Calculated as the excess of the GMDB value over the current AV. Represents the insurer's economic exposure on the death benefit guarantee. Key input for actuarial reserving and reinsurance cession.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB)
description: The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMWB rider as of the valuation date. Represents the present value of projected future guaranteed withdrawals in excess of projected AV. Key input for LDTI/IFRS 17 liability measurement.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Actuarial Guideline 43 (AG43); Actuarial Valuation System

description: Code identifying the specific annuity product type (e.g., FIA, SPIA, variable annuity, fixed annuity) associated with this contract. Drives product-specific valuation logic and benefit calculations.
reference: NAIC Product Filing Standards; Internal Product Taxonomy

description: The Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount calculated for the contract for the current tax year as of the valuation date. Applicable for qualified annuity contracts (IRA, 401(k)). Ensures IRS compliance under IRC Section 401(a)(9).
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS RMD Rules; Annuity Administration Platform — RMD Tracking Module

description: Code identifying the operational source system that originated this valuation data. Supports data lineage tracking across the lakehouse Silver layer.

The estimated statutory reserve for this contract as of the valuation date, calculated under SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles) per NAIC requirements. Includes Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method (CARVM) or Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) as applicable. Feeds state regulatory reserve filings.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; SAP; PBR (Principle-Based Reserving); Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

description: The contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) or surrender charge applicable to the contract as of the valuation date. Deducted from AV to arrive at CSV. Typically declines over the surrender charge period.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 830; SEC (for variable products); Annuity Administration Platform

description: The date on which the surrender charge period expires for this contract. After this date, the contract owner may surrender without incurring a surrender charge. Key date for policyholder communications and surrender processing.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 830; Annuity Administration Platform — Contract Administration Module

description: The tax qualification status of the annuity contract. Determines applicable IRC tax rules, RMD requirements, and 1099-R reporting codes. Non-qualified contracts are funded with after-tax dollars; qualified contracts are funded with pre-tax dollars. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_qualified|traditional_ira|roth_ira|401k|403b|sep_ira|simple_ira — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Sections 72, 401, 403, 408; IRS Form 1099-R; Annuity Administration Platform

description: The timestamp when this contract valuation record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Tracks restatements, corrections, and status transitions for audit purposes.

Frequency classification of this valuation run. Indicates whether the snapshot was generated as part of a daily, monthly, quarterly, or annual valuation cycle. Drives downstream actuarial and reporting workflows.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; Internal Actuarial Valuation Policy

description: The as-of date for this valuation snapshot. Represents the specific calendar date on which all financial positions (AV, CSV, DB, guarantees) are measured. Used as the primary time dimension for actuarial feeds, LDTI/IFRS 17 reporting, and policyholder statements.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IFRS 17; NAIC Actuarial Guideline; Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS; Prophet)

description: Lifecycle status of this valuation record. Preliminary records are in-process; final records are approved for reporting; restated records reflect corrections; superseded records have been replaced by a newer run for the same contract and valuation date.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); Internal Actuarial Governance Policy

description: Total withdrawal amounts taken from the contract from the beginning of the current contract year through the valuation date. Used to determine excess withdrawal amounts that may reduce the GMWB benefit base and for IRC Section 72 tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Tax Reporting; Annuity Administration Platform — Withdrawal Processing Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each cost basis ledger record in the annuity domain. Primary key for the cost_basis_ledger data product.

Reference to the annuity contract for which this cost basis ledger record is maintained. Links to the annuity contract master.
reference: Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Reference to the contract owner (policyholder) party record. The owner is the taxpayer responsible for IRC Section 72 cost basis tracking and 1099-R reporting.

The annual dollar amount of annuity payments that is excludable from gross income under the exclusion ratio. Equals the periodic payment amount times payment frequency times the exclusion ratio. Reported as the non-taxable amount on IRS Form 1099-R.
reference: IRC Section 72(b); IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R Instructions

description: The date on which the annuity contract entered the payout (annuitization) phase. Marks the transition from accumulation to distribution phase and triggers the exclusion ratio calculation for periodic payments.
reference: IRC Section 72(b); Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: Indicates the event that established the annuity start date for IRC Section 72 purposes. Determines which date is used as the reference point for expected return and exclusion ratio calculations.
The date the annuity contract was originally issued. Establishes the start of the investment-in-contract accumulation period for IRC Section 72 cost basis purposes.
reference: IRC Section 72; Sapiens Annuity Contract Administration Module

description: The reason code for the most recent adjustment to the investment in contract. Used for audit trail and tax reporting reconciliation. description: The timestamp when this cost basis ledger record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

description: Total after-tax premium contributions made to the annuity contract since inception or since the last 1035 exchange. Represents the gross cost basis before adjustments for tax-free distributions received.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this cost basis ledger record. Typically USD for domestic annuity contracts.

The IRS distribution code to be reported in Box 7 of IRS Form 1099-R. Indicates the type of distribution for tax purposes (e.g., 1=early distribution no exception, 2=early distribution exception applies, 4=death, 6=1035 exchange, 7=normal distribution). Indicates whether the 10% additional tax on early distributions under IRC Section 72(q) applies to distributions from this contract. True if the owner is under age 59½ and no exception applies.
The cost basis transferred into this annuity contract from a prior contract via a tax-free 1035 exchange. Carries over the investment-in-contract from the surrendered contract per IRC Section 1035.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R Instructions

description: The cost basis transferred out of this annuity contract to a receiving contract via a tax-free 1035 exchange. Reduces the investment-in-contract of this contract.

The ratio of the investment in contract to the expected return, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.35000000 = 35%). Applied to each annuity payment to determine the non-taxable portion under IRC Section 72(b). Calculated as remaining_investment_in_contract divided by expected_return.
reference: IRC Section 72(b); IRS Publication 939; IRS Publication 575

description: Indicates whether the investment in contract has been fully recovered through prior non-taxable distributions. When True, all future annuity payments are fully taxable with no exclusion allowed under IRC Section 72(b)(2).
The date on which the investment in contract was fully recovered and the exclusion ratio was exhausted. Null if the exclusion ratio has not yet been exhausted.

The total amount expected to be received under the annuity contract over the payment period, calculated using IRS actuarial tables (life expectancy multiples) per IRC Section 72(c). Used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation.
reference: IRC Section 72(c); IRS Publication 939; IRS Actuarial Tables

description: The life expectancy multiple from the applicable IRS actuarial table (IRS Publication 939) used to calculate the expected return. Expressed in years (e.g., 20.0 years). Multiplied by the annual payment amount to derive the expected return.

business_glossary_term: IRS Form 1099-R Box 1 Gross Distribution Amount
description: The gross distribution amount to be reported in Box 1 of IRS Form 1099-R for the applicable tax year. Represents total distributions paid from the contract during the tax year.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Instructions; IRC Section 72; FinCEN AML/BSA

description: The taxable amount to be reported in Box 2a of IRS Form 1099-R for the applicable tax year. Represents the portion of gross distributions that is includable in gross income after applying the exclusion ratio.

business_glossary_term: IRS Form 1099-R Taxable Amount Not Determined Flag
description: Indicates whether the taxable amount cannot be determined by the insurance company and Box 2b 'Taxable amount not determined' should be checked on IRS Form 1099-R. True when the company lacks sufficient cost basis information to compute the taxable amount.

The cumulative after-tax investment in the annuity contract as defined under IRC Section 72(e)(6). Represents the total premiums paid less any amounts previously received tax-free. This is the primary cost basis figure used for exclusion ratio calculations and 1099-R reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72(e)(6); IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R Instructions

description: The date of the most recent adjustment to the investment in contract (cost basis). Used for audit trail and reconciliation with the annuity administration platform.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; IRC Section 72

description: The business date through which this cost basis ledger snapshot is current. Used for point-in-time reporting, year-end tax reporting, and 1099-R preparation.
The date on which this cost basis ledger record became effective, typically the contract issue date or the date of a 1035 exchange that established the initial investment in contract.

Current lifecycle status of the cost basis ledger record. Active indicates ongoing tracking; closed indicates the contract has been fully surrendered or annuitized; suspended indicates a hold pending review or correction; pending_review indicates an audit or reconciliation is in progress.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity Contract Administration Module

description: The date on which the contract was determined to be or not to be a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). Null if MEC determination has not been triggered.

Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of distributions, subjecting them to LIFO (last-in, first-out) income recognition and potential 10% penalty tax.

Cumulative amount of distributions that have been treated as non-taxable return of cost basis (investment in contract) under the IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio. Reduces the remaining investment in contract.

Cumulative reduction to the investment in contract resulting from partial surrenders or withdrawals that returned cost basis to the owner. Tracks the pro-rata cost basis recovery applied to partial surrenders of non-qualified annuities.
The annuitization payout option elected by the contract owner. Determines the expected return calculation method and applicable IRS actuarial table (Table V, VI, or VII) for the exclusion ratio.
reference: IRC Section 72(c); IRS Publication 939; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The fixed periodic payment amount received under the annuitization option. Used to calculate the annual exclusion amount (periodic_payment_amount multiplied by payment frequency multiplied by exclusion_ratio).

The remaining unrecovered cost basis in the annuity contract as of the ledger_as_of_date. Calculated as investment_in_contract minus nontaxable_distributions_received. Drives the exclusion ratio for future annuity payments.
The contract number of the prior annuity or life insurance contract from which cost basis was received via a 1035 exchange. Null if no 1035 exchange occurred.
The calendar tax year for which this cost basis ledger snapshot is prepared. Used for annual 1099-R tax reporting and IRS compliance. Format: YYYY (e.g., 2024).
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Instructions; IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575

description: Cumulative amount of distributions that have been treated as taxable income (earnings above cost basis) under IRC Section 72. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a.

Cumulative gross distributions (withdrawals, partial surrenders, annuity payments) received from the contract since inception. Used in conjunction with the exclusion ratio to determine the taxable and non-taxable portions of each distribution.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R Instructions

description: The timestamp when this cost basis ledger record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Model Audit Rule; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Unique identifier for the surrender charge schedule record.

Foreign key linking to product.charge_schedule. Business justification: annuity.surrender_charge is the contract-specific instantiation of a product.charge_schedule definition. Actuarial reserve calculations, rate filing compliance, and surrender charge waiver processing
Reference to the annuity contract to which this surrender charge schedule applies.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Surrender charge collections are recognized as revenue and require journal entries for GAAP deferred revenue release and SAP income recognition. Finance teams reconcile surrender charge income to GL p

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Surrender charge schedules (period length, rates) are actuarially derived from pricing assumptions (investment return, lapse rates, DAC recovery). This link supports rate filing validation and actuari

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Surrender charge schedules require rate filing approval in many states. Links surrender charge schedules to approved rate filings for regulatory compliance validation. Required for market conduct exam
Self-referencing FK on surrender_charge (superseded_surrender_charge_id)
Method by which surrender charges are applied to withdrawals (e.g., pro-rata across all withdrawals, only on amounts exceeding free withdrawal limit, or tiered based on withdrawal amount).
The basis upon which the surrender charge is calculated (e.g., current account value, premium paid, or withdrawal amount).
The contract year to which this surrender charge rate applies, starting from 1 at contract issue.
Timestamp when this surrender charge schedule record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether unused free withdrawal amounts from prior years can be carried forward and used in subsequent years.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this surrender charge schedule.
Date when this surrender charge schedule becomes effective for the contract.
Date when the surrender charge schedule expires or is no longer applicable, typically at the end of the surrender charge period.
The basis upon which the free withdrawal amount is calculated (e.g., account value, premium paid, or benefit base).
The percentage of account value that can be withdrawn annually without incurring a surrender charge, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 for 10%).
Indicates whether a market value adjustment is applied to surrenders in addition to the surrender charge.
Code identifying the specific formula or calculation method used to determine the market value adjustment.
The interest rate index or benchmark used to calculate the market value adjustment (e.g., 10-Year Treasury, LIBOR).
Total number of years during which surrender charges apply to the contract.
Code identifying the annuity product to which this surrender charge schedule applies.
Type of annuity product (fixed, variable, indexed, or immediate) to which this surrender charge schedule applies.
The percentage rate applied to calculate the surrender charge for this contract year, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 for 8%).
Business identifier for the surrender charge schedule, typically aligned with product or contract version.
Current lifecycle status of the surrender charge schedule.
Version identifier for the surrender charge schedule, used to track changes over time.
Code identifying the source system from which this surrender charge schedule data originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens, EXL).
Two-letter state code where the contract was issued, relevant for state-specific surrender charge regulations.
Timestamp when this surrender charge schedule record was last updated in the system.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Annuitization Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the contract is annuitized according to the available payout options.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Death Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived upon the death of the owner or annuitant.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Disability Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the owner becomes disabled, subject to policy terms.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Long-Term Care (LTC) Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the owner qualifies for long-term care benefits under the contract.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Nursing Home Confinement Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the owner is confined to a nursing home for a specified period.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived for withdrawals taken to satisfy IRS required minimum distribution requirements.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Terminal Illness Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the owner is diagnosed with a terminal illness.

business_glossary_term: Surrender Charge Waiver on Unemployment Flag
description: Indicates whether surrender charges are waived if the owner becomes involuntarily unemployed.
Unique surrogate identifier for each annuity premium payment transaction record in the annuity payment receipt ledger.
Reference to the AML case record in the compliance domain when this payment has been escalated for AML investigation. Null if no AML case has been opened for this payment.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; Bank Secrecy Act; NAIC Model Regulation 830

description: Reference to the annuity contract to which this premium payment is applied. Links the payment to the contract administration record in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Premium receipts require journal entries for cash receipt (debit), premium revenue recognition (credit), premium load (credit), and DAC capitalization (debit). Core accounting transaction for every pr

Reference to the modal premium schedule that governs the payment frequency and amount for flexible premium deferred annuities. Null for single-premium or ad-hoc payments not tied to a scheduled billing cycle.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Reference to the party record of the annuity contract owner who is making or authorizing the premium payment. Used for KYC/AML validation and tax reporting under IRC Section 72.
reference: IRC Section 72; FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Annuity premium reconciliation requires tracing each annuity_premium_payment to the premium_bill that triggered it. Supports billing statement reconciliation, audit trails, and NSF/grace period proces

Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: BSA/AML regulations require SAR filings for suspicious premium payments (e.g., large cash deposits, structuring). annuity_premium_payment needs a direct sar_filing_id for the SAR reporting workflow —

Reference to the suitability review record in the compliance domain associated with this premium payment, particularly for subsequent premium payments that may trigger re-suitability assessment under NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation (#275); FINRA Rule 2330; DOL Fiduciary Rule

description: Reference to the suspense account record where the payment funds are held pending NIGO resolution or contract identification. Null for payments applied directly without suspense.
reference: SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Premium payments on annuities may reference the originating underwriting application for audit trail, especially for 1035 exchanges from life policies or initial annuity applications requiring underwr

The ACH trace number assigned by the originating depository financial institution (ODFI) for ACH electronic payment transactions. Used for NACHA reconciliation and return item tracking when payment_source is 'ach'.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; FinCEN AML/BSA; ACORD Standards

description: Flag indicating whether this payment has been flagged for AML review due to large cash transaction reporting thresholds, structuring patterns, or other suspicious activity indicators per FinCEN BSA requirements.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; Bank Secrecy Act; NAIC Model Regulation 830

description: The net dollar amount actually credited to the annuity contract Account Value (AV) after deduction of any applicable premium load, surrender charges on exchanged contracts, or other fees. May equal gross_payment_amount if no deductions apply.
reference: IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: The actual dollar amount of premium bonus credited to the contract AV as a result of this payment. Calculated as bonus_eligible_amount multiplied by the applicable bonus rate per the contract terms.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: The portion of the gross payment amount that qualifies for a premium bonus credit under the annuity contract terms. Some annuity products offer a percentage bonus on qualifying premium payments. Null if the contract does not offer a premium bonus.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: The check number printed on the physical check when payment_source is 'check'. Used for bank reconciliation and payment tracing. Null for non-check payment sources.
reference: SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; ACORD Standards

description: The contract year in which this payment falls, calculated from the contract issue date. Used for surrender charge schedule application, bonus crediting eligibility, and actuarial valuation purposes.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: The after-tax cost basis amount contributed by this payment to the annuity contract's cumulative investment in the contract (IIC). For non-qualified annuities, this is the after-tax portion of the payment. For qualified annuities, this is typically zero. Critical for IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations and IRS Form 1099-R reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 575

description: Timestamp when this payment record was first created in the annuity administration system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount. Typically 'USD' for domestic US annuity contracts. Required for multi-currency reporting and GAAP/SAP financial statements.

The contract effective date on which the payment is credited to the annuity contract Account Value (AV). May differ from payment_date due to processing lag, back-dating rules, or 1035 exchange settlement timing.
reference: IRC Section 1035; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The cost basis (investment in the contract) transferred from the surrendered contract in a 1035 exchange. This amount carries over to the new annuity contract and is critical for IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations. Null for non-exchange payments.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575

description: Name of the insurance carrier or financial institution from which 1035 exchange proceeds were transferred. Required for 1035 exchange documentation and AML source-of-funds verification.
reference: IRC Section 1035; FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: The contract or policy number of the surrendered/exchanged contract from which 1035 exchange proceeds originated. Required for IRS Form 1035 documentation and audit trail when is_1035_exchange = true. Null for non-exchange payments.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Form 1035; ACORD Standards

description: The total gross dollar amount of the premium payment as received from the payor before any deductions, fees, or adjustments. This is the base monetary amount used for AV crediting and cost basis calculations.
reference: IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; SAP

description: Flag indicating whether this payment represents proceeds received from a tax-free 1035 exchange (IRC Section 1035 — Tax-Free Insurance Policy Exchange). When true, additional 1035 exchange tracking fields apply and the payment requires IRS Form 1035 documentation.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Form 1035; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Flag indicating whether this payment is an unscheduled additional premium contribution not tied to the modal premium schedule. Common for flexible premium deferred annuities where owners make ad-hoc contributions.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Not In Good Order (NIGO) Reason Description
description: Free-text description of the NIGO deficiency reason when nigo_status is 'nigo_pending' or 'nigo_rejected'. Examples: missing suitability form, missing signature, missing source of funds documentation, incomplete beneficiary designation.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FINRA Suitability Rules; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the NIGO deficiency was resolved and the payment was cleared for application to the annuity contract. Null if nigo_status is 'good_order' or 'nigo_pending'.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether the payment application was received in good order or flagged as Not In Good Order (NIGO). 'good_order' = all required documentation and information received; 'nigo_pending' = deficiency identified, awaiting resolution; 'nigo_resolved' = NIGO deficiency resolved and payment applied; 'nigo_rejected' = NIGO could not be resolved and payment returned.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Standards; FINRA Suitability Rules

description: The business date on which the premium payment was received by the company. This is the principal real-world event date used for contract crediting, cost basis tracking, and IRC Section 72 tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The modal frequency at which this payment is made under the contract's premium schedule. 'single' = one-time single premium; 'annual' = annual mode; 'semi_annual' = semi-annual mode; 'quarterly' = quarterly mode; 'monthly' = monthly mode. Drives premium billing schedule and APE (Annual Premium Equivalent) calculations.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this premium payment transaction by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Used for reconciliation, customer correspondence, and audit trails.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: The remittance instrument or channel through which the premium payment funds were received. Drives reconciliation workflows and AML monitoring thresholds. 'check' = personal or cashier's check; 'wire' = wire transfer; 'ach' = ACH electronic funds transfer; '1035_exchange' = proceeds from a 1035 tax-free exchange; 'rollover_check' = qualified plan rollover check; 'credit_card' = credit card payment.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the premium payment transaction. 'pending' = received but not yet applied to contract AV; 'posted' = successfully applied; 'reversed' = payment reversed after posting; 'returned' = payment returned by financial institution (e.g., NSF); 'suspended' = held in suspense pending NIGO resolution; 'rejected' = rejected and not applied.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Classification of the premium payment by its business purpose. 'initial_single_premium' = first and only premium for SPIAs or single-premium deferred annuities; 'subsequent_flexible_premium' = additional premium for flexible premium deferred annuities; '1035_exchange_proceeds' = funds received via a tax-free 1035 exchange; 'rollover' = qualified plan rollover (IRA, 401k); 'transfer' = direct trustee-to-trustee transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_single_premium|subsequent_flexible_premium|1035_exchange_proceeds|rollover|transfer|direct_transfer|trustee_transfer — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Full legal name of the individual or entity that remitted the premium payment. May differ from the contract owner when a third party remits payment. Required for AML/KYC source-of-funds verification and IRS tax reporting.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; IRS Form 1099-R; KYC Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Describes the relationship of the payor to the annuity contract owner when the payor is not the owner. Used for AML third-party payment monitoring and suitability review. 'self' = owner is the payor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|parent|child|trust|employer|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Dollar amount of premium load or front-end charge deducted from the gross payment before crediting to the contract AV. Represents the difference between gross_payment_amount and applied_amount attributable to premium loads.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; SAP

description: The date on which the payment reversal was processed and the AV adjustment was made to the annuity contract. Null for non-reversed payments.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; SAP; FASB ASC 944

description: Description of the reason for payment reversal when payment_status is 'reversed'. Examples: duplicate payment, incorrect contract application, owner request, NSF return. Null for non-reversed payments.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; SAP; ACORD Standards

description: The contractually scheduled premium amount for this payment period as defined in the modal premium schedule. Compared against gross_payment_amount to identify over-payments, under-payments, or unscheduled additional premiums.
reference: NAIC Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY' = Sapiens Annuity platform; 'FAST_ANNUITY' = FAST Annuity platform; 'POLICY_ADMIN' = Policy Administration System; 'MANUAL' = manually entered correction.
reference: ACORD Standards; SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944

description: The native transaction identifier assigned by the originating operational system (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity) for this payment record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between Silver Layer and source systems, and audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Standards; FASB ASC 944

description: Indicates the tax qualification status of the premium payment, which determines cost basis treatment and tax reporting obligations under IRC Section 72. 'qualified' = pre-tax qualified plan funds (IRA, 401k, 403b); 'non_qualified' = after-tax funds; 'roth_ira' = Roth IRA funds; 'sep_ira' = SEP-IRA; 'simple_ira' = SIMPLE IRA; '403b' = 403(b) tax-sheltered annuity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualified|non_qualified|roth_ira|sep_ira|simple_ira|403b|457b|pension — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: Timestamp when this payment record was last modified in the annuity administration system. Tracks status changes, NIGO resolution updates, and corrections.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: The wire transfer reference or IMAD (Input Message Accountability Data) number assigned by the sending financial institution when payment_source is 'wire'. Used for Fedwire reconciliation and AML source-of-funds documentation.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; Federal Reserve Fedwire Standards; ACORD Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) index crediting event record in the Silver layer. Primary key for this transactional entity.
Reference to the FIA annuity contract to which this index crediting event applies. Links the crediting event to the owning contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Index credit events reference product crediting strategy definitions to validate cap/participation rates, crediting methods, and index calculation rules. Required for credit calculation validation, he

Foreign key linking to annuity.index_crediting_strategy. Business justification: index_credit_event records each FIA crediting event applied at the end of a crediting term. index_crediting_strategy defines the strategy (cap rate, participation rate, floor, buffer, term) that gover

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: FIA index crediting events generate journal entries for interest credited to policyholder accounts (GAAP interest expense, SAP reserve increase). Finance teams reconcile index credit amounts to GL pos

Reference to the original index credit event record that this record reverses, when reversal_flag is True. Enables audit trail linkage between reversal and original crediting events. Null for original (non-reversal) events.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Reference to the specific index allocation segment within the FIA contract that this crediting event applies to. A contract may have multiple index allocations across different indices and crediting strategies.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: Code identifying the actuarial valuation basis under which this index credit event is recognized for reserve and liability calculations (e.g., GAAP, SAP, IFRS). Supports multi-basis reporting in the Actuarial Valuation System (AXIS, Prophet, MoSes) and LDTI compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); NAIC SAP; IFRS 17; GAAP

description: The Account Value (AV) of this specific index allocation segment immediately after the index credit has been applied. Equals allocation_av_start plus credited_amount, adjusted for any withdrawals processed at term end.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

business_glossary_term: Allocation Account Value (AV) at Term Start
description: The Account Value (AV) of this specific index allocation segment at the beginning of the crediting term, before any withdrawals or adjustments during the term. Used as the base for calculating the credited dollar amount.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: The percentage of index loss absorbed by the insurance company before any loss is passed to the policyholder in a buffer-based FIA crediting strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.100000 = 10% buffer). Null if a floor applies instead of a buffer.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The maximum index-linked interest rate that can be credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term, as specified in the contract terms. Limits the upside participation in index gains. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.0%). Null if no cap applies to this crediting strategy.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Contract Total Account Value (AV) After Credit
description: The total Account Value (AV) across all allocation segments of the FIA contract immediately after this index credit has been applied. Reflects the contract-level AV used for policyholder statements and benefit base calculations.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: Timestamp when this index credit event record was first created in the source Annuity Administration Platform system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The dollar amount of index-linked interest credited to the FIA contract allocation for this term, calculated by applying the credited_interest_rate to the allocation_av_start. This is the actual monetary value added to the Account Value (AV).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI)

description: The final index-linked interest rate credited to the FIA contract allocation for this term, after applying cap, participation rate, floor, buffer, and spread constraints to the raw index return. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.0%). This is the rate that drives the credited dollar amount.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The principal real-world business event timestamp representing when the index credit was officially applied to the FIA contract Account Value (AV) in the Annuity Administration Platform. Distinct from record audit timestamps.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: The date on which the insurance company declared the cap rate, participation rate, or spread applicable to this crediting term. Required for regulatory disclosure and policyholder notification compliance.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation 245; State Departments of Insurance

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this index crediting event, as assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Used on policyholder statements and audit trails.

Current lifecycle status of the index crediting event within the Annuity Administration Platform workflow. 'pending' = term ended but credit not yet calculated; 'calculated' = credit computed awaiting posting; 'applied' = credit posted to Account Value (AV); 'reversed' = previously applied credit reversed; 'voided' = event cancelled before application.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: The minimum index-linked interest rate that will be credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term, protecting the policyholder from negative index returns. Typically 0% (zero floor). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.000000 = 0%). Null if a buffer applies instead of a floor.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation 245

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) Benefit Base After Credit
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMAB rider is attached to the contract. Tracks whether the AV meets or exceeds the guaranteed accumulation target. Null if no GMAB rider is present.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Benefit Base After Credit
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMIB rider is attached to the contract. Updated when the credited AV triggers a step-up. Null if no GMIB rider is present.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Benefit Base After Credit
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMWB rider is attached to the contract. Updated when the credited AV exceeds the prior benefit base (step-up). Null if no GMWB rider is present.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); IRC Section 72

description: The closing value of the external market index on the crediting term end date. Used in conjunction with the index start value to calculate the raw index return percentage.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The type of derivative option instrument used by the insurance company to hedge the index crediting obligation for this allocation segment. Relevant for actuarial valuation, ALM (Asset Liability Management), and investment portfolio management.
reference: NAIC ORSA; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); Investment Management System (Charles River IMS)

description: The raw percentage change in the external market index over the crediting term, calculated as (index_end_value - index_start_value) / index_start_value. This is the pre-cap, pre-floor, pre-buffer gross index performance. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.085000 = 8.5%).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The closing value of the external market index on the crediting term start date. Used as the baseline for calculating the index return percentage at the end of the term.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The percentage of the index gain that is credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term. Applied to the index return before cap and floor constraints. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.800000 = 80%). Null if no participation rate applies to this crediting strategy.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the cap rate, participation rate, or spread applied in this crediting term was a renewal rate (True) or the initial declared rate at contract issue (False). Renewal rates may differ from initial rates and are subject to NAIC disclosure requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation 245

description: Indicates whether this index credit event record represents a reversal of a previously applied credit (True) or an original crediting event (False). Reversals occur due to data corrections, index value restatements, or system errors.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 245

description: The native event or transaction identifier from the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity transaction ID or FAST Annuity event reference). Used for reconciliation between the Silver layer and the source system.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The identifier of the Annuity Administration Platform source system that originated this index credit event record (e.g., 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY', 'FAST_ANNUITY'). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: A fixed percentage deducted from the index return before crediting in a spread-based FIA crediting strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.020000 = 2.0% spread). Null if cap/participation rate applies instead of a spread.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 245; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this index crediting event triggered a benefit base step-up on any attached guaranteed living benefit rider (GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB). True when the post-credit AV exceeds the prior benefit base, resulting in a ratchet to the higher value.

The calendar tax year in which this index crediting event falls, relevant for IRC Section 72 tax reporting on annuity contract earnings. Used for IRS Form 1099-R preparation and tax deferred growth tracking.

description: The length of the index crediting term expressed in months (e.g., 12 for a one-year term, 24 for a two-year term). Determines the measurement window for index performance.

description: The last date of the index crediting term for this allocation segment. The index end value is typically captured on this date. Defines the end of the measurement period for index return calculation.

description: The first date of the index crediting term for this allocation segment. The index start value is typically captured on this date. Defines the beginning of the measurement period for index return calculation.

description: Timestamp when this index credit event record was last modified in the source Annuity Administration Platform system. Tracks reversals, corrections, and recalculations.

description: The dollar amount of withdrawals processed during the crediting term that reduced the allocation base used for interest crediting. Reflects the impact of systematic or ad-hoc withdrawals on the credited amount calculation.

business_glossary_term: Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) New Contract Indicator
description: Indicates whether the new annuity contract issued as a result of this exchange is classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). Determined based on MEC carryover from source and new contract funding.

Indicates whether the source contract was classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status carries over to the new contract in a 1035 exchange, affecting tax treatment of distributions.
Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) the exchange application was flagged as Not In Good Order (NIGO). Documents the specific deficiency requiring remediation.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

business_glossary_term: Not In Good Order (NIGO) Resolved Date
description: The date on which all NIGO deficiencies were resolved and the exchange application was cleared for processing.
Tracks the Not In Good Order (NIGO) resolution status for this exchange application. NIGO occurs when required documentation or information is missing or incorrect, preventing processing.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether this is a partial 1035 exchange (only a portion of the source contract's value is transferred) as opposed to a full exchange. Partial exchanges have specific IRS rules regarding basis allocation.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Revenue Ruling 2003-76; IRS Publication 575

description: The date on which the replacement notice was sent to the contract owner as required by NAIC Model Regulation #613 and applicable state replacement regulations.

description: Indicates whether the required replacement notice (NAIC Model Regulation on Replacement of Life Insurance and Annuities) was sent to the contract owner prior to processing the exchange.

description: The date on which the contract owner formally submitted the 1035 exchange request. Used as the business event initiation date for processing timelines and regulatory reporting.
reference: IRC Section 1035; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The total account value (AV) of the source contract at the time of the exchange request. Used for partial exchange basis allocation calculations and suitability documentation.

description: The name of the insurance company or financial institution from which the existing contract is being exchanged. Required for coordination of transfer and IRS Form 1099-R reporting.
The original issue date of the source contract being exchanged. Used to determine the contract's age, surrender charge schedule applicability, and MEC status carryover.

description: The product type of the source contract being exchanged. Determines eligibility under IRC Section 1035 and influences tax treatment. description: The surrender charge or contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) assessed by the source carrier upon surrender of the existing contract as part of the exchange. Reduces the net transferred amount.

description: The calendar tax year in which the 1035 exchange is reportable for IRS purposes. Determines the tax reporting period for Form 1099-R and Form 5498 filings.

description: The method by which funds were transferred from the source carrier to the receiving company. Affects processing timelines and reconciliation procedures.

The annuity contract's Account Value (AV) immediately after this withdrawal transaction has been applied. Confirms the post-withdrawal contract value for owner statements, benefit base recalculation, and subsequent transaction processing.

description: The annuity contract's Account Value (AV) immediately prior to this withdrawal transaction being applied. Used for surrender charge calculation, benefit base adjustments, and reconciliation of AV movement.

description: Last four digits of the owner's bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement of withdrawal proceeds. Stored in masked format for security compliance. Full account details are maintained in the EFT authorization record.

description: The 9-digit ABA routing transit number of the financial institution receiving the withdrawal proceeds via ACH or wire transfer. Required for electronic disbursement processing.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation E; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The non-taxable portion of the withdrawal representing return of the owner's after-tax investment (cost basis) in the annuity contract under IRC Section 72. Reduces the remaining cost basis tracked on the contract.

description: Timestamp when this withdrawal transaction record was first created in the annuity administration system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

description: IRS-defined distribution code reported in Box 7 of Form 1099-R indicating the nature of the distribution for tax purposes. Common codes: '1'=early distribution no exception; '2'=early distribution exception applies; '3'=disability; '4'=death; '7'=normal distribution; 'D'=annuity payments from non-qualified annuity; 'Q'=qualified distribution from Roth IRA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|7|D|Q|G|L|M|N|P|R|S|T|U|W — promote to reference product]

This is the valuation date used for surrender charge calculation, MVA adjustment, and 1099-R tax year determination. May differ from request_date due to processing cycles or market close timing.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from this withdrawal per the owner's withholding election or mandatory withholding rules under IRC Section 3405. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 4.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 575

description: The contract owner's federal income tax withholding election for this withdrawal. 'withhold' = owner elected voluntary withholding; 'do_not_withhold' = owner opted out of withholding; 'mandatory' = mandatory withholding applies (e.g., eligible rollover distributions). Governs IRS Form W-4P compliance.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS Form W-4P; IRS Publication 575

description: The contractual free withdrawal allowance amount available for the current contract year, typically 10% of Account Value (AV) or accumulated premiums, within which no surrender charge applies. Used to determine the surrender-charge-free portion of this withdrawal.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Benefit Base After Withdrawal
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base amount immediately after this withdrawal has been applied. Reflects any proportional reduction due to excess withdrawal above the contractual GMWB annual withdrawal amount.
reference: NAIC Variable Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) Benefit Base Before Withdrawal
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base amount immediately prior to this withdrawal. Excess withdrawals above the GMWB annual withdrawal amount reduce the benefit base proportionally. Required for variable and indexed annuity contracts with GMWB riders.
reference: NAIC Variable Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944

description: The total gross dollar amount of the withdrawal before deduction of surrender charges, Market Value Adjustment (MVA), and tax withholding. Represents the amount deducted from the contract's Account Value (AV). Core monetary field for the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines; IRS Form 1099-R

description: Indicates whether this withdrawal is part of a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035) where proceeds are being transferred directly to another annuity or life insurance contract. True = 1035 exchange; proceeds are not taxable to the owner at time of withdrawal.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 575; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this withdrawal exceeds the contractual GMWB or GMIB annual withdrawal amount, triggering a proportional reduction of the guaranteed benefit base. True = excess withdrawal occurred; False = withdrawal is within the guaranteed annual withdrawal amount.
reference: NAIC Variable Annuity Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this withdrawal is coordinated with the contract's Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) calculation under IRC Section 401(a)(9). True = RMD coordination is active and this withdrawal satisfies or partially satisfies the annual RMD obligation. Applicable to qualified annuity contracts.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the entire gross withdrawal amount falls within the contractual free withdrawal allowance corridor and therefore no surrender charge was applied. True = fully within free withdrawal; False = surrender charge applies to some or all of the withdrawal.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Dollar amount of the Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applied to this withdrawal. Can be positive (in owner's favor when interest rates have fallen) or negative (charge when rates have risen). Applicable only to fixed annuity contracts with MVA provisions. Zero if not applicable.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Fixed Annuities; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Net dollar amount disbursed to the contract owner after deducting surrender charges, MVA adjustment, and federal/state tax withholding from the gross withdrawal amount. Represents the actual cash received by the owner. Core monetary field for the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: The disbursement method used to deliver net withdrawal proceeds to the contract owner. 'ach' = Automated Clearing House electronic funds transfer; 'check' = paper check mailed to owner; 'wire' = wire transfer; 'direct_deposit' = direct deposit to owner's bank account; '1035_transfer' = direct transfer to receiving carrier for 1035 exchange.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The precise date and time when the withdrawal transaction was executed and posted to the annuity contract in the administration system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for audit, reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.

description: Calendar date on which the withdrawal request was received from the contract owner or their authorized representative. Establishes the business event initiation date for processing, valuation, and regulatory timing purposes.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: The calculated Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount for the current tax year applicable to this annuity contract under IRC Section 401(a)(9). Populated when is_rmd_coordinated is True. Used to ensure the withdrawal satisfies the minimum distribution requirement and avoid IRS excise tax penalties.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Form 5329

description: Identifies the operational annuity administration system that originated this withdrawal transaction record. 'sapiens_annuity' = Sapiens Annuity platform; 'fast_annuity' = FAST Annuity platform. Required for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL audit in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Dollar amount of state income tax withheld from this withdrawal per the owner's state withholding election or mandatory state withholding requirements. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 14.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R; State Department of Revenue Regulations; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: Two-letter US state code identifying the state for which income tax withholding was applied on this withdrawal. Determines applicable state withholding rates and mandatory withholding rules. Aligns with the owner's state of residence.
reference: State Department of Revenue Regulations; IRS Form 1099-R; NAIC Privacy Model Regulation

description: Dollar amount of the contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) or surrender charge applied to this withdrawal. Calculated based on the contract's surrender charge schedule and the amount withdrawn in excess of the free withdrawal allowance. Zero if within free withdrawal corridor.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The applicable surrender charge rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.07 = 7%) applied to the excess withdrawal amount above the free withdrawal allowance. Derived from the contract's contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) schedule for the current surrender charge year.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements

description: The contract year number within the surrender charge schedule at the time of this withdrawal. Determines the applicable surrender charge rate percentage per the contract's CDSC schedule. For example, year 1 may carry a 7% charge, declining to 0% by year 8.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Prospectus Requirements

description: Specifies whether the SWP disbursement is a fixed dollar amount, a percentage of the current Account Value (AV), or a system-calculated RMD amount. Drives the withdrawal amount calculation logic in the annuity administration platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 401(a)(9)

description: The date on which the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction is scheduled to terminate or was terminated. Null if the SWP is open-ended (e.g., lifetime income or until contract exhaustion). Populated for fixed-duration SWP plans.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: The payment frequency of the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction. Applicable when withdrawal_type is 'systematic'. Determines the schedule on which recurring withdrawals are processed from the annuity contract.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: The date on which the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction became effective and the first scheduled withdrawal was processed. Establishes the SWP plan inception date for scheduling and anniversary calculations.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: Current status of the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction associated with this transaction. 'active' = SWP is running per schedule; 'suspended' = temporarily halted by owner request; 'terminated' = SWP permanently stopped; 'pending_setup' = SWP instruction received but not yet activated.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: The portion of the gross withdrawal amount that is taxable as ordinary income under IRC Section 72, reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a. Equals gross amount minus the non-taxable cost basis (investment in contract) recovered in this withdrawal.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 575

description: Externally visible, human-readable transaction reference number assigned by the annuity administration platform (Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity) at the time of withdrawal processing. Used for customer correspondence, audit trails, and reconciliation.

description: Current lifecycle state of the withdrawal transaction or SWP instruction. 'pending' = request received but not yet processed; 'in_process' = funds movement initiated; 'completed' = disbursement confirmed; 'reversed' = transaction unwound; 'cancelled' = request voided before processing; 'suspended' = SWP plan temporarily halted.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Administration Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this withdrawal transaction record in the annuity administration system. Supports change tracking, reconciliation, and audit compliance.

description: Classification of the withdrawal event. 'systematic' = scheduled SWP disbursement; 'ad_hoc' = owner-initiated one-time withdrawal; 'rmd' = Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) mandated under IRC Section 401(a)(9); 'full_surrender' = complete contract liquidation; 'partial_surrender' = partial contract liquidation with surrender charge; 'free_withdrawal' = withdrawal within the contractual free withdrawal allowance.
