reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Regulations; FINRA Rule 2330; Sapiens Annuity

description: The date and time when the annuitization election was formally approved by the insurance company's new business or contract servicing team. Null if election is still pending or was cancelled.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; SOX Section 404

description: The Assumed Interest Rate (AIR) used in the annuity factor calculation at the time of annuitization. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 = 3.50%). Relevant for variable annuity payout calculations where actual performance is compared to AIR.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this election record. Typically USD for domestic US annuity contracts.

The date on which the contract officially transitions from the accumulation phase to the payout phase. Also known as the Annuity Start Date (ASD) or Annuity Commencement Date. Governs IRC Section 72 tax treatment.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module

description: The calendar date on which the contract owner formally submitted the annuitization election request. Serves as the principal business event date for this transaction.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Externally-visible business identifier assigned to this annuitization election by the Annuity Administration Platform. Used in correspondence, confirmations, and regulatory filings.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date and time when the annuitization election request was received and logged in the Annuity Administration Platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for this transaction.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; SOX Section 404

description: The distribution or service channel through which the annuitization election was submitted. Used for operational analytics and agent commission impact assessment.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; NAIC Market Conduct Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the annuitization election. 'pending' = submitted but not yet approved; 'approved' = approved but payout not yet started; 'in_force' = payout phase active; 'cancelled' = withdrawn before effective date; 'rescinded' = reversed after effective date per applicable free-look or regulatory provision.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio representing the proportion of each annuity payment that is a tax-free return of the owner's cost basis. Calculated as investment in contract divided by expected return. Used for 1099-R tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 939; NAIC Tax Reporting Standards

description: The total expected return from the annuity contract as calculated under IRC Section 72 tables, used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation. For life annuities, derived from IRS actuarial tables based on annuitant age and payout option.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 939; IRS Actuarial Tables

description: The scheduled date of the first annuity income payment to the payee following annuitization. May differ from the effective date depending on payout frequency and payment timing convention (immediate vs. deferred first payment).
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; IRC Section 72

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Benefit Base Amount
description: The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base amount at the time of annuitization election, when the GMIB rider is exercised. Represents the guaranteed minimum value used to calculate the income stream, which may exceed the current account value.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

business_glossary_term: Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) Rider Applied Indicator
description: Indicates whether the Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) rider was exercised as the basis for this annuitization election. True = GMIB benefit base used; False = standard account value annuitization.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: The owner's total after-tax cost basis in the annuity contract at the time of annuitization, as defined under IRC Section 72. Used to calculate the exclusion ratio for tax-free recovery of basis in annuity payments.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 939; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Indicates whether the annuitization election involves a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035) from another insurance or annuity contract. True = the annuitization basis includes proceeds from a 1035 exchange; False = no exchange involved.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 575; Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module

description: Indicates whether the annuitization election is irrevocable once the effective date is reached. True = the owner cannot reverse or modify the election after the annuity start date; False = election may be modified within applicable free-look or rescission period.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; State Department of Insurance Regulations

description: The date and time when this annuitization election record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module

description: The last date by which the contract owner may rescind or cancel the annuitization election under applicable state free-look or rescission provisions. Null for irrevocable elections or after the deadline has passed.
reference: State Department of Insurance Free-Look Regulations; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The native identifier assigned to this annuitization election record in the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity). Supports data lineage tracing from Silver Layer back to the operational system of record.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Annuitization Module; ETL Data Lineage Standards

description: The surrender charge amount that would have applied at the time of annuitization, prior to any waiver. Captured for audit and actuarial analysis even when waived. Null if no surrender charge schedule applies.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the surrender charge was waived upon annuitization. Many annuity contracts waive surrender charges when the owner elects to annuitize, as an incentive for lifetime income election.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Regulations

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract is tax-qualified (e.g., IRA, 403(b), 401(a)) or non-qualified. Governs IRC Section 72 tax treatment, RMD applicability, and 1099-R reporting codes.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRC Section 401; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Tax Reporting Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each benefit payment record disbursed under an annuity payout schedule or living benefit claim. Primary key for the benefit_payment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Annuitization benefit payments are disbursed through billing accounts. Real business process: annuity payout processing, recurring payment execution, tax withholding coordination. Critical for post-an
Reference to the annuitant or beneficiary receiving this benefit payment. Supports party-level reporting and 1099-R tax reporting under IRC Section 72.
reference: IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Reference to the annuity contract under which this benefit payment is disbursed. Links the payment to the governing contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.exclusion_rider. Business justification: Benefit payment adjudication requires checking applicable exclusion riders to determine if the triggering cause (cause of death, disability type) is excluded. Claims and benefit operations teams use t

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Annuity benefit payments require journal entries for benefit expense (debit), tax withholding liability (credit), and cash disbursement (credit). Direct GL posting for every annuitization payment and

Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Life-contingent annuity benefit payments (period certain + life, joint life) require mortality table reference for reserve release calculations and expected payment duration. Actuaries use this link f

Reference to the original benefit_payment_id that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only when payment_reversal_indicator is True. Enables tracing of correction chains for audit and reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to annuity.payout_schedule. Business justification: benefit_payment records each periodic income payment disbursed under an annuity payout. payout_schedule defines the payment schedule (frequency, amount, option) that governs those payments. A benefit_

Foreign key linking to annuity.rider_benefit. Business justification: benefit_payment records periodic income payments including rider-based payments (GMWB income, GMIB annuitization, GMAB maturity). rider_benefit is the master record for each optional rider. When a pay

Foreign key linking to annuity.rmd_schedule. Business justification: benefit_payment has is_rmd_payment and rmd_tax_year fields indicating RMD-satisfying payments. When a benefit_payment satisfies an RMD obligation (common for qualified annuities in payout phase), it m

Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: Annuity payments to suspicious parties or unusual payment patterns may trigger SAR filing per BSA requirements. Links benefit payments to SAR filings for AML transaction monitoring and regulatory repo

Foreign key linking to annuity.surrender_charge. Business justification: benefit_payment captures surrender_charge_amount for payments that incur surrender charges (e.g., early annuitization or partial surrenders processed as benefit payments). surrender_charge holds the s

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Benefit payments generate 1099-R tax documents that must be retained and linked to the originating payment for IRS reporting, owner tax inquiries, and state tax authority audits. The benefit_payment a

The annuity contract's Account Value (AV) as of the payment date, prior to the disbursement of this payment. Used to assess the relationship between the benefit base and AV, determine if the contract is 'in the money' on living benefit riders, and support actuarial reserving.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: The guaranteed benefit base (rider base) value at the time of this payment, applicable for GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB riders. The benefit base is the notional amount used to calculate guaranteed income or withdrawal amounts and may differ from the contract Account Value (AV).
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Benefit Calculations Module; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: The sequential check number assigned to a paper check disbursement. Populated only when payment_method is 'check'. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail.

Indicates whether the annuity contract is qualified (funded with pre-tax dollars under ERISA/IRS qualified plans such as IRA, 401(k), 403(b)) or non-qualified (funded with after-tax dollars). Determines tax treatment, exclusion ratio applicability, and RMD obligations.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRC Section 401(a); ERISA; IRS Publication 575

description: The timestamp when this benefit payment record was first created in the Sapiens Annuity payment processing system. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; SOX Section 404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this payment record (e.g., 'USD'). Supports multi-currency operations for international annuity contracts.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: IRS-defined distribution code reported in Box 7 of Form 1099-R that characterizes the nature of the distribution for tax purposes (e.g., '1'=early distribution no exception, '2'=early distribution exception applies, '3'=disability, '4'=death, '7'=normal distribution, 'D'=annuity payments from non-qualified annuity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|7|D|G|L|P|Q|R|S|T|U|W — promote to reference product]
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Box 7 Instructions; IRC Section 72(t); IRS Publication 575

description: Dollar amount of the 10% federal early withdrawal penalty tax assessed on this distribution under IRC Section 72(t) when the annuitant is under age 59½ and no exception applies. Reported on IRS Form 5329.
reference: IRC Section 72(t); IRS Form 5329; IRS Publication 575

description: The non-taxable portion of the gross payment for non-qualified annuity contracts, calculated by applying the IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio (investment in contract divided by expected return) to the gross payment. Represents recovery of the annuitant's after-tax investment in the contract.
reference: IRC Section 72(b); IRS Publication 939; IRS Form 1099-R

description: The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.312500 = 31.25%) applied to this payment to determine the non-taxable recovery of investment in contract. Calculated as investment in contract divided by expected return at annuity start date.
Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from the gross benefit payment per the annuitant's W-4P election or mandatory withholding rules under IRC Section 3405. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 4.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS Form 1099-R Box 4; IRS W-4P

description: The total scheduled benefit payment amount before any federal or state tax withholding deductions. Represents the full contractual income entitlement for the payment period. Used as the base for 1099-R gross distribution reporting.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R; IRC Section 72; Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module

description: Cumulative after-tax investment in the annuity contract that has been recovered (returned tax-free) through all benefit payments up to and including this payment. Tracks the running total of basis recovered under IRC Section 72 to determine when the full investment has been recouped.
reference: IRC Section 72(b); IRS Publication 575; IRS Form 1099-R

description: Indicates whether this payment is a distribution from a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) as defined under IRC Section 7702A. MEC distributions are subject to income-first taxation and potential 10% penalty. Reported in IRS Form 1099-R Box 7 with code 'D'.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS Form 1099-R; IRS Publication 575

description: Indicates whether this benefit payment satisfies all or part of the annuitant's Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) obligation for the tax year under IRC Section 401(a)(9). True = this payment counts toward the RMD; False = not an RMD payment.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; DOL ERISA

description: The actual dollar amount remitted to the annuitant or beneficiary after deducting federal and state tax withholding from the gross payment amount. This is the amount deposited via ACH, issued by check, or wired.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; IRC Section 72

description: Last four digits of the payee's bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement. Stored as a masked reference for reconciliation and payment verification without retaining full account details. Full account data is stored in the EFT authorization record.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; PCI DSS; Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module

description: ABA routing transit number of the payee's financial institution used for ACH or wire transfer disbursement. Nine-digit identifier assigned by the American Bankers Association.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ABA Routing Number Standard; Sapiens Annuity

description: The actual calendar date on which the benefit payment was disbursed or issued to the annuitant or beneficiary. This is the principal real-world event date used for tax reporting, reconciliation, and regulatory compliance.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R Instructions; Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module

description: The contractual frequency at which benefit payments are scheduled to be disbursed. Drives the payment schedule generation in the Sapiens Annuity platform and is used for actuarial cash flow projections.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Model Regulation

description: The disbursement instrument used to remit the net payment to the annuitant or beneficiary. 'ach' = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; 'check' = paper check mailed to payee; 'wire' = domestic or international wire transfer; 'direct_deposit' = direct deposit to a designated financial account.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module

description: The last day of the benefit period covered by this payment. Together with payment_period_start_date, defines the income interval for tax and actuarial reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944; Sapiens Annuity

description: The first day of the benefit period covered by this payment. Used for income period attribution, tax year allocation, and actuarial reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; FASB ASC 944; Sapiens Annuity

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned by the Sapiens Annuity payment processing module to uniquely identify this disbursement for reconciliation, customer service, and audit purposes.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this payment record represents a reversal or correction of a previously issued payment. True = this is a reversal/correction entry; False = original payment. Used for reconciliation and audit trail integrity.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Sequential ordinal number of this payment within the annuity contract's payout schedule (e.g., 1 = first payment, 24 = 24th monthly payment). Used for payment tracking, duration analysis, and identifying the position within the payout phase.

Current lifecycle state of the benefit payment. 'scheduled' = payment queued for future disbursement; 'issued' = payment sent to payee; 'cleared' = confirmed received by payee's financial institution; 'returned' = payment rejected or returned by bank; 'stopped' = payment halted before issuance; 'voided' = payment cancelled and reversed.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Classification of the benefit payment by its contractual or regulatory basis. Distinguishes annuitization income, Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) payments, Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) income, systematic withdrawals, Required Minimum Distributions (RMD), death benefit payouts, partial surrenders, and lump-sum settlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annuitization|gmwb_withdrawal|gmib_income|systematic_withdrawal|rmd_distribution|death_benefit|surrender_partial|lump_sum — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; IRC Section 401(a)(9) RMD Rules; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Indicates the contractual phase of the annuity contract at the time of this payment. 'accumulation' = contract still in growth phase (e.g., systematic withdrawal); 'annuitization' = contract has been annuitized and is in income phase; 'payout' = living benefit payout phase; 'surrender' = payment associated with full or partial surrender.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation

description: The gross distribution amount to be reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 1 for this payment. For most payments equals gross_payment_amount; may differ for corrected or adjusted payments. Drives annual 1099-R tax form generation for the annuitant.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Box 1; IRC Section 6047; Sapiens Annuity

description: The IRS tax year (four-digit year) to which this RMD payment is attributed. Populated only when is_rmd_payment is True. Used for RMD compliance tracking and IRS reporting under IRC Section 401(a)(9).

The contractually scheduled date on which the benefit payment was originally planned to be disbursed per the annuity payout schedule. Used to identify late payments and measure payment timeliness against contractual obligations.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Dollar amount of state income tax withheld from the gross benefit payment per the annuitant's state withholding election or applicable state mandatory withholding rules. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 14.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Box 14; State Department of Revenue Regulations; Sapiens Annuity

description: Two-letter USPS state code identifying the state for which state income tax was withheld on this payment. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 15. Required for multi-state tax reporting compliance.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R Box 15; State Department of Revenue Regulations

description: Contractual deferred sales charge (surrender charge) deducted from the payment amount when a withdrawal or surrender occurs within the surrender charge period defined in the annuity contract. Reduces the net payment to the annuitant.
reference: Sapiens Annuity — Contract Administration Module; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; SEC Variable Annuity Disclosure Rules

description: The IRS tax year (four-digit calendar year) to which this benefit payment is attributed for 1099-R reporting purposes. Typically the year in which the payment_date falls, but may differ for year-end payments processed in January.
reference: IRS Form 1099-R; IRC Section 6047; IRS Publication 575

description: The portion of the gross payment amount that is subject to ordinary income tax. For non-qualified contracts, this equals gross payment minus the exclusion ratio amount. For qualified contracts, typically equals the full gross payment. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a; IRS Publication 575

description: The timestamp when this benefit payment record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail maintenance.
reference: Sapiens Annuity Payment Processing Module; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates the basis for federal tax withholding on this payment. 'elected' = annuitant submitted a W-4P withholding election; 'mandatory' = mandatory withholding applies (e.g., eligible rollover distributions); 'waived' = annuitant elected to waive withholding where permitted under IRC Section 3405.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS W-4P; IRS Publication 505

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the annuity payout schedule record in the Annuity Administration Platform. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the parent annuity contract for which this payout schedule is defined. Links the payout schedule to the annuity contract administration record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Life-contingent payout schedules (life only, joint-and-survivor, period certain and life) require mortality tables for payment calculation. Essential for accurate benefit payments, reserve adequacy, a

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Annuitization payout options, mortality tables, and annuity option rates are locked to plan version at election date. Critical for payout calculations, mortality table selection, and regulatory compli

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Payout schedule payment amounts are calculated using actuarial pricing_basis assumptions (assumed interest rate, mortality). This link enables SPIA/annuity payment rate validation against filed pricin
Reference to the primary annuitant (measuring life) whose life expectancy governs the payout duration. Required for life-contingent payout options.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Life-contingent payout schedules (life-only, life with period certain) use the annuitant's underwriting risk class to apply appropriate mortality assumptions for scheduled payment amounts and reserve

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity payout schedules calculate periodic payments using annuity unit values from a specific separate account. This link is required for annuity unit value lookups during payment calculatio

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Payout schedules must comply with state-specific nonforfeiture regulations, minimum annuity payout options, and withholding rules. Payout administrators reference the applicable state regulation to va

Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Payout annuity schedules drive statutory reserve calculations (CARVM payout phase, Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method). Appointed actuaries link payout schedules to statutory reserve recor
The Account Value (AV) of the deferred annuity contract at the time of annuitization, used as the premium basis for calculating the annuity income payment amount. Null for SPIA contracts.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date on which a deferred annuity contract was formally annuitized, converting the accumulated account value into a stream of annuity income payments. Null for SPIA contracts where annuitization occurs at contract issue.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The annuity commencement date (ACD) — the date on which the annuity contract formally enters the payout phase and benefit calculations are locked. Distinct from payment_start_date, which is the first actual disbursement date. Governs IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Annual date on which the Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) is applied to increase the scheduled annuity income payment amount. Typically the contract anniversary date. Null if no COLA applies.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Type of inflation index used to determine the Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) applied to annuity income payments. Fixed indicates a contractually specified fixed rate; CPI-U and CPI-W reference U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Consumer Price Index measures; none indicates no COLA applies.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI

description: Annual Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) rate applied to the scheduled payment amount to increase annuity income payments over time to offset inflation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%). Zero or null if no COLA applies.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this payout schedule record was first created in the system of record. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; Enterprise Data Governance Policy; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this payout schedule (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency annuity contracts.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date from which this version of the payout schedule is in effect. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking for schedule amendments such as COLA adjustments, payment amount changes, or payout option modifications.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944

description: The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio applied to each annuity income payment to determine the non-taxable return-of-investment portion. Calculated as the investment in the contract divided by the expected return. Used for IRS Form 1099-R tax reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 939; NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245)

description: The total amount expected to be received under the annuity contract over its payout period, as calculated under IRC Section 72 using IRS actuarial tables. Used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 939; IRS Actuarial Tables

description: Federal income tax withholding rate applied to the taxable portion of each annuity income payment, as elected by the annuitant or as mandated by IRS default withholding rules. Used for IRS Form 1099-R reporting and payment net calculation.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS Publication 575; IRS Form W-4P

description: The assumed interest rate used in the actuarial calculation of the annuity income payment amount at the time of annuitization or SPIA pricing. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.035 for 3.5%). Used in actuarial reserve calculations and FASB ASC 944 LDTI discount rate determinations.
reference: NAIC Standard Valuation Law; FASB ASC 944 (LDTI); Society of Actuaries (SOA) standards

description: The annuitant's after-tax cost basis in the annuity contract at the annuity start date, as defined under IRC Section 72. Used to calculate the exclusion ratio and the non-taxable portion of each annuity income payment for IRS Form 1099-R reporting.
Gross amount of the most recently issued annuity income payment. May differ from scheduled_payment_amount due to COLA adjustments, partial-period proration, or manual adjustments.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944

description: Date on which the most recent annuity income payment was issued under this payout schedule. Used to confirm payment continuity and detect missed payments.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity / FAST Annuity platform conventions

description: Date on which the next scheduled annuity income payment is due to be issued. Updated after each payment cycle by the Annuity Administration Platform. Used in benefit payment processing and cash flow management.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Sapiens Annuity / FAST Annuity platform conventions

description: Date on which the last scheduled annuity income payment is due. Populated for period-certain and fixed-term payout options; null for life-only payout options where payments continue until the annuitant's death.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); IRC Section 72

description: Frequency at which annuity income payments are disbursed to the annuitant. Determines the payment schedule cadence and is used in benefit payment processing and actuarial liability calculations.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Method by which annuity income payments are disbursed to the annuitant (e.g., Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT), check, wire transfer). Drives payment processing routing in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NACHA Operating Rules (for EFT)

description: Date on which the first annuity income payment is scheduled to be issued. For Single Premium Immediate Annuities (SPIA), this is typically one payment period after the contract issue date. Governs the commencement of the payout phase.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Cumulative count of annuity income payments that have been issued to date under this payout schedule. Used to track progress against total expected payments and to determine remaining guaranteed payments.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FASB ASC 944

description: Standardized code identifying the annuity payout form elected by the annuitant (e.g., life-only, life with period certain, joint-and-survivor, period certain only, installment refund, cash refund). Drives payment duration logic and survivor benefit calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life_only|life_period_certain|joint_survivor|period_certain_only|installment_refund|cash_refund|life_with_refund — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Human-readable description of the elected annuity payout option as it appears on the annuity contract and benefit statements (e.g., 'Life with 10-Year Period Certain', 'Joint and 50% Survivor'). Used in annuitant communications and regulatory disclosures.

Number of years for which annuity income payments are guaranteed regardless of the annuitant's survival (e.g., 5, 10, 20 years). Applicable to life-with-period-certain and period-certain-only payout options. Null for life-only options.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); IRC Section 72

description: Indicates whether the payout schedule includes a pop-up provision, whereby the annuity income payment increases (pops up) to the single-life amount if the joint annuitant predeceases the primary annuitant. Applicable to joint-and-survivor payout options only.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation (MDL-245); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Type of tax-qualified retirement plan under which the annuity contract is held, governing Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) rules and tax reporting requirements. Non-qualified for contracts not held in a qualified plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IRA|Roth_IRA|403b|401a|457b|457f|SEP_IRA|SIMPLE_IRA|non_qualified — promote to reference product]

The ALM strategy employed for this portfolio segment and liability cohort pairing. Defines the approach used to manage interest rate risk and ensure asset-liability alignment.
The actual measured Macaulay or modified duration (in years) of the portfolio segment as of the measurement date. Represents the weighted average time to receive cash flows from the asset portfolio.
The difference between asset convexity and liability convexity. Measures the mismatch in non-linear interest rate sensitivity between assets and liabilities.
The date and time when this ALM analysis record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
The dollar change in portfolio value for a 1 basis point parallel shift in interest rates. Expressed in currency units. Combines duration and portfolio size to quantify absolute interest rate risk.

The minimum acceptable duration (in years) for the portfolio segment. Defines the lower bound of the duration tolerance band.

The difference between asset duration and liability duration (asset_duration minus liability_duration). A positive gap indicates assets are longer than liabilities; a negative gap indicates the opposite. Critical metric for interest rate risk exposure.

business_glossary_term: Dollar Value of One Basis Point (DV01)
description: The dollar value change in the portfolio for a one basis point change in yield. Also known as PV01 or basis point value. Key metric for hedging and risk management.
The status of the duration gap relative to the defined tolerance band. Indicates whether the gap is acceptable or requires management action.

business_glossary_term: Interest Rate Sensitivity Parallel Shift Down 100 Basis Points (bp)

reference: NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) C-3 Interest Rate Risk

business_glossary_term: Interest Rate Sensitivity Parallel Shift Up 100 Basis Points (bp)

reference: NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) C-3 Interest Rate Risk

description: Current lifecycle status of the investment position. description: The economic sector or industry classification of the security issuer using NAIC or GICS taxonomy (e.g., financial, utilities, technology, government, sovereign). description: The contractual date on which a fixed income security's principal is scheduled to be repaid in full. Used for ALM duration matching, cash flow projection in actuarial valuation systems, and NAIC Schedule D reporting. Null for equity and perpetual instruments.

business_glossary_term: National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) Designation
description: NAIC credit quality designation assigned to fixed income securities (1 through 6, where 1 is highest quality and 6 is in or near default). reference: NAIC Securities Valuation Office (SVO); NAIC RBC Instructions; State Insurance Regulations

business_glossary_term: National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) Risk-Based Capital (RBC) Factor
description: The NAIC-prescribed RBC investment risk charge factor applicable to this asset based on its asset class, NAIC designation, and issuer type. Multiplied by the book value or amortized cost to calculate the C-1 (asset default) risk charge component in the RBC formula.
reference: NAIC RBC Instructions; NAIC Life RBC Formula; State Insurance Regulations

business_glossary_term: National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) Schedule Code
description: NAIC Annual Statement Schedule classification code indicating which statutory schedule this asset is reported on: D-1 (bonds owned), D-2 (bonds acquired/disposed), E (cash and short-term investments), BA (other long-term invested assets), B (mortgage loans). Mandatory for statutory financial reporting.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; NAIC Schedule D, BA, B, E

description: The face or nominal value of the security as stated on the instrument, representing the principal amount for bonds or the stated value for equities. Used in NAIC Schedule D reporting, amortized cost calculations, and coupon income accrual.

description: The number of units, shares, or contracts held for this investment position. For bonds, represents the face amount in units; for equities, the number of shares; for fund interests, the number of units or partnership interests. Core measurement field for the resource.

description: The Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organization (NRSRO) that assigned the credit rating for this security. Required for NAIC SVO cross-reference and regulatory credit quality validation.

description: The difference between the fair market value and the amortized cost (or book value) of the investment position, representing the mark-to-market gain or loss not yet realized through sale. Reported in NAIC Schedule D and used for GAAP OCI reporting under FASB ASC 320.
reference: FASB ASC 320; NAIC Schedule D; SAP Accounting Principles

description: The date on which this manager assignment became effective. Used for succession planning, performance attribution period determination, and regulatory fiduciary responsibility documentation.

business_glossary_term: Tactical Asset Allocation (TAA) Equity Weight Percentage

The specific condition or event that must occur for the rider benefit to be payable. Examples: accidental death, disability, critical illness diagnosis, inability to perform ADLs (Activities of Daily Living), account value depletion, etc.

description: The accumulated cash value or account value associated with this rider, if applicable. Some riders (e.g., paid-up additions riders) accumulate cash value. Null for term riders or pure protection riders. Expressed in policy currency.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Life and Annuity Framework

description: The per-unit cost of insurance rate applied to the rider face amount to calculate the rider charge. Common for universal life and variable universal life riders. Expressed as a rate per thousand of coverage.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Life and Annuity Framework

description: The date and time when this rider record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The date when this specific rider coverage became effective on the policy. May differ from policy issue date if rider was added post-issue.

The waiting period (in days) from the date of a qualifying event until benefits begin to be paid. Common for disability income and long-term care riders. Zero or null if no elimination period applies.
Date on which the rider coverage ends or matures. Nullable for riders with lifetime coverage or those tied to base policy duration.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Life and Annuity Framework

description: The benefit amount for this specific rider attachment. For ADB riders, this is the accidental death benefit amount. For term riders, this is the additional death benefit. Amount is specific to this policy-rider combination.

Indicates whether this rider includes a guaranteed insurability option, allowing the policyholder to increase coverage at specified intervals without additional underwriting. True if option is included, False otherwise.
The age of the insured at the time the rider was issued. Used for premium calculation and benefit determination. Expressed in years.
The outstanding loan amount taken against this riders cash value, if loans are permitted. Expressed in policy currency. Null or zero if no loan exists.

The premium charged for this specific rider on this policy, calculated based on rider premium_basis, insured age, risk class, and face amount. This is the actual premium for this policy-rider instance.
Frequency at which the rider premium is charged. Annual: once per year. Semi-annual: twice per year. Quarterly: four times per year. Monthly: twelve times per year. Single: one-time premium payment.

Pattern of premium payments over the rider term. Level: constant premium. Increasing: premium increases over time. Decreasing: premium decreases over time. Flexible: premium varies based on policyholder elections or account performance.
Additional premium percentage applied for substandard underwriting risk. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 means 25% additional premium). Null or zero for standard and preferred classes.
Indicates whether the risk for this rider has been ceded to a reinsurer. True if reinsured, False if retained. Used for risk management and financial reporting.

Current lifecycle status of the rider. Active: rider is in force and providing coverage. Suspended: temporarily not in force. Terminated: rider has been cancelled. Lapsed: rider has lapsed due to non-payment. Pending: rider application is under review. Matured: rider has reached its maturity date.

The name or identifier of the operational system from which this rider record originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The two-letter US state code where the rider was issued. Determines applicable state insurance regulations and tax treatment.

The penalty or charge applied if the rider is surrendered or terminated early. Common for riders with cash value or guaranteed benefits. Expressed in policy currency. Null if no surrender charge applies.
Indicates whether the rider is part of a tax-qualified plan (e.g., 401k, IRA, 403b). True if tax-qualified, False otherwise. Affects tax treatment of premiums and benefits.
The date when this rider coverage was terminated, either due to rider expiry age, policy termination, or voluntary removal by policy owner.
The date and time when this rider record was last modified in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The version or edition of the rider form. Used to track changes in rider terms, benefits, or pricing over time. Important for compliance and benefit administration.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Life and Annuity Framework

description: Indicates whether this rider includes a waiver of premium benefit, meaning rider premiums are waived if the insured becomes disabled or meets other qualifying conditions. True if waiver applies, False otherwise.

The maximum annual withdrawal percentage allowed under a GMWB (Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit) rider without penalty. Expressed as a percentage of the benefit base (e.g., 5.00 means 5% per year).
Unique identifier for the policy conversion transaction. Primary key for the policy conversion record.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model
