Reference to the original term life policy being converted. Links to the source policy record in the in-force policy table.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Reference to the newly issued permanent policy resulting from the conversion. Links to the target policy record in the in-force policy table.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Reference to the internal user or system agent who processed the conversion transaction. Used for audit and workflow tracking.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Reference to the agent or producer who facilitated the conversion. Links to the producer record in the agent management system.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Term-to-permanent conversions generate conversion application forms, new policy contracts, and evidence-of-insurability waiver documents required for regulatory filing and new policy issuance. Direct

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Policy conversions (term-to-permanent) trigger journal entries for reserve transfers, DAC reclassification, and premium income reclassification. Finance must trace conversion events to GL impact for G
Self-referencing FK on conversion (original_conversion_id)

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Policy conversions result in a new policy issued on a new approved form. Compliance requires verifying the converted-to product form was approved in the issuing jurisdiction at conversion time. No exi

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Policy conversions require pricing basis to calculate new premium rates, face amounts, and underwriting class adjustments. Underwriting teams reference pricing basis for conversion privilege calculati

Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Policy conversion to annuity (e.g., term-to-annuity or whole life conversion privilege) is a named life insurance business process requiring direct traceability to the resulting annuity contract for c

Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A term-to-permanent policy conversion is a post-issue servicing transaction initiated through a service request (request_type='CONVERSION'). Linking conversion to the originating service_request provi

Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Policy conversions, especially to annuity or variable products, require suitability review under NAIC model regulations and state rules. Linking conversion to suitability_review enables compliance tra

Foreign key linking to product.plan. Business justification: Conversion records the target plan a policy converts to. Linking conversion to the target plan_id enables conversion privilege validation against plan eligibility rules, new policy issuance workflow,

Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: Conversions with evidence of insurability requirements produce a formal underwriting decision (approve/decline/rate). The conversion table has denormalized decision context but no FK to the authoritat
Date when the conversion request was approved by underwriting or policy services. Indicates when the conversion was authorized to proceed.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Indicates whether managerial or underwriting approval is required for this conversion. True if the conversion requires special authorization beyond standard processing.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The insureds age at the time of conversion. Used for underwriting and pricing calculations for the new permanent policy.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The age basis used for pricing the new permanent policy. Determines whether the new policy is priced at the insureds current attained age or original issue age.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Policy Conversion Standards

description: The distribution channel through which the conversion request was initiated. Indicates how the policyholder submitted the conversion request.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context related to the conversion transaction. Used for documenting unique circumstances or processing notes.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Date when the conversion transaction was fully completed and the new policy was issued. Marks the end of the conversion lifecycle.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Unique business transaction number assigned to this conversion event. Used for tracking and reference in policy administration systems.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Current lifecycle status of the conversion transaction. Tracks the conversion through its workflow from initial request to final completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|rejected|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Classification of the conversion based on the contractual provision being exercised. Indicates whether this is a standard contractual right or a special conversion offer.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Timestamp when this conversion record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Any credit or value transferred from the original term policy to the new permanent policy. May include accumulated dividends, paid-up additions, or other policy values.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Date when the conversion becomes effective and the new permanent policy is in force. The original term policy terminates on this date.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Policy Conversion Standards

description: Indicates whether medical evidence of insurability is required for the conversion. True if underwriting evidence is needed; false if conversion is guaranteed without evidence.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Policy Conversion Standards

description: Indicates whether the evidence of insurability requirement was waived for this conversion. True if the conversion privilege allows conversion without medical underwriting.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Policy Conversion Standards

description: Death benefit face amount of the newly issued permanent policy. May differ from the original face amount based on conversion terms and policyholder election.
The premium amount for the new permanent policy based on the selected payment frequency. Represents the recurring premium obligation for the converted policy.
The policy number of the newly issued permanent policy. Business identifier for the converted policy.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The payment frequency selected for the new permanent policy. Indicates how often premiums will be paid on the converted policy.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Any table rating or substandard rating applied to the new permanent policy. Expressed as a percentage or table letter indicating additional mortality risk.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Underwriting Standards

description: The underwriting risk class assigned to the new permanent policy. Determines the premium rate structure for the converted policy.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Underwriting Standards

description: Indicates whether the conversion application was flagged as Not In Good Order due to missing or incomplete information. True if the application requires additional documentation or corrections.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

business_glossary_term: Not In Good Order (NIGO) Reason Code
description: Code indicating the specific reason the conversion application was flagged as Not In Good Order. Used for tracking application quality and processing efficiency.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Death benefit face amount of the original term policy at the time of conversion. Represents the coverage amount being converted.
The insureds age when the original term policy was issued. May be used for pricing if the conversion is on an original-age basis.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The policy number of the original term life policy being converted. Business identifier for the source policy.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Type of term life insurance product being converted. Identifies the specific term product category of the source policy.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The last date on which the policyholder can exercise the conversion privilege under the original term policy. After this date, the conversion right expires.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation Service - Policy Conversion Standards

description: Number of days from conversion request to completion. Used for service level agreement monitoring and process efficiency analysis.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Code indicating the reason or motivation for the conversion. Used for tracking conversion patterns and policyholder behavior analysis.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Code indicating the reason for conversion rejection if the request was denied. Used for tracking denial patterns and compliance reporting.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Detailed explanation of why the conversion request was rejected. Provides context for the rejection decision and supports customer communication.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Date when the policyholder submitted the conversion request. Marks the beginning of the conversion transaction lifecycle.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Code identifying the source policy administration system from which the conversion record originated. Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: The unique transaction identifier from the source policy administration system. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the originating system.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Timestamp when this conversion record was last modified. Used for tracking changes and maintaining data currency.
Unique identifier for the dividend record. Primary key for the dividend product.

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Dividend scales are built from actuarial assumption sets containing mortality, interest, and expense factors. Actuaries use assumption_set to calculate declared_amount and apportionment factors. Real

Foreign key linking to actuarial.experience_study. Business justification: Dividend factors (mortality_factor, interest_factor, expense_factor on dividend record) are directly derived from experience study results. Actuarial teams set annual dividend scales based on experien
Reference to the participating whole life policy to which this dividend applies. Links to the policy master record.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Dividend declarations and payments generate journal entries for policyholder dividend expense, cash disbursements, and PUA purchases. Finance requires direct traceability from dividend records to GL p

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Participating whole life dividend calculations are driven by general account portfolio performance (mortality, interest, expense factors). Required for dividend scale setting, apportionment methodolog

Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Participating policy dividend declarations are based on annual actuarial valuation results (mortality, interest, expense experience). Linking dividend to valuation_run enables actuarial teams to trace
The current balance of dividends left on deposit with the company when the accumulate-at-interest option is elected. Includes principal and accrued interest.
The annual interest rate credited to accumulated dividends left on deposit. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 for 3.50%). Rate may be guaranteed or declared annually.
The interest credited to accumulated dividends during the current policy year. Used for tax reporting and policy value reconciliation.
reference: IRS Publication 525 - Taxable and Nontaxable Income

description: The actuarial method used to allocate divisible surplus to this policy. CONTRIBUTION = contribution method; THREE_FACTOR = mortality, interest, expense factors; EXPERIENCE = experience rating; ASSET_SHARE = asset share method.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Dividend Illustrations

description: The amount paid in cash to the policyholder when the cash dividend option is elected. May differ from declared amount if partial amounts are applied to other options.
The date on which the cash dividend payment was issued or electronically transferred to the policyholder. Used for cash flow tracking and reconciliation.
The method used to disburse the cash dividend payment. CHECK = paper check; ACH = automated clearing house; WIRE = wire transfer; EFT = electronic funds transfer.
The date and time when this dividend record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Management Assessment of Internal Controls

description: The date on which the board of directors or authorized committee formally declared the dividend for the policy year. Represents the official authorization date.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Dividend Illustrations

description: The total dividend amount declared for the policy for the dividend year, expressed in the policy currency. Represents the policyholder's share of divisible surplus before any option election.

Current processing status of the dividend. DECLARED = authorized but not yet processed; PENDING = awaiting option election; PAID = cash payment issued; APPLIED = applied per elected option; FORFEITED = lost due to lapse; REVERSED = reversed due to policy adjustment.
The expense component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder's share of favorable expense experience relative to pricing assumptions.
Indicates whether this dividend amount was used in a policy illustration. True if included in an illustration provided to the policyholder; false otherwise. Used for compliance tracking.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Life Insurance Illustrations

description: The investment earnings component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder's share of investment income exceeding guaranteed rates.
The amount of the dividend applied to reduce the outstanding policy loan balance when the loan reduction option is elected. Reduces principal and/or accrued loan interest.
The mortality component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder's share of favorable mortality experience for the dividend year.

The dividend option elected by the policyholder. CASH = cash payment; PREM_RED = premium reduction; PUA = paid-up additions; ACCUM = accumulate at interest; OYT = one-year term insurance; LOAN_RED = loan reduction.
The date on which the elected dividend option becomes effective. May differ from declaration date if the policyholder changes their election within the allowed window.
The face amount of one-year term insurance purchased with the dividend when the OYT option is elected. Provides temporary additional death benefit for one policy year.
reference: NAIC Model Policy Form Standards - One-Year Term Rider

description: The portion of the dividend used to purchase the one-year term coverage. Represents the cost of the temporary insurance benefit.
The amount of the dividend applied to reduce the next premium due when the premium reduction option is elected. Reduces the net premium payable by the policyholder.
The date and time when the dividend record was processed by the policy administration system. Represents the system processing event, distinct from business effective dates.
The cash surrender value of the paid-up additions purchased with this dividend. Contributes to the total policy cash value and is available for loans or surrender.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions - Schedule D

description: The face amount of paid-up additional insurance purchased with the dividend when the PUA option is elected. Represents incremental death benefit added to the base policy.
reference: NAIC Model Policy Form Standards - Paid-Up Additions Rider

description: The date on which a previously declared or paid dividend was reversed. Used for audit trail and financial reconciliation.

The reason for reversing a previously declared or paid dividend. POLICY_LAPSE = policy lapsed before dividend paid; POLICY_RESCISSION = policy rescinded; DATA_CORRECTION = data error correction; CALCULATION_ERROR = actuarial calculation error; OPTION_CHANGE = policyholder changed option election.
The version identifier of the dividend scale used to calculate this dividend. Links to the actuarial dividend scale table and supports audit trail for rate changes.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Dividend Illustrations

description: The identifier of the source policy administration system from which this dividend record originated. Supports multi-system environments and data lineage tracking.
The unique identifier of this dividend record in the source policy administration system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.
Indicates whether this is a special or extraordinary dividend declared outside the normal annual dividend cycle. True for special dividends; false for regular annual dividends.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Dividend Illustrations

description: The calendar year in which the dividend is reportable for tax purposes. May differ from dividend year due to timing of payment or option application.
reference: IRS Publication 525 - Taxable and Nontaxable Income

description: The portion of the dividend that is taxable to the policyholder. Generally zero for participating whole life unless dividends exceed premiums paid, but tracked for IRS reporting compliance.
reference: IRC Section 72 - Annuities; Certain Proceeds of Endowment and Life Insurance Contracts

description: Indicates whether this is a terminal dividend paid upon policy maturity, death claim, or surrender. True for terminal dividends; false for annual dividends.
The date and time when this dividend record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Management Assessment of Internal Controls

description: The policy year or calendar year for which the dividend was declared. Represents the experience period underlying the dividend calculation.
Unique identifier for the policy loan record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Policy loans require executed loan agreement documents and disclosure forms as legal instruments. Regulators and auditors require direct traceability from the loan record to its governing document. Di
Reference to the underlying life insurance policy against which this loan is issued.
reference: ACORD Life and Annuity Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Policy loan disbursements, interest accruals, and repayments generate journal entries for interest income recognition and loan balance reconciliation. Finance teams must trace each loan transaction to

Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Maximum policy loan amount is determined by the CSV, which is derived from the investment valuation at loan origination. Recording the specific valuation snapshot used supports loan origination audit

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable product loans are collateralized by separate account portfolio value. Required for loan-to-CSV ratio monitoring, automatic premium loan (APL) trigger detection, collateral adequacy assessment
Reference to the agent or service representative responsible for administering this loan and handling borrower inquiries.

Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A policy loan origination is initiated through a service request (request_type='LOAN_REQUEST'). Linking loan to the originating service_request provides traceability from the policyholder's loan appli
Total interest accrued on the loan that has not been paid or capitalized into principal.
Indicates whether this loan was automatically triggered due to insufficient funds to pay a premium. True if the loan was initiated by the APL provision; False if borrower-initiated.
Date the loan was fully closed (either through full repayment, offset at death, offset at surrender, or foreclosure). Null if loan is still active.

Reason the loan was closed. Full_repayment indicates borrower paid off loan; offset_death_claim indicates loan was deducted from death benefit; offset_surrender indicates loan was deducted from surrender proceeds; policy_lapse indicates policy lapsed due to loan exceeding CSV; loan_forgiveness indicates loan was forgiven (rare, typically taxable event).
Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the policy at the time of loan origination, representing the maximum collateral available to secure the loan.
Timestamp when this policy loan record was first created in the system.
Date on which loan proceeds were disbursed to the policyholder or applied to premium/loan interest.

Method by which loan proceeds were disbursed. Check indicates mailed check; EFT indicates electronic funds transfer to bank account; wire indicates wire transfer; applied_to_premium indicates loan was used to pay premium (APL); applied_to_loan indicates loan was used to pay interest on existing loan.
Date on which accrued interest was capitalized (added to principal balance), typically occurring annually on the policy anniversary if interest remains unpaid.
Frequency at which interest is calculated and added to the accrued interest balance.
Annual interest rate applied to the policy loan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 for 5%). Rate may be fixed at origination or variable based on an index.

Classification of how the loan interest rate is determined. Fixed rates remain constant; variable rates adjust periodically based on an index such as Moody's Corporate Bond Yield Average, US Treasury rates, or LIBOR; guaranteed_max indicates a rate with a contractual ceiling.
Most recent date on which interest was calculated and posted to the accrued interest balance.
Amount of the most recent repayment transaction. Null if no repayments have been made.
Date of the most recent repayment transaction applied to this loan. Null if no repayments have been made.
Business identifier for the policy loan, typically displayed on customer statements and correspondence.

Current lifecycle status of the policy loan. Active indicates outstanding balance; repaid indicates full repayment; foreclosed indicates policy lapsed due to loan exceeding CSV; offset_at_death indicates loan was deducted from death benefit at claim; offset_at_surrender indicates loan was deducted from surrender proceeds.

Classification of the policy loan. Standard loans are borrower-initiated; Automatic Premium Loan (APL) loans are triggered when premium payment fails and policy has sufficient CSV; systematic loans are recurring scheduled loans; emergency loans are expedited loans for urgent needs.
Maximum amount that can be borrowed against the policy, typically a percentage of CSV (e.g., 90% of CSV) as defined in the policy contract.

Indicates whether this loan was taken on a policy classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC), which has different tax treatment for loans and withdrawals. True if policy is a MEC; False otherwise.
Date of the next scheduled loan repayment, if a repayment schedule is in place. Null if no repayment schedule exists or loan is fully repaid.
Total loan balance (principal plus accrued interest) that was deducted from the death benefit when a claim was paid. Null if policy has not resulted in a death claim.
Total loan balance (principal plus accrued interest) that was deducted from the cash surrender value when the policy was surrendered. Null if policy has not been surrendered.
Principal amount of the loan at origination, before any interest accrual or repayments.
Date the policy loan was issued and funds were disbursed to the policyholder or applied to premium.
Current outstanding principal balance of the loan after partial repayments, excluding accrued interest.

Type of repayment schedule established for the loan. None indicates no scheduled repayments (loan repaid at death or surrender); monthly/quarterly/annual indicate regular scheduled payments; custom indicates a non-standard repayment plan.
Amount of each scheduled repayment installment, if a repayment schedule is in place. Null if no repayment schedule exists.
Name of the source policy administration system from which this loan record originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration).
Tax year in which a taxable event related to this loan must be reported to the IRS (e.g., when loan causes policy to become a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) or when loan is forgiven at lapse).
Amount subject to income tax reporting related to this loan (e.g., gain recognized on MEC loan distribution or loan forgiveness at lapse). Null if no taxable event has occurred.
Indicates whether this loan has triggered a taxable event requiring IRS reporting (e.g., loan on a MEC policy, loan forgiveness at lapse). True if taxable event occurred; False otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Loan To Cash Surrender Value (CSV) Ratio
description: Ratio of total outstanding loan balance to current policy CSV, expressed as a decimal. Used to monitor loan collateralization and lapse risk. A ratio approaching 1.0 indicates high lapse risk.
Combined outstanding principal and accrued interest balance. This is the amount that would be deducted from death benefit or surrender value.
Cumulative amount of all repayments (principal and interest) made on this loan since origination.
Timestamp when this policy loan record was last modified in the system.
Unique identifier for the policy reinstatement record. Primary key for the policy reinstatement entity.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Reinstatement requiring evidence of insurability generates a new underwriting application. The reinstatement record must reference this application to track the underwriting submission, decision, and

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Reinstatement underwriting uses actuarial assumption sets to determine evidence of insurability thresholds, back-premium interest rates, and contestability period restart rules. Actuarial and complian

Foreign key linking to reinsurance.reinsurance_cession. Business justification: Policy reinstatement requires reinstating the associated reinsurance cession, which may have lapsed or been terminated. Reinsurance operations must coordinate cession reinstatement with the reinsurer.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinstatement applications require health statements, evidence of insurability forms, and back-premium receipts as regulatory documents. Insurers must retain these for contestability period compliance
Reference to the policy being reinstated. Links to the master policy record in the policy administration system.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Reinstatements collect back premiums and fees, generating journal entries for premium income and fee revenue recognition. Finance reconciliation of reinstatement collections to GL postings is required

Foreign key linking to billing.lapse_event. Business justification: Reinstatement applications must reference the specific lapse event to verify lapse reason, duration, outstanding amounts, and reinstatement eligibility window. Critical for underwriting decision workf
Reference to the agent who assisted with or submitted the reinstatement application. Supports commission tracking and agent performance metrics.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.underwriting_risk_assessment. Business justification: Reinstatement underwriting produces a formal risk assessment. The reinstatement record carries denormalized `underwriting_decision` and `underwriting_decision_date` fields that belong on the risk asse

Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A policy reinstatement application is initiated through a service request (request_type='REINSTATEMENT'). Linking policy_reinstatement to the originating service_request provides full traceability fro

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Reinstatement eligibility windows, evidence of insurability requirements, and back-premium interest rules are state-regulated. Compliance reporting on reinstatement practices during market conduct exa
Date the adverse action notice was sent to the applicant following a denial or rated decision. Required for regulatory compliance.
Total amount collected from the policyholder toward reinstatement. Tracks partial payments and full settlement.
Channel through which the reinstatement application was submitted. Supports channel performance analysis and customer experience tracking.
Date the policyholder submitted the reinstatement application. Key date for calculating time-sensitive requirements and deadlines.
Indicates whether an Attending Physician Statement is required as part of the reinstatement underwriting. Typically required for longer lapse periods or health-related concerns.
Total amount of unpaid premiums owed from the lapse date to reinstatement date. Must be paid for reinstatement approval.
Interest charged on unpaid back premiums calculated per state regulation and company policy. Added to total reinstatement cost.
Free-form notes and comments from underwriters, processors, or customer service regarding the reinstatement case. Supports case management and audit trail.
Indicates whether the two-year contestable period restarts from the reinstatement effective date. Impacts claims adjudication and fraud investigation rights.
Timestamp when the reinstatement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: Cash Surrender Value (CSV) at Lapse Amount
description: Cash surrender value of the policy at the time of lapse. May be applied toward back premiums or returned to policyholder based on policy provisions.
Code indicating the primary reason for denial of the reinstatement application. Required for regulatory reporting and adverse action notices.
Detailed explanation of why the reinstatement application was denied. Supports adverse action letter generation and appeals process.
Date the policy coverage is restored if reinstatement is approved. May be retroactive to lapse date or prospective based on company policy and state regulation.
Indicates whether the applicant must provide evidence of continued insurability for reinstatement. Typically required if lapse exceeded a threshold period.
Administrative fee charged for processing the reinstatement application. May vary by policy type and jurisdiction.
Date the applicant signed and attested to the accuracy of the health statement. Legal date for representation and warranty purposes.
Date the completed health statement was received from the applicant. Tracks compliance with evidence of insurability requirements.
Indicates whether the applicant must complete a health statement questionnaire. Standard requirement for reinstatements beyond the grace period.
Date the policy originally lapsed due to non-payment of premium or other reason. Determines reinstatement eligibility window and requirements.
Code indicating the reason the policy lapsed. Influences reinstatement requirements and underwriting criteria.

business_glossary_term: Policy Loan Balance at Lapse Amount
description: Outstanding policy loan balance at the time of lapse. Must be repaid or reinstated along with the policy.
Indicates whether the application was initially submitted incomplete or with errors requiring rework. Tracks application quality and processing efficiency.

business_glossary_term: Not In Good Order (NIGO) Reason Code
description: Code indicating the specific deficiency that caused the application to be marked as not in good order. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.
Method used by the policyholder to pay reinstatement amounts. Influences processing time and reconciliation procedures.
Date the reinstatement payment was received by the company. Key date for determining when reinstatement can be processed.
Number of calendar days from application receipt to final decision. Key performance indicator for operational efficiency and customer experience.
Current status of the reinstatement application in the workflow. Tracks progression from initial application through final decision.
Classification of the reinstatement based on requirements and conditions. Automatic requires only payment; conditional requires underwriting; unconditional is within grace period.
Indicates whether the suicide exclusion period restarts from the reinstatement effective date. Impacts death benefit eligibility for suicide claims.
Total amount required for reinstatement including back premiums, interest, and fees. Must be collected before policy is reinstated.
Timestamp when the reinstatement record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for compliance and operational monitoring.
Last date the policy is eligible for reinstatement per policy terms and state regulation. Typically 3-5 years from lapse date.

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) Calculation Date
