The maximum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this retention limit record applies. Combined with age_band_min to define the age band. Null indicates no upper age restriction for this limit record.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The minimum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this retention limit record applies. Combined with age_band_max to define the age band. Retention and automatic binding limits typically vary by age due to increasing mortality risk at older ages.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The date on which this retention limit record was formally approved by the authorized actuarial or reinsurance management authority. Required for SOX compliance and internal governance documentation of limit changes.
reference: SOX Compliance; NAIC Model Regulation; ORSA Guidance Manual

description: Name or identifier of the individual (typically Chief Actuary, VP Reinsurance, or CRO) who authorized this retention limit record. Supports governance, audit trail, and SOX internal control requirements.
reference: SOX Compliance; NAIC Model Regulation; ERM Framework

description: Indicates whether this limit record governs automatic cessions (risks ceded within treaty terms without individual submission), facultative cessions (risks requiring individual reinsurer approval), or facultative-obligatory arrangements. Automatic binding limits apply only to automatic cession type.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The date and time when this retention limit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Compliance; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the reinsurer qualifies for credit for reinsurance under applicable state insurance law, allowing the ceding company to take a reserve credit on its statutory balance sheet. Determined by reinsurer licensure status and collateral arrangements.
reference: NAIC Credit for Reinsurance Model Law; State Departments of Insurance; SAP Accounting Principles

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the limit amount is denominated (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency treaty arrangements and international reinsurance partners.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The date from which this retention or binding limit record becomes operative and governs cession routing decisions. Aligns with treaty effective dates and policy year boundaries.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; ACORD Data Standards

description: The date on which this retention or binding limit record ceases to be operative. Null indicates an open-ended limit with no scheduled expiration. Used in conjunction with effective_date to determine the applicable limit for a given valuation or cession date.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module

description: The maximum flat extra premium per thousand of face amount (expressed in dollars per thousand) eligible for automatic cession under this limit. Risks with flat extras exceeding this threshold require facultative submission. Null indicates no flat extra restriction.
reference: Underwriting Workbench — RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum; NAIC Underwriting Standards

description: Gender classification band to which this retention limit applies. Some treaty structures differentiate retention limits by gender due to mortality table differences. Unisex indicates the limit applies to all genders. Null indicates no gender restriction.
reference: Underwriting Workbench — RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The absolute maximum face amount threshold above which facultative reinsurance submission is required, regardless of whether the risk falls within the automatic binding limit. Risks exceeding the jumbo limit cannot be automatically ceded and must be individually underwritten by the reinsurer. Null if this record is not of type jumbo_limit.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The principal monetary threshold value for this limit record, expressed in the applicable currency. For retention_per_life and retention_per_policy, this is the maximum net face amount retained by the company. For automatic_binding_limit, this is the maximum face amount that can be auto-ceded. For jumbo_limit, this is the threshold above which facultative submission is mandatory. Drives the cession routing decision engine.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance — Treaty Management Module

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this retention or binding limit configuration within the reinsurance management system (e.g., RET-WL-NS-M-01). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and bordereaux reporting.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance — Treaty Management Module; ACORD Data Standards

description: Free-text description providing additional context about this retention limit record, including any special conditions, exceptions, or treaty-specific notes that are not captured in structured fields. Used by reinsurance analysts and actuaries for reference.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance

description: Current lifecycle state of the retention limit record. Active records drive live cession routing decisions. Superseded records have been replaced by a newer version. Expired records are past their effective period. Pending records are approved but not yet in effect.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Classifies the nature of the limit record: retention_per_life (maximum net amount retained across all policies on a single life), retention_per_policy (maximum net amount retained on a single policy), automatic_binding_limit (maximum face amount that can be automatically ceded without facultative submission), or jumbo_limit (threshold above which facultative reinsurance submission is required regardless of treaty).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance

description: Specifies the basis on which the retention limit amount is measured: face_amount (total death benefit face), NAR (Net Amount at Risk — death benefit minus reserve), account_value (for UL/IUL/FIA products), or death_benefit (total contractual death benefit including riders). Drives the NAR-based cession calculation methodology.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Data Standards

description: The maximum total net amount the company will retain across all policies insuring the same life (insured). When total in-force exposure on a single life exceeds this threshold, the excess must be ceded. Critical for managing concentration risk on high-value lives.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The maximum policy year (inclusive) for which this retention limit applies. Null indicates the limit applies through the end of the policy term. Used in conjunction with policy_year_min to define the policy year band.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The minimum policy year (inclusive) for which this retention limit applies. Some treaties define different retention schedules for early policy years versus later years, particularly for YRT (Yearly Renewable Term) treaties where NAR changes over time.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Version identifier of the actuarial rate table used to derive the retention limit amounts (e.g., 2017CSO, 2001CSO, company-specific mortality table). Ensures traceability between limit values and the underlying actuarial assumptions used in their derivation.
reference: Actuarial Valuation System — AXIS; Prophet; NAIC Model Regulation; PBR Compliance

description: Indicates whether cessions made under this retention limit qualify for RBC (Risk-Based Capital) credit under NAIC RBC formula requirements. RBC-eligible cessions reduce the company's required capital, making this flag critical for capital optimization and ORSA reporting.
reference: NAIC RBC Model Act; NAIC Model Regulation; ORSA Guidance Manual

description: Tobacco use classification band to which this retention limit applies. Smoker and non-smoker populations carry materially different mortality rates, often resulting in distinct retention schedules. Null indicates the limit applies regardless of smoker status.
reference: Underwriting Workbench — RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum; NAIC Underwriting Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this retention limit record originated (e.g., SWISS_RE_IRIS, RGA_AURA, INTERNAL_TREATY_MGR). Supports data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance; ACORD Data Standards

description: The native identifier or reference number assigned to this retention limit record in the originating operational system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS internal limit ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the silver layer back to the source system.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance; ACORD Data Standards

description: The maximum substandard table rating (expressed as a letter A–P or numeric percentage, e.g., Table D or 150%) eligible for automatic cession under this limit. Risks with table ratings exceeding this threshold require facultative submission regardless of face amount.
reference: Underwriting Workbench — RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum; NAIC Underwriting Standards

description: The date and time when this retention limit record was last modified. Tracks changes to limit amounts, effective dates, or status. Supports audit trail and change management requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Compliance; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Sequential version number of this retention limit record. Incremented each time the limit is revised. Enables tracking of limit changes over time and supports point-in-time queries for historical cession routing validation and audit purposes.
reference: Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Management Module; SOX Compliance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the reinsurance treaty record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the treaty master entity.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Treaty repricing, experience refund provisions, and RBC credit eligibility are governed by the actuarial assumption set in force. Actuaries reference assumption_set when validating treaty terms and se

Foreign key linking to actuarial.experience_study. Business justification: Treaty renegotiation and recapture eligibility reviews are triggered by experience study findings. Regulators and auditors require traceability from treaty rate changes to the experience study that ju

Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: Under IFRS17, each reinsurance treaty held maps to an IFRS17 reinsurance held contract group for initial recognition and subsequent measurement. This link is required for IFRS17 reinsurance held accou

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinsurance treaties are executed legal contracts requiring formal document management (treaty text, amendments, endorsements). Linking the treaty record to its executed document supports treaty lifec

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Treaties are executed by specific legal entities for regulatory reporting, RBC credit allocation, and statutory filing. Reinsurance and finance teams track treaty ownership by legal entity for Schedul

Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Treaties specify mortality tables for YRT rate calculations, risk classification, and automatic binding limit determination. Critical for treaty pricing, facultative underwriting, and reinsurance prem

Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Treaties specify covered product lines. Essential for treaty administration, cession eligibility determination, and bordereaux reporting by product. Product_lines_covered is text field; structured FK

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: ModCo and funds-withheld treaties require designated investment portfolios for reserve backing and collateral. Treaty setup process mandates portfolio assignment for ALM matching and regulatory credit

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Treaties must comply with state reinsurance regulations governing retention limits, collateral requirements, and credit for reinsurance eligibility. Regulatory obligations drive treaty structure and f
Foreign key linking to the reinsurer counterparty who is party to this treaty arrangement

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Reinsurance treaties are governed by state-specific credit-for-reinsurance laws (e.g., NAIC Credit for Reinsurance Model Law). Compliance officers must verify treaty terms comply with the governing st

Percentage allowance (expense reimbursement) paid by the reinsurer to the ceding company to offset acquisition and maintenance costs. Applicable primarily to coinsurance and modified coinsurance treaties. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%).
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Comma-separated list of product risk lines that this reinsurer is authorized to accept for this product plan (e.g., mortality, morbidity, longevity, investment risk). Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.

Maximum face amount up to which risks are automatically ceded without individual reinsurer approval. Risks exceeding this limit require facultative placement. Key parameter in the automatic cession workflow.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; RGA AURA Reinsurance Module

description: Frequency at which bordereaux (detailed cession and premium reports) are submitted to the reinsurer under this treaty. Governs the reporting schedule in the Reinsurance Management System.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Bordereaux Module

description: Maximum aggregate face amount or account value (in USD) that this reinsurer is willing to accept in cessions for this specific product plan. This is product-specific capacity, distinct from the reinsurer-level credit_exposure_limit. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.

Indicates whether risk is ceded automatically under treaty terms without individual underwriting review (automatic), on a case-by-case basis requiring reinsurer approval (facultative), or a hybrid of both (automatic-facultative). Drives cession processing workflow in the Reinsurance Management System.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; RGA AURA Reinsurance Module

description: Percentage of risk ceded to the reinsurer under quota share or coinsurance arrangements. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Not applicable for YRT or excess-of-loss treaties where cession is NAR-based.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Type of collateral arrangement securing the reinsurer's obligations under this treaty. Required for unauthorized reinsurers to obtain credit for reinsurance. Options include letter of credit, trust account, funds withheld, or none for authorized reinsurers.
reference: NAIC Credit for Reinsurance Model Law; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Standards

description: Commission percentage paid by the reinsurer to Life Insurance on ceded premiums for this product, compensating for acquisition costs and administrative expenses.
Timestamp when the treaty record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and LDTI/IFRS 17 compliance requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302; FASB ASC 944; IFRS 17; ACORD Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which treaty premiums, allowances, claims recoverables, and settlements are denominated.
reference: ISO 4217; NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the reinsurance treaty becomes binding and cessions may commence. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category. Used in cession eligibility checks and bordereaux reporting.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Date on which the reinsurance treaty was formally signed and executed by both parties. May differ from the effective date if the treaty was backdated or signed after the effective date.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; SOX Section 302; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether the treaty includes an experience refund provision, whereby the reinsurer returns a portion of profits to the ceding company if actual claims experience is favorable relative to treaty pricing assumptions.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether this treaty arrangement includes an experience refund provision where favorable claims experience results in profit sharing with Life Insurance.
The date on which the reinsurance treaty expires and no new cessions may be made. Nullable for evergreen/open-ended treaties. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether this treaty operates on a funds-withheld basis, where the ceding company retains the assets backing the ceded reserves rather than transferring them to the reinsurer. Relevant for modified coinsurance and funds-withheld coinsurance structures.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Maximum insured issue age (in years) eligible for cession under this treaty. Policies with insured issue ages above this threshold are not eligible for automatic cession.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Minimum insured issue age (in years) eligible for cession under this treaty. Policies with insured issue ages below this threshold are not eligible for automatic cession.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Date on which the capacity limit and terms for this reinsurer-product relationship were last reviewed and confirmed or updated.
Maximum face amount or NAR that can be ceded to the reinsurer per life or per policy under this treaty. Caps the reinsurer's exposure on any single risk.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Free-text notes capturing special terms, conditions, or operational considerations specific to this reinsurer-product treaty relationship.

The preferred reinsurance treaty basis for this reinsurer-product combination (yrt = Yearly Renewable Term, coinsurance, mod_co = Modified Coinsurance, funds_withheld). May differ from the reinsurer's general preferred_treaty_basis depending on product characteristics. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.
Frequency at which reinsurance premiums are remitted to the reinsurer under this treaty. Drives the billing and settlement schedule in the Reinsurance Management System.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Module

description: Basis on which reinsurance premiums are calculated. NAR (Net Amount at Risk) is standard for YRT; face_amount for coinsurance; account_value for modified coinsurance; flat_extra for substandard risks.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Comma-delimited list or description of insurance product lines covered under this treaty (e.g., 'Term Life, Whole Life, UL'). Defines the scope of eligible policies for cession.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Assessment of this reinsurer's pricing competitiveness for this specific product plan relative to market alternatives. Used in reinsurer selection and capacity allocation decisions. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.

Indicates whether this treaty qualifies for RBC credit under NAIC Risk-Based Capital requirements, meaning the reinsurer meets credit-for-reinsurance standards (e.g., authorized reinsurer or collateral posted). Critical for statutory capital optimization.
reference: NAIC RBC Framework; NAIC Credit for Reinsurance Model Law; State Departments of Insurance

description: Number of years after treaty inception after which the ceding company is eligible to recapture ceded risks (i.e., bring them back in-house). A key treaty term governing the ceding company's long-term risk management flexibility.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Number of days advance written notice required by the ceding company to the reinsurer prior to exercising recapture rights under this treaty.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether Life Insurance has the right to recapture (take back) ceded business from this reinsurer for this product, and under what conditions.

Current lifecycle state of the reinsurance treaty. Active treaties govern ongoing cessions; pending treaties are awaiting execution; terminated treaties are closed to new cessions; suspended treaties are temporarily halted.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Name of the primary relationship contact at the reinsurer for this treaty. Used for treaty administration, claims recoverable follow-up, and bordereaux dispute resolution.
Date on which this reinsurer's authorization to accept new cessions for this product plan was terminated or expired. Null if relationship is currently active.
Date on which this reinsurer was first authorized to accept cessions for this product plan under treaty arrangements. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.

Maximum face amount or Net Amount at Risk (NAR) that the ceding company retains per life before ceding the excess to the reinsurer under this treaty. A core parameter for NAR-based cession calculations and RBC optimization.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; NAIC RBC Framework; ACORD Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the retention limit amount. Supports multi-currency treaty arrangements.
reference: ISO 4217; NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Percentage of risk that Life Insurance retains on policies ceded under this treaty arrangement (e.g., 10.00 means Life Insurance retains 10% and cedes 90% to the reinsurer).
Indicates whether this treaty is a retrocession arrangement, meaning the company is acting as a reinsurer ceding risk to a retrocessionaire, rather than as a primary ceding company.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Defines which underwriting risk classifications are eligible for cession under this treaty. 'Standard' covers only standard-rated lives; 'substandard' covers rated/impaired lives; 'all' covers both.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; Swiss Re Magnum Underwriting Module

description: Number of calendar days after the reporting period end by which premium settlements and claim recoverables must be remitted between the ceding company and the reinsurer.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Native treaty identifier as recorded in the operational Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA Reinsurance). Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the system of record.
reference: ACORD Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Module; RGA AURA Reinsurance Module

description: Actual date on which the treaty was terminated for new cessions, if applicable. Distinct from expiration_date (contractual end) — this captures the operational termination event date.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Number of days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the treaty for new cessions. Does not affect in-force ceded risks, which typically run off under treaty terms.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Reason code for treaty termination. Used for regulatory reporting, actuarial experience studies, and reinsurer relationship management.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Descriptive business name of the reinsurance treaty (e.g., 'RGA YRT Life 2020 Agreement'). Used for human-readable identification in reporting and reinsurer communications.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the reinsurance treaty, as referenced in bordereaux reporting, cession schedules, and reinsurer correspondence. Sourced from the Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance).
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Module

description: Current lifecycle status of this reinsurer-product treaty relationship. Active treaties are available for new cessions.

Classification of the reinsurance arrangement structure. YRT (Yearly Renewable Term) is NAR-based; coinsurance shares proportional risk and reserve; modified coinsurance retains reserves with the ceding company; excess of loss provides catastrophic coverage above a retention limit; quota share cedes a fixed percentage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: YRT|coinsurance|modified_coinsurance|excess_of_loss|quota_share|stop_loss|funds_withheld — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; FASB ASC 944-40; ACORD Standards

description: Timestamp when the treaty record was last modified in the system of record. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; FASB ASC 944; IFRS 17; ACORD Standards

description: Version or amendment number of the treaty document (e.g., '1.0', '2.3', 'Amendment 4'). Tracks the evolution of treaty terms through amendments and endorsements over the treaty lifecycle.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Law; ACORD Standards; SOX Section 302

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the reinsurance treaty rate and retention schedule record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: YRT reinsurance rates in treaty schedules are benchmarked against the product's COI rate schedule. Reinsurance pricing actuaries reference the COI rate schedule to set yrt_rate_per_thousand. This link

Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: YRT rates per thousand in treaty_schedule are derived directly from a specific mortality table. Schedule validation, rate adequacy reviews, and reserve calculations require this link. mortality_table_

Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Schedules define reinsurance rates and terms per product plan. Critical for automatic binding limit checks, YRT rate lookups, cession processing, and treaty schedule maintenance. Plan_code is denormal

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Treaty schedules define reinsurance rates for specific plan versions — when a plan version changes (new rates, benefit structure), a new treaty schedule is negotiated. Reinsurance pricing and treaty a

Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Treaty schedules for coinsurance and modified coinsurance reference the product's premium rate table to define the reinsurance premium basis. Rate reconciliation between ceded premiums and product rat

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Treaty schedules reference pricing bases to ensure reinsurance rates align with product pricing, rate guarantees, and profitability targets. Critical for treaty negotiation and rate setting.

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Treaty schedules define rates and retention structures that vary by state due to state-specific reinsurance regulations (e.g., minimum retention requirements, credit for reinsurance rules differ by st
Reference to the parent reinsurance treaty to which this schedule belongs. A single treaty may have multiple schedules covering different plan codes or underwriting classes.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Reinsurance rates in treaty schedules are structured by underwriting class (preferred, standard, substandard). YRT and coinsurance rates differ materially by class. Reinsurance actuaries and treaty ad

Percentage of reinsurance premium returned to the ceding company as an expense allowance under coinsurance arrangements, representing a share of acquisition costs (DAC) and ongoing maintenance expenses.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; FASB ASC 944 (DAC); NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether YRT rates in this schedule are applied on an attained age basis (True) or issue age basis (False). Attained age basis recalculates the rate each policy anniversary based on the insured's current age.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Maximum face amount or NAR up to which the reinsurer is automatically bound to accept cessions without individual underwriting review. Risks exceeding this limit require facultative placement.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; RGA AURA Reinsurance Module

description: Indicates whether cessions under this schedule are automatic (bound by treaty terms without individual underwriting), facultative (individually negotiated per risk), or facultative-obligatory (reinsurer must accept if ceding company offers).
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: The percentage of the policy's face amount or account value ceded to the reinsurer under a coinsurance or modified coinsurance arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Null for YRT schedule types.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this treaty schedule record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and NAIC examination standards.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the YRT rates, flat extra premiums, and retention limits are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD). Required for multi-currency treaty arrangements with international reinsurers.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Date on which this treaty schedule becomes binding and applicable for NAR-based cession calculations and premium determination. Aligns with treaty effective date or amendment effective date.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this schedule is subject to an experience refund (profit-sharing) arrangement where the reinsurer returns a portion of profits to the ceding company if actual mortality experience is favorable relative to expected.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Maximum percentage of reinsurance profit that may be returned to the ceding company under the experience refund arrangement. Null if experience_refund_eligible is False.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Date on which this treaty schedule ceases to be applicable. Null for open-ended schedules. When populated, a superseding schedule should exist with an effective date equal to or following this date.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Number of policy years for which the flat extra premium applies. After this duration, the flat extra expires and only the standard YRT rate applies. Null if the flat extra is permanent.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA)

description: Additional reinsurance premium per $1,000 of NAR charged for substandard risks with specific hazards (e.g., aviation, occupation) that cannot be fully captured by table ratings. Applied in addition to the standard YRT rate.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Gender classification for which this schedule's rates apply. Unisex rates are used in jurisdictions prohibiting gender-based pricing; composite rates blend male and female experience.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance Illustrations; State Department of Insurance Gender Rating Rules

description: Maximum insured issue age (in whole years) for which this schedule's rates and terms apply. Defines the upper bound of the age band for YRT rate lookup and cession eligibility.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance Illustrations

description: Minimum insured issue age (in whole years) for which this schedule's rates and terms apply. Defines the lower bound of the age band for YRT rate lookup and cession eligibility.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance Illustrations

description: Actuarial mortality improvement projection scale applied to the base mortality table for this schedule (e.g., Scale AA, Scale G2, Scale MP-2021). Used in PBR (Principle-Based Reserving) and cash flow projection models.
reference: NAIC Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) Model Regulation; Society of Actuaries Mortality Improvement Scales; Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes; AXIS)

description: Maximum policy year (duration) for which this schedule's rates apply. Null if rates apply for the full remaining policy term beyond the minimum duration.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Minimum policy year (duration) for which this schedule's rates apply. Enables duration-graded rate schedules where YRT rates vary by policy year in addition to attained age.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Frequency basis on which the YRT rate per thousand is expressed. Annual rates are most common; modal factors may be applied for other frequencies in premium billing calculations.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; Billing and Premium Collection System (Sapiens BillingPro)

description: Number of days advance written notice the reinsurer must provide to the ceding company before implementing a rate change under this schedule. Governs treaty renegotiation timelines.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Date through which the reinsurer guarantees the YRT rates or coinsurance terms in this schedule will not be changed. After this date, the reinsurer may renegotiate rates subject to treaty notice provisions.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the ceding company has the right to recapture ceded risks under this schedule after a specified recapture waiting period. Recapture rights are a key treaty negotiation term affecting long-term reinsurance strategy.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards

description: Minimum number of years from the original cession date before the ceding company may exercise recapture rights for risks ceded under this schedule. Null if recapture is not permitted.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Module

description: The basis on which reinsurance premiums and cessions are calculated. NAR (Net Amount at Risk) is standard for YRT; face amount or account value may apply for coinsurance arrangements.
reference: ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation

description: Maximum dollar amount of net amount at risk or face amount that the ceding company retains per life before ceding the excess to the reinsurer under this schedule. Fundamental to automatic cession eligibility determination.
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Reinsurance Data Standards; Swiss Re IRIS Treaty Module

description: Current lifecycle state of the treaty schedule. Active schedules are used for current cession calculations; superseded schedules have been replaced by a newer version; terminated schedules are no longer in force.

description: Classification of the reinsurance arrangement type governing this schedule. Maximum age (in years) for which this rule applies. Defines the upper age boundary for rule applicability. Null if rule applies to all ages above the minimum.

Standard code identifying a specific medical impairment or health condition (e.g., DIAB2 for Type 2 Diabetes, CAD for Coronary Artery Disease). Used for medical underwriting and impairment rating. Null for non-medical rules.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this rule record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.

description: Describes how this rule affects the Net Amount at Risk (NAR) calculation. NAR is the death benefit minus the policy cash value or account value. Rules that increase mortality risk typically increase NAR exposure.
reference: NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: Free-form text field for additional context, special instructions, or clarifications related to the rule. Used by underwriters and rule administrators for operational guidance.

description: Indicates how this rule affects reinsurance placement decisions: automatic (ceded under automatic treaty), facultative (requires facultative reinsurance submission), retained (risk retained by direct writer), or none (no reinsurance impact).

description: Classification of the rule type. Medical rules evaluate health conditions and impairments; financial rules assess income and net worth; product eligibility rules determine product suitability; STOLI detection rules identify stranger-originated life insurance; AML/KYC rules support anti-money laundering compliance; age band rules apply age-specific underwriting criteria.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rule within the automated rules engine or underwriting manual. Used for system integration and rule invocation.
Human-readable name of the underwriting rule or medical impairment guideline. Provides clear identification for underwriters and business users.

Origin of the rule: company underwriting manual (proprietary guidelines), reinsurer guide (reinsurer-provided rating manual), regulatory requirement (mandated by state or federal regulation), or industry standard (NAIC, ACORD, MIB best practices).

Current lifecycle status of the rule. Active rules are in production use; inactive rules are disabled; pending approval rules await governance review; retired rules are archived; suspended rules are temporarily disabled pending review.

Indicates whether the rule is executed automatically by the rules engine, serves as a manual guideline for underwriter reference, defines an impairment rating action, triggers an automatic decline, or triggers a referral to senior underwriter or medical director.
Indicates whether the rule applies to smokers, non-smokers, or all applicants. Supports tobacco-use-specific underwriting criteria and rating adjustments.
Name of the operational system from which this rule was sourced (e.g., RGAX AURA, Swiss Re Magnum, proprietary underwriting workbench). Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.

Default underwriting action prescribed by the rule: standard (no extra premium), table rating (mortality multiplier), flat extra (per-thousand charge), decline (application rejected), or refer (escalate to senior underwriter or medical director).

description: Maximum table rating (A through P) for the impairment or risk factor. Defines the upper bound of mortality surcharge. Null if rule does not use table ratings.
reference: NAIC Table Rating Standards, Reinsurer Rating Manuals

description: Minimum table rating (A through P) for the impairment or risk factor. Table ratings represent mortality multipliers (e.g., Table B = 150% of standard mortality). Null if rule does not use table ratings.
reference: NAIC Table Rating Standards, Reinsurer Rating Manuals

description: Specifies the underwriting method(s) to which this rule applies: full underwriting (medical exams, APS), simplified issue (health questions only), guaranteed issue (no health questions), accelerated underwriting (automated data sources), or all methods.
Sequential version number of the rule. Incremented with each update to support change tracking and historical analysis. Version 1 is the initial rule definition.

business_glossary_term: Rule Execution Result - Rule Execution Result Id
description: Primary key for the rule_execution_result association
Foreign key linking to the specific underwriting rule that was evaluated

business_glossary_term: Rule Execution Result - Rules Engine Output Id
description: Foreign key linking to the rules engine evaluation in which this rule was fired

Comma-separated list of reason codes for rules that triggered an automated decline recommendation. Used for adverse action notices and regulatory compliance. [Moved from rules_engine_output: This comma-separated string on rules_engine_output is a denormalized encoding of individual rule FAIL outcomes. Each decline reason code corresponds to a specific rule that fired with a FAIL result. The correct model is one rule_execution_result record per fired rule with result=FAIL, making this field redundant and replaceable by querying the association table.]

The ordinal position in which this rule was evaluated within the rules engine execution. Critical for audit and debugging purposes, as rule execution order can affect downstream rule conditions in chained rule logic.

Indicates whether this rule actually triggered (fired) during the evaluation. A rule may be in scope for an evaluation but not fire if its preconditions were not met. Distinguishes between rules that were evaluated and rules that actively influenced the decision.
Indicates whether a human underwriter overrode the automated result of this specific rule. Supports underwriter override tracking, exception reporting, and model performance monitoring.

Free-text justification provided by the underwriter when overriding this rule's automated result. Populated only when override_flag is TRUE. Supports audit trail, compliance review, and underwriter decision quality monitoring.

Comma-separated list of reason codes for rules that triggered a refer outcome, requiring human underwriter review. Each code represents a specific underwriting concern or exception. [Moved from rules_engine_output: This comma-separated string on rules_engine_output is a denormalized encoding of individual rule REFER outcomes. Each refer reason code corresponds to a specific rule that fired with a REFER result. The correct model is one rule_execution_result record per fired rule with result=REFER, making this field redundant and replaceable by querying the association table.]

The outcome of evaluating this specific rule within this evaluation. PASS indicates the applicant met the rule criteria, FAIL indicates a decline trigger, REFER indicates the rule routed the case to a human underwriter, NOT_APPLICABLE indicates the rule was loaded but conditions for firing were not met.

Numeric score or weight contributed by this rule to the overall evaluation. Used in scoring-based rules engines where individual rule contributions are summed or weighted to produce the final automated decision recommendation.

Date and time when the rules engine evaluation was executed. Represents the business event time of automated decisioning.

description: Method used by the rules engine to calculate the net amount at risk (NAR) for reinsurance and risk assessment purposes. NAR represents the insurance company's exposure on the policy.
Free-text notes or comments regarding the rules engine evaluation, including any system warnings, edge cases, or contextual information for underwriter review.
The life insurance or annuity product line being underwritten. Different product lines have different underwriting rules and risk tolerances.
Flat extra premium per thousand dollars of coverage recommended by the rules engine for specific impairments or occupational hazards.
Table rating (A through P or STD for standard) recommended by the rules engine for substandard risk pricing. Each letter represents an incremental mortality charge.
Indicates whether the automated decision requires regulatory approval or notification based on jurisdiction-specific requirements or policy characteristics.
Indicates whether the rules engine determined that facultative or automatic reinsurance placement is required based on retention limits and risk characteristics.
Type of reinsurance recommended by the rules engine. Automatic reinsurance follows pre-agreed treaty terms; facultative reinsurance requires case-by-case negotiation with reinsurers.
Indicates whether the application exceeds the company's retention limit, triggering reinsurance requirements. True means the risk exceeds internal capacity.
Total execution time in milliseconds for the rules engine evaluation. Used for performance monitoring and STP rate optimization.
Current status of this rules engine output record. Active indicates the current evaluation; superseded indicates a newer evaluation exists; archived indicates historical record retention.
Version identifier of the automated underwriting rules engine that performed the evaluation (e.g., 2.5.1). Critical for audit trail and reproducibility of automated decisions.
Number of rules that evaluated to a fail outcome, indicating non-compliance with automated underwriting criteria.
Total number of individual rules that were triggered and evaluated during this rules engine run.

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Suspense account records hold unidentified or unallocated premium payments. When the intended premium schedule can be identified (e.g., from policy number or remittance advice), the suspense record sh

System identifier of the agent or broker associated with the target policy, if identified. Used to notify the writing agent of suspense items related to their book of business and to support commission management workflows.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission); NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Suspense items involve unidentifiable remittances with accompanying correspondence, checks, or payment stubs requiring research. Real business process: payment reconciliation, research resolution work

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Unclaimed property/escheatment dormancy periods and reporting requirements are state-regulated. suspense_account has escheatment_state (denormalized), escheatment_reference, dormancy_expiry_date. Repl

Categorical aging classification of the suspense item based on the number of days elapsed since receipt_date. Used for management reporting, escalation workflows, and state unclaimed property dormancy period monitoring. Recalculated daily by the billing system.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws; GAAP ASC 944

description: Indicates whether this suspense item has been flagged for Anti-Money Laundering (AML) review due to unusual payment characteristics such as large cash amounts, structuring patterns, or unidentified remitters. True = flagged for AML review; False = no AML flag.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Compliance; Bank Secrecy Act (BSA); NAIC AML Model Regulation; ACORD Data Standards

description: The billing period (YYYY-MM) to which the suspense payment was applied upon resolution. Used for premium reconciliation and to ensure the payment is credited to the correct modal billing cycle (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly).
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Last four digits of the bank account number from which the EFT/ACH payment was drawn. Stored in masked form for reconciliation and refund routing without retaining full account data per PCI DSS requirements.
reference: PCI DSS; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; FinCEN AML/BSA Compliance

description: Check number from the remitter's payment instrument when the payment source type is 'check'. Used for bank reconciliation, duplicate payment detection, and NSF (Non-Sufficient Funds) tracking.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Model Regulation; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Timestamp when the suspense account record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; Sapiens BillingPro Audit Log

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the received payment amount (e.g., USD, CAD). Required for multi-currency operations and foreign national policyholder payments.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Date on which the policyholder or remitter was notified about the suspense item, requesting additional information or documentation to resolve the NIGO or unmatched payment condition.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Model Regulation; State Insurance Regulations — Policyholder Communication Requirements

description: Number of calendar days the payment has been held in suspense since receipt_date. Drives aging bucket assignment, escalation triggers, and unclaimed property dormancy period calculations per state law.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws

description: Date on which the suspense item reaches the end of the applicable state unclaimed property dormancy period, after which the funds must be escheated to the state. Calculated based on receipt_date plus the state-specific dormancy period (typically 3–5 years).
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: State unclaimed property filing reference number assigned when the suspense amount is transferred to a state's unclaimed property program. Populated when resolution_type is 'transferred_to_unclaimed'. Required for state unclaimed property law compliance and audit.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: Reference number associated with a tax-free 1035 Exchange transfer when the suspense reason is '1035_exchange_in_transit'. Links the suspense item to the originating carrier's transfer documentation. Required for IRS tax compliance under IRC Section 1035.
reference: IRC Section 1035 (1035 Exchange); IRS Tax Compliance; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: Indicates whether the target policy was in a grace period at the time the suspense payment was received. True = policy was in grace period; False = policy was current. Critical for lapse administration and determining urgency of suspense resolution.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Grace Period Module; NAIC Model Regulation; State Insurance Regulations

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the suspense account record. Used for change tracking, reconciliation, and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; Sapiens BillingPro Audit Log

description: Bank lockbox batch identifier associated with the physical or electronic payment deposit batch in which this suspense item was received. Used for bank reconciliation and cash posting audit trail.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Premium Collection Module; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; GAAP ASC 944

description: Specific reason the payment or accompanying documentation was classified as Not In Good Order (NIGO), when suspense_reason_code is 'nigo'. Examples include missing policy number, incorrect payee name, unsigned check, or missing 1035 Exchange transfer form.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC New Business Processing Standards; ACORD Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the payment was returned by the bank due to Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF). True = NSF return received; False = no NSF. NSF payments placed in suspense require special handling including policyholder notification and potential lapse processing.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro NSF Handling Module; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Name of the insurance carrier from which funds were transferred in a 1035 Exchange. Populated only when payment_source_type is '1035_exchange'. Required for IRS reporting and exchange documentation.
reference: IRC Section 1035 (1035 Exchange); IRS Tax Compliance; NAIC Data Standards

description: Additional detail about the payment instrument, such as bank name, check number, wire reference number, or ACH trace number. Supports payment tracing and reconciliation with the bank lockbox or EFT/ACH processor.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; ACORD Data Standards

description: Classification of the payment instrument or channel through which the suspense amount was received. Drives refund processing method and AML monitoring. '1035_exchange' indicates a tax-free policy exchange under IRC Section 1035.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; IRC Section 1035; FinCEN AML/BSA; NACHA ACH Rules

description: The premium due date on the target policy that this suspense payment is intended to satisfy. Used to assess whether the payment, once applied, will prevent a lapse or grace period entry.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; Policy Administration System; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The business date on which the unallocated or unidentified payment was received and entered into suspense. This is the principal real-world event date for the suspense item and drives aging calculations and unclaimed property dormancy periods.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Precise date and time the payment was received and logged into the suspense module, including timezone offset. Used for intraday reconciliation and audit trail purposes.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements

description: Gross amount of the payment received and placed into suspense, in the currency of receipt. This is the raw amount as received before any adjustments, fees, or partial allocations.
reference: GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; FASB LDTI; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: Full name of the individual or entity that submitted the payment currently held in suspense. May differ from the policyholder if payment was made by a third party, trustee, or ILIT. Required for refund processing and AML/KYC compliance.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Compliance; NAIC KYC Guidelines; ACORD Data Standards

description: Business date on which the suspense item was fully or partially resolved. Null if the suspense item remains open. Used for cash reconciliation, premium posting, and audit trail.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Precise date and time the suspense item was resolved, including timezone offset. Supports intraday cash management and SOX audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; GAAP ASC 944

description: Method by which the suspense item was resolved. 'applied_to_policy' = premium applied to identified policy; 'refunded_to_sender' = payment returned to remitter; 'transferred_to_unclaimed' = funds transferred to state unclaimed property; 'partial_applied' = portion applied to policy with remainder refunded or still open; 'voided' = record cancelled due to error. Null if suspense is still open.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws; GAAP ASC 944

description: Amount of the suspense balance that has been resolved (applied to policy, refunded, or transferred). May be less than received_amount if partial resolution occurred. Null if suspense is still open.
reference: GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: Native record identifier from the Sapiens BillingPro suspense module. Enables lineage tracing back to the operational system of record for reconciliation and data quality validation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: Coded reason explaining why the payment was placed into suspense rather than applied directly to a policy. 'policy_not_found' = no matching policy located; 'nigo' = Not In Good Order (NIGO) — missing or incorrect documentation; 'amount_mismatch' = payment does not match expected premium; '1035_exchange_in_transit' = tax-free 1035 Exchange funds awaiting policy setup; 'duplicate_payment' = potential duplicate of an existing payment; 'unidentified_remitter' = payer cannot be identified; 'currency_mismatch' = payment currency does not match policy currency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy_not_found|nigo|amount_mismatch|1035_exchange_in_transit|duplicate_payment|unidentified_remitter|currency_mismatch — promote to reference product]
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 1035 (1035 Exchange); ACORD Data Standards

description: Free-text narrative providing additional detail about why the payment was placed in suspense. Supplements the suspense_reason_code with case-specific context entered by the billing operations team.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the suspense item at receipt. Used in correspondence with policyholders, agents, and remitters to track unallocated payments. Format: SUS-YYYY-NNNNNNNN.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; ACORD Data Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the suspense item. 'open' = unresolved and pending action; 'resolved' = successfully applied to a policy; 'refunded' = returned to remitter; 'transferred_to_unclaimed' = moved to state unclaimed property; 'escheatment_pending' = awaiting formal escheatment filing; 'voided' = cancelled due to error or duplicate.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module; NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Unclaimed Property Laws

description: Remaining balance of the suspense item that has not yet been resolved. Equals received_amount minus resolved_amount. Used for open-item reconciliation and cash management reporting under GAAP/SAP.
reference: GAAP ASC 944; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; FASB ASC 944; Sapiens BillingPro Suspense Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction record in the billing system. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Reference to the billing account associated with the policy for which the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was executed. Links to the premium billing and collection record.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; Sapiens BillingPro

description: Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: APL (Automatic Premium Loan) activation triggers mandatory policyholder disclosure notices under state insurance regulations (e.g., NAIC model regulations). This is distinct from the grace period noti

Foreign key linking to billing.grace_period. Business justification: APL (Automatic Premium Loan) transactions are triggered during grace periods to prevent lapse when premium is unpaid and CSV is sufficient. This FK links the APL transaction to the grace period that t
Reference to the life insurance policy (Whole Life or Universal Life) against which this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered. Links to the policy master record.
reference: Policy Administration System — Policy Issuance Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Automatic Premium Loan transactions create policy loan accounting entries affecting asset balances and interest income. Required for balance sheet accuracy, loan portfolio reporting, and GAAP/SAP comp

Foreign key linking to policy.loan. Business justification: When APL is triggered, billing creates an apl_transaction AND policy creates a loan record for the automatic premium loan. This FK links the billing APL transaction to the specific policy loan it orig
Reference to the policyholder (insured owner) whose policy triggered the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). Used for customer-level reporting and lapse prevention analytics.
reference: Policy Administration System — Policy Servicing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: APL availability, terms, and interest rates are product-specific features. Critical for product feature utilization tracking, lapse prevention effectiveness analysis by product, product profitability

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: APL is triggered by a specific unpaid bill. While apl_transaction references grace_period, it should also reference the specific bill that triggered the APL. This enables bill-level APL tracking and s

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: APL transactions are triggered when a policy's premium schedule obligations aren't met and cash value is available. Linking APL to the schedule enables tracking which schedules trigger APL most freque

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Automatic Premium Loan provisions are a nonforfeiture option mandated and governed by state insurance codes. APL interest rates, notification requirements, and eligibility rules are state-specific. ap

Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: APL loans reduce CSV and net amount at risk, directly affecting statutory reserve net of policy loans. SAP reporting requires linking APL transactions to statutory reserves for net reserve calculation

The cumulative interest accrued on the outstanding Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance from the posting date to the current record date. Used for policy loan account reconciliation and policyholder statement generation. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; FASB ASC 944; SAP Accounting Principles; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the policyholder has elected and authorized the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) provision in their policy contract. True = APL provision is active and authorized; False = APL provision not elected. APL can only be triggered when this flag is True.
reference: Policy Administration System — Policy Servicing Module; NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: Sequential count of Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transactions executed against this policy, starting at 1 for the first APL. Indicates repeat APL usage, which is a key indicator of policyholder financial stress and lapse risk.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The modal billing frequency of the policy at the time the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly). Determines the premium amount due and the APL loan amount.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The timestamp when this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and ETL reconciliation.
reference: SOX Compliance; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

business_glossary_term: Cash Surrender Value (CSV) at Automatic Premium Loan (APL) Trigger
description: The gross Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the policy at the moment the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered. Must be sufficient to cover the loan amount; if CSV is insufficient, APL cannot be executed and lapse proceedings begin. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; FASB ASC 944

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this APL transaction record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international or expatriate policies.
reference: ISO 4217; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The annual interest rate applied to the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.080000 = 8.0%). Set per policy contract terms and applicable state regulations. Used for accrued interest calculations.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; IRC Section 7702

business_glossary_term: Automatic Premium Loan (APL) Interest Rate Type
description: Indicates whether the interest rate on the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) is fixed for the life of the loan, variable (adjustable per contract terms), or indexed to a benchmark rate. Determines how interest accrual is calculated over time.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: Indicates whether this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction successfully prevented a policy lapse. True = lapse was prevented by the APL; False = APL was insufficient or not executed, and lapse proceedings were initiated.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; Billing and Premium Collection System — Lapse Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The gross dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL), equal to the overdue premium amount (including any applicable modal loading) drawn from the policy's Cash Surrender Value (CSV) to prevent lapse. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; FASB ASC 944

description: Indicates whether this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction has tax implications under Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) rules per IRC Section 7702A. True = policy is a MEC and APL may be treated as a taxable distribution; False = policy is not a MEC. Critical for tax compliance reporting.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; IRS IRC Section 7702A; NAIC Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Net Cash Surrender Value (CSV) After Automatic Premium Loan (APL)
description: The remaining net Cash Surrender Value (CSV) after deducting the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) amount and any existing policy loan balance. Represents the policyholder's accessible equity post-APL. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The date on which the policyholder notification of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) execution was sent. Populated only when notification_sent_flag is True. Required for regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Automatic Premium Loan (APL) Notification Sent Flag
description: Indicates whether the required regulatory notification was sent to the policyholder informing them that an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was executed against their policy. True = notification sent; False = notification pending or not sent. Required by most state insurance regulations.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The total outstanding balance of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) at the time of this transaction record, including principal and accrued interest. Reduces the net Cash Surrender Value (CSV) available to the policyholder. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; IRC Section 7702

description: The scheduled premium amount that was unpaid and triggered the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). May differ slightly from loan_amount if fees or interest adjustments are included in the loan. Denominated in USD.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Policy Loan Account Balance After Automatic Premium Loan (APL)
description: The total outstanding policy loan balance immediately after this APL transaction was posted. Equals policy_loan_account_balance_before plus loan_amount. Used for policy loan account reconciliation. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

business_glossary_term: Policy Loan Account Balance Before Automatic Premium Loan (APL)
description: The total outstanding policy loan balance (including any prior APL or voluntary policy loans) immediately before this APL transaction was posted. Used for loan account reconciliation and audit trail. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: The policy year (anniversary year) in which the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered, calculated from the policy issue date. Used for actuarial experience studies, lapse rate analysis, and persistency reporting.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; Actuarial Valuation System — Experience Studies Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The accounting date on which the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) amount was posted to the policy loan account and the overdue premium was credited. Used for statutory and GAAP accounting period alignment.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; FASB ASC 944; SAP Accounting Principles

description: The portion of the overdue premium amount allocated to the base life insurance policy component within the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). Used for premium allocation reporting across policy components. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The portion of the overdue premium amount allocated to the Cost of Insurance (COI) charge within the Automatic Premium Loan (APL), applicable primarily to Universal Life (UL) policies. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The portion of the overdue premium amount allocated to policy riders (e.g., ADB, LTC, waiver of premium) within the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). Riders may lapse separately if their premium component cannot be covered. Denominated in USD.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The original scheduled due date of the overdue premium that triggered the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). Establishes the billing cycle reference for the missed payment.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The type of life insurance product associated with this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction. APL is only applicable to Whole Life (WL) and Universal Life (UL) products that accumulate Cash Surrender Value (CSV).
reference: Policy Administration System — Policy Issuance Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The date on which the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was reversed, if applicable. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Triggers reinstatement of the full Cash Surrender Value (CSV) and removal of the loan from the policy loan account.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; SAP Accounting Principles

description: Indicates whether this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction has been reversed. True = APL was reversed (e.g., policyholder subsequently paid the overdue premium or policy was reinstated); False = APL remains active and outstanding.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; SAP Accounting Principles

description: The reason code for the reversal of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL). 'premium_paid' = policyholder paid the overdue premium after APL was posted; 'reinstatement' = policy reinstated with back-premium payment; 'policy_surrender' = policy surrendered and loan offset against CSV; 'system_correction' = administrative correction; 'other' = other reason.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Code identifying the Policy Administration System instance or module that originated this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction record (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, EXL). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; ACORD Standards; SOX Compliance

description: The native transaction identifier or reference number assigned by the originating Policy Administration System for this APL event. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system of record.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; ACORD Standards; SOX Compliance

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction by the Policy Administration System. Used in policyholder correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory reporting.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; ACORD Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction. 'initiated' = APL triggered but not yet posted to policy loan account; 'posted' = loan amount applied and premium paid; 'reversed' = APL reversed due to subsequent premium payment or reinstatement; 'cancelled' = APL voided before posting; 'pending_reversal' = reversal in progress.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; SAP Accounting Principles

description: The exact date and time when the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered by the Policy Administration System, typically at the end of the grace period when the overdue premium remains unpaid. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
reference: Policy Administration System — APL Module; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The timestamp when this Automatic Premium Loan (APL) transaction record was last modified in the data platform (Silver Layer), such as when a reversal or status update was applied. Used for change data capture and audit trail.
reference: SOX Compliance; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the policy reinstatement record in the billing domain. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: A billing reinstatement requires a new underwriting application for evidence of insurability. The billing reinstatement process is initiated by and dependent on the underwriting application. State ins

business_glossary_term: Reinstatement Billing Premium Payment Id (Foreign Key)

Reference to the policyholder (owner) who submitted the reinstatement application. Used for customer-level reinstatement analytics.

description: Actual total amount received from the policyholder in connection with this reinstatement. May differ from total_amount_due if partial payments were accepted or waivers applied.

description: Indicates whether an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered to fund the back-premium collection at reinstatement. True if the policy's cash value was used via APL to satisfy the reinstatement amount due.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Policy Administration System (APL module); IRC Section 7702

description: The date on which the policyholder formally submitted the reinstatement application. Starts the reinstatement workflow clock and determines eligibility under state-mandated reinstatement windows (typically 3–5 years from lapse).
