description: The date on which the reinstatement application was formally approved by the underwriting or policy servicing team. Null if the application is pending, denied, or withdrawn.
reference: Policy Administration System reinstatement module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Current workflow status of the reinstatement application. Drives downstream processing: 'approved' triggers policy restoration and premium posting; 'denied' triggers adverse action notice; 'conditionally_approved' requires additional evidence before final approval.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Policy Administration System reinstatement module

description: Date on which the Attending Physician Statement (APS) was received from the policyholder's physician. Null if APS is not required or not yet received. Tracks evidence collection progress in the reinstatement workflow.
reference: Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA / Swiss Re Magnum); HIPAA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Total gross premium amount due for the lapsed period that must be collected to reinstate the policy. Covers all modal premiums (base policy plus riders) from the lapse date to the reinstatement effective date.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Billing and Premium Collection System; SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles)

description: Interest charged on the back-premium amount for the lapsed period, calculated at the policy contract rate or state-mandated rate. Added to back_premium_amount to determine total amount due at reinstatement.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; State Departments of Insurance; IRC Section 7702

description: Timestamp when this reinstatement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation point for SOX and regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 944; Policy Administration System

description: The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the policy at the time of lapse. Used to determine whether APL was available to prevent lapse and to assess the financial position of the policy at reinstatement.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Policy Administration System; SAP

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this reinstatement record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international life insurance portfolios.
reference: ISO 4217; GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles); IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: The date on which the reinstatement application was formally denied. Null if the application is approved, pending, or withdrawn. Required for adverse action notification compliance.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for reinstatement denial (e.g., insurability not demonstrated, back-premium not paid, reinstatement window expired, fraud indicator). Null if not denied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insurability_failed|back_premium_unpaid|window_expired|fraud_indicator|incomplete_evidence|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance adverse action requirements

description: The date on which the policy coverage is restored following successful reinstatement approval and back-premium collection. This is the binding date from which the reinstated policy is in force.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; IRC Section 7702; State Departments of Insurance

description: Type of underwriting evidence required or submitted for this reinstatement. 'none' applies when lapse is within the simplified reinstatement window. 'aps' refers to Attending Physician Statement (APS). 'full_underwriting' applies for long lapses or high face amounts.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA / Swiss Re Magnum); HIPAA

description: The death benefit face amount of the policy being reinstated. Denormalized from the policy record for reinstatement underwriting thresholds and reinsurance notification requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Reinsurance Management System; Policy Administration System

description: Indicates whether this reinstatement has been flagged for potential fraud or STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) activity. Triggers enhanced review by the Special Investigations Unit (SIU).
reference: NAIC Model Insurance Fraud Act; FinCEN; STOLI detection guidelines; Claims Management System (fraud detection module)

description: The date on which the policy's contractual grace period expired, triggering the lapse. Typically 30 or 31 days after the premium due date per state regulation. Used to validate lapse date accuracy.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; State Departments of Insurance; Billing and Premium Collection System (grace period management module)

description: Indicates whether the signed personal statement of insurability (good health declaration) has been received from the policyholder as part of the reinstatement application package.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Policy Administration System reinstatement module

description: Number of calendar days the policy was in lapsed status, calculated from lapse_date to application_date. Used to assess reinstatement eligibility, back-premium calculation, and underwriting evidence requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; State Departments of Insurance

business_glossary_term: Medical Information Bureau (MIB) Check Performed Indicator
description: Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau (MIB) inquiry was performed as part of the reinstatement underwriting process. MIB checks are standard for reinstatements requiring evidence of insurability.
reference: MIB Group Inc. data exchange standards; HIPAA; Underwriting Workbench

description: Free-text field for underwriter or policy servicing analyst notes regarding special circumstances, conditional requirements, or exceptions associated with this reinstatement. Not used for structured data.
The date on which the back-premium and associated charges were received and posted by the billing system. Null if payment has not yet been collected.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System; NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act

description: The payment instrument used to collect the back-premium and reinstatement charges. 'automatic_premium_loan' (APL) indicates the policy loan provision was used to fund reinstatement.
reference: Billing and Premium Collection System (EFT/ACH module); NAIC Model Regulation

description: Outstanding policy loan balance at the time of lapse. Relevant for reinstatement calculations as outstanding loan interest may be required to be paid or capitalized as part of the reinstatement amount due.
reference: Policy Administration System; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act

description: Number of times this policy has previously been reinstated. Used for underwriting risk assessment, fraud detection, and STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) screening. Policies with multiple reinstatements may require enhanced scrutiny.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; FinCEN AML/BSA compliance; STOLI detection guidelines

description: Code identifying the insurance product type being reinstated (e.g., WL, UL, IUL, VUL, Term, DI). Used to apply product-specific reinstatement rules and underwriting guidelines.
reference: NAIC Product Filing; Policy Administration System; ACORD Standards

description: Externally-visible business identifier for the reinstatement transaction, assigned by the Policy Administration System reinstatement module. Used in policyholder correspondence and regulatory filings.
reference: ACORD Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro / FAST reinstatement module)

description: Classification of the reinstatement process type. 'standard' requires full underwriting evidence; 'simplified' uses a short-form health statement; 'automatic' applies within the contractual automatic reinstatement window; 'conditional' is subject to additional requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Policy Administration System reinstatement module

description: Code identifying the operational Policy Administration System instance from which this reinstatement record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, EXL). Supports multi-system environments and data lineage tracking.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; EXL; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle Insurance Policy Administration); ACORD Standards

description: Two-letter US state code for the state in which the policy was originally issued. Determines which state's reinstatement regulations, grace period rules, and evidence requirements apply.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; ACORD Standards

description: Total amount required from the policyholder to complete reinstatement, comprising back-premium amount plus back-premium interest plus any outstanding loan interest. This is the gross amount billed at reinstatement.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Billing and Premium Collection System

description: The underwriting outcome for the reinstatement evidence review. 'approved_rated' indicates reinstatement approved with a substandard rating or exclusion. 'not_required' applies when no evidence was needed.
reference: Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA / Swiss Re Magnum); NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the reinstatement requires the policyholder to provide evidence of insurability (e.g., health statement, APS). Typically required when the lapse duration exceeds the contractual or state-mandated threshold.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA / Swiss Re Magnum); State Departments of Insurance

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this reinstatement record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer ETL pipeline.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 944; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: The last date by which the policyholder may apply for reinstatement under the policy contract and applicable state law. Typically 3–5 years from the lapse date. Applications received after this date are ineligible.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Policy Act; State Departments of Insurance; Policy Administration System

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the grace period record. Primary key for the grace_period data product in the billing domain.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module

Reference to the life insurance policy for which this grace period has been initiated. Links the grace period record to the master policy entity.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro)

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Grace period notices must identify the insured per state DOI requirements. Waiver-of-premium eligibility during grace period (disability waiver) is insured-specific. Regulatory grace period reporting

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Grace periods trigger mandatory regulatory lapse warning notices required by state insurance laws. Real business process: consumer protection compliance, third-party notification requirements, proof o
Reference to the policyholder (owner) associated with the policy under grace period. Supports customer-level grace period reporting and lapse prevention outreach.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; CRM and Customer Engagement Platform (Salesforce Financial Services Cloud)

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Grace period rules (duration, APL eligibility, notice requirements) are product-specific design features. Required for lapse management, conservation efforts, product design compliance validation, and
Reference to the premium bill or billing notice that triggered the grace period due to a missed or insufficient payment. Establishes the causal link between the billing event and the grace period.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Grace periods are triggered by missed payments on a premium schedule. While grace_period references premium_bill, it should also reference the schedule to enable schedule-level grace period tracking a

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Grace period management for variable products requires real-time separate account value monitoring to determine APL eligibility and CSV sufficiency to cover overdue premiums. The grace_period stores c

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Grace period duration (typically 30-31 days) and notice requirements are mandated by state regulation and vary by jurisdiction. Each grace period event must reference the applicable state regulation t
The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) applied to cover the outstanding premium, if APL was triggered as the resolution outcome. Null if APL was not triggered.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro — APL Triggers Module

description: Indicates whether the policy is eligible for an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) to cover the outstanding premium during the grace period. Eligibility is based on sufficient policy cash surrender value (CSV) and the presence of an APL provision in the policy contract.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro)

description: The modal billing frequency of the policy at the time the grace period was triggered. Determines the premium amount due and the billing cycle context for the grace period. Supports Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Modal Billing Module; ACORD Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The timestamp when this grace period record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the audit creation time for data lineage and regulatory compliance purposes.
reference: SOX Compliance; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this grace period record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international or expatriate life insurance products.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The number of calendar days remaining in the grace period window as of the last record update. Supports lapse prevention workflow prioritization and regulatory notice timeline management. Null if the grace period is closed.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module

description: The total number of calendar days in the grace period window, as defined by the applicable state regulation or policy contract. Typically 30 or 31 days for life insurance per NAIC model regulation. Stored explicitly to support regulatory compliance validation without requiring date arithmetic.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements

description: The date on which the grace period expires. Typically 30 or 31 days from the start date per NAIC model regulation, but may vary by state DOI requirement, product type, or contractual terms. After this date, the policy lapses if no cure action has been taken.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance (all 50 US states); ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the first regulatory grace period notice was sent to the policyholder, informing them of the missed payment and the outstanding premium required to cure. Required for state DOI regulatory notice timeline compliance.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements — Notice of Lapse Regulations; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the formal lapse notice was sent to the policyholder, notifying them that the policy has lapsed or is imminent to lapse due to non-payment at the end of the grace period. Required by most state DOI regulations.
reference: State DOI Requirements — Notice of Lapse Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The scheduled premium amount due for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly) that was missed, triggering the grace period. Represents the full modal premium before any partial payment credit.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Modal Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, exception handling comments, or special circumstances related to this grace period record (e.g., customer hardship accommodation, regulatory exception, manual override reason). Used by billing operations staff.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The primary channel through which grace period notices were delivered to the policyholder (e.g., postal mail, email, SMS, phone call, online portal). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and lapse prevention workflow tracking.
reference: State DOI Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The number of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) payment returns associated with this grace period event. Multiple NSF returns may indicate a pattern requiring escalated lapse prevention action or payment method change.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — NSF Handling Module; NACHA Operating Rules; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The net premium amount still required to cure the grace period and restore the policy to active status. Equals the modal premium amount less any partial payments received. This is the cure amount communicated in regulatory notices.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements; Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module

description: The amount of any partial premium payment received during the grace period that has been credited toward the outstanding premium. Zero if no partial payment was received.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Payment Processing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The payment method on file for the policy at the time the grace period was triggered. Identifies whether the failure was an EFT/ACH failure, a returned check (NSF), or a direct bill non-response. Supports root cause analysis for lapse prevention.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — EFT/ACH Payment Records Module; NAIC Model Regulation; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Current lifecycle status of the grace period record. 'open' indicates the grace period is active and the policy is still in force. 'cured' indicates the outstanding premium was paid within the grace period. 'lapsed' indicates the policy lapsed due to non-payment. 'apl_triggered' indicates an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was applied. 'reinstated' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse. 'expired' indicates the grace period window closed without resolution.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation §§ 4-6; State DOI Requirements; Sapiens BillingPro — Lapse Administration Module

description: Classification of the grace period based on its governing basis. 'standard' reflects the default NAIC model regulation grace period (30-31 days). 'extended' reflects a grace period longer than standard, granted by company policy or state mandate. 'state_mandated' reflects a grace period required by a specific state DOI regulation. 'contractual' reflects a grace period defined within the policy contract terms.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance (all 50 US states); IRC Section 7702

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the policy under grace period, as assigned by the Policy Administration System. Used for regulatory notices, customer correspondence, and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: ACORD Standards; Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro)

description: The original scheduled premium due date that was missed or insufficiently paid, triggering the grace period. Used to reconcile the grace period against the premium billing schedule.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The number of prior grace period events on this policy before the current grace period. A high count indicates a pattern of payment difficulty and may trigger enhanced lapse prevention workflows or underwriting review.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The type of life insurance or annuity product associated with the policy under grace period. Determines applicable grace period rules, state regulatory requirements, and resolution options (e.g., APL eligibility). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_life|term_life|universal_life|variable_universal_life|indexed_universal_life|annuity|disability_income|long_term_care — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; State DOI Requirements; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether a regulatory grace period or lapse notice is required to be sent to the policyholder under applicable state DOI regulations for this policy. Drives the notice generation workflow in the billing system.
reference: State DOI Requirements — Notice of Lapse Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The last date by which the policyholder may apply for reinstatement of a lapsed policy, as defined by the policy contract and applicable state regulations. Typically 3-5 years from the lapse date. Null if the policy has not lapsed.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro)

description: The date on which the grace period was resolved, either through payment, lapse, APL trigger, reinstatement, or waiver. Null if the grace period is still open.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module

description: The final resolution outcome of the grace period. 'paid' indicates the outstanding premium was paid in full within the grace period. 'lapsed' indicates the policy lapsed due to non-payment. 'apl_triggered' indicates an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was applied to cover the outstanding premium. 'reinstated' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse. 'waived' indicates the premium was waived (e.g., due to disability waiver rider).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements; Sapiens BillingPro — Lapse Administration Module

description: The date on which the second or follow-up grace period notice was sent to the policyholder. Some states require multiple notices during the grace period window. Null if only one notice is required or sent.
reference: State DOI Requirements — Notice of Lapse Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The operational billing or policy administration system from which this grace period record originated. Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; Majesco Billing; FAST; EXL; Oracle Insurance Policy Administration

description: The native identifier or reference number assigned to this grace period record in the originating operational system (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro internal grace period ID). Supports cross-system reconciliation and ETL traceability.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; Majesco Billing; FAST; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the grace period commenced, typically the day after the premium due date when payment was not received. This is the principal business event date for the grace period lifecycle.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State DOI Requirements; Sapiens BillingPro — Grace Period Administration Module

description: The two-letter US state code of the state in which the policy was issued. Determines the applicable state DOI grace period regulations, required notice timelines, and lapse rules that govern this grace period record.
reference: State Departments of Insurance (all 50 US states plus territories); NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the lapse notice was sent to the designated third party (e.g., assignee, ILIT trustee). Null if no third party notice is required or if it has not yet been sent.
reference: State DOI Requirements — Third Party Notice Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether a lapse notice must also be sent to a designated third party (e.g., assignee, collateral assignee, or irrevocable beneficiary) per state DOI regulations or policy contract terms. Relevant for ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) and collateral assignment scenarios.
reference: State DOI Requirements — Third Party Notice Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation; ILIT Policy Administration

description: The specific reason that initiated the grace period. 'missed_payment' indicates no payment was received by the due date. 'insufficient_payment' indicates a partial payment was received but did not meet the required premium amount. 'nsf_return' indicates a Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) check was returned. 'eft_failure' indicates an Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT)/ACH payment failed. 'check_return' indicates a physical check was returned unpaid.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro — NSF Handling and EFT/ACH Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The timestamp when this grace period record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent status change, notice sent, or resolution update for audit trail purposes.
reference: SOX Compliance; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: Indicates whether a Waiver of Premium rider (e.g., Automatic Waiver of Premium — AWL, or disability waiver) is active on the policy at the time of the grace period. If true, the outstanding premium may be waived, affecting the resolution outcome.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each policy lapse event record in the billing domain. Primary key for the lapse_event data product.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

description: Reference to the billing account associated with the lapsed policy. Links the lapse event to the premium billing and collection record in Sapiens BillingPro.

Reference to the policy that has lapsed due to non-payment of premium after expiration of the grace period. Core linkage to the policy master record.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Lapse events generate mandatory regulatory notifications and non-forfeiture option election forms required by state law. Real business process: state DOI compliance, policyholder rights documentation,
Reference to the policyholder (insured owner) whose policy has lapsed. Supports customer-level lapse analytics and regulatory notification workflows.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Lapse behavior varies significantly by product design (term vs permanent, premium structure, surrender charges). Essential for product experience studies, pricing assumption validation, product profit

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Lapse occurs when a premium schedule's payment obligations aren't met after grace period expiry. Linking lapse to schedule enables schedule-level lapse analysis and supports identification of high-ris

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life/annuity lapse processing requires identifying the separate account holding policy value at lapse for liquidation, non-forfeiture option calculation, and regulatory reporting. The lapse_e

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: A lapse event is triggered by non-payment of a specific premium bill after the grace period expires. lapse_event has missed_premium_due_date but no direct reference to the specific premium_bill that t
The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) applied against the policy's Cash Surrender Value (CSV) to fund the outstanding premium. Null if APL was not applied.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

description: Indicates whether an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was actually applied to prevent or reverse the lapse. True if APL was triggered and the premium was funded via policy loan. Distinct from apl_eligible which reflects eligibility only.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

description: Indicates whether the policy is eligible for an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) to prevent lapse. True if the policy has sufficient Cash Surrender Value (CSV) to cover the outstanding premium and the APL provision is active in the policy contract.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

description: The premium payment frequency mode in effect at the time of lapse. Determines the modal premium amount and grace period calculation. Values: annual, semi_annual, quarterly, monthly.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Premium Billing Module; ACORD Standards

description: The date and time at which this lapse event record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Audit trail timestamp for data governance and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 944; Databricks Silver Layer audit standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this lapse event record. Typically USD for domestic US life insurance policies.

The date on which the extended term insurance coverage expires when the extended_term non-forfeiture option is elected. Calculated based on the CSV at lapse and the net single premium for term coverage. Null if extended term was not elected.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: The original face amount (death benefit) of the lapsed policy as of the lapse effective date. Used for reinsurance cession adjustments, actuarial reserving, and regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Standards

description: The date on which the policy officially lapsed, defined as the day following the expiration of the grace period without receipt of the required premium. This is the principal business event date for the lapse transaction.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; State Departments of Insurance

description: Externally-known business identifier for the lapse event, assigned by the Sapiens BillingPro lapse administration module. Used for cross-system reference and regulatory correspondence.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Coded reason for the policy lapse as recorded in Sapiens BillingPro. 'NSF' = non-sufficient funds; 'EFT_FAIL' = electronic funds transfer failure; 'VOLUNTARY' = policyholder elected to lapse; 'PREMIUM_UNAFFORDABLE' = policyholder cited affordability; 'ADDRESS_UNKNOWN' = undeliverable billing notices; 'POLICY_SURRENDER' = policyholder surrendered; 'OTHER' = other reason. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NSF|EFT_FAIL|VOLUNTARY|PREMIUM_UNAFFORDABLE|ADDRESS_UNKNOWN|POLICY_SURRENDER|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the lapse reason, supplementing the lapse_reason_code. May include notes from the billing administrator or automated system messages from Sapiens BillingPro.

Current workflow state of the lapse event. 'pending' indicates grace period has expired but lapse is not yet finalized; 'confirmed' indicates lapse is fully processed; 'reinstated' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse; 'reversed' indicates the lapse was reversed due to payment received; 'written_off' indicates the policy is permanently lapsed with no reinstatement.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module

description: The scheduled premium due date for which payment was not received, triggering the grace period and subsequent lapse. Corresponds to the billing cycle date in the premium schedule.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Premium Billing Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The scheduled periodic premium amount for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly) applicable at the time of lapse. Represents the single missed payment that triggered the grace period.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Premium Billing Module; ACORD Standards

business_glossary_term: Net Amount at Risk (NAR) at Lapse
description: The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — the difference between the death benefit and the policy reserve or account value — as of the lapse effective date. Used for reinsurance cession termination calculations and actuarial risk analysis.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Reinsurance treaty standards; Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: The date on which the non-forfeiture option was elected by the policyholder or automatically applied by the system. Null if no non-forfeiture option was applicable or elected.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: The non-forfeiture option elected or automatically applied upon lapse. 'extended_term' = policy continues as term insurance for the original face amount for a limited period; 'reduced_paid_up' = policy continues as paid-up whole life at a reduced face amount; 'cash_surrender' = policyholder receives CSV; 'none' = no non-forfeiture option applicable (e.g., term policy with no CSV).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; State Departments of Insurance; FASB ASC 944

description: The total unpaid premium balance outstanding at the time of lapse, including the missed modal premium and any arrears. Expressed in USD. Used for reinstatement calculations and regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles); FASB ASC 944

description: The number of complete months the policy was in force from issue date to lapse effective date. Used in actuarial lapse rate studies, persistency analysis, and DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) amortization modeling.
reference: FASB ASC 944; LDTI (Long Duration Targeted Improvements); Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: The original issue date of the lapsed policy. Used to calculate policy duration at lapse, which is a key metric for actuarial experience studies and lapse rate analysis.
reference: ACORD Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944

description: The outstanding policy loan balance (including accrued interest) at the time of lapse. Reduces the net CSV available for non-forfeiture options and APL eligibility. Relevant for IRC Section 7702 MEC (Modified Endowment Contract) compliance.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; IRS

description: The externally-known policy contract number that has lapsed. Denormalized from the policy master for operational reporting and regulatory notification without requiring a join.

The date and time at which the lapse event was formally processed and confirmed in the billing system. Represents the business event timestamp when the lapse transaction was executed in Sapiens BillingPro.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro Lapse Administration Module; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The life insurance product type of the lapsed policy. Drives non-forfeiture option availability, grace period rules, and regulatory notification requirements. Values: WL (Whole Life), UL (Universal Life), IUL (Indexed Universal Life), VUL (Variable Universal Life), term, annuity, DI (Disability Income), LTC (Long-Term Care). description: The user ID or system identifier of the individual or automated process that approved this premium adjustment. Null when approval_status is 'not_required'. Required for SOX audit trail on manual or exception adjustments.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Standards

description: The insured's attained age (in whole years) at the time of this premium adjustment. Relevant for attained-age recalculation adjustments on Yearly Renewable Term (YRT), Universal Life (UL), and Indexed Universal Life (IUL) products where COI rates are age-banded.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The modal billing frequency of the policy at the time of this adjustment (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly). Determines the modal factor applied to convert annual premium to the scheduled payment amount.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; Sapiens BillingPro

description: The Cost of Living Adjustment (COLA) percentage rate applied to the premium when the adjustment_reason_code is COLA. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3%). Null for non-COLA adjustments.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Consumer Price Index (CPI) Standards

description: Identifies the specific premium component affected by this adjustment: 'base' for the base policy premium, 'rider' for a policy rider premium, 'coi' for Cost of Insurance (COI) charges on universal life products, or 'apl' for Automatic Premium Loan (APL) repayment component.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this premium adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international or expatriate life insurance products.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Life Data Model

description: The calendar date on which the premium adjustment becomes effective on the policy billing schedule. This is the business event date — the date from which the new premium amount applies, which may differ from the transaction processing date.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Policy Administration Standards

description: Indicates whether this premium adjustment triggered a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) test review under IRC Section 7702A. True when the adjustment requires validation that the new premium level does not cause the policy to fail the 7-pay test and become classified as a MEC.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; IRS MEC Compliance Standards

description: The annualized premium amount (Annual Premium Equivalent) that will apply after this adjustment, calculated by annualizing the new modal premium based on the billing mode. Supports APE reporting, actuarial reserving, and regulatory filings.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Annual Statement; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: The policy face amount (death benefit) that applies after this adjustment. Captured when the adjustment is triggered by a face amount change. Used to verify 7702 compliance and recalculate guideline premium limits.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; IRC Section 7702 GPT/CVAT; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: The modal premium amount (per billing frequency) that will apply to the policy billing schedule after this adjustment is applied. This becomes the new scheduled premium for future billing cycles.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, exception handling comments, or supplementary context recorded by the billing operations team regarding this premium adjustment. Not used for structured data; intended for human-readable commentary.
reference: NAIC Market Conduct Standards; SOX Section 404

description: The policy year (1-based count from policy issue date) in which this premium adjustment is effective. Used for anniversary-based rate change processing, attained age recalculations, and actuarial experience studies.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP)

description: The annualized premium amount (Annual Premium Equivalent) in effect before this adjustment, calculated by annualizing the prior modal premium based on the billing mode. Supports APE reporting and actuarial valuation.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Annual Statement; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: The policy face amount (death benefit) in effect before this adjustment. Captured when the adjustment is triggered by a face amount change, as face amount directly drives base premium calculations.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; IRC Section 7702 GPT/CVAT; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: The modal premium amount (per billing frequency) in effect on the policy billing schedule immediately before this adjustment was applied. Provides the baseline for calculating the adjustment delta and supports audit trail requirements.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: The date and time when the premium adjustment was fully processed and posted to the billing schedule in the Policy Administration or Billing System. Supports audit trail and SLA monitoring for billing operations.
reference: SOX Section 404; Sapiens BillingPro Audit Trail Requirements

description: The internal product code identifying the life insurance or annuity product type associated with the policy (e.g., WL, UL, IUL, VUL, YRT, SPIA, FIA). Supports product-level premium adjustment analytics and actuarial segmentation.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Product Filing Standards

description: Reference identifier for the regulatory filing, state department of insurance bulletin, or NAIC model regulation revision that mandated this premium rate adjustment. Populated when adjustment_reason_code is REG_RATE_REV. Supports regulatory compliance audit trail.
reference: NAIC Market Conduct Standards; State Departments of Insurance Rate Filing Requirements; SOX Section 404

description: Flag indicating whether this adjustment record is a reversal of a previously applied adjustment. True when this record cancels and backs out a prior adjustment; False for original adjustments. Used in financial reconciliation to net out reversed transactions.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; SOX Section 404

description: The standardized code identifying the specific policy rider whose premium is being adjusted, when the component_type is 'rider'. Examples include ADB (Accidental Death Benefit), AWL (Automatic Waiver of Premium), LTC (Long-Term Care), or DI (Disability Income) riders. Null when adjustment applies to base premium or COI.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: The maximum annual premium allowed under the IRC Section 7702A seven-pay test at the time of this adjustment. Captured when mec_test_flag is True to document the compliance threshold against which the new premium was evaluated.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS MEC Compliance Standards; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this premium adjustment transaction. 'PAS' = Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST); 'BILLING' = Billing and Premium Collection System (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro); 'UW_WORKBENCH' = Underwriting Workbench; 'ACTUARIAL' = Actuarial Valuation System; 'MANUAL' = manual override by authorized user.
reference: ACORD Life Data Model; SOX Section 404; NAIC Market Conduct Standards

description: The transaction identifier from the originating source system (Policy Administration System or Billing System) that authorized and triggered this premium adjustment. Provides end-to-end traceability from the billing data product back to the system of record.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards

description: The date and time when the premium adjustment transaction was created and recorded in the source system. Represents the system-of-record capture time, distinct from the business effective date.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life Data Model; NAIC Market Conduct Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each premium allocation transaction record in the billing system. Primary key for the premium_allocation data product.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro internal record identifier convention

description: Reference to the billing account under which the premium payment was received and from which this allocation is derived.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro billing account module; ACORD Data Standards

description: Reference to the parent premium payment transaction from which this allocation is derived. Links the allocation detail back to the received payment header.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro payment processing module

description: Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: Premium allocation splits payments into COI, expense load, and savings components. The COI component is computed from the COI rate schedule. This link enables actuarial validation of per-policy COI de

Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: For indexed and fixed annuity products, premium allocations are directed to specific crediting strategies (index strategies, fixed account). This link is required for accurate interest crediting, acco

Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: IFRS 17 insurance revenue recognition requires premium allocations to be mapped to contract groups for CSM release and insurance revenue calculation. Finance teams reconcile allocated premium by IFRS
Reference to the life insurance policy to which this premium allocation applies. Establishes the policy context for the allocation record.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Oracle Insurance Policy Administration; NAIC Policy Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: Premium allocation performs MEC testing using seven-pay premium limits defined in irc7702_parameter. Direct link enables automated MEC compliance validation at allocation time. seven_pay_premium_limit

Reference to the original premium_allocation_id that this record reverses or adjusts. Populated only when allocation_status is REVERSED or ADJUSTED, enabling a complete audit chain from original allocation through all corrections.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro reversal and adjustment audit trail

description: Reference to the policyholder (owner) associated with the policy for which this premium is being allocated. Supports customer-level billing analytics and PARTY_REFERENCE requirement.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Premium allocations follow product plan component structure (base premium, rider premiums, COI rates, expense loads). Critical for accurate accounting, DAC calculations, statutory reserve computations

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable life/annuity premium allocations are directed to specific investment portfolios (general account segments or separate account sub-portfolios). Portfolio-level premium inflow reporting, ALM an

Foreign key linking to product.separate_account_fund. Business justification: For variable life/annuity products, premium allocations are directed to specific separate account funds. This link validates that allocations target only plan-approved fund options, supports SEC-requi

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life and annuity products require tracking which separate account fund each premium allocation is invested into. This is the core operational link for policyholder-directed investment electio

The net dollar amount credited to the policy Account Value (AV) after deducting COI charges and expense loads from the gross premium payment. Applicable for UL/IUL/VUL products. Populated when component_type is ACCOUNT_VALUE_FUNDING.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; Sapiens BillingPro account value module

description: The gross dollar amount of the premium payment allocated to this specific policy component. This is the primary monetary fact for the allocation line. For UL/IUL/VUL products, this represents the portion directed to COI, expense load, rider premium, or account value funding as indicated by component_type.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; FASB ASC 944

description: The business effective date on which the premium amount was allocated to the policy component. This is the principal real-world event date for the allocation, used for policy accounting, reserve calculations, and billing period reconciliation.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles

description: Externally visible business identifier for this premium allocation record, used in billing statements, reconciliation reports, and policyholder correspondence. Follows the format ALLOC-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro allocation reference numbering

description: Sequential line number ordering the allocation components within a single premium payment. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full allocation breakdown for a given payment_id, supporting reconciliation and audit.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro allocation sequencing; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle state of this premium allocation record. PENDING = allocation calculated but not yet posted to policy; APPLIED = allocation successfully posted to the policy component; REVERSED = allocation reversed due to payment return (NSF) or correction; ADJUSTED = allocation modified due to billing correction or reallocation; FAILED = allocation processing encountered an error.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro allocation status workflow; ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: The precise date and time when this allocation record was created and posted in the billing system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp and supports intraday reconciliation and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro audit trail

description: The annualized equivalent of the allocated component premium, calculated by applying the modal factor to the allocated_amount. Used for Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) reporting, new business value calculations, and actuarial reserving.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; FASB ASC 944; LDTI (Long Duration Targeted Improvements)

business_glossary_term: Automatic Premium Loan (APL) Balance After Allocation
description: The outstanding Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance on the policy immediately after this allocation is applied. Populated when component_type is APL_REPAYMENT. The difference between apl_balance_before and apl_balance_after equals the allocated_amount for APL repayment lines.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation on Policy Loans; IRC Section 7702; Policy Administration System

business_glossary_term: Automatic Premium Loan (APL) Balance Before Allocation
description: The outstanding Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance on the policy immediately before this allocation is applied. Populated when component_type is APL_REPAYMENT to track loan reduction. APL is triggered when a premium is unpaid and the policy has sufficient cash value.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation on Policy Loans; IRC Section 7702; Policy Administration System

description: The modal billing frequency under which the premium payment was collected and this allocation was generated. Determines the billing period duration and the modal premium factor applied to the annualized premium.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro modal billing module; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: The end date of the billing period to which this premium allocation is applied. Together with billing_period_start_date, defines the modal billing interval covered by this allocation.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro billing period module; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: The start date of the billing period to which this premium allocation is applied. Used for modal billing reconciliation (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly) and premium accounting.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro billing period module; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: The Cost of Insurance (COI) rate per thousand dollars of Net Amount at Risk (NAR) applied to calculate the COI charge for this allocation period. Applicable only when component_type is COI. Derived from the policy's mortality table, age, risk class, and policy year.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP)

description: Classification of the policy component to which this allocation amount is applied. BASE_PREMIUM covers the core policy premium; COI (Cost of Insurance) is the mortality charge for UL/IUL/VUL products; RIDER_PREMIUM covers supplemental benefit riders (ADB, AWL, CAR, LTC); EXPENSE_LOAD is the administrative expense charge; APL_REPAYMENT is repayment of an Automatic Premium Loan; ACCOUNT_VALUE_FUNDING is the net amount credited to the policyholder's account value for UL/IUL/VUL.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro component allocation module

description: The date from which the premium allocation provides coverage or account value credit for the associated policy component. Used to align premium allocation with the billing period and policy anniversary calculations.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; Policy Administration System

description: The date through which the premium allocation provides coverage for the associated policy component. Defines the end of the billing period covered by this allocation.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; Policy Administration System

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated amount. Typically USD for domestic US life insurance policies. Required for multi-currency policy support and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether this premium allocation is eligible for Deferred Acquisition Cost (DAC) capitalization under GAAP accounting. DAC-eligible premiums contribute to the deferral of policy acquisition costs amortized over the expected policy life. Relevant for FASB ASC 944 and LDTI compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 944; LDTI (Long Duration Targeted Improvements); GAAP; ACORD Data Standards

description: The administrative expense charge deducted from the premium payment before crediting to account value for UL/IUL/VUL products. Represents the per-premium or per-policy expense load as defined in the policy contract. Populated when component_type is EXPENSE_LOAD.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) accounting

description: Indicates whether this premium allocation was applied during the policy's grace period. When True, the allocation is a late payment that prevented lapse. Critical for grace period management, lapse administration, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Grace Period Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro grace period management module; State Department of Insurance regulations

description: The date and time when this allocation record was most recently modified, such as during a reversal, adjustment, or status change. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro audit trail

description: The result of the Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) 7-pay test for this premium allocation. PASS = premium does not cause the policy to become a MEC; FAIL = premium causes or contributes to MEC classification; NOT_APPLICABLE = MEC testing not required for this product type. MEC classification has significant tax implications under IRC Section 7702A.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; NAIC MEC Guidelines; IRS Revenue Ruling; Policy Administration System

description: The scheduled modal premium amount for the policy at the time of this allocation, representing the total premium due for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly). Used to validate that the total allocation equals the expected modal premium.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro modal billing module; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) used in the COI charge calculation for this allocation period, representing the difference between the death benefit and the policy account value. Applicable for UL/IUL/VUL products when component_type is COI.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP)

description: Indicates whether the premium payment associated with this allocation was returned due to Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF). When True, the allocation is typically reversed and the policy may enter a grace period. Supports NSF handling and lapse administration workflows.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Sapiens BillingPro NSF handling module; NAIC Grace Period Model Regulation

description: The payment instrument or method used to remit the premium payment from which this allocation was derived. EFT_ACH = Electronic Funds Transfer via ACH; CHECK = paper check; CREDIT_CARD = credit/debit card; WIRE = wire transfer; DIRECT_BILL = direct billing to policyholder; LIST_BILL = employer or group list billing.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Sapiens BillingPro payment processing module

description: The externally-known policy contract number associated with this allocation. Denormalized for operational reporting and reconciliation without requiring a join to the policy table.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Policy Data Standards; Policy Administration System

description: The policy year (1-based integer) in which this premium allocation falls, calculated from the policy issue date. Used for COI rate determination, MEC 7-pay test tracking, DAC amortization, and actuarial experience studies.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; Actuarial Standards of Practice (ASOP)

description: Classification of the insurance product type associated with the policy for this allocation. Determines applicable allocation logic: UL/IUL/VUL products require COI and expense load splits; WL and TERM products allocate entirely to base premium. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|YRT|TERM|SPIA|FIA|ANNUITY — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Product Classification; NAIC Universal Life Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether this premium allocation is associated with a policy reinstatement payment, where a lapsed policy is being restored to in-force status. Reinstatement allocations may include back-premium and interest charges.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Reinstatement Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro reinstatement processing module; State Department of Insurance regulations

description: Indicates whether a portion of the premium associated with this allocation has been ceded to a reinsurer under an active reinsurance treaty. When True, the corresponding reinsurance premium cession will be recorded in the reinsurance domain.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; SAP Statutory Accounting Principles; Reinsurance Management System

description: Code indicating the reason this allocation was reversed when allocation_status is REVERSED. NSF = Non-Sufficient Funds return; PAYMENT_ERROR = incorrect payment amount; POLICY_CANCEL = policy cancelled retroactively; BILLING_CORRECTION = billing system correction; DUPLICATE = duplicate payment reversal; REINSTATEMENT_REVERSAL = reinstatement declined. Null when allocation has not been reversed.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro reversal processing module; NAIC Premium Accounting Guidelines

description: Industry-standard code identifying the specific rider to which this allocation applies when component_type is RIDER_PREMIUM. ADB = Accidental Death Benefit; AWL = Automatic Waiver of Premium; CAR = Company Action Rider; LTC = Long-Term Care; DI = Disability Income; GMDB/GMWB/GMIB/GMAB = Guaranteed Minimum benefit riders; WP = Waiver of Premium. Null when component_type is not RIDER_PREMIUM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADB|AWL|CAR|LTC|DI|GMDB|GMWB|GMIB|GMAB|WP — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Rider Classification; Sapiens BillingPro rider premium module

description: Code identifying the operational source system that originated this premium allocation record (e.g., SAPIENS_BILLING, FAST_PAS, MAJESCO_BILLING). Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and ETL audit trails in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy; SOX Section 404 data lineage requirements

description: Indicates whether this premium allocation is associated with a tax-qualified plan (e.g., 401(k), IRA, pension plan). Tax-qualified allocations may have different contribution limits, tax treatment, and regulatory reporting requirements under ERISA and IRS regulations.
reference: IRC Section 72; ERISA; DOL Fiduciary Standards; IRS Publication 590; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each premium billing demand record in the Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing system. Serves as the primary key for the premium_bill data product.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Premium bills are issued against billing accounts. Each bill represents a demand for payment from a billing relationship entity. While premium_bill already links to policy and party, the billing_accou

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Premium bills generate regulatory billing notices, lapse warnings, and payment statements that must be retained for compliance and customer service. Real business process: state insurance law complian

Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Premium bills are issued for premium-paying annuity contracts. Currently premium_bill only has in_force_policy_id for life policies. A direct annuity_contract_id FK supports annuity billing statement
Reference to the life insurance policy for which this billing demand is generated. Links the bill to the master policy record in the Policy Administration System.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: State DOI regulations require premium bills to identify the insured. Waiver-of-premium billing notices and disability waiver activation are insured-specific. Customer service bill lookup by insured is

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each premium bill generates journal entries for premium revenue recognition and accounts receivable. Core accounting process linking billing operations to general ledger. Required for financial statem

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Premium bills are issued by a specific legal entity, required for multi-entity carrier financial reporting, intercompany billing reconciliation, and state insurance department regulatory filings. Insu

Foreign key linking to billing.list_bill_group. Business justification: For group list billing, a premium_bill is issued to the list_bill_group (employer/group administrator) rather than to individual policyholders. premium_bill has group_bill_indicator (boolean) but no d

Reference to the mailing or billing address record used for physical notice delivery for this billing demand. May differ from the policyholder's residential address when a separate billing address has been designated.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 615

description: Reference to the policyholder (owner) responsible for premium payment. Supports billing address resolution, communication preferences, and regulatory notice delivery requirements.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation 615

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Bills are generated per product plan billing rules (modal premium, grace period, notice requirements). Required for billing accuracy validation, product-level revenue reporting, regulatory compliance

Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Premium bills are generated from premium schedules. Each bill should reference the master schedule that defines the payment plan, modal frequency, and billing rules. This enables tracing bills back to

Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Premium bills require producer attribution for commission allocation, servicing responsibility tracking, and producer performance reporting. Essential for commission processing and producer book-of-bu

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Bills generated after a rate change must reference the authorizing rate filing for regulatory audit and consumer notice compliance. Market conduct exams routinely verify that billed amounts align with

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Premium bills must comply with state-mandated notice language, grace period disclosures, and billing format requirements. state_of_issue is a denormalized state reference; replacing with FK to state_r

The Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) for this billing demand, calculated as the annualized value of the modal premium (e.g., monthly premium × 12). Used for new business production reporting, agent commission calculations, and actuarial valuation inputs.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; ACORD Life Data Standards; IFRS 17

description: The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) advanced against the policy's cash surrender value (CSV) to cover the unpaid premium for this billing cycle. Null or zero when APL was not triggered.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro APL Module

description: Indicates whether an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered for this billing cycle (True) because the premium was not paid and the policy has sufficient cash surrender value (CSV) to fund the loan. APL prevents lapse by borrowing against the policy's cash value.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro APL Module

description: The portion of the modal premium attributable to the base policy coverage, excluding rider premiums and Cost of Insurance (COI) charges. Supports premium allocation reporting across policy components.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SAP Accounting Principles

description: The date on which the billing demand was generated and issued by the billing system. Represents the business event timestamp for the billing cycle demand. Used for billing cycle reconciliation and regulatory notice timeline tracking.
reference: ACORD 103 Life Premium Notice; NAIC Model Regulation 615

description: Externally visible, human-readable billing demand reference number assigned by the billing system (e.g., BL-2024-00012345). Printed on billing notices and used by policyholders and agents to reference a specific billing cycle demand.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; ACORD 103 Life Premium Notice

description: Current lifecycle state of the billing demand. 'issued' = bill generated and sent; 'paid' = full payment received; 'partial' = partial payment received; 'lapsed' = policy lapsed due to non-payment; 'cancelled' = bill voided; 'reinstated' = policy reinstated after lapse.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615 (Grace Period); Sapiens BillingPro Status Codes

description: The total amount demanded in this billing notice, which may differ from modal_premium_amount when outstanding balances, NSF fees, reinstatement arrears, or rider adjustments are included. This is the amount printed on the billing notice sent to the policyholder.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; ACORD 103 Life Premium Notice; NAIC Model Regulation 615

description: Sequential number identifying the billing cycle within the policy year (e.g., 1 for the first monthly bill, 12 for the last). Used for billing cycle reconciliation, APE calculations, and identifying the position of this demand within the annual premium schedule.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; ACORD Life Data Standards

description: The modal billing frequency elected by the policyholder, determining how often premium demands are generated. Drives Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations (e.g., monthly modal factor = 1/12 of annual premium).
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; Sapiens BillingPro

description: The last day of the coverage period for which this billing demand applies. Defines the end of the modal billing interval and is used for pro-rata premium calculations on mid-term changes.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro

description: The first day of the coverage period for which this billing demand applies. Together with billing_period_end_date, defines the modal billing interval (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual).
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; Sapiens BillingPro

description: The Cost of Insurance (COI) charge component within the billed amount, applicable to Universal Life (UL), Indexed Universal Life (IUL), and Variable Universal Life (VUL) policies. Represents the mortality charge deducted from the account value for the billing period.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The timestamp when this premium bill record was first created in the billing system or ingested into the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for billing cycle processing.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this billing record (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency policy support and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Life Data Standards; GAAP ASC 944

description: External reference number or tracking identifier confirming successful delivery of the billing notice (e.g., USPS tracking number, email message ID, ACH trace number). Serves as the audit trail for regulatory proof-of-notice compliance.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Proof-of-Notice Requirements; ACORD 103

description: Indicates whether delivery of the billing notice has been confirmed (True) or is unconfirmed/failed (False). For mail, confirmation may be via USPS tracking; for email, via read receipt or delivery receipt; for EFT, via ACH acknowledgment.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Proof-of-Notice Requirements

description: The date on which the billing notice was dispatched to the policyholder via the selected delivery method. Used to establish the regulatory notice timeline (e.g., state DOI mandated days-before-lapse notice requirements).
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Notice Requirements

description: The channel through which the billing notice was delivered to the policyholder or group administrator (e.g., mail, email, EFT notice, SMS, agent portal). Supports delivery preference management and regulatory proof-of-notice requirements.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Electronic Notice Rules; ACORD 103

description: The contractual date by which premium payment must be received to keep the policy in force without entering the grace period. Derived from the policy anniversary date and modal billing frequency.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation 615; ACORD Life Data Standards

description: Masked bank account number used for EFT/ACH premium collection, retaining only the last four digits for identification and audit purposes (e.g., XXXX1234). Full account number is stored in the secure payment vault and never persisted in the lakehouse.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The last date by which premium payment can be received before the policy lapses. Calculated as the due date plus the state-mandated or contractual grace period (typically 30 or 31 days for life insurance). Drives lapse administration and regulatory notice obligations.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Grace Period Requirements; IRC Section 7702

description: Indicates whether this billing demand is part of a group billing arrangement (True), where the bill is sent to a group administrator or employer rather than an individual policyholder (False). Drives group vs. individual billing workflow routing.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Group Life Model Regulation

description: The scheduled premium amount due for the current billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly). Represents the gross base premium before any adjustments, credits, or APL offsets. Core component of Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; IRC Section 7702

description: Classification of the billing communication type associated with this record. Drives regulatory notice compliance tracking per state DOI mandated timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_bill|reminder|grace_warning|final_notice|lapse_notice|reinstatement_offer|nsf_notification|apl_notification — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Notice Requirements; ACORD 103

description: Fee assessed on this billing demand as a result of a prior Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment event. Included in the billed_amount when applicable. Zero or null when no NSF event has occurred for this billing cycle.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; State DOI Fee Regulations; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The number of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment events recorded against this billing demand. Used to determine NSF fee escalation, EFT suspension thresholds, and fraud risk scoring.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Sapiens BillingPro; State DOI Regulations

description: The cumulative unpaid premium balance carried forward from prior billing cycles at the time this bill was generated. A non-zero value indicates the policyholder has arrears. Used in lapse determination and reinstatement calculations.
The payment instrument or mechanism elected for this billing demand (e.g., EFT/ACH, check, credit card, direct bill, payroll deduction). Drives EFT/ACH processing workflows and NSF risk assessment.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Sapiens BillingPro; ACORD Life Data Standards

description: The policy year (1, 2, 3, ...) in which this billing demand falls, measured from the policy issue date. Used for DAC amortization, persistency analysis, and first-year vs. renewal premium classification in GAAP (ASC 944) and SAP reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 944; GAAP; SAP; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Reference code or citation identifying the specific state Department of Insurance (DOI) regulation or NAIC model regulation that mandates the notice type and timeline for this billing communication (e.g., 'CA-INS-10113', 'NAIC-MOD-615'). Supports regulatory audit and compliance reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Notice Requirements; SOX Compliance

description: The total past-due premium amount required to reinstate a lapsed policy, representing the sum of all unpaid modal premiums from the lapse date to the reinstatement date. Included in billed_amount when reinstatement_required is True.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Reinstatement Requirements

description: Indicates whether this billing demand is associated with a reinstatement offer following a policy lapse (True). When True, the billed_amount includes arrears premiums and any applicable reinstatement fees required to restore the policy to in-force status.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 615; State DOI Reinstatement Requirements; Sapiens BillingPro

description: The aggregate premium amount attributable to all policy riders (e.g., Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Waiver of Premium, Long-Term Care (LTC) rider) included in this billing demand. Supports rider-level premium allocation and profitability analysis.
reference: ACORD Life Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: The native bill identifier assigned by the originating billing system (Sapiens BillingPro or Majesco Billing) before lakehouse ingestion. Preserved for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.
reference: Sapiens BillingPro; Majesco Billing; ETL Data Lineage Standards

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to this premium bill record, capturing status changes (e.g., issued → paid, issued → lapsed), payment postings, or notice delivery confirmations. Essential for change data capture (CDC) and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the premium schedule record. Primary key for this entity in the Billing and Premium Collection System (Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing).

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Premium schedules define billing arrangements that are managed by billing_account entities. The billing_account aggregates policies and manages payment collection. Currently premium_schedule has denor

Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: For annuity contracts, the contribution/premium schedule is driven by the annuitant's age, RMD required begin date, payout option, and benefit base. Annuity billing operations require direct annuitant
