Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: For UL and variable products, the premium schedule must reference the COI rate schedule to compute cost-of-insurance deductions. Actuarial billing reconciliation and in-force management reports requir

Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: A premium_schedule governs billing cadence for flexible premium deferred annuities. Currently premium_schedule references in_force_policy_id for life policies but has no annuity_contract_id. Direct li

Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: For indexed and fixed annuity products, the premium schedule must reference the crediting strategy (cap rate, participation rate, index type) to validate minimum premium requirements and support illus

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Premium schedules determine which GL account receives premium revenue postings. Essential for revenue recognition and financial reporting under GAAP/SAP. Links billing setup to chart of accounts struc

Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Premium schedule amounts, waiver-of-premium activation, flat extra premiums, and limited-pay periods are all insured-specific underwriting outcomes. Billing operations and waiver-of-premium processing

Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: Premium schedules for life insurance must comply with IRC 7702 (guideline annual premium, seven-pay limits). The irc7702_parameter defines these limits per plan version. This link enables automated ME

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Premium schedules are issued under a specific legal entity (insurer of record), essential for SAP/GAAP segmentation, multi-entity carrier statutory reporting, and state-of-domicile regulatory filings.

Reference to the list billing group when billing_method is 'list_bill'. Identifies the employer, association, or group sponsor responsible for consolidated premium remittance on behalf of multiple policyholders.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Group Life Insurance Regulation

description: Reference to the employer entity when billing_method is 'payroll_deduction'. Identifies the employer responsible for deducting and remitting premiums from employee payroll. Applicable to employer-sponsored life insurance plans subject to ERISA.
reference: ERISA; DOL ERISA regulations; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Premium schedules are configured according to product plan specifications (modal frequencies, grace periods, APL eligibility, billing methods). Essential for billing system configuration, product prof

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Premium schedules are governed by a specific plan version, not just the plan, because rate tables and IRC 7702 parameters are version-specific. In-force policies continue on their original version whe

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Premium schedules implement rates from state-approved policy forms. Billing configuration must reference the approved form number to ensure premiums charged match DOI-approved rates. Required for rate

Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Premium schedule generation requires the approved rate table to validate modal premium amounts, face amount bands, and COI rates. Actuarial billing reconciliation and regulatory audits require traceab
Reference to the insurance policy for which this premium schedule is defined. Links the billing schedule to the master policy record in the Policy Administration System.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Premium schedules need writing producer attribution for commission calculations, persistency tracking, and producer book-of-business reporting. Standard requirement for producer compensation systems a

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Premium schedules are built from DOI-approved rates. When a rate filing is approved, affected premium schedules must be updated and traceable to the authorizing filing. Actuarial and compliance audit

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: The risk class assigned during underwriting determines the premium rate applied in the premium schedule (COI rates, table ratings). Post-issue rating changes require updating the premium schedule. Act

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Premium schedules must comply with state-mandated grace periods, nonforfeiture rules, and billing frequency requirements. Insurers validate schedule parameters (grace_period_days, limited_pay_years) a

Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) owned policies require Crummey notice compliance tied to the trust's premium schedule. Trust EIN drives 1099 tax reporting on premium payments. ILIT billing ope

Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Premium schedules are set based on the underwriting class assigned at issue (preferred, standard, substandard), which determines base premium rates. Billing must reference the underwriting class to va

Free-text or coded description of the reason for the most recent amendment to this premium schedule (e.g., 'modal frequency change', 'face amount increase', 'rider addition', 'reinstatement'). Populated when schedule_version > 1. Supports audit trail and policyholder service history.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) representing the annualized value of the modal premium. Calculated as the modal premium multiplied by the modal frequency factor (e.g., monthly × 12). Used for new business reporting, sales performance measurement, and actuarial valuation inputs per industry APE conventions.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement; IFRS 17

description: Indicates whether the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) feature is currently active on this schedule. When True, the billing system has been configured to execute APL draws upon premium non-payment. Distinct from apl_eligible which reflects product eligibility.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the policy is eligible for Automatic Premium Loan (APL) processing. When True, the billing system will automatically trigger a policy loan to cover an unpaid premium before the policy lapses, provided sufficient cash surrender value (CSV) exists. Applicable to whole life and universal life products.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Act; IRC Section 7702; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The annualized premium attributable to the base policy coverage only, excluding rider premiums. Used for premium allocation across policy components (base vs. riders) and for Cost of Insurance (COI) calculations in universal life products.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The day of the month on which the premium billing notice is generated and/or the EFT/ACH debit is initiated. Valid values are 1 through 28 (capped at 28 to avoid month-end variability). Stored in the billing system as the recurring debit/invoice trigger date.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NACHA ACH Operating Rules

description: The method by which premiums are collected from the policyholder. 'direct_bill' sends invoices directly to the insured; 'eft_ach' uses Electronic Funds Transfer / Automated Clearing House (EFT/ACH) for automatic bank debits; 'payroll_deduction' applies to employer-sponsored plans; 'list_bill' is used for group or association billing; 'bank_draft' is a pre-authorized bank withdrawal. Drives payment processing workflow in Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The policyholder's preferred channel for receiving premium billing notices and payment reminders. 'paper' sends physical mail; 'email' sends electronic notices; 'sms' sends text message reminders; 'portal' delivers notices via the insurer's online self-service portal; 'none' suppresses notices (e.g., for EFT/ACH auto-pay enrollees).
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; State Department of Insurance e-delivery regulations

description: The timestamp when this premium schedule record was first created in the billing system. Represents the business event time of schedule establishment, which typically coincides with policy issuance or a subsequent schedule amendment. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this premium schedule (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency policy administration and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date on which this premium schedule becomes binding and the first premium is due. Aligns with the policy issue date or the date of a schedule amendment (e.g., modal frequency change, face amount change).
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 7702

description: Date on which this premium schedule ends. Null for open-ended schedules (e.g., whole life). Populated for term policies at the end of the premium-paying period or upon policy maturity.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Number of days after the premium due date during which the policy remains in force without payment. Standard grace period is 30 or 31 days per NAIC model regulations. After expiry of the grace period without payment, the policy enters lapse administration processing.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Act; State Department of Insurance regulations

business_glossary_term: Guideline Annual Premium (GAP) — Guideline Premium Test (GPT)
description: The Guideline Annual Premium (GAP) used in the Guideline Premium Test (GPT) under IRC Section 7702 to determine whether the policy qualifies as life insurance for tax purposes. Calculated by the actuarial system. Premiums paid must not exceed the greater of the GAP or the Guideline Single Premium (GSP).
reference: IRC Section 7702; IRS regulations; Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: The date on which the policy lapsed due to non-payment of premium beyond the grace period. Null for active schedules. Populated by the lapse administration module of the billing system. Critical for lapse experience studies and regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Act; State Department of Insurance regulations; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date through which the policy is paid up based on the most recent premium payment received. Used to determine the paid-to date for policy servicing, lapse calculations, and reinstatement eligibility.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Number of years over which premiums are payable for limited-pay policies (e.g., 10-pay, 20-pay, paid-up at 65). Null when premium_paying_period is not 'limited_pay'. Used to calculate the schedule expiry date and for MEC testing under IRC Section 7702A.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the policy has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. 'non_mec' policies retain favorable tax treatment; 'mec' policies are subject to LIFO tax treatment on distributions; 'pending_test' indicates the 7-pay test is in progress. Critical for tax compliance reporting to the IRS.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS Publication 575; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The multiplier applied to the annual premium to derive the modal premium amount for the selected billing frequency. Reflects the cost of installment billing (e.g., monthly factor ≈ 0.0875, quarterly ≈ 0.26, semi-annual ≈ 0.51, annual = 1.0). Stored for audit and recalculation purposes.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: The payment frequency selected by the policyholder for premium remittance. Determines the billing cycle and the modal premium amount applied. Standard life insurance modal frequencies per ACORD and NAIC conventions.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The scheduled premium amount due at each billing interval as determined by the selected modal frequency. Reflects the base policy premium plus any rider premiums and modal loading factor. This is the gross amount billed to the policyholder per billing cycle.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The date on which the next scheduled premium payment is due from the policyholder. Updated by the billing engine after each successful payment or following a reinstatement. Critical for grace period tracking and lapse administration.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Cumulative count of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment events on this premium schedule. Used to assess payment reliability, trigger EFT/ACH suspension, and support fraud detection and AML monitoring per FinCEN BSA requirements.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; FinCEN BSA/AML requirements; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The fee charged to the policyholder per Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment event, as defined in the policy contract and state regulations. Used for billing reconciliation and policyholder account management.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; State Department of Insurance regulations

description: The date through which the policy coverage is fully funded by premiums received. Distinct from last_paid_date in that it reflects the coverage period end rather than the transaction date. Used in policy servicing, reinstatement, and lapse administration workflows.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Defines the duration over which premiums are payable under the policy contract. 'whole_life' requires premiums for the life of the insured; 'limited_pay' specifies a fixed number of years; 'single_premium' is a one-time payment; 'term_to_age' requires premiums until a specified attained age. Drives schedule expiry and Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) testing.
reference: IRC Section 7702; IRC Section 7702A (MEC); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which a lapsed policy's premium schedule was reinstated following payment of overdue premiums and satisfaction of reinstatement requirements. Null for schedules that have never lapsed. Used in reinstatement processing and regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Life Insurance Act; State Department of Insurance regulations

description: The annualized premium attributable to all policy riders combined (e.g., Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Waiver of Premium, Long-Term Care (LTC) rider). Supports premium allocation reporting and rider-level profitability analysis.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Externally-known business identifier for this premium schedule, as assigned by the Billing and Premium Collection System (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro). Used in policyholder correspondence, billing notices, and reconciliation with payment records.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle state of the premium schedule. Drives billing engine behavior: 'active' schedules generate billing notices; 'suspended' schedules are on hold (e.g., waiver of premium); 'lapsed' indicates non-payment past grace period; 'reinstated' follows successful reinstatement processing; 'cancelled' or 'terminated' are end-states. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|lapsed|reinstated|cancelled|terminated — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Sapiens BillingPro lifecycle states

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for this premium schedule record. Incremented each time the schedule is amended (e.g., modal frequency change, face amount change, rider addition/removal). Supports audit trail and temporal data management in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The maximum cumulative premium that can be paid in the first seven policy years without triggering Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) classification under IRC Section 7702A. Calculated by the actuarial system and stored here for billing compliance monitoring.
reference: IRC Section 7702A; IRS Publication 575; Actuarial Standards of Practice

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this premium schedule record was sourced (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro, Majesco Billing). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and Silver Layer data quality management in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 404

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this premium schedule record in the billing system. Updated on any field change including status transitions, payment date updates, and schedule amendments. Used for incremental ETL processing and audit trail in the Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the Waiver of Premium (WP) benefit is currently active on this schedule, typically triggered by the insured's disability. When True, scheduled premiums are waived and the billing engine suspends premium collection while keeping the policy in force.
reference: NAIC Model Disability Income Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which the Waiver of Premium (WP) benefit became effective for this schedule. Null when waiver_of_premium is False. Used to track the duration of the waiver period and for actuarial experience studies.
reference: NAIC Model Disability Income Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each bonus payment disbursement record. Primary key for the bonus_payment data product.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Reference to the bonus or incentive program under which this payment was awarded, linking to the program definition including eligibility rules, tiers, and payout schedules.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Reference to the bonus_qualification record that triggered this payment, enabling reconciliation between qualification events and actual disbursements.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Bonus payments are compensation expenses requiring GL entries for accrual accounting, tax withholding, and cash disbursement. Incentive compensation must be journalized separately from base commission

Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: bonus_payment is a bonus/incentive award disbursement to an agent or broker. The recipient is a payee in the commission domain. bonus_payment currently references primary_bonus_producer_id (cross-doma
Reference to the payment run batch in which this bonus disbursement was processed, enabling batch-level reconciliation and audit.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Reference to the agent or broker receiving this bonus payment. Used for multi-level hierarchy payout calculations and 1099 tax reporting.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Agent Hierarchy Module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: Reference to the original bonus_payment record that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only for reversal or adjustment transactions, enabling full audit chain of payment corrections.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; SOX Internal Controls

description: Reference to the commission statement on which this bonus payment appears. Links the disbursement to the agent-facing statement for reconciliation and dispute resolution.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: The unique trace number assigned by the originating financial institution for ACH payment transactions. Used for payment tracing, reconciliation, and dispute resolution with the agent's bank.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; Federal Reserve ACH Network

description: Date on which the bonus payment was formally approved for disbursement by the authorized approver.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Approval workflow status for the bonus payment prior to disbursement. Payments above defined thresholds may require manager or finance approval before processing.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; SOX Internal Controls

description: Name or identifier of the manager or finance officer who approved this bonus payment for disbursement. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for compensation approvals.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Indicates whether IRS backup withholding was applied to this bonus payment due to missing or incorrect TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) on file for the agent.
reference: IRS Publication 15; IRC Section 3406; IRS Form W-9

description: Last four digits of the agent's bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement. Stored for payment verification and audit purposes without retaining full account details.
reference: PCI DSS Data Security Standard; NACHA Operating Rules

description: The qualifying production amount (e.g., APE, NBV, or premium volume) upon which the bonus calculation was applied. Represents the agent's measured performance metric for the qualification period.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; ACORD Data Standards

description: The metric upon which the bonus amount was calculated. Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) and New Business Value (NBV) are common bases for life insurance production bonuses; policy_count and points are used for contest-based programs.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; ACORD Data Standards

description: The percentage or multiplier applied to the basis amount to calculate the gross bonus. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5%). Derived from the applicable bonus program tier.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Classification of the bonus award by its business purpose. Production bonuses reward new business volume (NBV); persistency bonuses reward policy retention; recruiting bonuses reward agent growth; contest bonuses are time-limited incentive campaigns; override bonuses are hierarchy-based; special bonuses are discretionary awards.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; NAIC Producer Compensation Disclosure

description: Check number assigned when the bonus payment is disbursed via physical check. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Timestamp when this bonus payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bonus payment amount (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.
Amount of federal income tax withheld from the gross bonus payment, applied when backup withholding is required or elected. Reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC for the applicable tax year.
reference: IRS Publication 15; IRS Form 1099-NEC; IRC Section 3406 (Backup Withholding)

description: The total bonus amount earned before any tax withholding, adjustments, or deductions. Represents the full incentive award as calculated by the bonus program rules.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: The agent's position in the distribution hierarchy at the time of this bonus payment. Determines override bonus eligibility and multi-level payout calculations. MGA = Managing General Agent, GA = General Agent, BGA = Brokerage General Agency.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Hierarchy Management Module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: Indicates whether this bonus payment must be included in the agent's IRS Form 1099-NEC filing. Payments to incorporated entities (corporations) may be exempt from 1099 reporting under IRS rules.
reference: IRS Form 1099-NEC; IRC Section 6041; IRS Publication 15-A

description: The actual amount disbursed to the agent after deducting federal and state tax withholding from the gross bonus amount. This is the amount reflected on the agent's commission statement and payment.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or context related to this bonus payment. Used by commission administrators for exception handling and dispute documentation.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: The actual date on which the bonus payment was disbursed to the agent or broker. Used for 1099 tax year assignment and cash flow reporting.
reference: IRS Form 1099-NEC; Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: The disbursement method used to deliver the bonus payment to the agent. ACH and direct deposit are most common for recurring payments; wire for large awards; check for agents without electronic banking setup.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this bonus payment disbursement, used for reconciliation, agent statements, and 1099 tax reporting. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera incentive tracking module.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: Current lifecycle state of the bonus payment disbursement. Tracks progression from pending approval through paid or cancelled/reversed states.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: End date of the performance measurement period during which the agent qualified for this bonus. Together with qualification_period_start, defines the full evaluation window.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Start date of the performance measurement period during which the agent qualified for this bonus. Defines the window of production activity evaluated for eligibility.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Free-text description of the reason for reversing or cancelling a previously issued bonus payment. Common reasons include policy lapse, qualification recalculation, agent termination, or data correction.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: The originally scheduled date for disbursement of the bonus payment, before any processing delays or holds. Enables variance analysis between scheduled and actual payment dates.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this bonus payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: The native identifier of this bonus payment record in the originating operational system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.
reference: Sapiens Commission / Annocera Incentive Tracking Module; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: Amount of state income tax withheld from the gross bonus payment, based on the agent's state of residence or the state's withholding requirements. Supports state-level 1099 reporting.
reference: State Department of Revenue Regulations; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: The calendar tax year to which this bonus payment is attributed for IRS 1099-NEC reporting purposes. Determines the tax reporting period for the agent's compensation.

The Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) — either SSN or EIN — on file for the agent at the time of payment. Masked for display (e.g., XXXXX1234). Required for IRS 1099-NEC reporting and backup withholding determination.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRC Section 6109; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: Timestamp when this bonus payment record was last modified. Tracks changes to payment status, amounts, or approval state for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each bonus qualification tracking record. Primary key for the bonus_qualification data product.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Reference to the specific bonus or incentive program for which qualification is being tracked (e.g., annual sales contest, quarterly production bonus, convention qualifier).
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: Foreign key linking to commission.compensation_contract. Business justification: Bonus qualification tracking is tied to the specific compensation_contract governing an agent's or broker's bonus eligibility. The compensation_contract defines bonus_eligible status, ape_threshold, a
Reference to the agent's position within the distribution hierarchy (e.g., agent, GA, MGA, BGA) at the time of qualification tracking, which may determine applicable bonus tier.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — hierarchy management module

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Qualifications are measured against product-specific thresholds (APE, NBV vary by product). Product_line exists but plan_id FK enables accurate product-specific threshold lookup and qualification meas
Reference to the agent or broker whose progress toward bonus qualification is being tracked. Links to the agent master record in the agent domain.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — agent licensing module

description: Free-text description of the reason for any manual adjustment applied to this qualification record. Required when manual_adjustment_flag is True. Supports audit and dispute resolution.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the agent has simultaneously satisfied all mandatory qualification thresholds (APE, policy count, NBV, and persistency where applicable) as of the as_of_date. True triggers downstream bonus payment processing.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The ratio of cumulative APE to the APE threshold, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75% attainment). Used for real-time progress tracking and agent motivation dashboards.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

business_glossary_term: Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) Gap to Threshold
description: The remaining APE production the agent must generate to reach the APE threshold by the end of the qualification period (ape_threshold minus cumulative_ape). Negative values indicate the threshold has been exceeded. Used in agent coaching and motivation reporting.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The minimum APE production level the agent must achieve within the qualification period to satisfy the APE component of the bonus program. Sourced from the bonus program's qualification schedule.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The business date through which cumulative production metrics in this record have been calculated. Enables point-in-time snapshots for real-time incentive tracking and agent motivation reporting.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The incentive tier level the agent is currently tracking toward or has achieved within the bonus program (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum, diamond). Higher tiers correspond to greater production thresholds and larger bonus payouts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bronze|silver|gold|platinum|diamond — promote to reference product if tiers vary significantly by program]
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The distribution channel through which the agent operates (e.g., career, independent, BGA, MGA, bank, direct). Bonus program eligibility and thresholds may vary by channel.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — hierarchy management module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: The timestamp when this bonus qualification record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; FASB ASC 944; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Total Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) accumulated by the agent within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. APE normalizes single and regular premium business to an annualized basis (single premium / 10 + regular premium) and is the primary production metric for bonus qualification in life insurance.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Total New Business Value (NBV) generated by the agent within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. NBV represents the present value of future profits from new policies and is used in value-based bonus programs tied to profitability rather than volume.
reference: IFRS 17; FASB ASC 944; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Total number of new business (NB) policies issued and in-force that the agent has placed within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. Used as a secondary qualification metric alongside APE.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the agent has formally disputed the qualification status or production metrics recorded in this record. When True, the record is placed under review and bonus payment may be held pending resolution.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: The date on which a formal dispute regarding this qualification record was resolved. Null if no dispute has been filed or if the dispute remains open.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The date on which the agent was determined to have failed to meet one or more mandatory qualification thresholds, resulting in disqualification from the bonus program for the period.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: The primary reason the agent was disqualified from the bonus program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: persistency_below_threshold|ape_shortfall|policy_count_shortfall|compliance_violation|license_lapse|e_o_violation|voluntary_withdrawal — promote to reference product]
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards; DOL ERISA fiduciary standards

description: The projected bonus payout amount the agent would receive if current qualification status is maintained through the end of the period. Calculated based on current tier attainment and the bonus program's payout schedule. Used for agent motivation and financial accrual purposes.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; FASB ASC 944 (DAC and commission accruals)

description: Indicates whether any production metrics or qualification status in this record have been manually adjusted by a commission administrator (e.g., to correct a data error or apply a business exception). Triggers audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: The minimum NBV the agent must generate within the qualification period to satisfy the NBV component of the bonus program. Applicable only to programs that include a value-based qualification criterion.
reference: IFRS 17; FASB ASC 944; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Indicates whether the agent is eligible to receive hierarchy-based override bonuses in addition to direct production bonuses, based on their position in the distribution hierarchy (e.g., GA, MGA, BGA overrides on downline production).
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — hierarchy management module

description: The last date of the bonus qualification measurement window. Production activity after this date is not counted toward the current period's thresholds.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The first date of the bonus qualification measurement window during which the agent's production activity is counted toward threshold attainment.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The proportion of the agent's in-force policies that have remained active (not lapsed or surrendered) during the qualification period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9200 = 92%). Persistency is a key quality-of-business metric used in bonus qualification to discourage churning.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance Illustrations; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: The minimum persistency rate the agent must maintain during the qualification period to satisfy the persistency component of the bonus program. Falling below this threshold may result in disqualification.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance Illustrations; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: The remaining number of new business policies the agent must place to reach the policy count threshold (policy_count_threshold minus cumulative_policy_count). Negative values indicate the threshold has been exceeded.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The minimum number of new business policies the agent must place within the qualification period to satisfy the policy count component of the bonus program.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The insurance product line(s) whose new business production counts toward this bonus qualification (e.g., life, annuity, disability, long_term_care, all). Some bonus programs are product-line specific.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Estimated date by which the agent is projected to meet all bonus qualification thresholds based on current production pace. Used in agent motivation reporting and coaching conversations.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: The actual date on which the agent satisfied all required thresholds and achieved qualified status for the bonus program. Null if qualification has not yet been achieved.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: Externally visible business identifier for this bonus qualification record, used in agent statements, incentive reports, and dispute resolution (e.g., BQ-2024-00123456).
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module

description: Current lifecycle state of the agent's bonus qualification for this program and period. 'in_progress' indicates the period is active and thresholds not yet met or missed; 'qualified' indicates all thresholds have been satisfied; 'disqualified' indicates a threshold was permanently missed or a compliance violation occurred; 'pending_review' indicates manual review is required; 'forfeited' indicates the agent voluntarily or contractually relinquished eligibility.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; NAIC Producer Licensing Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this bonus qualification record originated (e.g., SAPIENS_COMMISSION, ANNOCERA). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); ACORD Data Standards

description: The timestamp when this bonus qualification record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every recalculation of cumulative metrics or status change.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; FASB ASC 944; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the commission payee record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the payee master entity.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: In life insurance, commissions are frequently paid to agency entities rather than individual producers. A payee can represent an agency (not just a producer). Linking payee to agency enables agency-le

Cross-reference identifier linking this payee to the corresponding agent record in the agent domain. Enables operational linkage between the financial disbursement identity (payee) and the licensing/appointment/hierarchy record (agent). Nullable for non-agent payees such as corporate BGAs or TPAs with no direct agent record.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Producer Licensing Model

description: Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Payee has w9_certified and w9_certification_date fields requiring the actual W-9/W-8 document on file. IRS backup withholding rules and FINRA recordkeeping require insurers to retain and reference the

Bank account number at the payee's financial institution for ACH direct deposit of commission payments. Required when payment_method is ACH. May be checking or savings account. Stored in encrypted/tokenized form per PCI DSS requirements. Only the last four digits are displayed in operational interfaces.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; PCI DSS v4.0; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements

description: Type of bank account used for ACH commission payment deposits. Checking accounts are most common for business payees; savings accounts may be used by individual agents. Required for ACH payment file generation per NACHA formatting standards.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ACORD Data Standards

description: Nine-digit ABA routing transit number identifying the payee's financial institution for ACH direct deposit of commission payments. Required when payment_method is ACH. Validated against the Federal Reserve's routing number database. Stored in encrypted form per PCI DSS requirements.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ABA Routing Number Standard; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Indicates whether the payee is eligible to receive commission advances — upfront payment of future expected commissions against new business production. Eligibility is typically based on production history, chargeback record, and contractual agreement. When True, the commission system may process advance payments subject to advance repayment tracking.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); NAIC Producer Licensing Model

description: Maximum outstanding commission advance balance permitted for this payee at any point in time. Expressed in the payee's payment currency. Enforced by the commission management system to limit financial exposure from advance chargebacks. Null if advance_eligible is False.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); SOX Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether the payee is subject to mandatory IRS backup withholding on commission payments. Set to True when the IRS notifies the company of a TIN mismatch, when the payee fails to certify their TIN on Form W-9, or when the payee has underreported income. When True, the payment processing system must withhold the statutory backup withholding rate from all reportable payments.
reference: IRC Section 3406; IRS Publication 1281; IRS CP2100 Notice

description: Name of the financial institution where the payee holds the account designated for commission payment deposits. Used for payment reconciliation, remittance advice generation, and ACH return resolution. Stored for operational reference alongside the routing and account numbers.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ACORD Data Standards

description: Payee's preferred delivery method for periodic commission statements. Electronic = PDF delivered via secure portal or email; Paper = printed statement mailed to mailing address; Both = electronic and paper. Drives statement generation and distribution workflows in the commission management system.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: FINRA-assigned Central Registration Depository (CRD) number for payees who are registered representatives or broker-dealers selling variable life insurance or variable annuity products. Required for FINRA regulatory reporting and suitability compliance for variable product commissions.
reference: FINRA Rules; SEC Regulation Best Interest (Reg BI); FINRA BrokerCheck

description: Timestamp when the payee record was first created in the commission management system. Represents the business onboarding event time, not the ETL load time. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and payee lifecycle analytics.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Data Model

description: Trade name or DBA name under which the payee operates, if different from the legal name. Common for BGAs, MGAs, and GAs operating under a brand name distinct from their legal entity name. Used on commission statements and correspondence.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model; State Department of Insurance Requirements

description: Date on which the payee record became active and eligible to receive commission payments. Typically corresponds to the date the payee's contract with the company was executed and all onboarding requirements (W-9, KYC, banking) were satisfied.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Producer Licensing Model; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Primary email address for the payee used for electronic commission statement delivery, payment notifications, W-9 re-certification requests, and compliance communications. For entity payees, this is typically the accounts payable or finance contact email.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; GDPR Article 4 (if applicable); CAN-SPAM Act

description: Position of the payee within the multi-level commission hierarchy used for override commission calculations. Determines eligibility for first-year commission (FYC), renewal commissions, and hierarchy-based override payments. Writing Agent = direct producer; Field Manager = first-level override; Regional Manager = second-level override; BGA/MGA = wholesale distribution override levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: writing_agent|field_manager|regional_manager|bga|mga|home_office|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Producer Licensing Model; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Current Know Your Customer (KYC) verification status for the payee as required by FinCEN AML/BSA regulations. Verified = identity confirmed and AML screening passed; Pending = verification in progress; Failed = identity verification or AML screening failed, payments blocked; Exempt = entity type exempt from KYC requirements (e.g., publicly traded corporations). Payments must not be processed to payees with Failed KYC status.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements; USA PATRIOT Act Section 326; NAIC AML Model Regulation

description: Date on which the payee's KYC identity verification and AML screening was most recently completed and approved. Used to determine KYC currency and trigger periodic re-verification workflows per the company's AML compliance program.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements; USA PATRIOT Act; NAIC AML Model Regulation

description: Full legal name of the payee as registered with the IRS and state authorities. For individuals, this is the name on the W-9 form. For entities, this is the registered business name. Used for 1099 tax reporting, ACH payment files, and check issuance. Must match the name associated with the TIN on file.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Publication 1220; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements

description: Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the payee holds their primary insurance producer license. Used to validate appointment status and commission payment eligibility per state-specific insurance regulations.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model; State Department of Insurance Requirements

description: Primary street address line for mailing commission checks, 1099 tax forms, and commission statements to the payee. For individuals, this is typically a home or office address. For entities, this is the registered business address.
reference: IRS Publication 1220 (1099 mailing requirements); USPS Addressing Standards

description: Secondary address line for suite, apartment, unit, floor, or PO Box information for the payee's mailing address. Optional field used when the primary address line is insufficient to fully specify the delivery location.
City component of the payee's mailing address. Used for check mailing, 1099 form delivery, and commission statement distribution.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the payee's mailing address. Defaults to USA for domestic payees. Required for international wire transfer payees and cross-border distribution arrangements. Drives FATCA and FBAR reporting obligations for foreign payees.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IRS FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); FinCEN FBAR Requirements

description: ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the payee's mailing address. Used for check delivery, 1099 form mailing, and geographic analytics on distribution channel coverage.

Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the payee's mailing address. Used for state income tax withholding determination, state-level 1099 reporting, and compliance with state Department of Insurance requirements.
reference: USPS State Abbreviations; NAIC State Reporting Requirements; IRS Publication 1220

description: NAIC-assigned National Producer Number (NPN) uniquely identifying the payee as an insurance producer across all US states. Used for cross-state license verification via the NAIC NIPR database, appointment validation, and regulatory reporting. Mandatory for all licensed producer payees.
reference: NAIC National Producer Number (NPN) Standard; NAIC NIPR Database; State Department of Insurance Requirements

description: Date on which the payee was most recently screened against OFAC sanctions lists. Used to enforce periodic re-screening requirements and ensure ongoing compliance with US sanctions regulations before each payment run.
reference: OFAC SDN List; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements; 31 CFR Part 501

description: Indicates whether the payee has been screened against the OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list and other sanctions lists. Must be True before any commission payment is disbursed. Payments to OFAC-listed entities are prohibited by US law.
reference: OFAC SDN List; USA PATRIOT Act; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements; 31 CFR Part 501

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier assigned to the payee by the Agent and Commission Management System (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera). Used on commission statements, 1099 forms, and payment remittance advices to uniquely identify the payee across operational systems.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; IRS Publication 1220 (1099 Reporting)

description: Distribution channel role of the payee within the life insurance and annuity sales ecosystem. Drives commission schedule selection, hierarchy override eligibility, and regulatory reporting. BGA = Brokerage General Agency; MGA = Managing General Agent; GA = General Agent; RIA = Registered Investment Advisor; TPA = Third Party Administrator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agent|broker|bga|mga|ga|ria|tpa|broker_dealer|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model; FINRA Rules (for broker-dealers); DOL ERISA Fiduciary Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the payee record in the commission management system. Active = eligible to receive commission payments; Inactive = no current production but record retained; Suspended = payments on hold pending investigation or compliance review; Pending = onboarding in progress, W-9 or banking not yet verified; Terminated = permanently closed, no further payments.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model; SOX Internal Controls; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements

description: Legal entity classification of the payee for tax reporting and payment processing purposes. Determines W-9 federal tax classification, 1099 reporting obligations, and backup withholding rules. Individual = natural person agent/broker; Corporation = incorporated entity; Partnership = general or limited partnership; LLC = limited liability company; Trust = ILIT or other trust entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporation|partnership|llc|trust|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRC Section 3406 (Backup Withholding); IRS Publication 1220

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for commission payment disbursements to this payee. Defaults to USD for domestic US payees. May be CAD or other currencies for cross-border distribution arrangements. Drives currency conversion logic in the commission payment run.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ACORD Data Standards

description: Preferred method for disbursing commission payments to this payee. ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic funds transfer (most common); Check = paper check mailed to payee address; Wire = domestic or international wire transfer for large or international payees. Drives payment run processing logic in the commission management system.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules (ACH); Federal Reserve Regulation J (Wire); ACORD Data Standards

description: Primary contact phone number for the payee. Used for payment issue resolution, W-9 certification follow-up, and commission dispute communications. For entity payees, this is the main business phone number.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act)

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this payee record was sourced (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera, Salesforce Financial Services Cloud). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and issue resolution in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; SOX Data Lineage Requirements

description: Native primary key or unique identifier of this payee record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Sapiens Commission internal payee ID, Annocera agent code). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system for reconciliation and issue resolution.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; SOX Data Lineage Requirements

description: Primary state insurance producer license number for the payee. For individual agents and brokers, this is the license number in their primary state of appointment. For entity payees (BGAs, MGAs), this is the entity license number. Used to validate commission payment eligibility — commissions may only be paid to licensed producers per state insurance regulations.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model; State Department of Insurance Requirements; NAIC NIPR Database

description: IRS 1099 form type classification for annual tax reporting of commission and fee payments to this payee. 1099-NEC = non-employee compensation for individual agents/brokers; 1099-MISC = miscellaneous income for certain entity payees; 1099-R = distributions from retirement/annuity contracts; Exempt = corporations exempt from 1099 reporting per IRS rules; Not Reportable = payments below IRS reporting threshold or otherwise excluded.
reference: IRS Publication 1220; IRC Section 6041; IRS Form 1099-NEC; IRS Form 1099-MISC

description: Date on which the payee's commission payment eligibility was terminated. Null for active payees. After this date, no new commission payments are processed except for renewal commissions or chargebacks governed by the payee's contract terms. Used in conjunction with payee_status to manage the full payee lifecycle.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Producer Licensing Model; State Department of Insurance Requirements

description: IRS-issued Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) used for 1099 tax reporting and backup withholding determination. For individuals, this is the Social Security Number (SSN); for entities, this is the Employer Identification Number (EIN). Stored in masked or encrypted form per PCI/PII data protection requirements. Must match the name on the W-9 certification.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRC Section 3406; IRS Publication 1220; FinCEN AML/BSA; HIPAA (if applicable)

description: Indicates whether the TIN on file is a Social Security Number (SSN) for individual payees or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for entity payees. Drives IRS 1099 form type selection (1099-NEC vs 1099-MISC) and backup withholding logic.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Publication 1220; IRC Section 3406

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the payee record in the commission management system. Tracks changes to banking details, W-9 status, KYC status, payment preferences, or any other payee attributes. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; ACORD Data Standards; NAIC Data Model

description: Date on which the payee's most recent IRS Form W-9 was signed and certified. Used to determine W-9 currency and trigger re-certification workflows when the form is stale or when payee information changes (e.g., name change, TIN change).
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Publication 1281; SOX Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether the payee has submitted a valid, signed IRS Form W-9 certifying their TIN and federal tax classification. Payments to payees without a certified W-9 are subject to mandatory backup withholding at the IRS-prescribed rate. Required before any commission disbursement can be processed.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRC Section 3406 (Backup Withholding); IRS Publication 1281

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the commission statement record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Commission statements are issued by a specific legal entity, determining the 1099 reporting entity, state tax withholding jurisdiction, and intercompany payable attribution. Multi-entity insurers requ

Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: A commission statement is issued to a specific payee. payee is the commission-domain master for all statement recipients. statement currently references producer_id (cross-domain to agent.producer) bu
Reference to the payment run batch that generated and funded this commission statement. Links the statement to the disbursement cycle.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — commission calculation module

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Commission statements contain sensitive producer financial data (SSN, earnings, bank details); breaches must be tracked as privacy incidents. Business process: incident response for statement data exp
Reference to the agent, broker, BGA, MGA, or GA for whom this commission statement is issued. Drives multi-level hierarchy payout calculations.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — agent licensing module

description: Amount withheld from this statement to recoup previously advanced commissions that have not yet been earned. Reduces net payable amount. Tracks outstanding advance balances per agent.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); GAAP ASC 944-40

description: Classification of the payee entity type: Agent (captive or independent), Broker, Brokerage General Agency (BGA), Managing General Agent (MGA), or General Agent (GA). Determines applicable commission schedule and hierarchy override rules.
reference: NAIC Producer Licensing Model Act; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Name or user identifier of the commission operations manager or system process that approved this statement for payment. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302 and 404; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Date and time when the commission statement was formally approved for payment processing. Null if not yet approved. Part of the SOX-compliant approval audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 302 and 404; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Indicates whether IRS backup withholding at the statutory rate (24%) was applied to this statement due to a missing, incorrect, or flagged TIN. True = backup withholding applied; False = no withholding required.
reference: IRS IRC Section 3406; IRS Form 1099-NEC; FinCEN AML/BSA

description: Total bonus and incentive compensation earned during the statement period, including production bonuses, persistency bonuses, and sales contest awards. Tracked separately from base commissions for incentive program reporting.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera) — incentive tracking module; IRS Form 1099-NEC

description: Date and time when this commission statement record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field used for data lineage and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this statement (e.g., USD). Life insurance operations are predominantly USD; multi-currency support required for international business.
reference: ISO 4217; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency Matters

description: Channel through which the commission statement is delivered to the agent: email, agent portal (self-service), physical mail, EDI transmission to BGA/MGA systems, or suppressed (no delivery). Tracks statement distribution compliance.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); ACORD data standards

description: Current delivery status of the commission statement to the agent: Pending (not yet sent), Delivered (successfully transmitted), Failed (delivery attempt unsuccessful), Acknowledged (agent confirmed receipt via portal).
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Date and time when the commission statement was successfully delivered to the agent via the designated delivery channel. Null if delivery is pending or failed.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Last date by which the agent may file a formal dispute against the commission amounts on this statement, per the contractual dispute window (typically 30–90 days from statement date). Enforces dispute eligibility rules.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); NAIC producer agreement standards

description: Indicates whether the agent has initiated a formal dispute against any commission amounts on this statement. True = dispute filed; False = no dispute. Triggers dispute workflow in the commission management system.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); NAIC producer compensation dispute resolution guidelines

description: Periodicity at which this statement is generated for the agent. Determines the cadence of commission payment cycles (e.g., monthly for renewal commissions, bi-weekly for FYC).
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera)

description: Portion of gross commissions attributable to first-year commission (FYC) earnings on new business (NB) policies issued during the statement period. Key metric for new business value (NBV) tracking and DAC amortization.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); GAAP ASC 944-40 DAC; NAIC

description: Total gross commission earned by the agent during the statement period before any chargebacks, adjustments, or withholdings. Includes first-year commissions (FYC), renewal commissions, and override commissions.
reference: Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission; Annocera); GAAP ASC 944-40 (Deferred Acquisition Costs); NAIC

description: Numeric level of the agent within the distribution hierarchy (e.g., 1 = writing agent, 2 = GA, 3 = MGA, 4 = BGA). Used to apply tiered override commission rates in multi-level payout calculations.

description: Indicates whether the net paid amount on this statement contributes to the agent's IRS Form 1099-NEC reportable compensation for the tax year. True if the agent is a non-employee and cumulative YTD payments meet or exceed the IRS reporting threshold ($600).

description: Distribution channel for which this rate applies: career = captive career agents; broker = independent brokers; bga = Brokerage General Agency (BGA); mga = Managing General Agent (MGA); ga = General Agent (GA); bank = bank distribution; direct = direct-to-consumer; tpa = Third Party Administrator (TPA). description: Number of months during which a commission chargeback applies if the policy lapses or is surrendered. After this period, the commission is considered fully earned. Critical for lapse administration and commission recovery.

description: Percentage of the original commission subject to chargeback upon policy lapse or surrender within the chargeback period. May be a full 100% recapture or a graded percentage depending on the schedule. Stored as a decimal fraction.

description: Describes the commission compensation design: heaped = large FYC with low renewals; level = uniform rate across all policy years; hybrid = combination of heaped and level; graded = rate steps down over defined policy years.

description: Timestamp when this commission rate row was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the commission rate (e.g., USD). Required for flat_dollar_amount rows and for multi-currency operations.

Indicates whether commissions under this rate row qualify as Deferred Acquisition Costs (DAC) for GAAP accounting purposes under FASB ASC 944 (LDTI). True = commission is deferrable as DAC; False = expensed immediately. Critical for actuarial reserving and financial reporting.

description: Commission rate applicable to premium paid above the target premium level for UL/IUL products. Typically lower than the target premium rate. Stored as a decimal fraction. Null if not applicable to this product type.

description: Date after which this commission rate row is no longer applicable to new calculations. Null indicates the rate is open-ended with no scheduled expiry. Supports rate versioning and schedule amendments.
