description: The date on which the AML/KYC screening was performed for this beneficiary designation. Required for regulatory audit trail under FinCEN AML/BSA compliance. Null if screening was not required.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act

description: The AML/KYC screening status of the designated beneficiary at the time of this designation event. Insurers are required under FinCEN AML/BSA rules to screen beneficiaries, particularly for large face amount policies and irrevocable designations. 'flagged' triggers a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) review.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act; AML (Anti-Money Laundering) Standards

description: Classifies whether the beneficiary is a natural person, a legal entity, or a special designation. Drives downstream tax withholding rules (e.g., trusts and estates have different IRC Section 72 treatment), KYC requirements, and claims payment processing workflows.
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation; IRS Publication 590-B; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The legal or familial relationship of the designated beneficiary to the insured or contract owner. Used for AML/KYC screening, insurable interest validation, tax reporting (spousal rollover eligibility under IRC Section 72), and claims adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|grandchild|domestic_partner|trust|estate|charity|employer|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; NAIC Model Regulation; AML/BSA FinCEN; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The business reason or triggering life event that prompted the beneficiary designation change. Used for actuarial experience studies, compliance monitoring, and customer lifecycle analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_policy|marriage|divorce|death_of_beneficiary|birth_of_child|court_order|owner_request|policy_assignment|adoption|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Free-text description of any special conditions or contingencies attached to this beneficiary designation (e.g., 'if beneficiary survives insured by 30 days', 'subject to divorce decree dated MM/DD/YYYY'). Supports complex designation structures and court-ordered conditions.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Probate Law; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit record creation time in the silver layer, distinct from the business effective date and the received date. Required for SOX audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The channel or legal instrument through which the beneficiary designation was submitted. 'paper_form' is a wet-ink signed ACORD or proprietary form; 'e_signature' is a digitally signed electronic form; 'court_order' is a legally mandated designation (e.g., divorce decree, QDRO); 'will_reference' designates the estate; 'online_portal' is a self-service digital submission; 'phone_request' is a voice-recorded request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper_form|e_signature|court_order|will_reference|online_portal|phone_request — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; ESIGN Act; UETA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Externally-known business identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST) to uniquely reference this designation transaction. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and claims adjudication workflows.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro)

description: Current lifecycle state of this designation transaction. 'pending' indicates the form has been received but not yet processed; 'active' indicates the designation is in force; 'superseded' indicates a newer designation has replaced this one; 'revoked' indicates the owner formally withdrew the designation; 'rejected' indicates the form was rejected (e.g., NIGO); 'cancelled' indicates administrative cancellation.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Classification of the beneficiary's priority tier and revocability. 'primary' beneficiaries receive proceeds first; 'contingent' receive proceeds if all primary beneficiaries predecease the insured; 'tertiary' is a third-tier designation; 'irrevocable' designations cannot be changed without the beneficiary's written consent; 'revocable' designations may be changed by the owner at any time.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which this beneficiary designation becomes legally binding and in force on the policy. Typically the date the completed, good-order form is received and accepted by the insurer. Critical for claims adjudication to determine which designation was active at the time of the insured event.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: The full legal name of the court-appointed or designated guardian or custodian for a minor beneficiary. Required when minor_beneficiary_flag is True to ensure proceeds can be legally disbursed. Populated only for minor beneficiaries.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; UTMA/UGMA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether this beneficiary designation is irrevocable, meaning the policy owner cannot change or remove the beneficiary without the beneficiary's written consent. Irrevocable designations are common in divorce settlements, ILIT structures, and collateral assignments. Drives policy servicing workflow restrictions.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Insurance Law; ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) Standards

description: Flags whether the beneficiary designation form was received in a Not In Good Order (NIGO) condition — i.e., missing required information, unsigned, or otherwise deficient. NIGO forms require follow-up with the policy owner before the designation can be processed and made effective.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro; FAST)

description: Two-letter US state code of the jurisdiction governing this beneficiary designation, typically the state where the policy was issued or the insured resides. Determines applicable state insurance law requirements for designation validity, notarization, and beneficiary rights.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation; ISO 3166-2:US

description: Indicates whether the designated beneficiary is a minor (under age 18 or applicable state age of majority). Minor beneficiaries require a guardian or custodian to receive proceeds, triggering special claims payment workflows and UTMA/UGMA account considerations.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; UTMA/UGMA; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Free-text or coded description of the specific deficiency that caused the designation form to be flagged as NIGO. Examples include missing beneficiary SSN/TIN, missing owner signature, illegible form, or missing notarization. Populated only when is_nigo is True.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Not In Good Order (NIGO) Resolved Date
description: The date on which all NIGO deficiencies were remediated and the designation form was accepted as complete and in good order. Null if the form has not yet been resolved or was not NIGO. Used to measure NIGO resolution cycle time for operational reporting.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which the beneficiary designation form was notarized, if notarization was required. Null if notarization was not required or has not yet been completed. Used to validate the legal completeness of the designation.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the designation form required notarization under applicable state law, policy contract terms, or court order. Notarization is commonly required for irrevocable designations and court-ordered changes.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: A serialized summary of the beneficiary designation(s) that were in force immediately before this designation change was processed. Stored as a structured text snapshot (e.g., JSON-encoded summary) to support audit trail requirements and claims adjudication without requiring a full historical join. Captures prior beneficiary name, type, allocation percentage, and effective date.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; SOX Section 404; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date the designation change was fully processed and recorded in the Policy Administration System. Represents the completion of the administrative workflow, distinct from the received date and the effective date.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date the beneficiary designation form or request was physically or electronically received by the insurer or its TPA. May differ from effective_date if the form required NIGO remediation or administrative processing time.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or administrative comments entered by the processing representative regarding this beneficiary designation event. Examples include notes on court order interpretation, special payment instructions, or follow-up actions required.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro; FAST)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this designation record originated. 'PAS' = Policy Administration System (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro); 'CRM' = CRM and Customer Engagement Platform; 'PORTAL' = online self-service portal; 'TPA' = Third Party Administrator submission; 'PAPER' = paper form manually keyed; 'ANNUITY' = Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; ETL Data Lineage Standards

description: Indicates whether this beneficiary designation triggered a STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) review. STOLI arrangements involve designating a stranger or investor as beneficiary to circumvent insurable interest requirements. Flagged designations require compliance review and may result in policy rescission.
reference: NAIC STOLI Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) Standards

description: The date on which this designation was superseded by a subsequent designation change. Null if the designation is currently active or was revoked/rejected. Enables point-in-time reconstruction of the beneficiary designation history for audit and claims adjudication.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks the most recent update to the record for change data capture, audit trail, and data quality monitoring purposes.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the designation form required a witness signature under applicable state law or policy contract terms. Certain states and irrevocable designations mandate witnessed signatures for legal validity.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

Version identifier of the AML/BSA compliance program ruleset or screening configuration in effect at the time of this verification. Enables audit traceability when program rules are updated.

description: Indicates whether the beneficial owner(s) of the policy or annuity contract (e.g., trust beneficiaries, ILIT trustees) were identified and verified as part of this KYC event, as required under FinCEN's Beneficial Ownership Rule for legal entity customers.
reference: FinCEN Beneficial Ownership Rule 31 CFR 1010.230; FATF Recommendation 24; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Date and time when this KYC/AML verification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record retention tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; NAIC Record Retention Guidelines

description: Indicates whether Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) is required for this party based on the outcome of this verification event. EDD is mandated for high-risk parties, PEPs, and parties from high-risk jurisdictions under FATF and FinCEN guidelines.
reference: FATF Recommendation 19; FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Date on which this KYC/AML verification record expires and re-verification is required. Distinct from next_review_date in that expiry_date represents the hard compliance deadline, while next_review_date may be an earlier scheduled review.
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881; FATF Recommendation 10

description: The expiration date of the identity document used for verification. Expired documents may trigger re-verification requirements under CIP rules.
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the government authority that issued the identity document. Used for OFAC screening and cross-border AML risk assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; OFAC SDN List; FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220

description: Type of government-issued identity document presented or verified during the identity proofing process. Required under FinCEN Customer Identification Program rules.
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Two-letter US state or territory code (ISO 3166-2 US subdivision) of the regulatory jurisdiction governing this verification event. Determines applicable state Department of Insurance (DOI) requirements and reporting obligations.
reference: NAIC State DOI Regulations; ISO 3166-2; FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements

description: The specific KYC program tier under which this verification was conducted. CIP (Customer Identification Program) is the baseline; CDD (Customer Due Diligence) is the standard ongoing program; EDD (Enhanced Due Diligence) applies to high-risk parties; ongoing_monitoring covers periodic re-screening.
reference: FinCEN CDD Rule 31 CFR 1010.230; FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; FATF Recommendations

description: Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau (MIB) inquiry was performed as part of this verification event. MIB checks are used in life insurance underwriting to detect undisclosed medical conditions or prior application activity.
reference: MIB Group Data Standards; HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR 164; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Indicates whether the MIB inquiry returned a coded record match for the party, suggesting prior insurance application activity or a reportable medical condition. Triggers underwriting review when True.
reference: MIB Group Data Standards; HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR 164; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic KYC/AML re-verification of this party, determined by the assigned risk rating and AML program policy. High-risk parties require more frequent review cycles.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements; FATF Recommendation 10; NAIC Model Regulation 881

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Match Flag
description: Indicates whether the party's name or identifiers returned a potential match against the OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list or other OFAC sanctions lists during this verification event.
reference: OFAC SDN List; 31 CFR Part 501; FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Match Score
description: Numeric confidence score (0.00–100.00) returned by the OFAC screening engine indicating the degree of similarity between the party's identity data and a potential SDN list match. Used to distinguish true positives from false positives.
reference: OFAC SDN List Screening Best Practices; FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements

description: The role of the policyholder party being verified in relation to the insurance contract. Determines the applicable KYC/AML requirements and risk thresholds for the verification.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 881; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the party has been identified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) — a current or former senior government official, military officer, or their close associate — requiring enhanced due diligence under FATF and FinCEN guidelines.
reference: FATF Recommendation 12; FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: AML/KYC risk rating assigned to the policyholder party as a result of this verification event. Drives ongoing monitoring frequency, enhanced due diligence requirements, and SAR filing thresholds per FinCEN and NAIC guidelines.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Risk Assessment; FATF Recommendation 10; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Numeric risk score (typically 0–100) assigned by the verification system or third-party bureau to quantify the AML/KYC risk level of the party. Complements the categorical risk_rating for fine-grained risk stratification and analytics.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Risk Assessment; FATF Recommendation 10

description: Indicates whether this KYC/AML verification event has triggered or is associated with a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filing with FinCEN. Drives escalation workflows and compliance reporting obligations.
reference: FinCEN SAR Filing Requirements 31 CFR 1020.320; Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) 31 USC 5318(g)

description: The FinCEN-assigned or internal reference number of the Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filed in connection with this verification event. Populated only when sar_flag is True. Classified as confidential due to legal sensitivity of SAR disclosures.
reference: FinCEN SAR Filing Requirements 31 CFR 1020.320; Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) 31 USC 5318(g)

description: Narrative description of the verified source of funds for premium payments or annuity contributions (e.g., 'Employment income', 'Proceeds from real estate sale', 'Inheritance', '1035 Exchange from prior policy'). Classified as confidential due to financial sensitivity.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements; FATF Recommendation 10; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: Indicates whether the source of funds for premium payments or annuity contributions was verified during this KYC/AML event. Required for high-risk parties and large premium transactions under AML/BSA program requirements.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Program Requirements; FATF Recommendation 10; NAIC Model Regulation 881

business_glossary_term: Social Security Number (SSN) / Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) Verified Flag
description: Indicates whether the party's SSN or TIN was successfully verified against IRS or Social Security Administration records during this verification event. Critical for tax compliance and identity proofing.
reference: IRS TIN Matching Program; FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; IRC Section 6109

description: Indicates whether this verification event identified characteristics consistent with a Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) arrangement, where a policy is originated by a third party with no insurable interest. Triggers enhanced review and potential policy rescission.
reference: NAIC STOLI Model Regulation; State DOI Regulations; ACORD Life Standards

description: The business event that initiated this KYC/AML verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_business|policy_renewal|beneficiary_change|ownership_change|large_transaction|periodic_review|regulatory_request|reinstatement|1035_exchange|annuity_payout — promote to reference product]
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881; FATF Recommendation 10

description: Date and time when this KYC/AML verification record was last modified. Tracks updates to verification status, risk rating, or SAR flag following analyst review or system re-screening.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; NAIC Record Retention Guidelines

description: The calendar date on which the KYC/AML verification event was initiated or performed. Used for regulatory timeliness tracking and compliance reporting.
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881

description: The operational method used to perform the verification. Manual indicates analyst-driven review; automated indicates rules-engine or system-driven check; third_party_bureau indicates use of an external data provider (e.g., LexisNexis, Experian); document_review indicates physical or digital document inspection; biometric indicates fingerprint, facial recognition, or other biometric confirmation.
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881; FATF Recommendation 10

description: Free-text or coded explanation of the reason for the verification outcome, particularly for fail, escalated, or inconclusive statuses. Documents the specific finding (e.g., 'Name mismatch on OFAC SDN list', 'Expired identity document', 'Insufficient documentation provided').
reference: FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220; NAIC Model Regulation 881; FATF Recommendation 10

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this verification event by the verifying system or third-party bureau, used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; FinCEN CIP Rule 31 CFR 1020.220

description: Current lifecycle status of the KYC/AML verification event. Pass indicates successful identity confirmation; fail indicates identity could not be confirmed or a match was found on a watchlist; pending indicates verification is in progress; escalated indicates the case has been referred for manual review or SAR filing; inconclusive indicates insufficient data to render a determination.

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the KYC/AML verification event was executed by the system or analyst. Distinct from verification_date; used for audit trail and SLA measurement.

description: Name or identifier of the system, platform, or third-party bureau that performed or facilitated the verification (e.g., 'LexisNexis Risk Solutions', 'Experian IdentityWorks', 'Salesforce FSC KYC Module', 'Manual Review'). Supports audit trail and vendor management.

business_glossary_term: Coding Accuracy Support System (CASS) Return Code

business_glossary_term: Coding Accuracy Support System (CASS) Validated Indicator

business_glossary_term: Coding Accuracy Support System (CASS) Validation Date
description: The date on which the address was last validated through the USPS CASS certification process. CASS certifications expire periodically; this date is used to determine whether re-validation is required for regulatory notice mailings.

description: US Census Bureau census tract code for the address location. Used for demographic analytics, Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) compliance reporting, actuarial territory studies, and market penetration analysis.

description: Municipality or city name of the address. Used in conjunction with state and ZIP code for USPS validation, state DOI jurisdiction determination, and regulatory notice delivery.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Required for international policyholders, ILIT trust addresses, and AML/KYC country-of-residence determination. Defaults to 'USA' for domestic policies.

description: County or parish name within the state. Used for local tax jurisdiction determination, actuarial territory assignments, and certain state-level regulatory reporting requirements. May be null for non-US addresses.

description: The timestamp when this address record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation.

description: Indicates that the party has requested suppression of physical mail to this address, or that the address has been flagged as undeliverable for mailing purposes. description: The two-letter state code identifying the state Department of Insurance (DOI) with regulatory jurisdiction over this party based on their address of record. Used to determine applicable state insurance regulations, required policy forms, mandated notices, and consumer protection rules.

description: USPS Delivery Point Validation (DPV) confirmation code indicating the deliverability status of the address. description: The date on which this address record ceased to be the active address of record. Null for currently active addresses. Used to maintain a complete address history for regulatory compliance, audit trails, and KYC/AML reviews.

business_glossary_term: Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) County Code

description: Indicates whether this address is located outside the United States. Foreign addresses trigger additional AML/KYC review requirements, FATCA reporting obligations, and may affect state DOI jurisdiction determination and premium tax situs.

description: Indicates the precision level of the geocoded coordinates for this address. Used to assess reliability of geographic analytics.

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Populated through geocoding services. Used for actuarial territory rating, catastrophe risk modeling, geographic segmentation analytics, and proximity-based agent assignment.

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Populated through geocoding services. Used in conjunction with latitude for actuarial territory rating, catastrophe risk modeling, and geographic analytics.

business_glossary_term: National Change of Address (NCOA) Move Date

description: The native identifier for this address record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Policy Administration System such as Sapiens LifePro, FAST, or CRM such as Salesforce Financial Services Cloud). description: The timestamp when this address record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer data product. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each contact point record associated with a policyholder party. Serves as the primary key for the party_contact entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

description: Numeric rank indicating the priority order for attempting contact via this channel when multiple contacts of the same type exist for a party. description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. 'bounced' applies to email addresses that have returned delivery failures. 'opted_out' reflects a party's explicit withdrawal from contact via this channel. Drives suppression logic in outreach campaigns.

description: Further qualifies the contact type to distinguish personal contacts from business, trust (ILIT), or estate-related contacts. Supports customer relationship hierarchy management for complex ownership structures.

description: Categorizes the communication channel for this contact record. Drives routing logic for CRM outreach, regulatory notices, billing alerts, and claims correspondence. description: Specifies the business purpose(s) for which this contact point may be used. Enables granular consent and suppression management — for example, a party may allow billing notifications but opt out of marketing. Aligns with GDPR purpose limitation principle.

description: Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.

description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this contact record, derived from verification status, bounce history, and last successful contact date. Used by data stewards and CRM teams to prioritize contact data cleansing efforts.

description: Indicates that the party has explicitly requested no outreach via this contact point. When true, all non-mandatory communications (marketing, service campaigns) are suppressed. description: Date from which this contact record is considered valid and active for use in outreach and regulatory notice delivery. Supports bi-temporal data modeling for contact history tracking.

description: Date on which the party provided or last updated their email opt-in consent. Required for CAN-SPAM and GDPR audit trails. Null if email_opt_in_status is opted_out or unsubscribed.

Indicates the party's email marketing and communication opt-in status for this email contact. Applies only when contact_type is email. Governs CAN-SPAM and CASL compliance for outbound email campaigns and service notifications.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act 15 U.S.C. § 7701; CASL; GDPR Article 7; CCPA

description: Date after which this contact record is no longer considered valid. Null for open-ended active contacts. Used in bi-temporal history management and to enforce contact data retention policies.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation)

description: Indicates whether this contact is the party's overall preferred method of communication across all channels. Distinct from is_primary, which is channel-scoped. Used for CRM campaign targeting and service notification routing.

description: Indicates whether this contact record is the designated primary contact for its channel type. Only one contact per party per contact_type may be flagged as primary. Used by CRM and policy administration systems to determine the default outreach channel.

description: Indicates whether this contact point is approved for delivery of mandatory regulatory notices (e.g., lapse notices, grace period warnings, annual statements, policy anniversary notices) as required by state insurance regulations. A contact may be suppressed for marketing but still eligible for regulatory notices.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance Regulations; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, es-MX, zh-CN) indicating the party's preferred language for communications delivered via this contact channel. Supports multilingual regulatory notice delivery and CRM localization.

description: Date of the most recent delivery failure for this contact point. Used alongside bounce_count to assess contact data quality and determine whether re-verification or deactivation is warranted.

description: Date of the most recent confirmed successful outreach via this contact point (e.g., email delivered without bounce, call answered, SMS delivered). Used to assess contact data quality and trigger re-verification or update workflows.

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context about this contact record, such as special handling instructions, agent-entered remarks, or explanations for non-standard contact configurations. Not used for PII storage.

description: International dialing prefix (e.g., +1 for USA, +44 for UK) for phone and fax contact types. Populated only when contact_type is home_phone, mobile_phone, work_phone, or fax. Supports E.164 number formatting for outbound dialing systems.

description: Optional telephone extension number for work phone contact types, typically used for corporate or group policyholder contacts. Appended after the main phone number during outbound dialing.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same contact appears in multiple source systems. description: The native identifier for this contact record in the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce Contact ID, Policy Administration System contact reference). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ETL lineage tracing.

description: Records the party's consent status under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) for automated calls, robocalls, and text messages to this contact number. Mandatory for mobile_phone contact types. Drives suppression of automated outreach to non-consented numbers.

description: IANA time zone identifier (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago) associated with this contact point. Used by CRM and outbound dialing systems to schedule calls and messages within permissible calling hours per TCPA regulations.

description: Business user ID or system process identifier that last modified this contact record. Supports audit trail and change accountability requirements under SOX and NAIC market conduct standards.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contact record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.

description: Date on which the contact point was most recently verified as reachable and accurate. Used to assess contact data freshness and trigger re-verification workflows when contacts exceed the maximum staleness threshold.

description: Method used to verify this contact point. Supports audit trail for KYC/AML compliance and regulatory notice delivery confirmation. 'third_party' indicates verification via an external data provider such as LexisNexis or Experian.

description: Indicates whether this contact point has been validated through an outreach verification process (e.g., email confirmation link, SMS OTP, phone call confirmation). Supports KYC/AML compliance and ensures regulatory notices reach the intended party.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; FinCEN AML/BSA Requirements; KYC Standards; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the inter-party relationship record within the policyholder domain. Primary key for the relationship data product.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: POA, guardianship, and insurable interest relationships require the authorizing legal document to be directly retrievable for compliance and claims processing. relationship stores legal_doc_reference

Identifier of the second party (object/grantee side) in the relationship. For a power-of-attorney record, this is the attorney-in-fact. For a guardianship record, this is the guardian/custodian. For a spousal relationship, this is the spouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Identifier of the first party (subject/grantor side) in the relationship. For a power-of-attorney record, this is the principal granting authority. For a guardianship record, this is the ward/minor. For a spousal relationship, this is the policyholder.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: FK to policyholder.trust

Indicates whether this relationship record has been flagged for AML review due to complex ownership structures (e.g., ILIT, corporate ownership, foreign trust), high-risk jurisdictions, or unusual transaction patterns. Triggers referral to the AML compliance team.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; Bank Secrecy Act (BSA)

description: Date on which consent was formally obtained from the relevant parties for the establishment and use of this relationship record. Supports HIPAA and state privacy law compliance audit trails.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule; State Privacy Laws; NAIC Privacy Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether explicit consent has been obtained from the relevant parties for the establishment and use of this relationship record, as required by HIPAA, state privacy laws, and GDPR for international policyholders. Particularly relevant for healthcare POA and medical information sharing relationships.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule; State Privacy Laws; GDPR Article 6; NAIC Privacy Model Regulation

description: Date on which the court formally appointed the guardian or conservator for the ward. Establishes the legal commencement of guardianship authority for claims payment and beneficiary management purposes. Null for non-court-appointed custodial arrangements (e.g., UTMA/UGMA).
reference: State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation; Uniform Guardianship and Protective Proceedings Act (UGPPA)

description: Timestamp when this relationship record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Establishes the audit trail for record origination and supports SOX and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ACORD Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Calendar date on which the relationship became legally effective and operative for policy servicing, claims, and financial transaction authorization purposes.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date on which the guardianship or custodianship legally expires, typically corresponding to the ward's age of majority (18 or 21 depending on jurisdiction) or a court-specified termination date. After this date, the ward assumes direct control of policy proceeds and beneficiary designations.
reference: Uniform Transfers to Minors Act (UTMA); Uniform Gifts to Minors Act (UGMA); State Departments of Insurance

description: US state or territory in which the guardianship or custodianship was legally established and is governed. Two-letter USPS state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Determines applicable state law for guardianship authority, age of majority, and UTMA/UGMA account rules.
reference: State Departments of Insurance; Uniform Transfers to Minors Act (UTMA); Uniform Guardianship and Protective Proceedings Act (UGPPA)

description: Classification of the guardianship or custodial arrangement when relationship_subtype is legal_guardian, utma_custodian, or ugma_custodian. Determines the legal framework governing the guardian's authority over the ward's insurance proceeds and beneficiary designations.
reference: Uniform Transfers to Minors Act (UTMA); Uniform Gifts to Minors Act (UGMA); State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Numeric level within the customer or ownership hierarchy at which this relationship exists. Level 1 = direct relationship to the policyholder; higher levels represent indirect relationships (e.g., trustee of an ILIT that owns a policy). Supports ILIT ownership chain modeling and group policy member-to-sponsor hierarchy analytics.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Legal basis establishing insurable interest between the parties, required for policy issuance and anti-STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) compliance. Documents the economic or familial nexus that justifies the insurance relationship. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: familial|financial_dependency|business_partner|creditor_debtor|employer_employee|trust_beneficiary|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; STOLI Model Act; IRC Section 7702; State Departments of Insurance

description: Indicates whether the insurable interest basis has been formally verified and documented during the underwriting or new business application process. Required for STOLI compliance and regulatory audit.
reference: NAIC STOLI Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Standards

description: Indicates whether this is the primary or controlling relationship of its type for the party pair. For example, a policyholder may have multiple beneficiary relationships but only one primary beneficiary. Used in beneficiary designation management and claims adjudication workflows.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the KYC identity verification process has been completed for the relationship parties, particularly for trust ownership, POA, and ILIT structures subject to AML/BSA compliance requirements.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Standards

description: Classification of the legal instrument that establishes or evidences this relationship. Drives document verification workflows and compliance audit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marriage_certificate|birth_certificate|court_order|poa_document|trust_agreement|corporate_resolution|divorce_decree|adoption_order — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Standards; State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date on which the supporting legal documentation was verified and accepted by the company. Establishes the audit trail for compliance with KYC/AML documentation requirements and policy servicing authorization controls.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; KYC Standards

description: Indicates whether the supporting legal documentation for this relationship has been received, reviewed, and verified by the policy servicing or new business team. Unverified relationships may be restricted from certain transaction types pending document receipt.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this relationship record entered by policy servicing staff. Examples include documentation of unusual ownership structures, pending legal proceedings, or special handling instructions for claims.
reference: ACORD Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Functional role of Party A within this specific relationship. Provides semantic context for the relationship direction and governs which policy servicing transactions Party A may initiate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: owner|insured|annuitant|beneficiary|principal|ward|grantor|employer|sponsor — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Functional role of Party B within this specific relationship. Governs the scope of authority, transaction eligibility, and legal standing of Party B in relation to Party A's policies and accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|dependent|trustee|attorney_in_fact|guardian|custodian|corporate_officer|key_person|employee|member — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Scope of authority granted under the power-of-attorney instrument. Full scope permits all policy servicing and financial transactions; limited scope restricts the attorney-in-fact to specific enumerated transactions as documented in the authorization_scope_description field. Null for non-POA relationships.
reference: Uniform Power of Attorney Act (UPOAA); State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Power of Attorney (POA) Authorization Scope Description
description: Free-text description of the specific transactions or authorities granted when poa_authorization_scope is limited. Documents the enumerated powers (e.g., premium payments only, surrender requests, beneficiary changes) for policy servicing and compliance audit purposes.
reference: Uniform Power of Attorney Act (UPOAA); State Departments of Insurance

description: Date on which the power-of-attorney instrument expires and the attorney-in-fact's authority ceases. Null for durable or open-ended POA instruments. System must enforce this date to prevent unauthorized transactions after expiry.
reference: Uniform Power of Attorney Act (UPOAA); State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date on which the principal formally revoked the power-of-attorney instrument, terminating the attorney-in-fact's authority. Null if the POA has not been revoked. Triggers status change to revoked and blocks further transaction authorization.
reference: Uniform Power of Attorney Act (UPOAA); State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Classification of the power-of-attorney instrument when relationship_subtype is power_of_attorney. Durable POA remains effective upon incapacity; limited POA is restricted to specific transactions; healthcare POA covers medical decisions; springing POA activates upon a triggering event. Null for non-POA relationships.
reference: Uniform Power of Attorney Act (UPOAA); State Departments of Insurance; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle state of the relationship record. Drives eligibility for policy servicing transactions, claims authorization, and beneficiary payouts. A terminated or revoked status prevents further use of the relationship for financial transaction authorization.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: High-level classification of the inter-party relationship. Drives downstream processing rules for policy servicing, claims authorization, and beneficiary management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: familial|legal_authorization|fiduciary|business|trust_ownership|group_sponsorship — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this relationship record originated. PAS = Policy Administration System; CRM = Customer Engagement Platform; UW = Underwriting Workbench; CLAIMS = Claims Management System; ANNUITY = Annuity Administration Platform; MANUAL = manually entered by operations staff.
reference: ACORD Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Native identifier of this relationship record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Financial Services Cloud relationship ID, FAST Policy Administration relationship key). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.
reference: ACORD Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Granular classification within the relationship type. Examples include: spouse, domestic_partner, parent, child, sibling, grandparent, trustee, ilit_trustee, corporate_officer, power_of_attorney, legal_guardian, utma_custodian, ugma_custodian, employer_sponsor, key_person. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|domestic_partner|parent|child|sibling|grandparent|trustee|ilit_trustee|corporate_officer|power_of_attorney|legal_guardian|utma_custodian|ugma_custodian|employer_sponsor|key_person — promote to reference product]
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Calendar date on which the relationship was legally terminated, dissolved, or expired. Null for open-ended active relationships. For guardianship, typically the ward's age of majority date.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this relationship record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change data capture, audit trail maintenance, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ACORD Standards; Enterprise Data Governance Policy

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the annuitant role record in the Annuity Administration Platform. Primary key for the annuitant entity within the policyholder domain Silver layer.

Foreign key linking to product.benefit_structure. Business justification: The annuitant's gmab_indicator, gmdb_indicator, gmib_indicator, gmwb_indicator directly correspond to benefit_structure guaranteed benefit flags. The benefit structure defines the formula parameters f

Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI/IFRS17 require annuitants to be grouped into cohorts by issue year and product for GAAP reserve measurement. Each annuitant's cohort determines the locked-in discount rate and initial CSM/LFPB am

Reference to the annuity contract record for which this individual serves as the annuitant. Drives benefit calculations, payout phase administration, and RMD tracking in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Reference to the party supertype record that holds the annuitant's demographic identity (full name, date of birth, gender, SSN/TIN, address). The annuitant role record is a specialization of the party entity.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Annuity suitability reviews assess product appropriateness for the annuitant (age, income needs, liquidity). Compliance teams link reviews to specific annuitants for state insurance department exam de

Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Annuitant mortality classification drives payout calculations, reserve requirements, and COI charges. The annuitant's issue_age, gender, tobacco_status, and mortality_table_code are inputs to underwri

Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Annuity contracts originate from underwriting applications. Linking annuitant to application tracks which underwriting case created the annuitant role, essential for suitability reviews, annuity-speci

Externally-visible business identifier assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity / FAST Annuity) to uniquely identify the annuitant role on a contract. Used in bordereaux reporting and customer correspondence.

The date on which annuity benefit payments commence (annuitization date). Governs the payout phase schedule, RMD calculation start, and GMIB/GMWB benefit base lock-in. Also known as the income start date or annuitization effective date in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS RMD Rules (IRC Section 401(a)(9)); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The type of annuity product associated with this annuitant role. 'FIA' = Fixed Indexed Annuity; 'VA' = Variable Annuity; 'SPIA' = Single Premium Immediate Annuity; 'MYGA' = Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity; 'DIA' = Deferred Income Annuity; 'QLAC' = Qualifying Longevity Annuity Contract. Drives product-specific benefit calculation logic and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIA|VA|SPIA|MYGA|DIA|QLAC — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Notice 2014-66 (QLAC); NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The current guaranteed benefit base amount used for GMIB, GMWB, or GMAB calculations. Represents the notional value upon which guaranteed benefit payments or withdrawal amounts are calculated, distinct from the actual account value. Updated annually or upon step-up elections per the rider terms.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the annuity contract is held in a qualified (tax-deferred) or non-qualified (after-tax) account. 'qualified' includes traditional IRA, 401(k), 403(b); 'non_qualified' is funded with after-tax dollars; 'roth_ira' has distinct RMD and distribution rules; 'inherited_ira' applies to beneficiary-owned contracts. Drives RMD applicability, tax withholding, and 1099-R reporting.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-A/590-B; SECURE Act 2.0

description: The annuitant's age in whole years as of the most recent valuation or processing date. Refreshed periodically by the Annuity Administration Platform for RMD factor lookups, benefit recalculations, and payout phase administration. Stored to avoid repeated real-time computation in batch actuarial runs.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B; IRC Section 401(a)(9); NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43)

description: The annuitant's date of birth as recorded on the annuity contract application. Used directly by the Annuity Administration Platform for mortality table lookups, age-based benefit calculations, RMD required beginning date determination, and GMDB/GMIB/GMWB payout factor selection. Stored here as a role-level denormalization for performance-critical actuarial calculations.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; HIPAA

description: The current death benefit amount payable upon the annuitant's death, reflecting the greater of the account value or the GMDB benefit base (if applicable). Updated at each valuation cycle. Used by the Claims Management System for death claim adjudication and by the Actuarial Valuation System for NAR calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The distribution option elected by the beneficiary for the death benefit payable upon the annuitant's death. 'lump_sum' is a single payment; 'annuitize' converts to an income stream; 'five_year_rule' distributes within 5 years per IRC Section 72(s); 'stretch_ira' uses life expectancy distributions for eligible designated beneficiaries under SECURE Act rules.
reference: IRC Section 72(s); SECURE Act 2.0; IRS Publication 590-B; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date of death of the annuitant as reported and verified by the Annuity Administration Platform. Triggers death benefit processing, payout cessation or continuation (for joint annuitants), beneficiary notification, and claim adjudication workflows. Null for living annuitants.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; State Departments of Insurance

description: The date on which the annuitant's death was officially verified by the insurer through receipt of a death certificate or equivalent documentation. Distinct from the reported death date. Initiates the formal death claim adjudication process and triggers statutory interest accrual on death benefits.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The annuitant's gender as recorded on the contract application. Used in mortality table selection and payout factor calculations where gender-distinct tables are applied. Some states mandate unisex tables; the mortality_table_code field captures the applicable table assignment.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance unisex table mandates; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) rider is active on this annuitant's contract. When True, the contract guarantees the account value will be at least equal to the GMAB benefit base at the end of the accumulation period, regardless of market performance.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) rider is active on this annuitant's contract. When True, the death benefit payable upon the annuitant's death is the greater of the account value or the GMDB benefit base. Drives death benefit calculation logic in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) rider is active on this annuitant's contract. When True, the annuitant may annuitize at a guaranteed minimum benefit base regardless of account value performance. Drives GMIB benefit base tracking and annuitization calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) rider is active on this annuitant's contract. When True, the annuitant is guaranteed a minimum annual withdrawal amount regardless of account value depletion. Drives GMWB benefit base and maximum annual withdrawal calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this annuitant record represents a joint annuitant on a joint-and-survivor annuity contract. When True, the payout continues to the surviving annuitant at the designated continuation percentage after the primary annuitant's death. Drives joint-life payout factor selection in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The annuitant's age in whole years at the time the annuity contract was issued. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot used for benefit base calculations, payout factor selection, and actuarial reserving. Derived from date_of_birth and contract issue date at time of issuance and stored for audit stability.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43)

description: The percentage of the primary annuitant's benefit payment that continues to the joint annuitant (survivor) after the primary annuitant's death. Common values are 50%, 66.67%, 75%, and 100%. Null if is_joint_annuitant is False. Used in joint-life payout factor calculations.
reference: IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The current Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification status for this annuitant. 'verified' indicates all identity checks have passed; 'pending' indicates verification is in progress; 'failed' indicates a verification failure requiring remediation; 'exempt' applies to certain institutional or trust-owned contracts. Required for AML/BSA compliance.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Rules; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act Section 326

description: The date on which the annuitant's KYC identity verification was most recently completed and confirmed. Used for AML compliance tracking and periodic re-verification scheduling.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA Rules; USA PATRIOT Act Section 326; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Code identifying the actuarial mortality table assigned to this annuitant for payout factor calculations and reserve valuation (e.g., '2012 IAR', '2000 CSO', 'UP-1984', 'RP-2014'). Assigned at contract issue and may be updated upon annuitant change. Used by the Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes/AXIS/Prophet) for PBR reserve calculations.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline XLIII (AG43); NAIC Valuation Manual (VM-21); FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The annuity payout option elected for this annuitant's contract. Governs the income stream structure: 'life_only' pays for the annuitant's lifetime only; 'life_period_certain' guarantees payments for a minimum period; 'joint_survivor' continues to a joint annuitant; 'period_certain' pays for a fixed term; 'lump_sum' is a single payment; 'systematic_withdrawal' is a non-annuitized withdrawal schedule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life_only|life_period_certain|joint_survivor|period_certain|lump_sum|systematic_withdrawal — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The number of years guaranteed under a life-with-period-certain or period-certain-only payout option. Common values are 5, 10, 15, or 20 years. Null if the payout option does not include a period-certain guarantee. Used to calculate the minimum benefit obligation and reserve.
reference: IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The timestamp when this annuitant role record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance. Follows the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The timestamp when this annuitant role record was most recently updated in the Silver layer data product. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance. Follows the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The IRS life expectancy table method used to calculate the annuitant's annual RMD amount. 'uniform_lifetime' uses the Uniform Lifetime Table (most common); 'joint_life_expectancy' applies when the sole beneficiary is a spouse more than 10 years younger; 'single_life_expectancy' applies in certain inherited IRA scenarios. Null for non-qualified contracts.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; IRS Life Expectancy Tables (Rev. Proc. 2022-38)

business_glossary_term: Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Required Beginning Date
description: The date by which the annuitant must begin taking Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) from a qualified annuity contract (IRA, 403(b), etc.). Determined by IRS rules based on the annuitant's date of birth and the applicable SECURE Act age threshold (age 73 under SECURE 2.0). Null for non-qualified contracts.
reference: IRC Section 401(a)(9); IRS Publication 590-B; SECURE Act 2.0 (2022); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which this annuitant role became effective on the contract. May differ from the contract issue date if the annuitant was added or changed via a contract endorsement or owner-driven change.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The date on which this annuitant role was terminated, replaced, or became inactive. Null for currently active annuitant roles. Populated upon death, contract surrender, or annuitant change endorsement.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Current lifecycle state of the annuitant role record. 'active' indicates the annuitant is the current measuring life; 'deceased' triggers death benefit processing and payout recalculation; 'replaced' indicates a successor annuitant has been designated; 'cancelled' applies to voided or rescinded contracts.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Classification of the annuitant's role on the contract. 'primary' is the measuring life for payout calculations; 'joint' triggers joint-and-survivor benefit rules; 'contingent' activates if the primary annuitant predeceases; 'successor' applies under certain IRA/qualified plan continuation rules.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72; NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation

description: The native annuitant identifier as assigned by the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Preserved for cross-system reconciliation, ETL lineage, and audit traceability back to the system of record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Code identifying the operational source system from which this annuitant record was ingested (e.g., 'SAPIENS_ANNUITY', 'FAST_ANNUITY'). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: Social Security Number / Taxpayer Identification Number (SSN/TIN)
description: The annuitant's Social Security Number (SSN) or Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) as recorded on the annuity contract. Required for IRS 1099-R tax reporting, RMD compliance, and AML/KYC identity verification. Stored at the role level to support tax reporting workflows independent of the party supertype.
reference: IRC Section 6041; IRS Form 1099-R; FinCEN AML/BSA Rules; NAIC Model Regulation; HIPAA

description: The two-letter US state code of the state in which the annuity contract was issued. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, unisex mortality table requirements, free-look period, surrender charge rules, and state premium tax obligations.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The status of the annuity suitability review conducted for this annuitant at the time of contract issuance or material change. Required under NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation (#275) and applicable state regulations. 'approved' indicates the product was deemed suitable; 'rejected' blocks issuance; 'waived' applies to certain replacement or exchange transactions.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation (#275); DOL Fiduciary Rule; FINRA Rule 2330

description: The annuitant's federal income tax withholding election for annuity payments and distributions. 'withhold' elects mandatory withholding; 'do_not_withhold' opts out where permitted; 'state_only' applies state withholding only. Governs 1099-R reporting and payment processing in the Annuity Administration Platform.
reference: IRC Section 3405; IRS Publication 505; NAIC Model Regulation

description: The elected federal income tax withholding percentage applied to annuity payments and distributions. Applicable when tax_withholding_election is 'withhold'. Null if the annuitant has elected no withholding or if a flat dollar amount is used instead.
Primary key for contract_owner

Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract for which this party holds the owner role. A single policy may have one active owner at a time; history is tracked via ownership transfer flags.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle OIPA)

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: The contract_owner table has an is_also_insured boolean flag indicating when the owner is also the insured on the policy. However, there is no FK to the actual insured role record. Adding insured_id c

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Contract owners hold policies issued by a specific licensed legal entity. SAP/GAAP financial reporting, RBC filings, and state regulatory submissions require attributing contract ownership to the issu

Reference to the party supertype record that holds identity attributes (legal name, SSN/TIN, date of birth, contact details) for this contract owner. Supports individual, trust, corporate, and ILIT ownership structures.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: The servicing producer of record for a contract owner drives policy conservation campaigns, owner-directed service request routing, and producer commission statements. Life insurance operations requir

Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation requires a suitability review for the contract owner (purchaser) before policy issuance. The contract owner is the regulated buyer; this link
FK to policyholder.trust

Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Contract owners are established during application underwriting. Linking owner to originating application tracks ownership from inception, supports STOLI investigations (ownership patterns from applic

Indicates whether an absolute (irrevocable) assignment of ownership has occurred, transferring all ownership rights to a new party. When True, the original owner loses all policy rights and the new owner record becomes active.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk classification assigned to the contract owner based on KYC due diligence, transaction patterns, and OFAC/sanctions screening. Drives enhanced due diligence (EDD) requirements and transaction monitoring thresholds.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; OFAC SDN List; USA PATRIOT Act; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Level of authority the contract owner holds to change beneficiary designations on the policy. 'full' allows unrestricted changes; 'restricted' requires co-owner or irrevocable beneficiary consent; 'none' applies when ownership rights have been assigned.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Legal name of the lender or creditor to whom the policy has been collaterally assigned. Required for death benefit payment processing and CSV surrender authorization when collateral_assignment_flag is True.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date on which the collateral assignment of the policy became effective. Used to determine priority of claims against policy proceeds and to track assignment duration for servicing restrictions.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the policy has been assigned as collateral to a lender or creditor by the contract owner. When True, the assignee has priority rights over the cash surrender value (CSV) and death benefit proceeds up to the loan balance.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Contract owner's preferred method for receiving policy correspondence, statements, and regulatory notices. Drives document delivery routing in the Policy Administration System and CRM platform.
reference: CRM and Customer Engagement Platform (Salesforce Financial Services Cloud); NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance

description: Date on which the contract owner provided consent for electronic document delivery. Required for regulatory audit trails under the NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act and state e-delivery regulations.
reference: NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; E-SIGN Act (15 USC 7001)

description: Indicates whether the contract owner has provided affirmative consent to receive policy documents and regulatory notices electronically, as required by the NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act and applicable state e-delivery laws.
reference: NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; E-SIGN Act (15 USC 7001)

description: Two-letter US state code where the trust, corporation, partnership, or other non-individual entity was legally formed or domiciled. Used for state regulatory compliance and premium tax situs determination.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this ownership record is associated with a tax-free 1035 exchange transaction, where the policy was funded by proceeds from a prior life insurance policy or annuity contract. Triggers specific IRS cost-basis tracking requirements.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 544; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Classification of the legal insurable interest relationship between the contract owner and the insured life. Required at policy issuance to validate that the owner has a legitimate financial interest in the continued life of the insured. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|dependent|business_partner|employer|creditor|trust — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the contract owner is also the annuitant on an annuity contract. Relevant for annuity tax treatment, RMD calculations, and death benefit trigger rules under IRC Section 72.
reference: IRC Section 72; IRS Publication 575; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity; FAST Annuity)

description: Indicates whether the contract owner is also the insured life on the policy. When True, the owner-insured relationship triggers specific tax treatment under IRC Section 7702 and MEC testing rules.
reference: IRC Section 7702; IRS Publication 559; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether this party is the primary (first-named) owner of the policy or annuity contract. The primary owner holds full beneficiary designation authority and receives all policy correspondence.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Current Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification status for the contract owner. Drives AML compliance workflows and may restrict policy servicing transactions until verification is complete.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date on which the contract owner's identity was most recently verified and KYC status was set to 'verified'. Used for AML periodic review scheduling and regulatory audit trails.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; USA PATRIOT Act; NAIC Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Last Screened Date
description: Date on which the contract owner was most recently screened against OFAC sanctions lists. Used to ensure periodic re-screening compliance and to trigger alerts when screening is overdue.
reference: OFAC SDN List; FinCEN AML/BSA; USA PATRIOT Act

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Screening Status
description: Result of the most recent OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and sanctions list screening for the contract owner. 'blocked' status prevents policy issuance and servicing transactions per regulatory requirements.
reference: OFAC SDN List; FinCEN AML/BSA; USA PATRIOT Act; 31 CFR Part 500

description: Externally-known business identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System to uniquely identify this ownership role record. Used in correspondence, 1035 exchange documentation, and collateral assignment notices.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle OIPA); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date on which this party's ownership role became legally effective for the policy or annuity contract. For original owners, this aligns with the policy issue date. For transferred ownership, this is the assignment effective date.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle OIPA); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 1035

description: Date on which this party's ownership role ended, due to absolute assignment, policy surrender, death of owner, or contract termination. Null for currently active ownership records.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Percentage share of ownership held by this party when multiple co-owners exist on a single policy or annuity contract. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%). Must sum to 100.00 across all active owners for a given policy.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702

description: Sequential ordinal indicating the order of ownership for a given policy, used to distinguish primary owner (1) from co-owners (2, 3, etc.) and to order historical ownership records chronologically.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the contract owner role. Transitions from pending (application stage) to active (policy issued), and may move to transferred (absolute assignment) or terminated (policy lapse/surrender/maturity).
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro); NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Reason code for the transfer of policy ownership from a prior owner to this contract owner. Supports 1035 exchange tracking, gift tax reporting, and estate planning audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: absolute_assignment|divorce_decree|court_order|gift|sale|ilit_transfer|estate_transfer|bankruptcy — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 559; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Classification of the legal entity type holding ownership of the policy or annuity contract. Drives KYC/AML requirements, beneficiary designation authority, and tax reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|trust|corporation|ilit|partnership|estate|government|non_profit — promote to reference product]
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulation; IRS Publication 559; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Policy or contract number of the prior life insurance policy or annuity from which a 1035 exchange was executed. Required for IRS cost-basis carryover calculations and exchange documentation.
reference: IRC Section 1035; IRS Publication 544; Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro)

description: Timestamp when this contract owner role record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX compliance for financial data governance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this contract owner role record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this contract owner record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_OIPA). Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle OIPA); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Native identifier for this contract owner record in the originating Policy Administration System (e.g., FAST owner role ID, Sapiens LifePro owner record key). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Policy Administration System (FAST; Sapiens LifePro; Oracle OIPA); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this contract owner relationship has been flagged for Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) review. STOLI arrangements, where a third party with no insurable interest finances a policy, are prohibited in most US states.
reference: NAIC STOLI Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) for the contract owner, which may be a Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for trusts, corporations, and other entities. Required for IRS Form 1099 reporting and AML/KYC compliance.
reference: IRS Publication 1212; IRC Section 6041; FinCEN AML/BSA; HIPAA; NAIC Model Regulation
