description: Status of IRS TIN certification (W-9 or W-8 form) for the contract owner. Drives backup withholding obligations under IRS regulations. 'backup_withholding' indicates the owner is subject to 28% withholding on taxable distributions.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Form W-8BEN; IRC Section 3406; FinCEN AML/BSA

description: Classifies the type of TIN provided by the contract owner: SSN (Social Security Number for individuals), EIN (Employer Identification Number for entities), ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number for non-resident aliens), or foreign (non-US tax identifier).
reference: IRS Publication 1212; IRC Section 6041; FinCEN AML/BSA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the insured-life role record in the Silver layer. Primary key for the insured entity; globally unique across all policies and group certificates.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; NAIC Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to product.benefit_structure. Business justification: The benefit structure assigned to an insured defines their death benefit type, LTC acceleration eligibility, waiver provisions, and nonforfeiture options — directly corresponding to insured attributes

Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI (ASC 944) and IFRS17 require each insured to be assigned to a cohort for GAAP/IFRS reserve measurement. Cohort assignment drives locked-in discount rates, CSM/LFPB calculations, and profitability

Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract under which this insured-life role exists. Links the insured to the Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST) policy record.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; Policy Administration System

description: Reference to the party supertype record that holds demographic identity (legal name, date of birth, gender, SSN/TIN, address). The insured entity is a role record; all identity attributes are owned by the party record.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; NAIC Party Model

description: Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: The mortality table applied to each insured drives their net amount at risk, reserve calculation, and reinsurance cession rate. Underwriters and actuaries must know which approved mortality table gove

Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: The premium rate table used at underwriting is determined by the insured's issue_age, gender, tobacco_status, risk_class, and face_amount band. Regulatory and actuarial audit requirements mandate trac

FK to policyholder.party.party_id — The insured role record must link to the party supertype for demographic resolution. This is the foundational in-domain FK enabling the party-role pattern that the policyholder domain is built on.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: The insured's assigned risk class drives premium calculation, reinsurance cession class, and policy administration. insured.risk_class is a plain text denormalized value that should be a proper FK to

Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Underwriting class assignment is a core life insurance process: the insured's risk profile (age, tobacco, occupation, table_rating) is evaluated to assign an underwriting_class_id. This drives premium

Indicates whether the insured is eligible for the ADB (Accidental Death Benefit) rider, which pays an additional death benefit if death results from a covered accident. Eligibility is determined during underwriting based on age, occupation, and health status.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro; FAST)

business_glossary_term: Activities of Daily Living (ADL) Impairment Count
description: Number of ADLs (Activities of Daily Living) with which the insured requires assistance, as assessed for LTC rider benefit eligibility. Standard ADLs include bathing, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring, and continence. Benefit triggers typically require impairment in 2 or more ADLs.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; HIPAA; Policy Administration System

description: Date on which the APS (Attending Physician Statement) was received from the insured's attending physician. Used to track underwriting cycle time and NIGO resolution. Null if APS was not required or not yet received.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Indicates whether an APS (Attending Physician Statement) was required from the insured's treating physician as part of the underwriting process. Triggered by age, face amount thresholds, or medical history disclosures. Sourced from the Underwriting Workbench.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum); HIPAA

description: Current age of the insured as of the most recent policy anniversary or valuation date, expressed in whole years. Used in COI (Cost of Insurance) calculations for UL/IUL products, NAR (Net Amount at Risk) determination, and ongoing actuarial reserving.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline; SOA Mortality Tables; FASB ASC 944 LDTI

description: Indicates whether the insured has disclosed aviation activity (private pilot, commercial pilot, or frequent flyer in non-commercial aircraft) that constitutes a hazardous avocation. Triggers aviation exclusion riders or flat extra premium charges in underwriting.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the insured's country of citizenship. Used in AML/KYC compliance screening, OFAC sanctions checks, foreign national underwriting guidelines, and FATCA/FBAR reporting obligations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; FinCEN AML/BSA; OFAC; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: Date on which coverage for this insured-life role became effective (policy issue date or certificate effective date for group). Used in actuarial valuation, reserve calculations, and claims adjudication to determine coverage period.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; FASB ASC 944

description: Date on which coverage for this insured-life role ended or is scheduled to end. Null for in-force policies. Populated upon lapse, surrender, maturity, or death claim settlement.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944 LDTI

description: Timestamp when this insured-life role record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under SOX, and ETL processing tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Data Model

description: Date of birth of the insured individual. Critical for issue age and attained age calculations, MEC (Modified Endowment Contract) testing under IRC Section 7702, RMD (Required Minimum Distribution) tracking for annuities, and mortality risk assessment.
reference: IRC Section 7702; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; HIPAA; ACORD Life Standards

description: Free-text description of the exclusion rider(s) attached for this insured, specifying the excluded condition, activity, or body part. Used by claims adjusters during adjudication to determine whether a claim falls within an excluded category.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Claims Management System (Guidewire ClaimCenter; Majesco Claims)

description: Indicates whether one or more exclusion riders have been attached to the policy for this insured, limiting coverage for specific pre-existing conditions, hazardous activities, or body parts. Relevant for claims adjudication to determine benefit eligibility.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; Claims Management System (Guidewire ClaimCenter)

description: The death benefit (DB) face amount of coverage for this insured-life role, expressed in USD. Represents the base sum insured excluding riders. Used in NAR (Net Amount at Risk) calculation, reinsurance cession determination, and actuarial reserve calculations.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; IRC Section 7702; FASB ASC 944; ACORD Life Standards

description: Date on which the flat extra premium surcharge expires for the insured. After this date, the insured reverts to the base table rating without the temporary extra charge. Null if no flat extra applies or if the flat extra is permanent.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Additional per-thousand or per-unit premium charged to substandard insured lives for a defined period, typically reflecting temporary extra mortality risk (e.g., post-surgery recovery, hazardous occupation). Expressed as a dollar amount per $1,000 of face amount per year.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Indicates whether the insured has disclosed travel to or residence in countries with elevated mortality or political risk, as assessed during underwriting. May result in exclusion riders, flat extra premiums, or declination depending on destination and duration.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum); OFAC sanctions list

description: Gender of the insured as recorded for underwriting and actuarial purposes. M=Male, F=Female, X=Non-binary/Other, U=Unknown. Used in gender-distinct mortality table selection where permitted by state regulation. Classified as restricted PII.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; State Department of Insurance regulations; ACORD Life Standards

description: Certificate number assigned to an insured under a group life insurance master policy. Uniquely identifies the individual certificate within the group. Null for individual policies. Used in group policy administration, billing, and claims processing.
reference: NAIC Group Life Insurance Model Regulation; ACORD Life Standards; Policy Administration System

description: Indicates whether a valid insurable interest relationship has been verified between the policy owner and the insured at the time of application. Required by state insurance law to prevent wagering contracts and STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) arrangements.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; STOLI regulations

description: Classification of the insurable interest relationship between the policy owner and the insured. Self indicates the owner and insured are the same person. Business_partner and key_person apply to business-owned life insurance (BOLI). Trust applies to ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|child|business_partner|employer|key_person|trust|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; IRC Section 101

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System to uniquely identify this insured-life role on a policy. Used in ACORD transactions, bordereaux reporting, and reinsurance cession records.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; NAIC Data Model

description: Current lifecycle status of the insured-life role record. Active indicates coverage is in force; deceased triggers claims adjudication; lapsed/surrendered/terminated reflect policy end states; pending indicates underwriting not yet complete.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Policy Administration System

description: Age of the insured at the time the policy was issued, expressed in whole years. Fundamental actuarial input for mortality table selection, premium rate determination, and reserve calculations under PBR and LDTI.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline; Society of Actuaries (SOA) Mortality Tables; FASB ASC 944

description: Indicates whether an LTC (Long-Term Care) rider is attached to the policy for this insured, providing accelerated death benefits upon inability to perform ADLs (Activities of Daily Living). Triggers LTC benefit administration and state LTC regulatory reporting.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; Policy Administration System

description: Status of the MIB (Medical Information Bureau) inquiry conducted during underwriting for this insured. A 'hit' indicates a prior medical disclosure that requires further investigation. Used in underwriting risk assessment and NIGO (Not In Good Order) tracking.
reference: MIB Group Inc. Data Standards; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; HIPAA

description: Military service status of the insured as disclosed on the application. Active duty military may trigger war exclusion clauses or special underwriting considerations. Relevant for SGLI (Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance) coordination and exclusion rider application.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA); Underwriting Workbench

description: NAR (Net Amount at Risk) for this insured life, calculated as the death benefit minus the policy account value or reserve. Represents the insurer's net mortality exposure. Used in COI charge calculation for UL/IUL products, reinsurance cession amounts, and actuarial reserve modeling.
reference: NAIC Actuarial Guideline; SOA Mortality Tables; FASB ASC 944 LDTI; Actuarial Valuation System (AXIS; Prophet)

description: Occupational risk classification assigned to the insured based on their profession and associated hazard level. Used in DI (Disability Income) underwriting and life underwriting for hazardous occupations. Typically coded as Class 1 (professional) through Class 4+ (hazardous). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1|class_2|class_3|class_4|class_5|unclassifiable — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Disability Income Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Free-text description of the insured's occupation as stated on the application. Used in underwriting risk assessment, hazardous occupation exclusion determination, and DI benefit eligibility. Sourced from the Underwriting Workbench application data.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Indicates whether any portion of the risk on this insured life has been ceded to a reinsurer under an automatic or facultative treaty. Triggers reinsurance bordereaux reporting and cession processing in the Reinsurance Management System (Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA).
reference: NAIC Reinsurance Model Regulation; Reinsurance Management System (Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA Reinsurance)

description: Two-letter US state code representing the insured's state of residency at the time of policy issue. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, mandated benefit provisions, and state-specific exclusion limitations. Used in regulatory reporting to State Departments of Insurance.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life Standards

description: Classification of the insured's role on the policy. Primary insured is the primary life covered; secondary/joint applies to survivorship or joint-life products; group_certificate identifies an insured under a group master policy; dependent covers riders; key_person identifies business key-person coverage.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this insured record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_IPAS). Supports data lineage tracking, multi-system reconciliation, and Silver layer ETL audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Life & Health Data Standards; NAIC Data Model; SOX Section 404

description: Numerical or alphabetical table rating applied to substandard insured lives (e.g., Table 2, Table D). Each table increment typically represents an additional 25% of standard mortality. Used in premium surcharge calculation and reinsurance cession pricing.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum); Reinsurance Management System

description: Tobacco use classification of the insured as determined during underwriting. Directly impacts mortality risk class, premium rates, and COI charges. Non-tobacco and preferred non-tobacco classes receive lower rates. Status may be re-evaluated at policy anniversary for certain products.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Final underwriting decision rendered for this insured-life role. Approved indicates standard or preferred issuance; approved_rated indicates substandard issuance with table rating or flat extra; approved_modified indicates policy issued with exclusion riders; postponed indicates deferred pending additional information; declined indicates coverage refused.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Date on which the final underwriting decision was rendered for this insured-life role. Used to measure underwriting cycle time (NB processing KPI), track NIGO resolution, and support regulatory reporting on new business processing timelines.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Underwriting Workbench (RGAX AURA; Swiss Re Magnum)

description: Timestamp when this insured-life role record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance under SOX. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 944; NAIC Data Model

description: Indicates whether the AWL (Automatic Waiver of Premium) rider is active for this insured, waiving premium payments during a qualifying disability period. Relevant for billing system integration to suspend premium billing and for claims adjudication of disability claims.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Regulation; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro; FAST); Billing System

description: Unique system identifier for the party record. Primary key. Serves as the single source of truth for any individual or legal entity participating in the policyholder domain across all roles (insured, owner, annuitant, beneficiary, trustee, etc.).
Self-referencing FK on party (guardian_party_id)

Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk classification for the party based on customer due diligence (CDD) assessment. Low indicates standard risk. Medium indicates elevated risk requiring enhanced monitoring. High indicates significant risk requiring enhanced due diligence (EDD). Prohibited indicates party cannot be onboarded due to AML concerns.
reference: FinCEN CDD Rule, Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), NAIC Model Regulation

description: Country of citizenship for the party. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). For individuals with dual citizenship, primary citizenship for tax and regulatory purposes. Used for FATCA/CRS reporting, OFAC screening, and foreign national underwriting.
Timestamp when this party record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Birth date of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Critical for age-based underwriting, mortality calculations, annuity payout schedules, Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) calculations, and identity verification. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: IRS Publication 590-B (RMD), NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Party Model

description: Date of death for the party. Populated when party_status transitions to deceased. Triggers death benefit processing, annuity death benefit calculations, and beneficiary claim adjudication. Null for living parties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, Death Benefit Processing Standards

description: Date the party death was officially verified through death certificate, Social Security Death Master File (DMF), or other authoritative source. Used for death benefit claim processing and audit trail. Null for living parties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: Social Security Death Master File (DMF), NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date the legal entity (corporation, partnership, association) was formed or registered with the state. Null for individual and trust party types. Used for entity age verification and legal entity validation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

U.S. state or jurisdiction where the legal entity (trust, corporation, partnership) was formed or registered. Two-letter state code. Null for individual party types. Determines governing law, regulatory jurisdiction, and legal entity validation.
Given name of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types (trust, corporation, etc.). Used for personalized communications and identity verification.

Gender of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Used for mortality table selection, underwriting risk classification, and actuarial valuation. Non_binary and unknown options support inclusive data capture and regulatory compliance in jurisdictions requiring gender diversity recognition.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, SOA Mortality Tables

description: Name of the grantor (settlor) who established the trust. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for grantor trust tax rules, insurable interest verification, and estate planning documentation.

Current status of Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification for the party. Verified indicates successful identity verification. Pending indicates verification in progress. Failed indicates verification failure requiring manual review. Expired indicates verification has lapsed and requires renewal. Exempt indicates party is exempt from KYC (e.g., institutional entities).
reference: USA PATRIOT Act Section 326, FinCEN CDD Rule, NAIC Model Regulation

description: Date the party successfully completed KYC identity verification. Used for KYC expiration tracking and periodic re-verification scheduling per AML program requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: USA PATRIOT Act Section 326, FinCEN CDD Rule

description: Surname or family name of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Primary sorting and indexing field for individual parties.

Full legal name of the party as it appears on government-issued identification or legal formation documents. For individuals: full name including middle name/initial. For entities: registered legal name as filed with state or jurisdiction of formation. Used for policy issuance, tax reporting (1099, 1042-S), and regulatory filings.
reference: IRS Publication 1179, NAIC Model Regulation, ACORD Party Model

description: Middle name or middle initial of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Supports full name matching for identity verification and MIB (Medical Information Bureau) checks.
reference: ACORD Party Model, MIB Group Standards

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Last Screened Date
description: Date the party was last screened against OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list and other sanctions lists. Used for periodic re-screening scheduling per AML program requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

business_glossary_term: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Screening Status
description: Status of Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions screening for the party. Clear indicates no match to OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list. Match indicates potential match requiring investigation. Pending_review indicates match under investigation. Blocked indicates confirmed OFAC match prohibiting business relationship.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Programs, USA PATRIOT Act

description: Business-facing unique identifier for the party. Human-readable reference number used in correspondence, customer service, and external communications.

Current lifecycle status of the party. Active indicates normal operational state. Deceased triggers death benefit processing. Incapacitated may require guardian or power of attorney. Sanctioned indicates OFAC or regulatory restriction. Fraud_flagged indicates suspected fraudulent activity requiring investigation.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation, OFAC Compliance Guidelines

description: Classification of the party entity. Distinguishes between natural persons (individual) and legal entities (trust, corporation, partnership, association, estate). Drives downstream processing rules for identity verification, tax reporting, and regulatory compliance.

Indicates whether the party is a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) - an individual who holds or has held a prominent public position (government official, senior executive of state-owned enterprise, etc.). True indicates PEP status requiring enhanced due diligence (EDD). False indicates non-PEP status.

Country of tax residency for the party. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Determines tax reporting obligations, withholding requirements, and regulatory jurisdiction. May differ from citizenship_country for expatriates and foreign residents.

U.S. state or territory of primary residence for the party. Two-letter state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Determines state insurance department jurisdiction, premium tax situs, and state-specific regulatory requirements. Null for non-U.S. residents.

Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) has been filed for this party with FinCEN. True indicates SAR filing history requiring ongoing monitoring. False indicates no SAR filing history. SAR details are maintained in separate secure system per regulatory confidentiality requirements.
reference: Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), FinCEN SAR Filing Requirements

description: Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this party record (e.g., FAST, EXL, Sapiens, RGAX, Guidewire, Salesforce). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and source system traceability in multi-system environments.

Unique identifier for the party in the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and bidirectional synchronization between lakehouse and operational systems.

Social Security Number for U.S. individual parties. Masked or tokenized in storage per PII compliance requirements. Used for tax reporting (1099-R, 1042-S), IRS TIN matching, and identity verification. Null for non-U.S. parties or entities using EIN/TIN.
reference: IRS Publication 1179, IRC Section 6109, HIPAA Privacy Rule

description: Indicates whether the party has been flagged for potential Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) or investor-owned life insurance activity. True indicates STOLI concern requiring enhanced underwriting review and insurable interest verification. False indicates no STOLI concern.
reference: NAIC Model Act on STOLI, State Insurance Regulations

description: Generational or professional suffix appended to the individual party name (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III, MD, Esq.). Null for non-individual party types. Supports accurate name matching and identity verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Jr|Sr|II|III|IV|V|MD|PhD|Esq — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Taxpayer Identification Number for the party. For individuals: SSN or ITIN. For entities: EIN (Employer Identification Number). Masked or tokenized in storage. Used for IRS tax reporting, 1099 issuance, FATCA/CRS compliance, and TIN matching validation.
reference: IRS Publication 1179, IRC Section 6109, FATCA Regulations

description: Status of IRS TIN certification and validation. Certified indicates successful IRS TIN matching. Pending indicates awaiting validation. Failed indicates TIN mismatch requiring B-notice processing. Exempt indicates party is exempt from TIN certification (e.g., foreign entities under treaty).

Type of taxpayer identification number. SSN for U.S. individuals, EIN for entities, ITIN for non-resident aliens, foreign_tax_id for non-U.S. tax identifiers. Drives tax reporting format and regulatory compliance requirements.

Employer Identification Number (EIN) assigned to the trust by the IRS. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for tax reporting (1099-R, 1042-S) and IRS TIN matching. Masked or tokenized in storage per PII compliance.
Date the trust was legally established or executed. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for trust age calculations, grantor trust rules, and estate planning compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

Legal name of the trust entity. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for policy ownership records, beneficiary designations, and trust-owned life insurance (TOLI) or irrevocable life insurance trust (ILIT) administration.
reference: IRC Section 671-679 (Trust Taxation), ACORD Party Model

description: Classification of the trust entity. ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) for life insurance ownership. Revocable_living for revocable living trusts. CRT (Charitable Remainder Trust), GRAT (Grantor Retained Annuity Trust), QPRT (Qualified Personal Residence Trust) for specialized estate planning trusts. Null for non-trust party types.

Name of the current trustee(s) of the trust. Populated only when party_type is trust. May be individual or corporate trustee. Used for trust administration, correspondence, and fiduciary responsibility tracking.
Timestamp when this party record was last updated. Updated on every modification to track data freshness and change frequency. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Unique surrogate identifier for each beneficiary designation record in the policyholder domain. Serves as the primary key for the policyholder_beneficiary data product. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY — represents a named party designated to receive death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Beneficiary AML screening at claim payout is a named compliance process. policyholder_beneficiary already carries aml_screening_status, confirming AML review occurs at the beneficiary role level. An A

Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: The policyholder_beneficiary master record serves both life insurance policies and annuity contracts (evidenced by the annuity_contract_id FK). For annuity contracts, the beneficiary is designated wit

Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Annuity contracts require beneficiary records for death benefit distribution (primary, contingent, per stirpes designations). Current model only links beneficiaries to life insurance policies. Annuity
Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract under which this beneficiary designation applies. Enables cross-domain linkage to the policy or annuity contract record.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST)

description: Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: The policyholder_beneficiary master record represents a named beneficiary designated to receive death benefit proceeds upon the insured's death. The relationship_to_insured string field on policyholde
Reference to the policyholder (contract owner or insured) who made this beneficiary designation. Links the beneficiary record to the owning party in the policyholder master.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST)

description: Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Beneficiary designation changes are processed as service requests in policy administration. Linking the resulting beneficiary record to the originating service request supports audit trails, NIGO trac

Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: Beneficiaries can be trust entities. Currently trust_formation_date and trust_ein are stored in policyholder_beneficiary. Normalizing to trust.trust_id FK allows joining to the authoritative trust ent

Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Beneficiaries are designated during application. Linking beneficiary to application tracks initial designation, supports STOLI investigations (unusual beneficiary patterns from application), and enabl

Primary street address line for the beneficiary's mailing address. Used for claims correspondence, benefit payment delivery, and regulatory reporting. Required for state-mandated unclaimed property (escheatment) compliance when a beneficiary cannot be located.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Secondary address line for the beneficiary (apartment, suite, unit, floor, PO Box). Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing address capture.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Current status of the beneficiary's AML (Anti-Money Laundering) screening against OFAC sanctions lists, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) databases, and adverse media. 'cleared' indicates no matches found; 'flagged' indicates a potential match requiring review; 'pending' indicates screening is in progress; 'escalated' indicates the case has been referred to the AML compliance team.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; OFAC SDN List; USA PATRIOT Act; NAIC Model Regulation; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Bank account number for EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) disbursement of death benefit proceeds to the beneficiary. Stored in encrypted/tokenized form per PCI DSS and data security standards. Populated only when payment_method is 'eft'.
reference: PCI DSS; NACHA Operating Rules; NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; SOX

description: ABA routing transit number (RTN) for the beneficiary's bank, used for EFT disbursement of death benefit proceeds. Nine-digit code identifying the financial institution. Populated only when payment_method is 'eft'.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules; ABA Routing Number Standard; NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; PCI DSS

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary is a natural person (individual), a legal trust (including ILIT — Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust), the insured's estate, a charitable organization, or another legal entity such as a corporation or partnership. Drives KYC/AML identity verification requirements and tax reporting obligations.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; IRS Publication 559; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; FinCEN AML/BSA

description: Classification of the beneficiary's priority tier in the designation hierarchy. Primary beneficiaries receive proceeds first; contingent beneficiaries receive proceeds if all primary beneficiaries predecease the insured; tertiary beneficiaries are the third-tier fallback. Governs the order of benefit distribution upon a death benefit (DB) claim.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: City of the beneficiary's mailing address. Used for claims correspondence, state jurisdiction determination, and unclaimed property escheatment filings.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the beneficiary's country of residence (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Required for FATCA/FBAR foreign beneficiary reporting, AML/KYC country risk assessment, and international claims payment processing.
reference: ISO 3166-1; IRS FATCA (IRC Section 1471); FinCEN AML/BSA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Date of birth of the individual beneficiary. Used to verify identity during claims adjudication, determine minor beneficiary status (requiring guardian or trust appointment), calculate required minimum distributions (RMD) for inherited annuity contracts, and comply with HIPAA and state insurance regulations.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; IRS RMD Rules (IRC Section 401(a)(9)); HIPAA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Reference number or document identifier of the signed beneficiary designation or change form submitted by the policyholder. Provides an audit trail linking the data record to the physical or digital source document stored in the Document Management system. Supports compliance and legal dispute resolution.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; SOX; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; State Departments of Insurance

description: The percentage share of the death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds allocated to this beneficiary within their priority tier (primary, contingent, or tertiary). All beneficiaries within the same tier must sum to 100.00%. Governs pro-rata distribution of proceeds at claim settlement.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 72

description: Ordinal sequence number used to order multiple beneficiaries within the same priority tier (primary, contingent, or tertiary) on a given policy. Supports display ordering on policy documents and beneficiary designation forms. Does not imply priority — all beneficiaries in the same tier share proceeds per their designation_percentage.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST)

description: Current lifecycle status of the beneficiary designation record. 'active' indicates the designation is in force; 'revoked' indicates the policyholder has removed this beneficiary; 'superseded' indicates a newer designation has replaced this record; 'pending' indicates the change request is awaiting processing or approval; 'expired' indicates the designation has passed its effective end date.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which this beneficiary designation became legally effective. Typically corresponds to the date the signed beneficiary change form was received and processed by the insurer. Used to determine which designation was in force at the time of the insured's death for claims adjudication.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST); ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Primary email address for the beneficiary. Used for digital claims notification, document delivery, and beneficiary portal access. Supports CRM-driven customer engagement workflows for beneficiary outreach.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; GDPR Article 4; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Full legal name of the trust, estate, charity, or corporate entity designated as beneficiary. Populated when beneficiary_category is 'trust', 'estate', 'charity', or 'entity'. Supports ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) and other non-individual beneficiary structures. Required for AML entity due diligence.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; FinCEN AML/BSA; IRS Publication 559; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: The date on which this beneficiary designation ceases to be effective, if applicable. Null for open-ended designations. Used for time-limited beneficiary arrangements such as collateral assignments or court-ordered designations with defined end dates.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Legal given name of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the beneficiary designation form. Populated only when beneficiary_category is 'individual'. Required for identity verification, KYC compliance, and death benefit (DB) claim processing.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; HIPAA; GDPR Article 4; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Gender of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the designation form. Used for identity verification during claims processing and for actuarial life expectancy calculations on inherited annuity contracts subject to RMD rules.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; State Departments of Insurance

description: Full legal name of the court-appointed or designated guardian or custodian for a minor beneficiary. Populated only when minor_flag is True. Required for claims payment processing to ensure proceeds are directed to the legal guardian rather than the minor directly.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; UTMA/UGMA

description: Indicates whether this beneficiary designation is irrevocable. When True, the policyholder cannot change or revoke the beneficiary without the beneficiary's written consent. Common in ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) structures, collateral assignments, and divorce decree requirements. Restricts policy servicing actions such as loans, surrenders, and assignment changes.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; State Departments of Insurance

description: Date on which the beneficiary's KYC identity verification was completed and approved. Used to track the currency of identity verification and trigger re-verification workflows when the verification has aged beyond the insurer's AML program refresh threshold.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary's identity has been verified through the insurer's KYC (Know Your Customer) process. When True, identity documents have been reviewed and validated per AML/BSA program requirements. Required for high-value death benefit (DB) payments and trust/entity beneficiaries.
reference: FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; USA PATRIOT Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Legal surname of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the beneficiary designation form. Populated only when beneficiary_category is 'individual'. Required for identity verification and death benefit (DB) claim processing.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; HIPAA; GDPR Article 4; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Legal middle name or initial of the individual beneficiary. Used to disambiguate beneficiaries with common first and last names during claims adjudication and identity verification.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Indicates whether the individual beneficiary is a legal minor (under age 18 or applicable state age of majority) at the time of designation. When True, a guardian, custodian, or trust must be appointed to receive and manage death benefit proceeds on behalf of the minor, triggering special claims handling workflows.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; UTMA/UGMA; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Preferred method for disbursing death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds to this beneficiary. 'eft' indicates electronic funds transfer to a bank account; 'check' indicates paper check; 'wire' indicates domestic or international wire transfer; 'annuity_settlement' indicates proceeds are to be paid as an annuity settlement option rather than a lump sum.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Policy Administration System (Sapiens LifePro, FAST)

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary's share is designated per stirpes (by representation). When True, if this beneficiary predeceases the insured, the beneficiary's share passes to their lineal descendants rather than being redistributed among surviving beneficiaries. Governs death benefit (DB) distribution logic in claims adjudication.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Primary contact telephone number for the beneficiary. Used by the claims department to notify and communicate with beneficiaries upon the insured's death. Supports outbound claims notification workflows in the Claims Management System.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; GDPR Article 4; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: ZIP or postal code for the beneficiary's mailing address. Used for claims correspondence routing, state jurisdiction determination, and unclaimed property escheatment compliance.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was first created in the system of record. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX; NAIC Model Regulation; FASB ASC 944; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Describes the legal or familial relationship between the beneficiary and the insured (e.g., spouse, child, parent, sibling, domestic partner, business partner, trust, estate, charity). Used in insurable interest determinations, claims adjudication, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|domestic_partner|business_partner|grandchild|grandparent|other_relative|trust|estate|charity|employer|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; IRC Section 101

description: The elected settlement option governing how death benefit (DB) proceeds will be paid to this beneficiary. 'lump_sum' pays the full amount at once; 'life_income' provides lifetime annuity payments; 'fixed_period' pays over a specified number of years; 'interest_only' retains principal and pays interest; 'joint_survivor' provides income for two lives. Drives annuity contract administration workflows.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Model Act; IRC Section 72; ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; Annuity Administration Platform (Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity)

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this beneficiary designation record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_IPAS). Supports data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ACORD Life & Annuity Data Standards; SOX; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Two-letter US state or territory code for the beneficiary's mailing address (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Used to determine applicable state insurance regulations, unclaimed property escheatment jurisdiction, and state income tax withholding on death benefit proceeds.
reference: NAIC Unclaimed Property Model Act; State Departments of Insurance; IRS Publication 559

description: The IRS-issued Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) for the beneficiary, which may be a Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for trusts and entities. Required for IRS Form 1099-R tax reporting on death benefit proceeds and for AML/KYC identity verification. Stored in encrypted form per data security standards.
reference: IRS IRC Section 6109; IRS Form 1099-R; FinCEN AML/BSA; NAIC Model Regulation; SOX

Primary key for charge_schedule

Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Charge schedules (M&E fees, surrender charges, admin fees) must map to specific GL accounts for GAAP revenue recognition and SAP statutory reporting. Finance teams require this link to automate journa
Reference to the life insurance or annuity product to which this charge schedule applies. Links to the product master catalog.

Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Product charge schedules (surrender charges, fees) are version-specific. Charge changes may trigger new versions. The charge_schedule_version and predecessor_schedule_id suggest version-level tracking
Reference to the previous version of this charge schedule if this is a revision. NULL for the initial version. Enables lineage tracking and historical analysis.

Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Surrender charge schedules and expense charges for LTC and annuity products require regulatory rate filing approval. The charge_schedule must reference its governing compliance rate_filing. filing_num
Date on which the state insurance department approved this charge schedule for use. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trails.
reference: State Department of Insurance Approval Process

description: Fixed dollar amount of the charge when charge_basis is flat_dollar. Used for per-policy administrative fees or fixed withdrawal processing fees. NULL for percentage-based charges.

The calculation basis for the charge. Determines how the charge amount is computed: as a percentage of premium paid, percentage of account value (AV), flat dollar amount per policy, per $1,000 of face amount, or percentage of withdrawal amount.
reference: NAIC Model Variable Annuity Regulation, IRC Section 7702 Premium Limitations

description: Maximum dollar amount that can be charged under this schedule, regardless of the calculated charge. Used to limit total charges on high-value policies. NULL if no cap applies.
reference: State Insurance Regulations, Consumer Protection Standards

description: Minimum dollar amount that will be charged under this schedule, regardless of the calculated charge. Used to ensure minimum cost recovery. NULL if no floor applies.

Frequency at which the charge is assessed. One-time charges apply at a single event (e.g., surrender); recurring charges apply monthly, quarterly, or annually; per-transaction charges apply each time a specific transaction occurs.
Unique business identifier for the charge schedule. Used in policy administration systems and illustrations to reference specific charge structures.
Human-readable name of the charge schedule for business user identification and reporting purposes.

Current lifecycle status of the charge schedule. Active schedules are in use for new business; pending approval schedules are awaiting regulatory approval; approved schedules are ready for use; inactive schedules are no longer offered; superseded schedules have been replaced by newer versions.

Classification of the charge or fee. Surrender charges are back-end loads applied when policies are surrendered; premium loads are front-end charges on premium payments; admin fees are per-policy or per-unit charges; M&E charges are mortality and expense risk charges for variable products; fund management fees are investment management charges; withdrawal fees apply to partial withdrawals.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation, SEC Variable Product Disclosure Requirements

description: Strategic market positioning of this charge schedule. Premium indicates higher charges with enhanced features; competitive indicates market-rate charges; value indicates lower charges for cost-conscious segments. Confidential business strategy.
Timestamp when this charge schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards, SOX Compliance

description: The current charge rate being applied to in-force policies. Must be less than or equal to the guaranteed charge rate. For percentage-based charges, expressed as a decimal. For flat charges, expressed in dollars. This rate may be adjusted by the company within guaranteed limits.
reference: NAIC Model Variable Annuity Regulation, Policy Illustration Regulation

description: Tax treatment of this charge for Deferred Acquisition Cost purposes under IRC Section 848. Indicates whether the charge is capitalized and amortized, immediately expensed, or not subject to DAC tax rules.
Date on which this charge schedule becomes effective for new policies or in-force policy changes. Must align with state approval dates and product filing effective dates.
reference: NAIC Product Filing Requirements, State Department of Insurance Approval Standards

description: Date on which this charge schedule is no longer available for new policies. NULL indicates the schedule is currently active with no planned expiration. In-force policies continue under their original schedule.

The percentage of account value (AV) or cash surrender value (CSV) that can be withdrawn annually without incurring surrender charges. Commonly 10% for annuities. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 for 10%). NULL if no free withdrawal provision exists.
reference: NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation, State Annuity Regulations

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Revenue Recognition
description: GAAP revenue recognition method for this charge under FASB ASC 944 and LDTI (Long Duration Targeted Improvements). Indicates whether revenue is recognized immediately, deferred, or recognized over the coverage period.

The maximum charge rate guaranteed in the policy contract. This is the contractual ceiling that cannot be exceeded. For percentage-based charges, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0125 for 1.25%). For flat charges, expressed in dollars.
reference: NAIC Model Variable Annuity Regulation, State Insurance Code Requirements

description: Indicates whether this charge must be explicitly disclosed in policy illustrations and sales materials. True if disclosure is required by regulation or company policy; False otherwise.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Illustrations Model Regulation, FINRA Rule 2210 (Variable Products)

business_glossary_term: Internal Revenue Code (IRC) Section 7702 Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether this charge schedule complies with IRC Section 7702 requirements for life insurance tax qualification. Charges must be reasonable and not excessive to maintain tax-favored status. True if compliant; False otherwise.
reference: IRC Section 7702, IRS Revenue Rulings on Life Insurance Taxation

description: User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this charge schedule record. Used for audit trails and accountability.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards, SOX Compliance

description: Timestamp when this charge schedule record was last modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards, SOX Compliance

description: Indicates whether a Market Value Adjustment applies to surrenders or withdrawals under this charge schedule. MVA adjusts the surrender value based on interest rate movements. True if MVA applies; False otherwise.
reference: NAIC Model Variable Annuity Regulation, Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) Standards

description: Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or implementation guidance for this charge schedule. Used for business user reference and operational documentation.
Maximum policy duration (in years) for which this charge schedule applies. NULL indicates no upper limit.
Minimum policy duration (in years) for which this charge schedule applies. Used for charges that vary by policy age or duration bands.

The policy year to which this charge applies. Used primarily for surrender charge schedules that decrease over time. Policy year 1 is the first year after issue; NULL indicates the charge applies across all years.
Target profit margin for this charge schedule as a percentage. Used in product profitability tracking and pricing governance. Confidential business metric.
reference: Internal Product Profitability Standards, Actuarial Pricing Governance

description: Two-letter US state code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction for which this charge schedule is approved. Charge schedules may vary by state due to differing regulatory requirements.
reference: NAIC State Jurisdiction Standards, State Departments of Insurance

description: Statutory accounting treatment of this charge under NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles. Determines how the charge impacts statutory surplus and Risk-Based Capital (RBC) calculations.
reference: NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Version number of this charge schedule. Incremented when the schedule is revised. Used to track changes over time and ensure in-force policies use the correct historical schedule.

Condition under which surrender charges or other fees are waived. Common waivers include nursing home confinement, terminal illness diagnosis, total disability, death of the owner/annuitant, or unemployment. 'none' indicates no waiver provisions.
reference: NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation, Long-Term Care (LTC) Partnership Standards

description: Number of days that must elapse after the waiver condition is met before the charge waiver takes effect. Common for nursing home or disability waivers (e.g., 90 days). NULL if no waiting period applies.
reference: NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Act

business_glossary_term: Cost of Insurance (COI) Rate Schedule ID
description: Unique identifier for the COI rate schedule. Primary key.
Reference to the life insurance or annuity product (UL, IUL, VUL, FIA) to which this COI rate schedule applies.

Status of the regulatory filing for this form with state insurance departments. Tracks whether the form has been filed, approved, rejected, or is pending regulatory review.
reference: NAIC System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing (SERFF)

description: Business category of the form indicating its primary purpose within the insurance lifecycle. Distinguishes contract documents from administrative, regulatory, marketing, compliance, or operational forms.
Detailed business description of the forms purpose, content, and usage within the policy lifecycle. Provides context for underwriters, administrators, and compliance personnel.
The official title or name of the insurance form as it appears on the document and in regulatory filings.
The official form number or identifier assigned by the insurance company. This is the externally-known unique identifier used in policy documents, filings, and regulatory submissions.

Current lifecycle status of the form. Tracks the forms progression through drafting, regulatory approval, active use, and eventual retirement or supersession. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|retired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the form document type. Categorizes whether this is a base policy form, application form, rider form, amendment endorsement, disclosure form, or other contract document type.
The number of days after policy delivery during which the policyholder may cancel the policy and receive a full refund, as specified on this form and required by state regulation.
Indicates whether a policy illustration conforming to NAIC Life Insurance Illustrations Model Regulation must accompany this form during the sales process.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Illustrations Model Regulation

business_glossary_term: Internal Revenue Code (IRC) Section 7702 Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the form and its associated product structure comply with IRC Section 7702 requirements for life insurance tax qualification. Critical for ensuring policy death benefits remain tax-free.

Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the language in which the form is written. Supports multilingual form libraries for diverse customer populations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|FRE|CHI|KOR|VIE|RUS|ARA|POR|GER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
User identifier or system account that last modified this form record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
The date and time when this form record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
Indicates whether this form must be included with every policy issuance for the associated product, or if it is optional based on customer election or underwriting decisions.
Assessment of the risk that policies issued using this form may become Modified Endowment Contracts under IRC Section 7702A, which would subject withdrawals to taxation and penalties.
The number of pages in the printed or PDF version of this form. Used for document assembly and printing cost estimation.
The order in which this form should appear in the policy document package when multiple forms are assembled for delivery to the policyholder.
Indicates whether a prospectus must be delivered to the customer along with this form at point of sale, as required for variable insurance products.
Explanation of why this form was retired or superseded. Examples include regulatory change, product redesign, compliance update, or business strategy shift. Nullable for active forms.

For rider forms, specifies the type of rider benefit. Examples include Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Waiver of Premium, Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB), Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB), Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB), Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB), Long-Term Care (LTC), or Disability Income (DI). Nullable for non-rider forms.

business_glossary_term: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Registered Flag
description: Indicates whether this form is associated with a variable product that requires SEC registration and prospectus delivery. Applies to Variable Universal Life (VUL) and Variable Annuity products.

Comma-separated list of US state postal codes where this form is approved and valid for use. Supports multi-state forms and state-specific variations. Examples: NY, CA, TX or ALL for nationwide forms.
Indicates whether this form is a state-specific variation required by particular state regulations, or a standard form used across multiple jurisdictions.
Operational guidance and special instructions for when and how to use this form. May include underwriting rules, state-specific requirements, or system processing notes.
Unique identifier for the product plan. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Products map to specific GL accounts for premium revenue recognition, reserve liability booking, and DAC asset capitalization. Required for automated journal entry generation, financial statement prep

Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan includes a cash value accumulation component. True for permanent life insurance and annuities; false for term life and pure protection products.
The number of years from policy issue during which the insurer can contest claims based on material misrepresentation in the application. Typically 2 years per state regulation.
The timestamp when this product plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The accounting method used to amortize deferred acquisition costs for this product plan under GAAP and LDTI standards. Used in actuarial valuation and financial reporting.

Comma-separated list of distribution channels through which this product plan can be sold (e.g., career_agent, independent_broker, bank_channel, direct_to_consumer, worksite). The date on which this product plan becomes available for sale and new business issuance. Used in policy administration and new business processing.
The date on which this product plan is no longer available for new business. Nullable for plans with no planned retirement date. Existing policies remain in force after this date.

description: The three-digit NAIC product type code used for statutory reporting and regulatory filings. Required for Annual Statement Schedule T reporting.

Detailed description of the product plan including key features, benefits, and target market. Used for agent training and customer education materials.

Current lifecycle status of the product plan. Active plans are available for new business; filed plans are pending regulatory approval; approved plans are cleared but not yet active; suspended plans are temporarily unavailable; retired plans are closed to new business; withdrawn plans have been removed from filing.

Comma-separated list of premium payment frequency options available for this product plan (e.g., annual, semi_annual, quarterly, monthly, single). Used in billing system configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|semi_annual|quarterly|monthly|single|flexible — promote to reference product]
The product family grouping used for portfolio management, profitability analysis, and strategic planning. Examples include term life family, universal life family, fixed annuity family.

business_glossary_term: Profitability Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR)

The number of months after policy lapse during which the policyholder can reinstate the policy without new underwriting. Typically 3-5 years per state regulation.
The code identifying the reinsurance treaty under which this product plan is ceded. Used in reinsurance management system for cession processing and bordereaux reporting.
The primary reserve calculation basis for this product plan. Statutory (SAP), GAAP, IFRS 17, or Principle-Based Reserving (PBR). Used in actuarial valuation system.

description: The business or regulatory reason for retiring this product plan. Used in product lifecycle management and strategic planning.
Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan uses simplified underwriting with limited health questions and no medical exam. Used in underwriting workbench and new business processing.
The number of US states and territories in which this product plan has received regulatory approval for sale. Used in distribution planning and compliance monitoring.
The number of years from policy issue during which death by suicide results in limited or no death benefit payment. Typically 2 years per state regulation.
Reference to the surrender charge schedule identifier that defines the penalty structure for early policy termination. Used in policy servicing and cash surrender value calculations.

Indicates whether the product is tax-qualified under IRC Section 7702 and whether it is classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. Critical for tax reporting and compliance.

Comma-separated list of underwriting risk classes eligible for this product plan (e.g., preferred_plus, preferred, standard, substandard). Used in underwriting workbench and rate table selection. The timestamp when this product plan record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

description: Primary key for the plan_rider_eligibility association
Foreign key linking to the base product plan for which this rider eligibility record applies.

The date on which this plan-rider eligibility combination became active and available for new business. Belongs to the association because the same rider may become eligible for a plan at a different time than it was originally created.

description: Indicates whether this rider is mandatory (automatically included) or optional (customer-elected) when issued with this specific base plan. This designation can differ by plan — the same rider may be mandatory on one plan and optional on another.

The display and processing sequence order of this rider relative to other riders on the same base plan. Used by illustration software and policy administration systems to order riders consistently on policy documents and admin screens.

description: Primary key for the plan_underwriting_eligibility association

description: Foreign key linking to the underwriting risk classification category that is eligible under this plan configuration.

Plan-specific override to the standard underwriting class eligibility criteria defined on the underwriting_class record. Captures additional or modified health, lifestyle, or risk requirements that apply only when this class is used with this plan. Null if no override applies.

Plan-specific minimum death benefit face amount required to qualify for this underwriting class under this plan. Overrides the class-level face_amount_minimum when set. Null if the class-level default applies without modification.

description: Business justification for creating this version. Documents the driver for the version change such as regulatory mandate, competitive positioning, profitability improvement, actuarial assumption update, or product redesign.

Identifier or description of the stakeholder communication strategy for this version change. Documents agent bulletins, policyholder notices, and internal training materials associated with the version transition.

business_glossary_term: Expected New Business Value (NBV) Per Policy
description: Projected new business value per policy for this plan version based on actuarial pricing assumptions. Represents the present value of future profits at issue. Confidential metric used for product portfolio management and sales incentive design.
State insurance department filing identifier or SERFF (System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing) tracking number. Links this version to the regulatory approval documentation.
Indicates whether this plan version satisfies the Guideline Premium Test under IRC Section 7702. One of two tests (GPT or Cash Value Accumulation Test) required for life insurance tax qualification.

Strategy for managing existing policies after version retirement. Full servicing maintains all policy features; limited servicing restricts certain transactions; closed block segregates reserves; reinsured transfers risk; sold indicates block sale to another carrier.

Indicates whether this plan version meets IRC Section 7702 definition of life insurance for federal tax qualification. Critical for ensuring death benefit tax-free treatment and cash value accumulation tax deferral.
Final date on which new policies may be issued under this plan version. Critical for product retirement and closed-block management. Typically precedes or coincides with superseded date.
Highest death benefit amount permitted for policies issued under this plan version without special underwriting or reinsurance approval. Supports risk management and capacity planning.

Date and time when this plan version record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.

business_glossary_term: Profitability Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR)
description: Target internal rate of return percentage for this plan version as established during product pricing. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.50 for 12.5%). Confidential business metric used for product performance monitoring.
Identifier or name of the premium rate table set associated with this plan version. Links to actuarial rate tables for premium calculation, cost of insurance charges, and benefit pricing.

Primary business driver for retiring this plan version. Regulatory change indicates compliance requirements; profitability reflects financial performance; product redesign signals strategic repositioning; market exit covers business line discontinuation; competitive pressure addresses market dynamics; technology limitation reflects system constraints.

description: Date when this plan version is replaced by a subsequent version. Null for the current active version. Marks the end of new business eligibility for this version.

Narrative summary of this plan version including key features, target market, competitive positioning, and material changes from prior versions. Supports product training and agent sales materials.
Sequential or semantic version identifier for this plan iteration (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0-A). Supports both numeric and alphanumeric versioning schemes.

Current lifecycle state of this plan version. Draft indicates work in progress; pending approval awaits regulatory review; active is available for new business; superseded is replaced by newer version but may have in-force policies; retired is closed to new business; withdrawn is cancelled before activation.

Classification of the version change. New filing represents initial product launch; amendment covers contract language changes; rate revision updates premium or cost structures; benefit enhancement adds or modifies benefits; compliance update addresses regulatory requirements; retirement marks the terminal lifecycle event.

Market index used as the performance benchmark for the fund (e.g., S&P 500, Russell 2000, Bloomberg Barclays Aggregate Bond Index), against which fund returns are measured.
Date when the fund was closed to new allocations while continuing to service existing investments. Used to track fund capacity management decisions.
System timestamp indicating when this fund record was first created in the separate account fund catalog.

business_glossary_term: Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (CUSIP) Number
description: Nine-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the fund for securities trading and settlement purposes.
Indicates whether this fund is eligible for automatic dollar cost averaging programs where systematic transfers are made from a stable value fund into this investment option over time.
Date when this fund became available for selection within the company's variable product separate accounts.

Annual fund operating expense ratio expressed as a percentage of average net assets, representing the cost charged to policyholders for fund management, administrative services, and other operational expenses.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the separate account fund across all systems and product lines. Used as the operational identifier for fund selection and allocation processing.
Detailed narrative description of the fund's investment strategy, holdings, risk profile, and suitability for different investor types. Used in policyholder communications and prospectus materials.
Name of the investment management company or fund family that manages the separate account fund (e.g., Fidelity, Vanguard, BlackRock, PIMCO).
Full legal name of the separate account investment fund as registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and disclosed in product prospectuses.

Current operational status of the separate account fund indicating availability for new allocations and existing account management. Active funds accept new investments; closed funds maintain existing positions but accept no new money; liquidating funds are winding down.
Date when the fund was first established and began operations, used for calculating fund age and historical performance metrics.
Primary investment goal of the fund as stated in the prospectus, describing the fund's strategy and target outcomes (e.g., long-term capital appreciation, current income, capital preservation).
Investment management style or approach employed by the fund manager, indicating whether the fund follows active management, passive indexing, growth-oriented, or value-oriented strategies.
User identifier of the person or system process that most recently modified this fund record, used for audit trail and accountability.
System timestamp indicating when this fund record was most recently modified, used for change tracking and data quality auditing.
Maximum percentage of total account value that may be allocated to this fund, used to enforce diversification limits and manage concentration risk in policyholder portfolios.
Maximum number of transfers allowed into or out of this fund within a calendar year, enforced to prevent excessive trading and market timing.
Minimum percentage of total account value that must be allocated to this fund if the policyholder chooses to invest in it. Used to enforce diversification requirements and operational minimums.
Minimum dollar amount required for the initial allocation to this fund when a policyholder first selects it as an investment option.
Morningstar fund classification category used for peer group comparison and performance benchmarking (e.g., Large Blend, Intermediate Bond, Foreign Large Growth).
Morningstar's proprietary star rating for the fund (1-5 stars), based on risk-adjusted returns relative to peer funds. Used for fund selection and marketing materials.

Mortality and expense risk charge assessed by the insurance company on variable product separate account values, covering insurance guarantees and administrative costs. Expressed as an annual percentage of account value.
Frequency at which the fund's net asset value per unit is calculated and published, determining how often policyholder account values are updated.
Date from which fund performance is calculated and reported. May differ from fund inception date if the fund underwent reorganization or name change.
Indicates whether this fund is available for Variable Universal Life (VUL) products, variable annuity products, or both product lines.
Effective date of the current fund prospectus, indicating when the disclosed fund information was last updated and filed with the SEC.
Indicates whether this fund can be included in automatic portfolio rebalancing programs that periodically adjust allocations back to target percentages.
Qualitative assessment of the fund's investment risk level based on volatility, asset allocation, and investment strategy. Used for suitability analysis and investor education.

business_glossary_term: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Registration Number
description: SEC registration number assigned to the investment company or fund, used for regulatory tracking and compliance reporting. Format: 811-XXXXX.
Date when the fund was removed from availability or ceased operations. Null for active funds.
Stock market ticker symbol for the fund, used for public market identification and price quotation.
Indicates whether the fund has restrictions on the frequency or timing of transfers in and out, used to prevent market timing abuse and protect long-term investors.
Unique identifier for the product filing record. Primary key for the product filing entity.

Reference to the product plan being filed for regulatory approval.
