description: The acknowledgment or confirmation number returned by the governing body's filing system upon successful receipt of the submission (e.g., NAIC confirmation number, SEC accession number, DOL EFAST2 confirmation, FinCEN BSA tracking number). Serves as the authoritative proof of submission.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Accession Number; DOL EFAST2; FinCEN BSA E-Filing

description: The date and time at which this regulatory filing record was first created in the compliance system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the filing lifecycle. Aligns with SOX internal control documentation requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for any monetary amounts reported in this filing (e.g., 'USD'). Primarily USD for domestic US regulatory filings. Required when penalty_amount or other monetary fields are populated.

Number of calendar days by which the filing was submitted after the statutory due date. Zero or negative if filed on time. Used to assess late-filing penalties and regulatory exposure. Calculated as submission_date minus due_date.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Late Filing Rules; DOL EFAST2

description: The statutory or regulatory deadline by which this filing must be submitted to the governing body. Used to calculate timeliness, trigger escalation workflows, and assess late-filing penalties. Derived from the regulatory calendar for the applicable filing type and jurisdiction.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2; IRS Publication 1220

business_glossary_term: Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) Plan Type
description: Classification of the ERISA-covered plan reported in this DOL Form 5500 filing (e.g., group life, 401k annuity, pension, welfare). Applicable only to ERISA filings. Null for non-ERISA filing types.
reference: DOL ERISA; DOL Form 5500 Instructions; IRC Section 401

description: Name or role of the designated fiduciary responsible for the ERISA plan covered by this filing. Required for DOL Form 5500 and DOL fiduciary compliance under ERISA. Null for non-ERISA filing types.
reference: DOL ERISA Section 3(21); DOL Form 5500 Instructions

description: The transmission method used to submit the filing to the governing body (e.g., electronic via NAIC SERFF, SEC EDGAR, DOL EFAST2, FinCEN BSA E-Filing portal, or paper submission). Relevant for audit trail and submission confirmation tracking.
reference: NAIC SERFF; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2; FinCEN BSA E-Filing

description: The official name or designation of the regulatory form used for this filing (e.g., 'NAIC Life/Health Annual Statement', 'SEC Form N-4', 'IRS Form 1099-R', 'FinCEN Form 112 CTR', 'DOL Form 5500', 'ORSA Summary Report'). Provides precise identification of the statutory instrument.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC Forms N-4/N-6; IRS Publication 1220; FinCEN Form 112; DOL Form 5500

description: Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the filing — either generated internally or assigned by the receiving regulatory body upon submission (e.g., NAIC filing reference, SEC accession number, DOL confirmation number). Used for tracking and correspondence with regulators.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Filing System; DOL EFAST2

description: Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing submission. Tracks progression from internal preparation (draft) through submission to the regulator and final disposition (accepted, rejected, withdrawn). Drives compliance workflow and remediation tracking.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2; FinCEN BSA E-Filing

description: Name of the internal operational system or platform from which the filing data was sourced or generated (e.g., 'Policy Administration System', 'Actuarial Valuation System', 'Claims Management System'). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Version or amendment number of this filing submission (e.g., 'original', 'amendment_1', 'corrected'). Tracks whether this is the initial submission or a subsequent amendment/correction filed with the governing body.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Amendment Rules; IRS Publication 1220

description: Indicates whether this filing is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted filing. True if this is an amended submission; False if this is the original filing. Used to distinguish original from corrected statutory submissions.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Amendment Rules; IRS Publication 1220

description: The date and time at which this regulatory filing record was most recently modified in the compliance system. Tracks the most recent status change, amendment, or data correction. Supports audit trail and SOX change management controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Indicates whether this filing was submitted after the statutory due date. True if submission_date exceeds due_date; False if filed on time. Used for compliance monitoring, penalty assessment, and regulatory examination preparation.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR Late Filing Rules; DOL EFAST2; IRS Publication 1220

description: Two-character US state code identifying the lead state regulator for ORSA filings and multi-state regulatory submissions. The lead state coordinates review among all states where the insurer is licensed. Applicable primarily to ORSA and group-level filings.
reference: NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual; NAIC Group Supervision Framework

description: Free-text field for compliance officers to record supplemental context, internal commentary, regulator correspondence notes, or remediation instructions related to this filing. Not transmitted to the regulator; for internal compliance workflow use only.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

business_glossary_term: Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) Risk Categories
description: Comma-separated list of risk categories assessed in the ORSA filing (e.g., 'insurance_risk, market_risk, credit_risk, operational_risk, liquidity_risk'). Applicable only to ORSA filings. Null for non-ORSA filing types. Supports ERM and ORSA reporting requirements.
reference: NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual; IAIS Insurance Core Principles

description: Monetary penalty assessed by the governing body for late or deficient filing, expressed in USD. Null if no penalty was assessed. Used for financial reporting, regulatory expense tracking, and SOX control monitoring.

description: Name of the employer or organization sponsoring the retirement or group benefit plan covered by this ERISA filing (DOL Form 5500). Applicable only to ERISA filings. Null for non-ERISA filing types.

description: Full name of the individual responsible for preparing the regulatory filing. Retained for audit trail and regulatory examination purposes even if the employee record changes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Reference number or identifier for any Prohibited Transaction Exemption (PTE) cited in the ERISA filing (e.g., PTE 84-24, PTE 2020-02). Applicable to ERISA filings where prohibited transaction exemptions are claimed. Null for non-ERISA filing types.
reference: DOL ERISA Section 408; DOL PTE 84-24; DOL PTE 2020-02

description: The date on which the governing body formally rejected the filing. Populated only when filing_status is 'rejected'. Triggers remediation workflow and resubmission tracking.

description: Textual description or regulator-provided code explaining why the filing was rejected. Populated when filing_status is 'rejected'. Used to drive remediation actions and resubmission preparation.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2

description: Description or comma-separated list of stress test scenarios included in the ORSA or actuarial filing (e.g., 'interest_rate_shock, pandemic_mortality, equity_market_decline'). Applicable to ORSA and actuarial valuation filings. Null for non-ORSA filing types.
reference: NAIC ORSA Guidance Manual; FASB ASC 944; LDTI

description: The actual calendar date on which the filing was transmitted to the governing body. Compared against due_date to determine timeliness and potential late-filing exposure. Populated upon successful transmission.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2; FinCEN BSA E-Filing

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the filing was electronically transmitted to the governing body. Provides audit-grade evidence of timely submission, particularly for filings with intraday deadlines (e.g., FinCEN CTR 15-day rule).
reference: FinCEN BSA E-Filing; SEC EDGAR; DOL EFAST2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory obligation record in the master compliance registry. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and compliance tracking workflows.

Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: A regulatory_obligation implements or is governed by a specific state_regulation. This FK establishes the relationship between the obligation and the state-specific regulatory requirement it fulfills.

description: Indicates whether this obligation is classified as material for SOX, ORSA, or ERM reporting purposes. Material obligations receive heightened monitoring, executive visibility, and are included in the Board-level compliance dashboard.

description: Indicates whether this obligation is imposed at the federal, state, multi-state, or international level. Drives routing to the appropriate legal and compliance team and determines which state DOI contacts are relevant.

reference: NAIC ORSA Model Regulation #505; IAIS Insurance Core Principles; ERM Framework

description: Indicates whether this obligation is related to NAIC Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) compliance, including VM-20 (life insurance reserves), VM-21 (variable annuity reserves), and VM-31 (PBR actuarial report) requirements. Used to scope actuarial compliance activities.
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20; NAIC Valuation Manual VM-21; NAIC Valuation Manual VM-31; PBR

description: Maximum or standard monetary penalty in US dollars that may be assessed for non-compliance with this obligation. Used in risk quantification, ERM reporting, and prioritization of compliance resources. Null if penalty is non-monetary (e.g., license suspension).
reference: NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards; IRS Penalty Provisions; SEC Enforcement Actions; State DOI Penalty Schedules

description: Narrative description of the regulatory penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance or late filing (e.g., '$100 per day late fee', 'license suspension', 'cease and desist order'). Informs risk prioritization and escalation urgency.
reference: NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards; IRS Penalty Provisions; SEC Enforcement Actions; State DOI Penalty Schedules

description: Enterprise-assigned priority classification for this obligation based on regulatory risk, penalty severity, and business impact. 'critical' = Board-level visibility, zero tolerance for non-compliance; 'high' = executive escalation required if at risk; 'medium' = standard compliance monitoring; 'low' = routine administrative obligation.
reference: NAIC ORSA Model Regulation #505; ERM Framework; SOX Section 302/404

description: Indicates whether this obligation is directly related to NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) reporting or solvency compliance. Used to prioritize obligations in the actuarial and finance compliance calendar and to scope RBC-related remediation activities.
reference: NAIC Risk-Based Capital (RBC) Instructions; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Formal legal or regulatory citation for the rule or statute that creates this obligation (e.g., 'IRC Section 7702', 'NAIC Model Regulation #820', 'ERISA Section 404', 'ASC 944-40 LDTI', 'FinCEN 31 CFR Part 1010'). Enables precise traceability to source regulation for audit and legal review.
reference: IRS IRC Section 7702; NAIC Model Regulations; DOL ERISA; FASB ASC 944; FinCEN 31 CFR Part 1010

description: Number of months in the renewal or recurrence cycle for this obligation (e.g., 12 for annual, 3 for quarterly, 1 for monthly). Used by the compliance calendar engine to auto-generate future obligation instances. Null for one-time or event-triggered obligations.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; IRS Filing Deadlines; SEC Reporting Calendar

description: Name of the internal department or business unit primarily responsible for fulfilling this obligation (e.g., 'Actuarial', 'Legal & Compliance', 'Finance', 'Tax', 'Operations', 'Investment Management'). Used for workload assignment and escalation routing.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this obligation record originates or is primarily managed (e.g., 'Compliance Calendar System', 'Policy Administration System', 'Actuarial Valuation System'). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the lakehouse.
reference: ACORD Data Standards; SOX Section 404 IT Controls

description: The legally mandated deadline by which this obligation must be fulfilled as specified by the governing body or regulation. For recurring obligations, this represents the due date for the current or next filing period. Failure to meet this date may result in regulatory penalties.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; IRS Filing Deadlines; SEC Reporting Calendar; DOL ERISA Filing Deadlines

description: The required or preferred method by which this obligation must be submitted to the governing body. 'electronic' = NAIC SERFF or similar; 'portal' = regulator-specific web portal (e.g., SEC EDGAR, IRS e-file); 'api' = machine-to-machine submission; 'paper' = physical mailing.
reference: NAIC SERFF; SEC EDGAR; IRS e-file; FinCEN BSA E-Filing System

description: The required data format or form number for the filing submission (e.g., 'NAIC Annual Statement Blank', 'Form N-CEN', 'Form 5500', 'SAR FinCEN 111', 'ACORD XML'). Ensures the compliance team prepares the filing in the correct format required by the governing body.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; SEC Form N-CEN; DOL Form 5500; FinCEN SAR; ACORD Data Standards

description: Unique identifier for the state-specific insurance regulation record. Primary key.
Reference to the newer state regulation that replaces this regulation, if applicable. Null if not superseded.
Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing accelerated death benefit riders. True if ADB regulations apply, False if not.
Indicates whether the state has adopted a best interest standard for annuity sales (similar to Regulation Best Interest). True if best interest standard applies, False if not.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation 275 - Annuity Best Interest

description: Internal notes documenting compliance considerations, implementation guidance, or special handling requirements for this state regulation.
Number of months during which the insurer may contest a life insurance policy for material misrepresentation, as mandated by state law. Typically 24 months but may vary by state.
Timestamp when this state regulation record was first created in the system.
Date when the state-specific regulation becomes enforceable and applicable to insurance operations.
Date when the state regulation ceases to be in effect, if applicable. Null for regulations with no defined end date.
State-mandated method for submitting and approving policy forms (prior approval required before use, file and use after DOI review, use and file with post-use notification, no filing required).
Number of days the policyholder has to review and cancel a newly issued policy without penalty, as mandated by state regulation. Varies by state and product type.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Free Look Provisions

description: Number of days after premium due date during which coverage remains in force without lapse, as required by state regulation. Typically 30-31 days but varies by state.
Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing life insurance policy illustrations. True if illustration rules apply, False if not.
reference: NAIC Life Insurance Illustrations Model Regulation

description: State-specific definition and requirements for establishing insurable interest in life insurance transactions. Describes when insurable interest must exist (at inception, at death, etc.).
reference: State Insurance Code - Insurable Interest Provisions

description: State-mandated minimum guaranteed interest rate for cash value accumulation in life insurance and annuity products. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 for 2%).
Name of the state regulatory body that issued or enforces this regulation (e.g., California Department of Insurance, New York Department of Financial Services).
Full name of the state or territory regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., California, New York, Texas).
Two-letter state code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Aligns with NAIC state codes.
Date when the state regulation was last amended or revised. Null if no amendments have been made since original enactment.
Timestamp when this state regulation record was last modified in the system.
Indicates whether the state participates in the Long-Term Care Partnership Program, allowing policyholders to protect assets while qualifying for Medicaid. True if partnership state, False if not.
reference: Deficit Reduction Act of 2005 - LTC Partnership Program

description: State-mandated minimum cash surrender value as a percentage of premiums paid or reserves, as required by nonforfeiture laws. Null if no state-specific requirement.
State-mandated minimum death benefit amount for life insurance policies issued in the jurisdiction. Null if no state-specific minimum applies.
State-mandated method for calculating nonforfeiture values (Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method, Commissioners Extended Term Method, Cash Surrender Value Method).
Indicates whether the state mandates nonforfeiture benefits (cash surrender value, reduced paid-up insurance, extended term insurance) for this product type. True if required, False if not.
Insurance product line(s) to which this state regulation applies (e.g., Life, Annuity, Variable Life, Variable Annuity, Long-Term Care). May contain multiple values separated by semicolons.
State-mandated method for submitting and approving insurance rates (prior approval required before use, file and use after DOI review, use and file with post-use notification, no filing required).
Official citation reference to the state statute, regulation, or administrative code section that establishes this requirement (e.g., California Insurance Code Section 10113.71).
reference: State Insurance Code and Administrative Regulations

description: Detailed description of the state-specific regulatory requirement, including key provisions, applicability conditions, and compliance obligations.
reference: State Insurance Code and Administrative Regulations

description: Current lifecycle status of the state regulation (active, superseded by newer regulation, pending approval, withdrawn).
Category of state-specific insurance regulation (policy form approval, rate filing requirements, nonforfeiture standards, free look period, grace period, replacement regulations).
Indicates whether the state requires written notice to the existing insurer when a policy replacement is proposed. True if notice required, False if not.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance and Annuity Replacements

description: Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing policy replacement transactions (1035 exchanges, internal replacements). True if state has replacement rules, False if not.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation on Life Insurance and Annuity Replacements

business_glossary_term: System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing (SERFF) Filing Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the state requires rate and form filings to be submitted through the NAIC SERFF system. True if SERFF required, False if alternative methods allowed.
Indicates whether the state has enacted specific prohibitions against stranger-originated life insurance transactions. True if STOLI prohibited, False if not.
Number of months during which death by suicide is excluded from life insurance coverage, as mandated by state law. Typically 24 months but may vary by state.
reference: State Insurance Code - Suicide Exclusion Provisions

description: Indicates whether the state mandates suitability reviews and documentation for annuity sales. True if suitability review required, False if not.
reference: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation

description: Indicates whether the state requires separate registration or approval for variable life and variable annuity products beyond standard form filing. True if additional registration required, False if not.
reference: SEC and State DOI Variable Product Standards

description: Unique identifier for the RBC calculation record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to investment.alm_analysis. Business justification: RBC C3 interest rate risk charge is sourced directly from ALM analysis (duration gap, DV01, interest rate sensitivity scenarios). NAIC RBC C3 Phase I/II instructions require ALM results as inputs. alm

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: RBC C-2 insurance risk and C-3 market risk components are computed using actuarial assumptions. Direct link to assumption_set supports NAIC RBC filing documentation and regulatory examination of assum

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: RBC filings require supporting schedules, investment listings, and reinsurance attestations filed with state regulators. Annual RBC filing and regulatory compliance depend on linking calculations to f
Identifier of the legal entity for which RBC is calculated. Links to the legal entity master.

Foreign key linking to actuarial.pbr_model_segment. Business justification: RBC C-2 insurance risk for PBR companies is computed at the pbr_model_segment level. Direct FK enables RBC-PBR segment reconciliation required under NAIC VM-20/VM-21 and supports the appointed actuary

Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: RBC C2 insurance risk component is calculated by product line for regulatory capital adequacy reporting. Required for Annual Statement Schedule S filing, capital planning, and product pricing. Links R

Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: RBC C1 asset risk charges (fixed income, equity, real estate) are computed at the investment portfolio level. NAIC RBC instructions require portfolio-level asset classification. rbc_calculation has c1

Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: RBC C3 market risk (Phase II) for variable annuities is computed at the separate account level using stochastic scenarios. NAIC RBC C3 Phase II instructions require separate account identification. rb

Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: RBC C2 insurance risk component calculations depend on actuarial reserve valuations (statutory reserves, PBR reserves). Direct link enables reconciliation between RBC filings and actuarial memorandums
Indicates which regulatory action level threshold has been triggered based on the RBC ratio (no_action, company_action, regulatory_action, authorized_control, mandatory_control).
reference: NAIC Model Act #312 - Risk-Based Capital for Insurers

description: The auditor's opinion on the RBC calculation if reviewed (unqualified, qualified, adverse, disclaimer, not_audited).
Indicates whether the RBC calculation has been reviewed by external auditors as part of the financial statement audit.
The authorized control level RBC amount. Represents the minimum capital threshold below which regulatory control is authorized.
reference: NAIC Model Act #312 - Risk-Based Capital for Insurers

description: RBC charge for asset risk related to investments in affiliates and subsidiaries.
RBC charge for asset risk on equity investments and common stock.
RBC charge for asset default risk on fixed income securities (bonds, mortgages).
RBC charge for asset risk on other invested assets not classified elsewhere.
RBC charge for asset risk on real estate holdings and property investments.
RBC charge for underwriting and pricing risk related to insurance liabilities and reserves.
RBC charge for interest rate and asset-liability mismatch risk.
RBC charge for market risk on variable annuity guarantees and other market-sensitive products.
RBC charge for general business risk including administrative expenses and guaranty fund assessments.
The date on which the RBC calculation was performed.
The methodology used for RBC calculation (formula_based, scenario_based, stochastic, hybrid).
Current lifecycle status of the RBC calculation (draft, preliminary, final, filed, amended, superseded).
Type of RBC calculation based on insurance line of business (life, property/casualty, health).
reference: NAIC RBC Instructions by Line of Business

description: The company action level RBC amount. Represents the threshold at which the company must submit a corrective action plan.
reference: NAIC Model Act #312 - Risk-Based Capital for Insurers

description: Adjustment factor applied to recognize diversification benefits across risk categories using the NAIC covariance formula.
Timestamp when this RBC calculation record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this RBC calculation has been officially filed with the state insurance regulator.
The date on which the RBC report was filed with the state insurance department.
Method used to file the RBC report with the regulator (electronic, paper, exempt).
The mandatory control level RBC amount. Represents the threshold at which the regulator must place the company under regulatory control.
reference: NAIC Model Act #312 - Risk-Based Capital for Insurers

description: User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this RBC calculation record.
Timestamp when this RBC calculation record was last modified.
Free-text notes and commentary regarding the RBC calculation, including assumptions, adjustments, or special circumstances.

business_glossary_term: Own Risk and Solvency Assessment (ORSA) Alignment Flag
description: Indicates whether this RBC calculation aligns with the company's ORSA capital assessment and risk management framework.
The ratio of Total Adjusted Capital to Authorized Control Level RBC, expressed as a percentage. Key metric for regulatory capital adequacy.
The state or jurisdiction code of the primary insurance regulator (e.g., NY, CA, TX).
The regulatory action level RBC amount. Represents the threshold at which the regulator may take corrective action.
reference: NAIC Model Act #312 - Risk-Based Capital for Insurers

description: The quarter within the reporting year (1-4). Null for annual-only calculations.
The calendar year for which RBC is being reported.
Total adjusted capital available to support risk. Calculated as statutory capital and surplus plus other adjustments per NAIC formula.
Total RBC requirement after applying the covariance adjustment for diversification.
Sum of all RBC risk components (C-0 through C-4) before applying the covariance adjustment.
Result of the RBC trend test comparing current year to prior year capital adequacy (pass, fail, not_applicable).
Primary key for reinsurance_recoverable

Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: reinsurance_recoverable has gl_account_code (STRING) and should FK to chart_of_accounts. Recoverables are booked to GL accounts; FK is essential for financial statement classification and RBC reportin
Identifier of the underlying claim to which this recoverable relates, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: reinsurance_recoverable has cost_center_code (STRING) and should FK to cost_center. Recoverables are allocated to cost centers for expense management; FK enables cost allocation tracking.
Identifier of the underlying insurance policy to which this recoverable relates, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: reinsurance_recoverable has journal_entry_number (STRING) representing the JE that posted the recoverable. FK to journal_entry provides critical audit trail linking reinsurance accounting to financial

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: reinsurance_recoverable has legal_entity_code (STRING) and should FK to legal_entity. Recoverables are owned by legal entities; FK is essential for statutory reporting, RBC calculations, and credit ri
Identifier of the reinsurer counterparty from whom the recoverable is due.

Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: reinsurance_recoverable amounts are derived from actuarial reserve_calculation.reinsurance_ceded_reserve. Direct link supports ceded reserve reconciliation between finance and actuarial — a mandatory

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinsurance recoverables require treaty documents, bordereaux, and collateral agreements for collectibility assessment. Quarterly reinsurance accounting and credit risk assessment depend on linking re
Identifier of the reinsurance treaty under which this recoverable arises.

The accounting framework under which this recoverable balance is reported: Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), International Financial Reporting Standards 17 (IFRS17), or tax basis.
The accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which this recoverable balance applies.
The aging category of the recoverable based on the number of days outstanding since the recoverable became due.
The estimated amount of the gross recoverable that may not be collectible due to reinsurer credit risk, recorded as a contra-asset.
The approval workflow status of this recoverable balance entry: draft, pending approval, approved, or rejected.
The name or identifier of the individual who approved this recoverable balance for financial reporting.
The date and time when this recoverable balance was approved for financial reporting.
The total value of collateral held to secure the recoverable, including letters of credit, trust accounts, and funds withheld.
The form of collateral securing the recoverable: letter of credit (LOC), trust account, funds withheld at source, or none.

description: The date and time when this recoverable balance record was first created in the system.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the recoverable amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The number of calendar days the recoverable has been outstanding since it became due for payment.
The fiscal quarter (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) to which this recoverable balance applies for quarterly financial reporting.
The fiscal year to which this recoverable balance applies for annual financial reporting.
The balance sheet classification of the recoverable under GAAP: current asset (collectible within 12 months) or non-current asset.
The general ledger account code to which this recoverable balance is posted in the chart of accounts.
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: The total amount recoverable from the reinsurer before any allowance for uncollectible reinsurance or offsets.

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standards 17 (IFRS17) Contractual Service Margin (CSM) Impact
description: The impact of this recoverable on the Contractual Service Margin under IFRS 17 accounting for insurance contracts.
The date and time when this recoverable balance record was last modified or updated.
The net recoverable balance after deducting the allowance for uncollectible reinsurance from the gross recoverable amount.
Free-form text field for additional comments, explanations, or special circumstances related to this recoverable balance.
The date and time when this recoverable balance was posted to the general ledger.
The product line code (e.g., term life, whole life, annuity) to which this recoverable relates for segment reporting.
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Exhibit of Premiums and Losses

description: The Risk-Based Capital category assigned to this recoverable for statutory capital adequacy calculations.
reference: NAIC Risk-Based Capital for Insurers Model Act

description: The current lifecycle status of the recoverable: billed to reinsurer, unbilled (accrued but not yet invoiced), disputed, collected, or written off.
Classification of the recoverable by its nature: ceded reserve, ceded claim payment, ceded premium refund, ceded benefit, or ceded commission.
The financial strength rating of the reinsurer as assigned by AM Best or equivalent rating agency (e.g., A++, A+, A, B++, NR for not rated).
The specific line item on the NAIC Annual Statement Schedule F or other exhibit where this recoverable is reported.
The name or code of the source system from which this recoverable balance originated (e.g., reinsurance management system, actuarial valuation system).
Indicates whether this recoverable balance is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures.
The as-of date for which this recoverable balance was calculated and recorded.
Unique identifier for the income tax provision record. Primary key for the tax provision entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Tax provisions are posted to specific GL accounts defined in the chart of accounts (e.g., current tax expense, deferred tax asset/liability accounts). tax_provision currently has no chart_of_accounts_
Reference to the legal entity for which this tax provision is recorded. Each legal entity files separate tax returns and maintains separate tax positions.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Tax provisions require supporting documentation: IRS correspondence, external auditor workpapers, tax return filings, and SOX control evidence. Life insurance finance teams attach provision documentat

Foreign key linking to policy.tax_compliance_test. Business justification: Tax provision IRC 7702 adjustment field is directly driven by policy-level compliance test results (MEC status, 7-pay limits). Finance tax teams reference compliance test records when computing irc_77

Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Tax provision deferred tax calculation requires investment fair values for unrealized gain/loss temporary differences (tax_provision.unrealized_gain_temporary_difference). ASC 740 and IRC Section 1221

Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: tax_provision.reserve_temporary_difference and dac_temporary_difference are derived from actuarial valuation results. Direct link to valuation_run supports IRC 807 tax reserve calculations, deferred t
Current status of tax authority audit or examination for this tax period. Tracks IRS or state tax authority review lifecycle.
The date and time when this tax provision calculation was performed. Used for audit trail and version control.
The date and time when this tax provision record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this tax provision record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The portion of total income tax expense attributable to taxes currently payable or refundable for the reporting period. Represents cash tax impact.
Temporary difference arising from DAC capitalization for GAAP versus immediate deduction for tax. Major source of deferred tax liability for life insurance companies.
reference: IRC Section 848 Capitalization of Policy Acquisition Expenses

description: The portion of total income tax expense attributable to changes in deferred tax assets and liabilities. Positive values represent expense; negative values represent benefit.
Total deferred tax asset balance as of the reporting period end. Represents future tax benefits from temporary differences, NOL carryforwards, and tax credit carryforwards.
Total deferred tax liability balance as of the reporting period end. Represents future tax obligations from temporary differences such as accelerated depreciation and DAC capitalization.
The ratio of total income tax expense to pretax income, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2100 for 21%). Key metric for tax efficiency analysis and rate reconciliation disclosure.
Indicates whether this tax provision has been reviewed by external auditors as part of financial statement audit procedures.
The fiscal year to which this tax provision applies (e.g., 2024). Used for multi-year tax analysis and trend reporting.
Income tax expense attributable to foreign jurisdictions. Includes taxes paid to foreign governments on international operations and investments.

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Reporting Basis
description: The accounting framework under which this tax provision was prepared (US GAAP, IFRS, or Statutory Accounting Principles).

business_glossary_term: Internal Revenue Code (IRC) Section 7702 Tax Compliance Adjustment
description: Tax provision adjustment related to IRC Section 7702 life insurance contract qualification testing. Ensures products meet tax-favored treatment requirements.
reference: IRC Section 7702 Life Insurance Contract Defined

business_glossary_term: Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) Tax Impact
description: Tax provision impact from FASB ASU 2018-12 LDTI implementation. Captures deferred tax effects of changes in insurance liability measurement and DAC amortization.
reference: ASC 944-40 LDTI and ASC 740-10 Tax Effects

description: Net deferred tax asset or liability, calculated as DTA balance minus DTL balance. Positive values indicate net asset; negative values indicate net liability.
Tax net operating loss carryforward available to offset future taxable income. Generates deferred tax asset based on expected utilization and statutory tax rate.
reference: IRC Section 172 Net Operating Loss Deduction

description: Free-text field for documenting significant assumptions, judgments, tax planning strategies, or unusual items affecting the tax provision. Supports audit documentation requirements.
Tax provision impact from permanent book-tax differences that do not reverse over time (e.g., tax-exempt interest, non-deductible expenses, dividends received deduction).
Income before income taxes per GAAP financial statements. Used as the base for effective tax rate calculation.
The date on which the tax provision was calculated and recorded. Represents the business event timestamp for this tax calculation.
The methodology used to calculate the tax provision for interim periods. Discrete method treats each period independently; annual effective rate method estimates full-year rate.
Current lifecycle status of the tax provision record. Tracks progression from initial draft through final audited state.
Impact on deferred tax balances from changes in enacted tax rates. Requires remeasurement of deferred tax assets and liabilities at new rates.
Temporary difference between GAAP insurance reserves and tax reserves. Reflects differences in reserve calculation methodologies between financial reporting and tax compliance.
Adjustment to reconcile prior period tax provision estimates to actual amounts reported on filed tax returns. Recorded when tax returns are finalized.
Name or identifier of the senior tax professional who reviewed and approved this tax provision. Required for segregation of duties.
Indicates whether this tax provision has been reviewed and certified under SOX Section 404 internal control requirements. Required for public companies.
Income tax expense attributable to state and local jurisdictions. Tracked separately from federal tax for rate reconciliation and multi-state tax planning.
reference: State Departments of Insurance Tax Regulations

description: The federal statutory corporate income tax rate applicable to the reporting period (e.g., 0.2100 for 21% under IRC Section 11). Starting point for effective tax rate reconciliation.
Tax credits available to reduce future tax liabilities, including general business credits, foreign tax credits, and alternative minimum tax credits. Generates deferred tax asset.
Indicates whether the tax return for this period has been filed with tax authorities. True when return is filed; False when provision is estimated.
Total income tax expense for the period, calculated as the sum of current tax expense and deferred tax expense. Reported on the GAAP income statement.
Temporary difference from unrealized gains and losses on investments recognized in GAAP financial statements but not yet taxable. Includes available-for-sale securities and fair value adjustments.
reference: IRC Section 1001 Realization of Gain or Loss

description: The date and time when this tax provision record was last modified. Tracks all subsequent changes after initial creation.
Reserve for unrecognized tax benefits under ASC 740-10 (formerly FIN 48). Represents tax positions that do not meet the more-likely-than-not recognition threshold.
Contra-asset account reducing deferred tax assets to the amount that is more-likely-than-not to be realized. Reflects management judgment on future taxable income sufficiency.
Unique identifier for the chart of accounts entry. Primary key for the chart of accounts master reference.
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles

description: The unique alphanumeric code identifying the general ledger account. This is the externally-known business identifier used in all financial transactions and reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: The full descriptive name of the general ledger account. Human-readable label used in financial statements and reports.
reference: FASB ASC 944, GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the account type (e.g., current asset, fixed asset, long-term liability, operating expense).
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles

description: The fundamental accounting classification of the account. Determines the account's position in the balance sheet or income statement and its behavior in the accounting equation.
Flag indicating whether changes to this account definition require formal approval workflow due to materiality, regulatory impact, or SOX controls.
reference: Internal Financial Control Standards, SOX Compliance

description: Flag indicating whether this account participates in the annual budgeting process and budget vs. actual variance reporting.
The section of the cash flow statement (operating, investing, or financing activities) where transactions in this account are classified.
reference: FASB ASC 230 Statement of Cash Flows, GAAP

description: Extended business description of the account's purpose, usage guidelines, and the types of transactions that should be posted to this account.
Current lifecycle status of the chart of accounts entry. Active accounts are available for posting; inactive accounts are retained for historical reporting but closed to new transactions.
Flag indicating whether a cost center dimension is required when posting transactions to this account for expense tracking and allocation.
The date and time when this chart of accounts entry was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and change management.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for accounts denominated in a specific currency. Null for accounts that aggregate multi-currency balances.
Flag indicating whether this account is eligible for deferred acquisition cost (DAC) asset tracking and amortization under insurance accounting standards.
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles

description: The date when this account became active and available for use in the general ledger. Supports chart of accounts change management and historical analysis.
The date when this account was or will be inactivated and closed to new postings. Null for accounts that remain active indefinitely.
The mapping code used to translate this internal account to external regulatory reporting formats (NAIC Annual Statement, SEC 10-K, state insurance department filings).
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions, SEC Regulation S-X

description: The specific line item on the financial statement (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement) where this account's balance is reported.
The classification of this account under US GAAP for external financial reporting and SEC filings.
reference: FASB ASC 944, Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)

description: The depth level of this account in the chart of accounts hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level summary accounts; higher numbers represent more detailed sub-accounts.
The classification of this account under IFRS 17 insurance contracts standard for international financial reporting. Includes insurance finance income/expense designation.
reference: IFRS 17 Insurance Contracts, International Accounting Standards Board (IASB)

description: Flag indicating whether this account is used for intercompany transactions requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements.
Flag indicating whether this account is impacted by FASB ASC 944 Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) accounting standard changes effective 2023.
reference: FASB ASC 944 Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI)

description: The user ID or name of the person who last modified this chart of accounts entry. Supports audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this chart of accounts entry was last modified. Supports audit trail and change management.
Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance. Assets and expenses typically have debit balances; liabilities, equity, and revenue have credit balances.
The account code of the parent account in the chart of accounts hierarchy. Used to build roll-up structures for financial reporting and consolidation.

description: Flag indicating whether direct journal entry postings are allowed to this account. Summary accounts typically do not allow direct posting.

description: Flag indicating whether this is a summary/rollup account that aggregates balances from child accounts, or a detail/posting account that receives direct journal entries.
The classification of this account under NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles for regulatory financial reporting to state insurance departments.

description: Flag indicating whether this account is subject to enhanced Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) internal controls and audit procedures due to materiality or risk.
The classification of this account for federal and state tax return preparation and IRS reporting under IRC Section 7702 and other tax regulations.

description: The user ID or name of the person who created this chart of accounts entry. Supports audit trail and accountability.

description: Classification of the journal entry based on its purpose in the accounting cycle. Actual amortization expense recognized in the current accounting period. May differ from scheduled due to experience adjustments, policy lapses, or surrenders.

Timestamp when this DAC asset record was first created in the system.
Total cumulative amortization recognized from policy issuance through the current accounting period.

Current lifecycle status of the DAC asset. Active indicates ongoing amortization; Fully Amortized indicates complete amortization; Impaired indicates recognized impairment; Terminated indicates policy lapse or surrender.

Total amount of acquisition costs capitalized as a DAC asset at policy issuance or acquisition. Includes commissions, underwriting costs, policy issuance expenses, and other directly attributable costs.
Date on which this DAC asset record became effective, typically the policy issue date or acquisition date.
Flag indicating whether this DAC asset has been reviewed by external auditors in the current audit cycle.

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Reporting Basis
description: Accounting reporting basis under which this DAC asset is recorded. US GAAP follows FASB ASC 944; IFRS follows IFRS 17; Statutory follows NAIC SAP.

business_glossary_term: IFRS 17 Insurance Acquisition Cash Flow Asset
description: Insurance acquisition cash flow asset recognized under IFRS 17 for acquisition costs directly attributable to a group of insurance contracts.

business_glossary_term: IFRS 17 Insurance Acquisition Cash Flow Release
description: Amount of insurance acquisition cash flow asset released to profit or loss in the current period under IFRS 17.
Flag indicating whether an impairment indicator exists for this DAC asset. True if adverse experience, policy lapses, or other factors suggest the asset may not be recoverable.
Impairment loss recognized in the current period if the DAC asset is determined to be unrecoverable.
Interest accretion recognized on the DAC asset balance for the current period under interest-related amortization methods.
Calendar year in which the underlying policies were issued. Used for cohort grouping and amortization tracking.
Date of the most recent amortization transaction for this DAC asset.
Timestamp when this DAC asset record was last modified.

business_glossary_term: Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) Cohort Grouping Key
description: Composite key identifying the LDTI cohort grouping for this DAC asset. Cohorts are defined by product type, issue year, underwriting class, and other contract characteristics.
Code identifying the product line (e.g., Term Life, Whole Life, Universal Life, Variable Annuity) to which this DAC asset belongs.

Status of the actuarial-to-finance reconciliation for this DAC asset. Reconciled indicates agreement between actuarial and finance systems; Variance Identified indicates discrepancies requiring resolution.
Planned or scheduled amortization amount for the current accounting period based on the amortization method and actuarial assumptions.
Shadow DAC adjustment for unrealized gains or losses on available-for-sale securities or other comprehensive income items that affect DAC amortization under GAAP.
Code identifying the source system from which this DAC asset record originated (e.g., actuarial valuation system, policy administration system, general ledger).
Flag indicating whether this DAC asset is subject to SOX financial reporting controls. True for material DAC balances requiring enhanced audit and control procedures.
Date on which this DAC asset was fully amortized, impaired, or terminated due to policy lapse or surrender. Null if still active.
Remaining unamortized DAC asset balance as of the accounting period end. Calculated as capitalized amount minus cumulative amortization.
Variance in unamortized DAC balance from the prior accounting period. Calculated as current period unamortized balance minus prior period unamortized balance.

business_glossary_term: Value of Business Acquired (VOBA) Amortization Amount
description: Amortization expense recognized in the current period for the VOBA component of the DAC asset.

business_glossary_term: Value of Business Acquired (VOBA) Component Amount
description: Value of Business Acquired component included in the DAC asset, arising from business combinations or block acquisitions. Amortized separately from internally generated DAC.

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Reserve ID
description: Unique identifier for the GAAP reserve record.

Reference to the insurance contract grouping for GAAP reporting purposes under LDTI cohort methodology.
Reference to the insurance policy contract associated with this reserve.

The financial impact on the reserve balance resulting from actual experience differing from expected assumptions (e.g., actual mortality vs. expected mortality).
The date when this reserve balance was approved by the Chief Actuary or designated authority for inclusion in financial statements.
The name of the individual (typically Chief Actuary or Finance Officer) who approved this reserve calculation.

The date when the cash flow assumptions were last updated or reviewed for this reserve calculation.
Version identifier for the set of actuarial assumptions (mortality, lapse, expense, etc.) used to project future cash flows for this reserve calculation.
The timestamp when this reserve record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the reserve amount.
The deferred acquisition cost asset balance associated with this contract group, amortized over the life of the contracts under GAAP.
The discount rate percentage applied to calculate the net present value of future policy benefits, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.04500 for 4.5%).

The type of discount rate applied to calculate the present value of future cash flows. LOCKED_IN=original cohort rate locked at issue, UPPER_MEDIUM_GRADE=current upper-medium grade fixed income instrument rate, CURRENT_MARKET=current market observable rate.
The assumed annual maintenance expense per policy used in the cash flow projections for this reserve calculation.
Indicates whether this reserve balance has been reviewed and validated by external auditors as part of the annual financial statement audit.
The total face amount (death benefit or account value) of all policies included in this reserve calculation.

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) vs Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP) Difference Amount
description: The difference between the GAAP reserve balance and the corresponding statutory reserve balance for the same contract group, used for reconciliation and regulatory reporting.
The assumed annual lapse (policy termination) rate used in the cash flow projections for this reserve calculation, expressed as a decimal.

business_glossary_term: Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) Cohort Year
description: The calendar year in which the insurance contracts were issued, used for LDTI cohort grouping under FASB ASC 944-40.

business_glossary_term: Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) Transition Adjustment Amount
description: The cumulative adjustment to the reserve balance resulting from the transition to LDTI accounting standards (ASU 2018-12), typically recorded as a cumulative effect adjustment to retained earnings.

business_glossary_term: Market Risk Benefit (MRB) Fair Value Amount
description: The fair value measurement of market risk benefits (e.g., GMDB, GMIB, GMWB) embedded in variable annuity and variable life contracts, measured at fair value under LDTI.

business_glossary_term: Market Risk Benefit (MRB) Instrument Credit Risk Adjustment
description: The adjustment to MRB fair value reflecting the company's own credit risk (non-performance risk) as required under fair value measurement guidance.
The net GAAP reserve balance after deducting reinsurance recoverables (reserve_amount minus reinsurance_recoverable_amount).
Free-text field for additional notes, explanations, or commentary regarding this reserve calculation, including any unusual adjustments or methodology changes.
The number of in-force insurance policies included in this reserve calculation for the contract group and reporting period.
Additional reserve established when a loss recognition event is identified (i.e., when the present value of expected future cash flows exceeds the existing reserve plus anticipated future premiums).

Insurance product line classification. WL=Whole Life, UL=Universal Life, IUL=Indexed Universal Life, VUL=Variable Universal Life, TERM=Term Life, SPIA=Single Premium Immediate Annuity, FIA=Fixed Indexed Annuity, VA=Variable Annuity, DI=Disability Income, LTC=Long-Term Care. The date when the most recent reserve adequacy test was performed.
The result of the most recent reserve adequacy testing (loss recognition testing) performed to ensure reserves are sufficient to cover expected future obligations.
The total GAAP reserve balance amount for this contract group and reporting period, measured in accordance with FASB ASC 944 and LDTI standards.
The actuarial methodology used to calculate the reserve balance (e.g., net premium method, gross premium method, benefit reserve method, fair value measurement).

Classification of the GAAP reserve type. FPB=Future Policy Benefit, PAB=Policyholder Account Balance, MRB=Market Risk Benefit, CLAIM=Claim Reserve, IBNR=Incurred But Not Reported, UNEARNED=Unearned Premium Reserve.

The current status of this reserve calculation in the financial close process. PRELIMINARY=initial calculation, FINAL=approved for financial statements, ADJUSTED=post-close adjustment, RESTATED=prior period restatement.
Indicates whether this reserve balance has been certified under SOX internal controls for financial reporting accuracy and completeness.
The timestamp when this reserve record was last modified.
Unique identifier for the IFRS 17 insurance contract group. Represents the unit of account for measurement and disclosure under IFRS 17.

Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: IFRS17 contract groups ARE cohorts under the standard (annual cohorts of onerous/non-onerous contracts). Direct link enables cohort-level CSM tracking, loss component management, and regulatory disclo

Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: IFRS 17 contract groups are defined by product and cohort year for international financial reporting. Required for CSM calculation, insurance revenue recognition, and onerous contract testing. Links c
Reference to the reinsurance contract group held that covers this direct insurance contract group, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: IFRS 17 requires contract groups to be segregated by profitability and risk profile at initial recognition. Underwriting risk class is a primary determinant of onerous vs. non-onerous contract group c
The unearned profit component at the end of the reporting period after all movements including release, interest accretion, and adjustments.
The present value of future cash flows at the end of the reporting period after all movements and adjustments.
Description of the contract boundary determination, identifying the point at which the entity has the practical ability to reassess risks and can set a price that reflects those risks.
Business identifier code for the contract group used in financial reporting and actuarial systems.
Indicates whether this measurement relates to the liability for remaining coverage or the liability for incurred claims.
The quantity of coverage units at the end of the period after adjustments for service provided and changes in expected coverage.
The quantity of coverage units at the beginning of the period, used to determine the pattern of CSM release. Coverage units represent the quantity of service provided by the contracts.
The date and time when this contract group record was first created in the system.

business_glossary_term: Contractual Service Margin (CSM) Assumption Change Impact
description: Adjustments to CSM resulting from changes in estimates of future cash flows relating to future service, excluding changes in financial assumptions when the entity has chosen to disaggregate insurance finance income or expense.

business_glossary_term: Contractual Service Margin (CSM) Currency Exchange Impact
description: The effect of changes in foreign exchange rates on the CSM for contracts denominated in foreign currencies.
Adjustments to CSM for differences between expected and actual cash flows relating to future service.
The interest accreted on the CSM balance during the period using discount rates determined at initial recognition.

business_glossary_term: Contractual Service Margin (CSM) New Business Addition
description: The CSM arising from new contracts added to the group during the period.
The amount of CSM recognized as insurance revenue in the current period based on coverage units provided.
The date when the contract group was derecognized from the statement of financial position, when all rights and obligations are extinguished or transferred.
The discount rate applied to the fulfilment cash flows at the end of the reporting period.
The discount rate applied to the fulfilment cash flows at the beginning of the reporting period, reflecting the time value of money and financial risk.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates for this contract group.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The current lifecycle status of the contract group: active (in force), closed (no longer accepting new contracts but existing contracts remain), or derecognized (fully extinguished).
The date when the contract group was first recognized in the financial statements, typically the earliest of: beginning of coverage period, first payment due, or when the group becomes onerous.
The change in the carrying amount of the group of insurance contracts arising from the time value of money, financial risk, and changes in financial risk.
The amount of insurance revenue recognized in profit or loss during the period, comprising the release of CSM, risk adjustment, and expected incurred claims.

The total insurance service expenses recognized in profit or loss, including incurred claims, other incurred expenses, amortization of insurance acquisition cash flows, and losses on onerous contracts.
The closing balance of the loss component for onerous contract groups after movements during the period.
The opening balance of the loss component for onerous contract groups. Represents the extent to which the fulfilment cash flows exceed the carrying amount of the liability for remaining coverage.
The IFRS 17 measurement model applied to this contract group: General Measurement Model (GMM), Premium Allocation Approach (PAA), or Variable Fee Approach (VFA) for direct participating contracts.
The portion of insurance finance income or expense recognized in other comprehensive income when the entity has elected to disaggregate insurance finance income or expense.

business_glossary_term: Other Comprehensive Income (OCI) vs Profit or Loss (P&L) Allocation Election
description: The accounting policy choice for presenting insurance finance income or expense: entirely in profit or loss, or disaggregated between profit or loss and OCI.
The unearned profit component at the beginning of the reporting period. Represents the unearned profit the entity will recognize as it provides services under the insurance contracts.
The present value of future cash flows at the beginning of the reporting period. Comprises estimates of future cash flows, discount rate adjustment, and risk adjustment for non-financial risk.
Classification of the contract group based on initial recognition assessment: onerous (expected to be loss-making), not onerous (profitable), or no significant possibility of becoming onerous.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the contract group measurements are reported.
The last day of the reporting period for which this contract group measurement applies.
The first day of the reporting period for which this contract group measurement applies.

business_glossary_term: Risk Adjustment for Non-Financial Risk Closing Balance
description: The compensation the entity requires for bearing the uncertainty about the amount and timing of cash flows from non-financial risk at the end of the period.

business_glossary_term: Risk Adjustment for Non-Financial Risk Opening Balance
description: The compensation the entity requires for bearing the uncertainty about the amount and timing of cash flows from non-financial risk at the beginning of the period.
The amount of risk adjustment recognized in profit or loss as the entity is released from risk during the period.
The transition method applied when IFRS 17 was first adopted for this contract group: full retrospective application, modified retrospective approach, or fair value approach.
The date and time when this contract group record was last modified.
Unique identifier for the IFRS 17 measurement record.

Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: CSM releases and risk adjustment changes require supporting actuarial reports and assumption change documentation. Quarterly IFRS 17 measurement and disclosure processes depend on linking measurements

Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: IFRS17 requires disclosure of assumption changes and their CSM/FCF impact. Direct link from ifrs17_measurement to assumption_set enables assumption unlock analysis and IFRS17 Note disclosures — a mand

Foreign key linking to actuarial.cash_flow_projection. Business justification: IFRS17 fulfilment cash flows (FCF) are directly sourced from actuarial cash_flow_projection. Direct FK enables IFRS17 FCF reconciliation and supports IFRS17 Note disclosures on future cash flow estima
Identifier for the insurance contract group to which this measurement applies. Contract groups are portfolios of insurance contracts managed together under IFRS 17.
Identifier for the actuarial valuation run that produced this measurement, linking to the actuarial system for audit trail and reconciliation.
Expenses incurred during the reporting period for acquiring new insurance contracts, including commissions and underwriting costs.
The user ID or name of the individual who approved this measurement for financial reporting, supporting SOX compliance and audit trail.
The date and time when this measurement was approved for financial reporting.
The total impact on the insurance contract liability from changes in actuarial assumptions during the reporting period.
The closing balance of the Contractual Service Margin at the end of the reporting period.
The closing balance of the present value of future cash flows at the end of the reporting period.
The closing balance of the risk adjustment for non-financial risk at the end of the reporting period.
The year in which the insurance contracts in this group were initially recognized, used for cohort-based measurement under IFRS 17.
The quantity of coverage units provided during the reporting period, used to allocate CSM release. Coverage units reflect the quantity of benefits provided under the insurance contracts.
The date and time when this measurement record was first created in the system.
Adjustment to CSM arising from changes in estimates of future cash flows that relate to future service (e.g., changes in mortality, lapse, expense assumptions).
Adjustment to CSM arising from differences between actual experience and previous estimates (experience variances) that relate to future service.
The interest accreted on the CSM balance during the reporting period, calculated using the discount rate locked in at initial recognition.
The amount of CSM released to profit or loss as insurance revenue during the reporting period, reflecting the insurance service provided.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the measurement amounts are denominated.
The discount rate applied to future cash flows for this measurement, reflecting the time value of money and characteristics of the cash flows.
The difference between actual experience and previous estimates during the reporting period, recognized immediately in profit or loss for past service.
The portion of insurance finance income or expense recognized in other comprehensive income (OCI) during the reporting period, when the entity elects the OCI option.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Disaggregation of Insurance Finance Income or Expenses

description: The portion of insurance finance income or expense recognized in profit or loss during the reporting period.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Disaggregation of Insurance Finance Income or Expenses

description: The amount of claims incurred during the reporting period, including claims paid and changes in claims liabilities.
Finance expense recognized during the reporting period from the unwinding of discount on insurance contract liabilities and changes in discount rates.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Insurance Finance Income or Expenses

description: Finance income recognized during the reporting period from the unwinding of discount on insurance contract liabilities and changes in discount rates.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Insurance Finance Income or Expenses

description: Total insurance revenue recognized in profit or loss during the reporting period, comprising CSM release, risk adjustment release, and expected claims and expenses.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Statement of Profit or Loss

description: Total insurance service expenses recognized in profit or loss during the reporting period, including incurred claims, acquisition expenses, and other directly attributable expenses.
reference: IFRS 17 Standard - Statement of Profit or Loss

description: The loss component balance for onerous contract groups, tracked separately to determine the allocation of subsequent changes in fulfilment cash flows.
The date as of which the IFRS 17 measurement is performed, typically the reporting period end date.

description: The IFRS 17 measurement model applied to this contract group: General Measurement Model (GMM), Premium Allocation Approach (PAA), or Variable Fee Approach (VFA).

The date and time when this measurement record was last modified.
Loss recognized immediately in profit or loss when a group of insurance contracts becomes onerous (i.e., the fulfilment cash flows exceed any remaining CSM).

The opening balance of the Contractual Service Margin at the beginning of the reporting period. CSM represents the unearned profit that will be recognized as insurance revenue over the coverage period.
The opening balance of the present value of future cash flows (estimates of future cash inflows and outflows) at the beginning of the reporting period.

The opening balance of the risk adjustment for non-financial risk at the beginning of the reporting period. Risk adjustment represents the compensation required for bearing uncertainty about the amount and timing of cash flows.
The CSM for reinsurance contracts held, representing the unearned benefit from reinsurance coverage.
The present value of future cash flows for reinsurance contracts held, representing the reinsurance asset or liability.
The risk adjustment for non-financial risk on reinsurance contracts held.
Change in risk adjustment during the period due to changes in estimates of non-financial risk.
The amount of risk adjustment released to profit or loss during the reporting period as risk is reduced through the passage of time and claim settlement.

Reference to the actuarial valuation run that produced the reserve calculation handed off to finance for booking. Links to the actuarial domain source computation.

description: The total statutory reserve amount booked by finance for this policy cohort and reporting period, expressed in USD. This is the finance-booked position after actuarial hand-off and management sign-off.

business_glossary_term: Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method (CARVM) Reserve Amount
description: The CARVM reserve component for annuity contracts calculated using Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method. Null if not applicable to the product line.
Identifier for the policy cohort grouping used for reserve aggregation. Cohorts are typically defined by issue year, product type, and underwriting class for reserve modeling purposes.
The timestamp when this statutory reserve record was first created in the finance system. Part of the audit trail for record lineage.

business_glossary_term: Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method (CRVM) Reserve Amount
description: The CRVM reserve component for life insurance policies calculated using Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method. Null if not applicable to the product line.
Additional reserve held when the statutory reserve basis produces a reserve lower than the net premium reserve, ensuring reserve adequacy. Required under NAIC guidelines when deficiency exists.
The specific line number on the NAIC annual statement exhibit (Exhibit 5, 6, 7, or 8) where this reserve is reported. Used for regulatory filing reconciliation.
The date when management approved the statutory reserve amount for booking in the general ledger. Part of the SOX-controlled financial close process.

description: The margin held above the minimum statutory reserve requirement to ensure reserve adequacy under adverse scenarios. Part of PBR stochastic reserve calculations.

The statutory reserve methodology basis. Values: PBR (Principle-Based Reserving per VM-20/VM-21), CRVM (Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method for life), CARVM (Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method), NET_PREMIUM (Net Premium Reserve), MODIFIED_RESERVE (Modified Reserve Method).
reference: NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20, VM-21, VM-A

description: Detailed description of the reserve calculation methodology applied, including any company-specific adjustments or assumptions beyond standard statutory methods.

High-level reserve category for NAIC annual statement exhibit classification. Values: LIFE (life insurance reserves), ANNUITY (annuity reserves), ACCIDENT_HEALTH (accident and health reserves), DEPOSIT_TYPE (deposit-type contract reserves).
reference: NAIC Annual Statement Instructions - Exhibit 5, Exhibit 6, Exhibit 7, Exhibit 8

description: The change in statutory reserve from the prior period to the current period, calculated as statutory_reserve_amount minus prior_period_reserve_amount. Represents reserve strengthening or release.

Code indicating the primary driver of reserve change. Values: NEW_BUSINESS (new policy issuance), MORTALITY (mortality experience), LAPSE (lapse/surrender activity), INTEREST (interest rate changes), ASSUMPTION_CHANGE (assumption updates), REINSURANCE (reinsurance treaty changes).

The status of the statutory reserve booking. Values: PRELIMINARY (initial actuarial calculation), FINAL (management approved and booked), ADJUSTED (post-close adjustment), RESTATED (prior period restatement).
reference: FASB ASC 944, NAIC Annual Statement Instructions

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this reserve record is subject to SOX internal controls over financial reporting. True for material reserve balances requiring enhanced control testing.
Two-letter US state code representing the state of domicile for the legal entity holding this reserve. Determines which state Department of Insurance has primary regulatory authority.
The reserve amount calculated for federal income tax purposes under IRC Section 807. May differ from statutory reserve due to different valuation methods and assumptions.
The timestamp when this statutory reserve record was last updated. Tracks modifications for audit and reconciliation purposes.
The statutory valuation interest rate used for discounting future cash flows in reserve calculations, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 for 3.50%). Prescribed by state law and NAIC guidelines.
